Events 15th Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad: Empowering the Leaders of Tomorrow Happenings bcs1 Events The Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad (BCS) stands as Bharat’s largest classroom for future political leaders. As a non-political platform, it is dedicated to strengthening the foundations of democracy, governance, and ethical leadership by encouraging educated youth to contribute to nation-building. bcs2 The 15th Edition of BCS, that held from February 21st to 23rd, 2026, at the MIT-WPU Pune, continued this legacy under the guidance of visionaries Revered Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad (Founder and Chief Patron, MIT-WPU Pune), Dr. R. A. Mashelkar (Padma Vibhushan and Eminent Scientist), Dr. Rahul V. Karad (Executive President, MIT-WPU Pune) and Dr. R.M.Chitnis (Vice Chancellor, MIT-WPU Pune). bcs3 The 15th BCS was an immersive three-day journey of leadership, wherein, bcs4 The Day 1 commenced with the Inauguration Ceremony graced by Shri Digvijaya Singh (Hon'ble Member of Parliament), Shri Vijender Gupta (Hon'ble Speaker, Delhi Legislative Assembly), and Shri Rajdeep Sardesai (Eminent Journalist). It included thought-provoking addresses that offered diverse perspectives on democracy. The Day 2 featured

entries.title: 500 GW by 2030: India’s Green Leap towards becoming a Superpower
entries.excerpt: Discover how India's push for 500 GW renewable energy by 2030 is driving its rise as a global green superpower. Explore the roadmap and its impact.
entries.text: 500 GW by 2030: India's Green Energy Superpower Leap 500 GW by 2030: India’s Green Leap towards becoming a Superpower Discover how India's push for 500 GW renewable energy by 2030 is driving its rise as a global green superpower. Explore the roadmap and its impact. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / 500 GW by 2030: India’s Green Leap towards becoming a Superpower Faculty Articles 500 GW by 2030: India’s Green Leap towards becoming a Superpower Discover how India's push for 500 GW renewable energy by 2030 is driving its rise as a global green superpower. Explore the roadmap and its impact. 1,392 words 6 min read You must have heard terms like ‘renewable energy’ or ‘clean energy’ a lot lately in the news, in school, maybe even at your dinner table. But what does it really signify? Consider it like this much of the energy that energises our houses, appliances, and devices is generated from the combustion of coal or oil. Burning these fuels generates electricity; however, it also emits significant smoke and harmful gases into the atmosphere. Additionally, these non-renewable resources are finite, meaning that once they are depleted, they cannot be replenished. Renewable energy, conversely, is derived from sources that nature continuously replenishes, such as sunlight, wind, rivers, and organic waste. According to these sources, a solar panel captures sunlight and transforms it into electricity. A wind turbine those tall structures with rotating blades you may have noticed along highways produces electricity from the flow of air. In the same way, hydropower stations harness the power of river currents to produce electricity. All these instances discuss the use of renewable energy. They generate no harmful gases - they are more environmentally friendly and, crucially, will never be

entries.title: A Future-Ready Management Program for Global and Tech-Driven Leaders
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU's PGDM 2026 blends AI & ML learning, 2–3 week global immersion in the USA, UK & Europe, and Harvard ManageMentor certifications in just 12 months.
entries.text: PGDM 2026: 12-Month Programme for Tech-Driven Leaders A Future-Ready Management Program for Global and Tech-Driven Leaders MIT-WPU's PGDM 2026 blends AI & ML learning, 2–3 week global immersion in the USA, UK & Europe, and Harvard ManageMentor certifications in just 12 months. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / A Future-Ready Management Program for Global and Tech-Driven Leaders Faculty Articles A Future-Ready Management Program for Global and Tech-Driven Leaders MIT-WPU's PGDM 2026 blends AI & ML learning, 2–3 week global immersion in the USA, UK & Europe, and Harvard ManageMentor certifications in just 12 months. 413 words 2 min read Management education is undergoing rapid transformation. Professionals today need to be strong in fundamentals, technologically nimble, globally conscious and ethically sound, which is what businesses require. Post Graduate Diploma in Management (PGDM), with effect from Academic Year 2026. This is a 12-Month, 44-credit NCrF Level programme that prepares next-generation leaders to become successful in a dynamic, technology-driven world. The programme is about the Visioning: “Creating Value Driven and Tech Enabled Leaders through Globally Immersive Experiential Learning. This demonstrates a resolve to combine management excellence with depth of leadership, international exposure and digital fluency. The PGDM is designed with academic rigour blended with experiential and practice-oriented learning, which is far different from traditional management programmes. A Future-Ready Management Program for Global and Tech-Driven Leaders One of the key elements that defines the programme is Global Immersion, an international exposure for 2–3 weeks. Anurag Vaidya's remarkable journey from being a student at MIT-WPU to becoming an integral part of ISRO's endeavors serves as an inspiration for both current and future generations of mechanical engineering students. His dedication, unwavering commitment, and passion have propelled him to make significant contributions to the monumental Chandrayaan 3 mission. Anurag's transition from stargazing dreams to actively shaping the trajectory of future space exploration stands as a testament to the resolute power of determination. entries.title: A Tribute to a Legend : MIT-WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Legacy
entries.excerpt: MIT‑WPU pays tribute to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, celebrating leadership, valor, and nation‑building ideals.
entries.text: MIT‑WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj’s Legacy: Events At MIT-WPU A Tribute to a Legend : MIT-WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Legacy MIT‑WPU pays tribute to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, celebrating leadership, valor, and nation‑building ideals. MIT-WPU / Events / A Tribute to a Legend : MIT-WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Legacy Events A Tribute to a Legend : MIT-WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Legacy MIT‑WPU pays tribute to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, celebrating leadership, valor, and nation‑building ideals. Events A Tribute to a Legend : MIT-WPU Honors Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Legacy Happenings Events shivaji-maharaj-2 MIT-WPU celebrated the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti on 19 February 2025, through a commemorative event honouring his remarkable vision and leadership. The programme provided a platform to reflect on the historical significance of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's contributions and their continued relevance today. shivaji-maharaj-3 The event witnessed the presence of Founder President Prof. Dr. Vishwanath Karad, Vice Chancellor Dr. R.M. Chitnis, and Registrar Mr. Ganesh Pokale, who shared insightful perspectives on Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's lasting impact. A significant emphasis was placed on how Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's enduring values of courage, resilience, and ethical governance can inspire and guide students in their academic pursuits and personal journeys. Interactive sessions encouraged students to reflect on his legacy and consider how they can contribute to a future built on wisdom and strength. shivaji-maharaj-1 MIT-WPU's tribute was a powerful reminder of a leader whose legacy continues to influence and motivate both students and faculty alike. A total of 78 B.Ed. students participated in this immersive learning experience. Events Happenings A visit to Naigaon Village, Satara Events satara-1 From 27th February to 1st March 2026, the Department of Education, School of Liberal Arts, MIT-WPU, Pune, organised a transformative three-day Rural Immersion Programme at Naigaon Village, Satara. A total of 78 B.Ed. students participated in this immersive learning experience. The programme was designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and lived rural realities. Students engaged directly with the community, gaining first-hand insight into pressing issues such as educational inequity, healthcare accessibility, water conservation, sanitation, and sustainable development. satara-2 The three days were filled with purposeful, hands-on activities. Students performed street plays on women's empowerment, conducted awareness sessions in village schools, and led social rallies to amplify community consciousness. Shramdan — a collective cleanliness drive — was carried out with active participation. Students also conducted household surveys and visited key rural institutions,

entries.text: A visit to Ranwad Village : Event At MIT-WPU A visit to Ranwad Village This year the Rural Immersion Program took place in Ranwad village, Nashik district, from April 1st to 5th. This initiative, led by Associate Dean (External Affairs) Dr. Swati Bankar and coordinated by Dr. Renuka Deshmukh, involved 36 female students from SY BBA Division F, accompanied by two female faculty members. The programme aimed to enhance students' sensitivity to rural issues through immersive, experiential learning experiences. entries.title: A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund
entries.excerpt: Explore MIT-WPU’s educational visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund, focused on rural development, community engagement, and experiential learning.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Students Visit Ravangaon Village, Daund A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund Explore MIT-WPU’s educational visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund, focused on rural development, community engagement, and experiential learning. MIT-WPU / Events / A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund Events A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund Explore MIT-WPU’s educational visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund, focused on rural development, community engagement, and experiential learning. Events Happenings A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund Events rural-immersion-program-1 The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT World Peace University organized a three-day Rural Immersion Program from 21–23 January 2026 at Ravangaon village, Taluka Daund, District Pune, for 48 second-year Mechanical Engineering students (Division 1), accompanied by Dr. Sanjay Havaldar and Dr. Girish Barpande. The initiative aimed to provide experiential learning by immersing students in rural life while fostering awareness of sustainability, governance, and socio-economic dynamics. rural-immersion-program-2 During the program, students experienced village life firsthand and actively engaged with the local community. Key activities included visits to the Gram Panchayat, poultry farms, Zilla Parishad school, and local healthcare facilities. Students also participated in Shramdaan at the Shirsai Temple and conducted household surveys across more than 30 homes to gain insights into rural living conditions. rural-immersion-program-3 The immersion offered valuable exposure to agriculture-based livelihoods, rural infrastructure, education systems, and healthcare services. Students observed strong community collaboration while also identifying areas for improvement, such as waste management, sanitation, and access to healthca

Events A visit to Sarole (Khurd) Village, Nashik Happenings rural-nashik-1 Events rural-nashik-2 As part of MIT-WPU's Rural Immersion Programme, female students from SY BBA IB visited Sarole (Khurd) village, Nashik from 1st to 5th April 2025. During this immersion programme, these students engaged directly with the rural community, bridging academic concepts with ground realities. The students conducted a comprehensive household survey and interacted with key village authorities, including the Sarpanch and Village Development Officer, gaining valuable insights into governance, resource allocation, and local development challenges. They also visited places like a poultry farm, Shri Gajanan Sarole (Khurd) Socio-Cooperative Society and Dhanalakshmi Agricultural Agency that enriched their understanding of rural entrepreneurship, financial mechanisms for farmers, and the agricultural support system. rural-nashik-3 Through this immersive experience, students identified critical challenges such as the abs

This year, the event revolved around the theme of the Events Happenings AAROHAN 2025-Featuring the Cascades of Oriental Mystique Events On 14-16 February 2025, MIT-WPU hosted the 13th edition of its annual cultural fest AAROHAN, Pune's largest cultural extravaganza. This year, the event revolved around the theme of the "Cascades of Oriental Mystique" – leading to a mesmerising journey through Japanese cultural aesthetics that transformed our campus into a vibrant celebration of art, music, and creativity. The attendees enjoyed the theme and the vibe of the event this year and found it truly captivating. aarohan Major Highlights from the 3-Day Ecstatic Cultural Fest AAROHAN wasn't just a college fest—it was a cultural phenomenon that captivated the hearts of students across Pune and beyond. The three-day event was entirely organised by the students of MIT-WPU and the celebration showcased extraordinary talent, fostered creativity, and created unforgettable memories for all those who attended. Achievement Achievements Aaryesh-Honrao MIT-WPU Pune, proudly congratulates Aaryesh Honrao, First Year Integrated B.Tech ECE (AI/ML) student, for his exceptional performance at the 63rd National Roller Skating Championship 2025 in Visakhapatnam—securing Gold in Classic Slalom and Silver in Speed Slalom. This national-level achievement demonstrates his commitment, perseverance, and ability to excel across multiple disciplines, balancing academics and sports with remarkable skill. Aaryesh's success inspires peers and exemplifies MIT-WPU Pune's culture of all-round excellence.

Achievement Achievements manvi-singhai MIT-WPU, Pune, proudly congratulates Abeer Atmesh Dhond, a BA Psychology student, on his commendable performance at the All India Inter-University Aquatics (M & W) Championship 2025–26 held in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. Abeer secured the 12th position in the Men’s Swimming category, placing him among the top 12 athletes selected for the prestigious Khelo India University Games. This achievement reflects his dedication, discipline, and perseverance in competitive sports. Abeer's success is a proud moment for the MIT-WPU community, and we wish him continued excellence and the very best at the upcoming Khelo India University Games.

MIT-WPU / Schools / Faculty Of Design Humanities And Liberal Arts / School Of Liberal Arts Humanities / Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute / Dean / Abhijit Panse Schools Abhijit Panse Meet the dynamic dean of MIT-WPU's Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute. The enthusiastic and multi talented personality drives the school of design humanities and liberal arts with passion and great wisdom of his knowledge. Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Meet the Creative Director and Mentor, DPIFS. Film Director, ‘Thackeray’, ‘RaanBaazaar’, ‘Rege’ Creative Director and Mentor, DPIFS. Film Director, ‘Thackeray’, ‘RaanBaazaar’, ‘Rege’ icon Message The MIT-WPU Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School is a film school with a difference: one that believes in the adage of ‘CLASSROOM TO SET.’ We believe it is important for students of filmmaking to immerse themselves in the real-life experience of a film set from the word go itself. In a professional course such as filmmaking, this is not only desirable but necessary as no amount of simulated instruction can take the place of real immersive learning. Abhijit Panse The MIT-WPU Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School sees itself as a catalyst in shaping young filmmakers, screenwriters, sound designers, visual communicators, actors, cinematographers, broadcasters, and film editors of tomorrow. We aim for our students to achieve positions of creative influence in whichever sphere of filmma

entries.title: Abhishek Nanaware-The Endurance King!
entries.excerpt: Presenting MIT-WPU's Endurance King, Abhishek Nanaware Learn his inspirational path of tenacity, will, and great success in endurance sports.
entries.text: Abhishek Nanaware - Student Achiever Of MIT-WPU Abhishek Nanaware-The Endurance King! Presenting MIT-WPU's Endurance King, Abhishek Nanaware Learn his inspirational path of tenacity, will, and great success in endurance sports. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Abhishek Nanaware-The Endurance King! Achievements Abhishek Nanaware-The Endurance King! Presenting MIT-WPU's Endurance King, Abhishek Nanaware Learn his inspirational path of tenacity, will, and great success in endurance sports. Achievement Achievements Abhishek’s Achievements Ironman Triathlon Finisher Ironman South Africa 2021 Time: 13.33.55 Ironman Finisher (2nd place) Ironman Kazakhstan 2022 st-achiv-img Time: 12.28.15 Beginners are many, but finishers are only a few. Abhishek, may you continue to be a strong Finisher and keep the MIT-WPU torch glowing with pride! A high endurance event like the Ironman Triathlon is certainly not for the faint-hearted. Abhishek Nanaware has proved his mettle not once, but twice already. He has achieved Finisher titles at Ironman South Africa 2021 and Ironman Kazakhstan 2022. Here’s putting the spotlight on Abhishek’s achievements and more about becoming Ironman! It’s no easy feat to receive the Ironman Finisher title since it requires one to finish 4 km swim, 180 km cycling and a 42 km run, all under 17 hours! Abhishek has been training for an Ironman Triathlon for the past two years, mentored by his fitness-enthusiast father, a three-time Ironman finisher himself! Abhishek shares his gratitude with MIT-WPU staff for all the encouragement and support he has received. He likes to emphasize that their support has driven him to always give his best! Related public links MIT-WPU

entries.title: About MAEERs Group
entries.excerpt: The institutional history of MAEER’s MIT Group, founded in Pune in 1983.
entries.text: About MAEERs Group · WPU Goa About MAEERs Group The institutional history of MAEER’s MIT Group, founded in Pune in 1983. WPU Goa / About / About MAEERs Group About WPU Goa About MAEERs Group The institutional history of MAEER’s MIT Group, founded in Pune in 1983. The Foundation Rooted in the profound ideal that “The union of Science and Spirituality alone will bring Harmony and Peace to Humanity,” as envisioned by Swami Vivekananda, MAEER’s philosophy blends cutting-edge knowledge systems with the timeless wisdom of India’s civilizational ethos. This philosophy has guided its sustained efforts to nurture a new generation of competent, compassionate, and conscious professionals. Legacy of 4 Decades MIT Art, Design & Technology University (MIT-ADT), Loni, Pune Avantika University, Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh MIT University of Meghalaya MIT Vishwaprayag University, Solapur About MAEER's Legacy of 4 Decades The Founding Father Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad ​ The establishment of MAEER’s Group of Institutions, Pune, was the outcome of an enduring quest for providing quality education to aspirants of higher professional education, by the visionary educationist Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad. Having established Maharashtra Instiute of Technology (MIT) in 1983 and made a niche position in the highly competitive field of engineering education. MIT diversified in other academic disciplines like Medicine, Management, School and College Education, Pharmacy, Distance Education, Marine Engineering, Food Technology, Music, Journalism and Fine Art, Politics and Government education, Peace Studies etc. MIT Group now has five functioning universities, apart from colleges, schools and other institutions. MAEER's Journey A pioneering institution of higher education with world-class academic stan

entries.text: About WPU Goa · WPU Goa About WPU Goa Institutional background, campus life, partnerships, leadership, careers, brand, and policies. WPU Goa / About WPU Goa About About WPU Goa Institutional background, campus life, partnerships, leadership, careers, brand, and policies. University identity Globally informed. Locally rooted. WPU GŌA exists to prepare leaders who think deeply, connect ideas across domains, and act with empathy. We educate leaders who can see the whole system, understand people, and design solutions with compassion. We generate research that pushes boundaries and enables real change, and we work to catalyse innovation and policy that shape the world we want to live in. Our pillars World-Class Programmes Top-quality UG and PG Programmes that meet global standards, providing students with practical skills and opportunities to succeed. Empowered Leaders Foster responsible leadership in students by developing ethics, awareness, and skills, preparing them to positively impact society. Global Partnerships Encourage students and faculty to form international partnerships, share ideas, and apply knowledge to solve real-world local challenges. Inclusive Sustainability Integrating eco-friendly practices and fostering an environment where everyone feels valued and supported. Chancellor and core advisors Shaping the vision of a future-first university grounded in integration, responsibility, human-centric thinking, and a culture of academic excellence. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Academic Facilities Life Wpu Academic Facilities Experience world-class academic facilities at MIT-WPU, designed to nurture excellence in education. Life@MIT-WPU classrooms-img Academic Facilities classrooms-img Classrooms classrooms-img MIT-WPU has 350+ Hi-tech classrooms equipped with smartboards and LCD projectors to facilitate a better understanding of the subject matter. But what brings alive our classrooms are the discussions, presentations and brainstorming our students engage in as they learn in an environment which shapes them holistically. auditorium-small Laboratories auditorium-small These labs are equipped with the latest equipment and industry-oriented apparatus which the students utilise for various experiments, innovations and research. The University has one of the world’s only three Subsea labs which prepare the students of Civil Engineering and Petroleum Engineering in the various processes of oil recovery, Industrial Safety and Health Engineering (ISHE), and drilling and well control operations, fluid machinery and more. auditorium-big Auditorium auditorium-big MIT-WPU has multiple state-of-the-art auditoriums with audio-visual and multimedia facilities to hold seminars, guest lectures, meetings, conferences, presentations, and other academic and socio-cultural events of all forms and formats.

entries.title: Academic Research
entries.excerpt: Academic research at MIT-WPU is deeply rooted in a systematic and methodical approach to problem-solving. Our institution places a strong emphasis on collaborative research endeavours, often conducted in partnership with faculty and students, and backed by…
entries.text: MIT-WPU : Academic Research Academic Research Academic research at MIT-WPU is deeply rooted in a systematic and methodical approach to problem-solving. Our institution places a strong emphasis on collaborative research endeavours, often conducted in partnership with faculty and students, and backed by the generous support of the MIT-WPU Seed Money Scheme. MIT-WPU / Research / Academic Research Research Academic Research Academic research at MIT-WPU is deeply rooted in a systematic and methodical approach to problem-solving. Our institution places a strong emphasis on collaborative research endeavours, often conducted in partnership with faculty and students, and backed by the generous support of the MIT-WPU Seed Money Scheme. Academic Research Academic research at MIT-WPU is deeply rooted in a systematic and methodical approach to problem-solving. Our institution places a strong emphasis on collaborative research endeavours, often conducted in partnership with faculty and students, and backed by the generous support of the MIT-WPU Seed Money Scheme. This initiative grants funding to faculty members embarking on research projects with a dual purpose: to foster a research-oriented mindset among our students and to tackle real-world challenges. The Research and Development Cell (RDC) plays a pivotal role in orchestrating this research ecosystem. We provide expert guidance, meticulous planning, and valuable resources to ensure the execution of high-quality research that aligns with the overarching vision of our university. entries.title: Achievement of Team Pro-Karters
entries.excerpt: The team has won 5 awards in different categories and cash prize worth 75000 rupees. The team is headed by Prof. Surendra Barhatte and prof. Shailendra Dayane as faculty advisors.
entries.text: MIT-WPU : Achievement of Team Pro-Karters Achievement of Team Pro-Karters The team has won 5 awards in different categories and cash prize worth 75000 rupees. The team is headed by Prof. Surendra Barhatte and prof. Shailendra Dayane as faculty advisors. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Achievement of Team Pro-Karters Achievements Achievement of Team Pro-Karters The team has won 5 awards in different categories and cash prize worth 75000 rupees. The team is headed by Prof. Surendra Barhatte and prof. Shailendra Dayane as faculty advisors. Achievement Achievements The team had participated in Indian Karting Race IKR 7.0 organized by Imperial Society of innovative engineers (ISIE). The team has won 5 awards in different categories and cash prize worth 75000 rupees. The team is headed by Prof. Surendra Barhatte and prof. Shailendra Dayane as faculty advisors. Team captain Niranjan Jadhav and vice-captain Mr. Apratim Ukalkar and kart drivers Atman Derashree and Mithil Nambiar lead the team to victory. This was possible with the blessings of HoS, Dr. Ganesh Kakandikar and guidance from our AHoS Dr. Deepak Hujare and Dr. Kundlik Mali A total of 350 teams had registered for the competition. Some of the teams could not qualify for the race due to failure in Technical Inspection and safety. Accolades pro-karters AIR 1 in EV category AIR 2 Overall IC n EV categories AIR 1 in Endurance Race AIR 1 in Acceleration AIR 2 in Crosspad Winner of the ISIE future award Cash prize worth 75000 rupees

entries.title: ACM Club
entries.excerpt: At the ACM Club, we are dedicated to fostering a supportive and empowering environment where every woman can thrive. We believe in the power of unity and together, we aim to empower, educate, and inspire women in the best possible way.
entries.text: ACM Club | MIT-WPU Student Chapter | Events & Membership ACM Club At the ACM Club, we are dedicated to fostering a supportive and empowering environment where every woman can thrive. We believe in the power of unity and together, we aim to empower, educate, and inspire women in the best possible way. MIT-WPU / ACM Club ACM Club At the ACM Club, we are dedicated to fostering a supportive and empowering environment where every woman can thrive. We believe in the power of unity and together, we aim to empower, educate, and inspire women in the best possible way. Student Clubs ACM1 ACM Club ACM2 At the ACM Club, we are dedicated to fostering a supportive and empowering environment where every woman can thrive. We believe in the power of unity and together, we aim to empower, educate, and inspire women in the best possible way. The platform serves as a hub for sharing knowledge, organising events, and creating opportunities for personal and professional growth. We invite you to join us on this journey of empowerment and education, as we work together to make a positive impact and create a brighter future for women in the field of technology and beyond. Student’s participation and engagement with the ACM Club will contribute to a more inclusive and diverse community, advancing the cause of women's empowerment. Together, we can achieve great things.

Quick Navigation Programmes Offered Admissions at WPU GŌA Admission Process Flow and Schedule Eligibility Criteria Selection Process Document Checklist Admission Status Fee Structure Education Loan Admission Process FAQs International Student Policy Quick Navigation For more details, contact : Dr. Prakash M Mainkar Director Admissions director.admissions@wpugoa.edu.in Or Write to admissions@wpugoa.edu.in Contact us at +91 86050 07433 General WPU GŌA is a fully residential campus that embraces a transdisciplinary philosophy of education. The WPU GŌA Admissions Committee looks beyond academic performance in standardized tests and Board examinations, seeking candidates who demonstrate strong character, meaningful experiences, and clear future motivation. Our admissions process is designed to identify well-rounded, high-potential students who can actively contribute to and enrich the learning environment at WPU GŌA. Applicants must have a minimum 50% aggregate in Class 12/10+2 in any stream with English as a compulsory subject for all undergraduate programmes. In addition, for B.Tech. programmes, Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics or Physics and Mathematics with a Technical or Vocational subject, are mandatory. Please do see the eligibility section for more details. The Admissions Committee of WPU GŌA evaluates the complete profile of each candidate holisticall

The WPU GŌA admissions committee looks beyond academic performance in standardised tests and the board exams and seeks candidates who demonstrate strong character, meaningful experiences, and clear future motivation. Our admission process is designed to identify well-rounded, high-potential students who can actively contribute to and enrich the learning environment at WPU GŌA. entries.text: Advance Certification Course At MIT-WPU | Photography Course At MIT-WPU Advance Certification in Photography The Advance Certification in Photography at MIT-WPU is a one-year part-time programme that provides a thorough grounding in the technical, creative, scientific, and aesthetic aspects of photography. MIT-WPU / Programme / Advance Certification in Photography Programme Advance Certification in Photography The Advance Certification in Photography at MIT-WPU is a one-year part-time programme that provides a thorough grounding in the technical, creative, scientific, and aesthetic aspects of photography. Programme Overview The Advance Certification in Photography at MIT-WPU is a one-year part-time programme that provides a thorough grounding in the technical, creative, scientific, and aesthetic aspects of photography. The programme covers a variety of photography genres, including advertising, fashion, wildlife, medical photography, and more. Students gain practical experience through workshops and guest lectures from ace photographers in each genre, as well as demonstrations in studios and in the field. The programme aims to prepare students to effectively practice photography and face real-life challenges in the field. Duration & Fees Duration 1 Year Last Date to Apply : 7th August 2026 Fee Per Year ₹ 1,30,000 Download Brochure Eligibility Minimum Pass Class in Class 10/ SSC from State Government approved Institution/ board. Learn about her inspirational path and contributions that best define greatness and commitment. Achievement Achievements Her achievements Akanksha Verma Co-authored four books, including ‘Winging Thoughts’ and ‘Unspoken Words’ from April to October 2022. Awarded "Best Co-Author of the Year", in August-2022 by Wordsmith Publisher. Her Journey Akansha Verma Akanksha discovered her love for writing while consoling with lockdown victims. She touched many hearts with her motivating and relatable philosophical Instagram posts and started getting calls for co-authoring books. Family: Her support system Akanksha’s parents supported her when she followed her heart instead of becoming a doctor, despite passing her NEET exams. They are her biggest cheerleaders and love highlighting her poetic achievements. Akansha Verma What’s next? Akanksha is now gearing up to publish her own book, ‘Mirror of Life’ which will be out for release by November 2022. It will soon be available on Amazon, Kindle, and Google Play Books. Akansha Verma We laud Akanksha for her efforts and for making the MIT-WPU community proud! In a span of just six months, Akanksha Verma has made an indelible mark in the field of English literature. She is a prime example of someone who turned their dreams into reality in a short span of time. She wishes to reach millions of children through her poetries published in NCERT and ICSE books. Shefali Arora is a landscape architect, academician and researcher with over 19 years of experience in teaching, professional practice and academic leadership. She holds a Masters degree in Landscape Architecture and is pursuing a PhD. MIT-WPU / Faculty / Ar. Shefali Deepak Arora Faculty Ar. Shefali Deepak Arora Ar. Shefali Arora is a landscape architect, academician and researcher with over 19 years of experience in teaching, professional practice and academic leadership. She holds a Masters degree in Landscape Architecture and is pursuing a PhD. Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Bio Ar. Shefali Arora is a landscape architect, academician and researcher with over 19 years of experience in teaching, professional practice and academic leadership. She holds a Masters degree in Landscape Architecture and is pursuing a PhD. focusing on urban parks, children’s well-being and sustainable urban design. At MIT-WPU, she aims to foster research-led learning, interdisciplinary collaborations and industry engagement while mentoring students to develop innovative, ethical and sustainable design solutions to create resilient, people-centric environments. Educational Qualifications : B.Arch (2007)- Sinhagad College of Architecture, Pune M.LA- Master’s in Landscape Architecture- (2009)- School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi Pursuing PhD- UID, Gandhinagar Areas of Expertise : Urban Ecology, Evidence- Based Design solutions, Sustainable Landscape planning. Teaching Experience : 19 Years of teaching experience in architecture and design education. Taught undergraduate and post-graduate students. Mentored thesis and dissertation projects at various institutions across Pune, Mumbai. Associated with B.N.Col

entries.excerpt: Explore how the global hospitality revolution is creating exciting career opportunities. Learn what it takes to build your future in this dynamic industry.
entries.text: Careers in Global Hospitality Management Atithi Devo Bhava: Mapping Your Future in the Global Hospitality Revolution Explore how the global hospitality revolution is creating exciting career opportunities. Learn what it takes to build your future in this dynamic industry. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Atithi Devo Bhava: Mapping Your Future in the Global Hospitality Revolution Faculty Articles Atithi Devo Bhava: Mapping Your Future in the Global Hospitality Revolution Explore how the global hospitality revolution is creating exciting career opportunities. Learn what it takes to build your future in this dynamic industry. 772 words 4 min read We are nurtured with the philosophy of Atithi Devo Bhava in India Guest is God. It is not just a slogan for a tourism campaign. It is woven into our DNA. Today, this ancient ethos is colliding with 21st-century infrastructure that is propelling the hospitality and tourism sector as one of the mightiest engines of the Indian economy. If you’ve ever wondered if a career in this field is all about hotels, it’s time to think again. We are witnessing a confluence of travel, luxury and service that is creating in real time a global powerhouse of opportunity. The New Age of Landscape Architecture: India, the Global Hub Today, the Indian hospitality scene is working at a whole other level. These are not just local increases. Across Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, every leading international brand from Marriott and Hyatt to Accor and Hilton is multiplying its footprint. The influx of global standards in tandem with India’s innate warmth and service culture has engendered an advanced market that requires professionals of a high skill level. entries.title: B.Arch vs B.E. Civil Engineering: Which One Should You Choose?
entries.excerpt: One profession begins with space, people, form and experience. The other begins with structure, materials, safety and performance. Both are important. Both shape the built environment. But they do not shape the student in the same way.
entries.text: B.Arch vs B.E. Civil Engineering: Which One Should You Choose? B.Arch vs B.E. Civil Engineering: Which One Should You Choose? One profession begins with space, people, form and experience. The other begins with structure, materials, safety and performance. Both are important. Both shape the built environment. But they do not shape the student in the same way. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / B.Arch vs B.E. Civil Engineering: Which One Should You Choose? Faculty Articles B.Arch vs B.E. Civil Engineering: Which One Should You Choose? One profession begins with space, people, form and experience. The other begins with structure, materials, safety and performance. Both are important. Both shape the built environment. But they do not shape the student in the same way. 1,045 words 5 min read One profession begins with space, people, form and experience. The other begins with structure, materials, safety and performance. Both are important. Both shape the built environment. But they do not shape the student in the same way. Architecture Begins With People A B.Arch degree, or Bachelor of Architecture, is not just about sketching buildings or making impressive models. A good B.Arch course trains students to observe how people live, move, gather, work and respond to space. When we explain about the B.Arch coMany aspirants are fascinated by buildings, cities and infrastructure, but are unsure whether they should choose B.Arch or B.E. Civil Engineering. On paper, both seem connected. In reality, both these programmes ask students to think very differently. urse to students, we often say this: architecture is where imagination meets responsibility. A building cannot be beautiful only in a drawing. It must work for its users, respond to climate, respect context and stand meaningfully wit

In seeking to achieve this, we hope to promote a culture of applied learning, encourage students to pursue exciting engineering careers, and imbibe in them a sense of teamwork and dedication. As a part of Piranha Racing, students get acquainted with the latest computer design and analysis software like SOLIDWORKS, Ansys, Hypermesh, etc. Students also learn innovative manufacturing and fabrication processes and various validation and testing methods. We are dedicated to designing and manufacturing an All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) for competing in BAJA SAE competitions worldwide. The team consists of about 30 aspiring engineers from the first year to the final year aiming to build the best BAJA SAE car in the world. entries.title: Best Paper Award
entries.excerpt: We are proud to share that a team from the Department of Polytechnic has been honoured with the 'Best Paper' Award at the 7th IEEE International Conference.
entries.text: Best Paper Award | Department of Polytechnic Wins 'Best Paper' Award Best Paper Award We are proud to share that a team from the Department of Polytechnic has been honoured with the 'Best Paper' Award at the 7th IEEE International Conference. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Best Paper Award Achievements Best Paper Award We are proud to share that a team from the Department of Polytechnic has been honoured with the 'Best Paper' Award at the 7th IEEE International Conference. Achievement Achievements Best-Paper-Award Department of Polytechnic Wins 'Best Paper' Award at Prestigious IEEE Conference We are proud to share that a team from the Department of Polytechnic has been honored with the 'Best Paper' Award at the 7th IEEE International Conference on Emerging Smart Computing and Informatics (ESCI-2025), held at AISSMS Institute of Information Technology, Pune. The team members Tanushree Balote, Mansi Joshi, Harshada Suryawanshi, and Neel Suryawanshi under the guidance of Prof. Hemlata Ohal, presented their research paper titled "Leveraging Convolutional Neural Networks for AI Artwork Classification," which highlights their significant contribution to the field of AI-driven art classification.

entries.title: Beyond the Blueprint: Why the Built Environment Needs Techno-Managers
entries.excerpt: Discover why techno-managers are shaping the future of construction, infrastructure, and smart cities by combining engineering expertise with business leadership.
entries.text: Why the Built Environment Needs Techno-Managers Beyond the Blueprint: Why the Built Environment Needs Techno-Managers Discover why techno-managers are shaping the future of construction, infrastructure, and smart cities by combining engineering expertise with business leadership. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Beyond the Blueprint: Why the Built Environment Needs Techno-Managers Faculty Articles Beyond the Blueprint: Why the Built Environment Needs Techno-Managers Discover why techno-managers are shaping the future of construction, infrastructure, and smart cities by combining engineering expertise with business leadership. 950 words 4 min read If you come from a background in civil engineering, architecture, planning, environmental engineering or construction engineering, you already understand how the built world takes shape. You have studied drawings, structures, materials, sites, services and systems. You know that every building or infrastructure project begins with a technical foundation. But today, that foundation is no longer enough on its own. India’s built environment is changing at a pace we have not seen before. It is no longer limited to buildings, roads and construction sites. It now includes smart cities, metro networks, logistics parks, airports, industrial corridors, sustainable townships, green buildings and large-scale urban infrastructure. The scale is significant. India’s real estate sector is projected to reach US$ 1 trillion by 2030 and contribute nearly 13% to the country’s GDP. The National Infrastructure Pipeline has also set in motion a planned investment of ₹111 lakh crore, or nearly US$ 1.5 trillion, across major infrastructure sectors. For young professionals, this creates an important question. Is technical knowledge enough to lead projects

The Bharat Asmita National Awards, initiated in 2005 by MAEER's MIT Group of Institutions, Pune, honors individuals contributing significantly to Nation Building in the realms of management, mass awakening, and Indian Parliamentary Practices. This esteemed award has grown in reputation and stature over the years. The board of trustees and Awards Selection Committee maintain high standards in recognizing exceptional contributions The committee invites over 15,000 nominations from diverse sectors, including government officials, legislators, bureaucrats, judiciary, media, educators, entertainers, writers, and NGOs The awards consist of three categories, with one award presented in each. In exceptional cases, a shared award may be given to two equally deserving individuals in a given year. The scope of the award encompasses all states and union territories throughout India. icon Background MAEER’s MIT Group of Institutes was established by Professor (Dr.) Vishwanath D. Events Happenings Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad : Empowering Future Leaders at MIT-WPU Events The 14 th Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad (BCS) wrapped up its impactful three-day run at MIT World Peace University, Pune on 8, 9, and 10 February 2025. As a premier leadership development forum, the event brought together India's promising young minds to engage with seasoned political leaders and visionaries. The gathering created a dynamic environment where academic theory met real-world governance challenges, empowering students to develop practical leadership skills. Through meaningful dialogue and mentorship opportunities, the BCS continued its mission of nurturing the next wave of democratic leaders who will help shape India's future. The COE was established by pairing the industry experience and skills of Snapper Future Tech with CIAP MIT-WPU and the Department of Computer Engineering and technology. MIT-WPU / Blockchain COE Blockchain COE The Blockchain Centre of Excellence was established in MIT-WPU with the aim to enhance technological skills among students and faculty, and make them industry ready. The COE was established by pairing the industry experience and skills of Snapper Future Tech with CIAP MIT-WPU and the Department of Computer Engineering and technology. Blockchain COE The Blockchain Centre of Excellence was established in MIT-WPU with the aim to enhance technological skills among students and faculty, and make them industry ready. The COE was established by pairing the industry experience and skills of Snapper Future Tech with CIAP MIT-WPU and the Department of Computer Engineering and technology. The workshops, initiatives and events organized under the purview of the COE aim to equip students with the knowledge and skills required to navigate Blockchain technology. The COE operates as a collaborative space, and a platform for students, researchers, and industry professionals to conduct interdisciplinary research. It aims to explore the vast potential of blockchain across numerous sectors, including finance, healthcare, supply chain, governance, and more. The COE is focused on the broader agenda of improving the skills and competency of the students and preparing them to be the industry leaders of tomorrow. Related public links Department of Computer Engineering and technology.

Centre for Women’s Studies and Development Centre for Women’s Studies and Development under Centre of Excellence (CoE), R&D department at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. The purpose of this centre is to promote research and development, address women’s issues and comply with UGC guidelines, NIRF and NAAC accreditation requirements. Below outline the key aspects of the proposed Centre at the university. Promoting Research and Development The Women’s Centre will serve as a hub for National and International research projects on gender issues, women’s rights, violence and other related fields. It will encourage interdisciplinary studies, foster collaborations with national and international institutions and secure funding for research projects. The focus on gender-related research will not only enhance the university’s academic credibility but also contribute valuable insights for societal development. entries.title: Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Hosts Industry-Academia Conclave
entries.excerpt: MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) and the National Institute of Personnel Management (NIPM) joined forces to host a groundbreaking Industry-Academia Conclave on January 31st, 2025.
entries.text: Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Events Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Hosts Industry-Academia Conclave MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) and the National Institute of Personnel Management (NIPM) joined forces to host a groundbreaking Industry-Academia Conclave on January 31st, 2025. MIT-WPU / Events / Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Hosts Industry-Academia Conclave Events Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Hosts Industry-Academia Conclave MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) and the National Institute of Personnel Management (NIPM) joined forces to host a groundbreaking Industry-Academia Conclave on January 31st, 2025. Events Happenings Bridging Education and Industry: MIT-WPU Hosts Industry-Academia Conclave Events MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) and the National Institute of Personnel Management (NIPM) joined forces to host a groundbreaking Industry-Academia Conclave on January 31st, 2025. The event, centered on Strategic Envisioning India 2047, brought together over 150 industry professionals and 25 distinguished speakers at Saint Dnyaneshwar Hall, MIT-WPU Kothrud Campus. IAC-2 The conclave highlighted a critical challenge in today's educational landscape ‘the growing gap between academic learning and industry requirements’. Rishikesh Patankar, VP, of Government Programs, Industry and Academia Collaboration, National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), serving as the Chief Guest, emphasised the importance of personalising education through reduced teacher-student ratio to create an industry-ready workforce. He presented two strategic approaches to address the demand-supply gap in industry manpower – a top-down approach involving government and industry initiatives, and a bottom-up approach focusing on personalised education

(Hons.) in Psychology And in a world increasingly shaped by artificial intelligence, digital platforms, social fragmentation, mental health crises, and rapidly changing work environments, who will help societies better understand what it means to be human? The B.Sc. (Hons.) Psychology at WPU GŌA prepares students to engage with these questions through a rigorous and contemporary psychological education grounded in science, research, ethics, and real-world application. You will learn how people perceive, learn, decide, relate, and thrive, grounded in evidence-based research while remaining deeply connected to practical applications. The programme stands apart through its integration of core psychology with systems thinking, behavioural science applications, technology, design, and ethical practice. Students learn to understand people not only through theory, but through observation, field engagement, reflective practice, immersion, research, and intervention design. WPU GŌA’s approach goes beyond traditional classroom learning. Through fieldwork, labs, immersions, studios, and research projects, students gain hands-on experience that prepares them for diverse careers in mental health, organisational development, education, UX research, public policy, sports, and emerging fields like behavioural design and human-AI interaction. entries.title: Build What's Next with Transdisciplinary Education at WPU
entries.excerpt: Discover how WPU Goa prepares learners to tackle real-world challenges through innovation, creativity, and transformative learning.
entries.text: Build What's Next with Transdisciplinary Education at WPU · WPU Goa Build What's Next with Transdisciplinary Education at WPU Discover how WPU Goa prepares learners to tackle real-world challenges through innovation, creativity, and transformative learning. WPU Goa / Academics / Build What's Next with Transdisciplinary Education at WPU Academic model Build What's Next with Transdisciplinary Education at WPU Discover how WPU Goa prepares learners to tackle real-world challenges through innovation, creativity, and transformative learning. The world doesn’t need more degrees. It needs people who can build what comes next. Most universities prepare you for the world as it is. WPU GŌA prepares you to build the world as it isn’t yet. Because the future won’t be shaped by those who fit into existing roles, but by those who can imagine, design, and create what doesn’t exist today. Building the future starts with understanding the present. The present is not divided into subjects. It is made up of problems. Complex. Interconnected. Human. The kind that cannot be solved by a single discipline. Why Problems Matter Solving them requires more than expertise. It requires the ability to connect. This is what it means to be transdisciplinary. Not learning more subjects. But learning how to bring them together. Build What's Next means building everything. Eight dimensions of creation, each one a different frontier waiting to be shaped. Build New ideas Build New Systems Build New Art, Design and Media Build a New Self Build New Technologies Build New Futures Build New Perspectives Build New Careers This is how the world has always moved forward The Smartphone A single device that combines computing, communication, photography, and design used by billions. Discover a vibrant community where friendships, creativity, and growth thrive. The Goa Proposition - Where Learning Comes Alive Goa is not just a location; it is our foundational case study. A microcosm of the world, it embodies the relations between development and preservation, culture and tourism, local and global. Here, solutions need to be holistic. Goa is a canvas for dreamers, innovators, and trailblazers. It is a psychologically elevating sanctuary designed for those who seek to build a future defined by intention. We chose this location for its unique cultural attributes that align perfectly with our goal of integrated human development. Designed for Discovery Student Life at Our Campus At WPU GŌA, we believe in nurturing responsible global citizens through a holistic, immersive experience. Rooted in empathy, ingenuity, and peace, these principles ensure a safe, vibrant, and professional environment where students thrive, and parents can have peace of mind. Student Clubs While the Living Learning Scholar Communities form the structured academic and residential foundation of your journey, Student Clubs are spaces for pure passion, creative expression, and voluntary peer-to-peer engagement. Overview The Centre for Business Innovation and Entrepreneurship (CBIE) is a dynamic and progressive organisation dedicated to fostering a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship in the business world. Primary purpose of CBIE is to provide a collaborative platform that empowers aspiring entrepreneurs and established businesses to explore, develop, and implement innovative ideas and solutions. By offering a range of comprehensive resources, mentorship programs, and networking opportunities, CBIE aims to inspire and support individuals in turning their innovative concepts into successful ventures by also helping them with Seed Grants and Connecting them to the right investors. entries.excerpt: MIT‑WPU opens a state‑of‑the‑art Central Research Facility to accelerate interdisciplinary research and innovation.
entries.text: MIT‑WPU Inaugurates Central Research Facility : MIT-WPU Events Celebrating Excellence With the Inauguration of MIT-WPU's Central Research Facility MIT‑WPU opens a state‑of‑the‑art Central Research Facility to accelerate interdisciplinary research and innovation. MIT-WPU / Events / Celebrating Excellence With the Inauguration of MIT-WPU's Central Research Facility Events Celebrating Excellence With the Inauguration of MIT-WPU's Central Research Facility MIT‑WPU opens a state‑of‑the‑art Central Research Facility to accelerate interdisciplinary research and innovation. Events Happenings Events Welcome to the future of research and innovation at MIT World Peace University! We are proud to announce the successful launch of our cutting-edge Central Research Facility (CRF), inaugurated on January 7, 2025, at the prestigious Maitreyi Building. This state-of-the-art facility marks a transformative milestone in our commitment to advancing scientific research and technological innovation. Celebrating Excellence With the Inauguration of MIT-WPU's Central Research Facility Under the visionary guidance of our Executive President, Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Chief Academic Officer Dr. Sanjay Kamtekar, and Dean Dr. Murthy Chavali, the CRF stands as a testament to MIT-WPU's dedication to pushing the boundaries of scientific exploration and academic excellence. The Central Research Facility houses world-class research infrastructure including advanced characterisation and research equipment, featuring : Field Emission Scanning Electron Microscopy (FESEM) for cutting-edge material analysis CRF Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS) for precise elemental analysis State-of-the-art Li-ion/Na-ion cell fabrication facilities Advanced analytical instruments and testing equipment At MIT-W

Under the theme 'Saluting Excellence, Inspiring Generations,' Events Happenings Celebrating Excellence: 21st BANA Ceremony Illuminates World Peace Dome Events The 21st Bharat Asmita National Awards (BANA) turned the World Peace Dome in Loni Kalbhor, Pune, into a source of inspiration on February 3rd, 2025. Under the theme 'Saluting Excellence, Inspiring Generations,' the event united prominent figures who have influenced India's advancement in multiple fields. BANA-2025 The event began with age-old customs - the global peace prayer, the ceremonial ringing of the democracy bell, and the illumination of the customary lamp. Students from MIT-WPU captivated the audience with an enchanting performance to the Bharat Asmita theme song, which was succeeded by the national song 'Vande Mataram.' A touching moment of silence paid tribute to the late Shyam Benegal's contributions to the BANA Committee. BANA-2025 Executive President Dr. Rahul V. Distinguished guests including Vice-Chancellor Dr. R. M. A thought-provoking panel discussion led by Dr. Monica Apte, Director of the Centre for Women Studies and Development, provided insights into women's challenges and triumphs in various fields. Under the Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative by Aker Solutions, the real-life laboratory aims at training students, industry professionals and carrying out research and consultancy projects. This joint initiative of developing a Center of Excellence (CoE) by Aker Solutions and MITWPU Pune is called Dr. Vishwanath Karad Centre for Subsea Engineering Research (CSER) Aker Solutions has been involved in the design, fabrication, installation and commissioning of the subsea infrastructure in the lab. subsea-img For the lab to see the light of the day, MITWPU has provided the required space and related infrastructure. As the global creative economy rapidly expands and converges with technology, there is a need for an institutional ecosystem that can bridge India’s artistic depth with future-ready creative innovation. This Centre addresses that need by fostering interdisciplinary learning, production, and experimentation across music, film, media, storytelling, and creative technologies preparing students for impactful careers in India’s cultural economy and globally connected creative industries. Swaminathan Prof. M. S. Swaminathan (1925–2023), revered as the Father of India’s Green Revolution, was a pioneering geneticist whose work transformed India from food scarcity to self-sufficiency. He led premier institutions such as the Indian Agricultural Research Institute and the International Rice Research Institute, and chaired the National Commission on Farmers, championing policies that uplifted smallholder farmers. As founder of the M. S. Swaminathan Research Foundation, he advanced sustainable agriculture and rural development with a strong focus on science serving society. A global icon of agricultural innovation, he was honoured with the Bharat Ratna, Padma Vibhushan, World Food Prize, Ramon Magsaysay Award, and many other international accolades. His legacy continues to inspire a future where science, sustainability, and social equity move hand in hand. Rajeev Sethi Shri. Rajeev Sethi is one of South Asia’s foremost designers, scenographers, and cultural visionaries, celebrated for elevating India’s craft heritage on the global stage. Founder of the Asian Heritage Foundation and a founding member of INTACH, he has led landmark exhibitions like Aditi and The Golden Eye, and created the iconic Jaya He GVK New Museum at Mumbai Airport (T2). His work bridges design, heritage, and community empowerment, earning him the Padma Bhushan, Order of Merit of the Federal Republic of Germany, and global recognition as a pioneer in cultural conservation and creative industries. Rationale In a world shaped by climate fragility, rapid technological evolution, and complex socio-cultural transitions, design research must evolve from craft-oriented problem-solving to systemic, transdisciplinary co-creation. Transdisciplinarity in Research and Education Transdisciplinary research is an approach that iteratively interweaves knowledge systems, skills, methodologies, values, and fields of expertise within inclusive and innovative collaborations. It bridges academic disciplines and community perspectives, merging science and practice to develop transformative outcomes that respond to complex societal challenges in a robust and meaningful way. However, transdisciplinarity is not merely a tool to generate optimal solutions. It is a way of living, working and thinking that democratizes knowledge-making practices and empowers diverse stakeholders to engage actively with the problems they experience. When scientists collaborate with society as equal partners on relevant problems, the process of knowledge discovery becomes a mutual learning process for all partners involved, shifting from a top-down to a transformative approach. With an unparalleled commitment towards research and advancement, the COEs are the forerunners of groundbreaking research and driving technological progress. They also serve as the platform that facilitates the exchange of ideas and knowledge among some of the best minds in the country. They are the embodiment of the University’s dedication towards the pursuit of knowledge and excellence to shape a brighter future. Connecting Society, Industry and Education Centres of Excellence at WPU GŌA serve as the intellectual bridges that connect the university with society, industry, government, and global thought leaders. They are designed to ensure that education is not confined to classrooms alone, but deeply influenced by real-world needs, emerging technologies, and societal aspirations. They transform the university into a living ecosystem of ideas, inquiry, and impact, enabling students to graduate not only as experts in their field, but as thoughtful contributors to society. At Chalchitra, we firmly believe that film and media serve as potent tools for both communication and artistic creativity. MIT-WPU / Chalchitra Chalchitra halchitra is a dynamic group of student clubs that passionately encourages the use of filmmaking as a powerful medium of expression. At Chalchitra, we firmly believe that film and media serve as potent tools for both communication and artistic creativity. Student Clubs Chalchitra Chalchitra is a dynamic group of student clubs that passionately encourages the use of filmmaking as a powerful medium of expression. At Chalchitra, we firmly believe that film and media serve as potent tools for both communication and artistic creativity. Our platform provides students with a unique opportunity to delve into the intricacies of filmmaking. With the guidance of experienced faculty mentors, we empower students to embark on exciting journeys in creating amateur film projects. Chalchitra is not just a club; it's a vibrant showcase of emerging talent. Throughout the year, we organise numerous events that feature the expressive filmmaking prowess of our young members. It's a union of Uday Film Club, Filmmakers Club, and Iptada Film Club, and together, we are dedicated to nurturing and celebrating the art of cinema. entries.text: MIT-WPU Blog : Chem E Car Chem E Car Established in 2018 under the School of Chemical Engineering, Team Chem-E-Car is a testament to the dedication and passion of chemical engineering students. We recognised the need for a platform that goes beyond the classroom to explore and apply our engineering skills, and we've been on an exciting journey ever since. MIT-WPU / Chem E Car Chem E Car Established in 2018 under the School of Chemical Engineering, Team Chem-E-Car is a testament to the dedication and passion of chemical engineering students. We recognised the need for a platform that goes beyond the classroom to explore and apply our engineering skills, and we've been on an exciting journey ever since. Student Clubs Chem E Car Established in 2018 under the School of Chemical Engineering, Team Chem-E-Car is a testament to the dedication and passion of chemical engineering students. We recognised the need for a platform that goes beyond the classroom to explore and apply our engineering skills, and we've been on an exciting journey ever since. Governed by the American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE), our primary mission is to participate in the prestigious Chem-E-Car competition during the Student Regional Conference for the Southeast Asian Region. This competition serves as a challenging arena for showcasing our engineering prowess and innovation. Join us at Team Chem-E-Car as we strive for excellence, innovation, and global recognition. We are the driving force behind the future of chemical engineering, and we invite you to be part of our journey. Let's engineer solutions and race towards success together. entries.title: Civil Engineering Students Participate in International Immersion Programme in China
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU Civil Engineering students gain international learning experience through an immersion programme in China, exploring global engineering practices, innovation, and industry trends.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Civil Engineering Students Join China Immersion Programme Civil Engineering Students Participate in International Immersion Programme in China MIT-WPU Civil Engineering students gain international learning experience through an immersion programme in China, exploring global engineering practices, innovation, and industry trends. MIT-WPU / Events / Civil Engineering Students Participate in International Immersion Programme in China Events Civil Engineering Students Participate in International Immersion Programme in China MIT-WPU Civil Engineering students gain international learning experience through an immersion programme in China, exploring global engineering practices, innovation, and industry trends. Events Happenings Civil Engineering Students Participate in International Immersion Programme in China Events The Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune successfully conducted an International Immersion Programme to China from 31 May to 6 June 2026 , offering students valuable global exposure to advanced engineering practices, urban development, and infrastructure systems. Team-Acceleracers-1 The seven-day programme covered Shanghai, Beijing, and Tianjin , combining academic learning, industry engagement, and cultural exploration. Students visited Shanghai Jiao Tong University and the Shanghai Urban Planning Exhibition Center , gaining insights into contemporary engineering education, sustainable urban planning, and smart city development. An industry visit to the Volkswagen Manufacturing Facility provided exposure to modern manufacturing processes and industrial engineering practices. Students also explored key infrastructure systems, including the Yu Yuan Metro Station , China's extensive metro network, and its world-renowned high-speed rail system

entries.title: College of Human Performance and Longevity
entries.excerpt: Explore human performance and longevity programs at WPU Goa focused on wellness, fitness, mental resilience, and holistic development.
entries.text: College of Human Performance and Longevity · WPU Goa College of Human Performance and Longevity Explore human performance and longevity programs at WPU Goa focused on wellness, fitness, mental resilience, and holistic development. WPU Goa / Academics / College of Human Performance and Longevity Academic model College of Human Performance and Longevity Explore human performance and longevity programs at WPU Goa focused on wellness, fitness, mental resilience, and holistic development. The College of Human Performance and Longevity is the sixth, but distinctly vital, dimension of the entire university framework. WPU GŌA is India’s first Human Performance and Longevity Campus. While other elements of the university framework focus on the mind and purpose, this College shapes the body, discipline, lifestyle, and resilience of students. The College of Human Performance & Longevity at WPU GŌA aims to create a future-ready generation by integrating sports science, health education, and human performance practices into everyday university life. The objective is to empower students with scientific awareness, disciplined habits, and lifelong wellness skills that support academic excellence, personal growth, and long-term health. Beyond Intellectual Capability Our guiding philosophy is simple: students must not only learn well, they should also live well. We believe that true success requires the synergy of a strong mind, a resilient body, a disciplined lifestyle, and a purpose-driven spirit. This College ensures students graduate with not just intellectual capability but also physical resilience, mental clarity, and lifelong wellness habits. Here we integrate sports, fitness science, lifestyle education, and world-class athletic development. Focus Areas Longevity Campus Initiativ

entries.title: Comparative Investigation on Battery Thermal Management System for Nano-Fluids.
entries.excerpt: The study demonstrates the promising potential of graphene nanofluids as a viable solution for enhancing the thermal management of lithium-ion batteries. By optimizing the use of nanofluids, we can improve battery safety, efficiency, and lifespan, leading…
entries.text: Battery Thermal Management System for Nano-Fluids : MIT-WPU Student Project Comparative Investigation on Battery Thermal Management System for Nano-Fluids. The study demonstrates the promising potential of graphene nanofluids as a viable solution for enhancing the thermal management of lithium-ion batteries. By optimizing the use of nanofluids, we can improve battery safety, efficiency, and lifespan, leading to advancements in various applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Student Projects / Comparative Investigation on Battery Thermal Management System for Nano-Fluids. Life Wpu Comparative Investigation on Battery Thermal Management System for Nano-Fluids. The study demonstrates the promising potential of graphene nanofluids as a viable solution for enhancing the thermal management of lithium-ion batteries. By optimizing the use of nanofluids, we can improve battery safety, efficiency, and lifespan, leading to advancements in various applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. Student Projects Student Projects Project Details The study demonstrates the promising potential of graphene nanofluids as a viable solution for enhancing the thermal management of lithium-ion batteries. By optimizing the use of nanofluids, we can improve battery safety, efficiency, and lifespan, leading to advancements in various applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. The present investigation delves into the intricate aspects of thermal management concerning batteries within a system. Our focus involved conducting comprehensive experiments pertaining to the Battery Thermal Management System (BTMS), wherein we employed graphene nanoparticles to formulate nanofluid. MIT-WPU has a distinguished 43-year legacy of fostering excellence in academics. With more than 150 contemporary academic programs and a global alumni network exceeding 100,000 professionals, the University is committed to advancing excellence in education anchored in peace, ethics, innovation, and national development. Under the visionary leadership of Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President, MIT-WPU continues to contribute meaningfully to the evolution of Indian higher education for the twenty-first century. entries.excerpt: The idea of sustainable technology has gained popularity and acceptance as a crucial trend. Read more about HSWTECH 2025 At MIT-WPU.
entries.text: International Conference on Sustainable Technologies: MIT-WPU Events Conference Theme The idea of sustainable technology has gained popularity and acceptance as a crucial trend. Read more about HSWTECH 2025 At MIT-WPU. Organising Committee Advisory Committee HSWTech-Web-banner Technical Program Committee Program Schedule VLSIFPGA-tool Call for Papers Submission Guidelines Important Dates Wearable-Antenna-Workshop-Flyer Call for Workshops / Tutorials The idea of sustainable technology has gained popularity and acceptance as a crucial trend. Adopting sustainable technology is vital for long-term success as businesses integrate their operations with the global environment. Embracing these principles now is an investment for the future, driven by responsible and resilient technology. Sustainable technology focuses on creating greener methods to improve the effects that homes, businesses, and communities have on the environment. A smart world is a digital environment that is continuously networked and equipped with sensors, actuators, displays, and computing components. The idea is to incorporate these components seamlessly into commonplace things. As the world moves toward a more connected and technology-driven future, the need for sustainable solutions has never been more urgent. Tiny-ML-to-Cloud-Workshop This conference explores the integration of sustainable technologies with smart innovations to create a robust and intelligent world. As businesses and societies align with global environmental goals, the adoption of responsible technology becomes crucial for long-term success. By seamlessl

Student Clubs CSI The Computer Society of India (CSI) is a prestigious organization representing computer professionals in India. Established in 1965, CSI boasts a network of 72 chapters, 511 student branches, and over 100,000 members. CSI is dedicated to upholding professional integrity, fostering collaboration, and providing knowledge exchange opportunities. CSI3 CSI's mission is to advance computer and IT knowledge in India through conferences, workshops, training programmes, and publications. It is committed to holistic student development, bridging the gap between industry and academia, and promoting social responsibility. Join us to explore the world of technology. MIT-WPU / Events / CXO Meet 2025: Industry Leaders Address AI's Potential and Ethical Challenges Events CXO Meet 2025: Industry Leaders Address AI's Potential and Ethical Challenges The Ramcharan School of Leadership successfully hosted the CXO Meet 2025, where industry leaders and experts gathered to discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) while emphasising critical concerns around ethics, regulation, and misuse. Events Happenings CXO Meet 2025: Industry Leaders Address AI's Potential and Ethical Challenges Events The Ramcharan School of Leadership successfully hosted the CXO Meet 2025, where industry leaders and experts gathered to discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI), while emphasizing critical concerns around ethics, regulation, and misuse. The event provided a platform for meaningful dialogues on the future of AI and its implications across various industries. CXO_student Prominent industry leaders who contributed to the panel discussions ‘Designing with Purpose: Ethics, Sustainability, and Innovation’ included Mr. Deepak Ahuja (CHRO, Atul Ltd), Mr. S. Rehman (CEO, Garware Technical Fibres), Mr. Farhan Pettiwala (Group President, Reliance Industries), and Mr. Gopal Sharma (CFO, NASSCOM), among others. Their discussions revolved around the need for robust oversight, ethical frameworks, and guardrails to ensure AI is used responsibly across

entries.title: Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU's Cybersecurity Bootcamp trained students in SOC, VAPT, Cloud Security & GRC - bridging the gap between academics and industry hiring. Read highlights.
entries.text: Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp MIT-WPU's Cybersecurity Bootcamp trained students in SOC, VAPT, Cloud Security & GRC - bridging the gap between academics and industry hiring. Read highlights. MIT-WPU / Events / Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp Events Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp MIT-WPU's Cybersecurity Bootcamp trained students in SOC, VAPT, Cloud Security & GRC - bridging the gap between academics and industry hiring. Read highlights. Events Happenings Events MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune conducted the Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp to bridge the gap between academic learning and industry expectations by providing students with practical exposure, domain-specific skills, and placement-oriented preparation in the field of cybersecurity. The bootcamp prepared students for entry-level cybersecurity roles across key domains, including: Security Operations Centre (SOC) Cybersecurity Placement Accelerator Bootcamp Vulnerability Assessment & Penetration Testing (VAPT) Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Cloud Security Data Privacy & Information Security The programme also focused on building strong technical fundamentals, enhancing problem-solving capabilities, and improving students' confidence for campus recruitment and industry interviews. Related public links Happenings

Data Protection and Privacy Statement This notice describes the information practices of MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) related to the websites and applications (“Site”) on which this statement is posted, including mobile websites and apps. Welcome to the official website of MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). This statement demonstrates our firm’s commitment to respect your individual privacy on the Site and to properly manage, protect, and process your personal data. For this reason, we would like to explain how we process your personal data when you interact with us. If you are only browsing this Site or using the Search function, we do not capture data that allows us to identify you individually. While user sessions will be tracked, the users will remain anonymous. If you choose to submit an application form or an email that contains personally identifiable data, for MIT-WPU to process or to render you a service, we may share that relevant data within the University, or if necessary, with other Government agencies or universities, so as to serve you efficiently and effectively.
