entries.title: Department of Bioengineering
entries.excerpt: Discover the mission, vision, and advanced research at MIT-WPU's Bioengineering Dept. Meet our expert faculty and explore our state-of-the-art labs.
entries.text: About the Bioengineering Dept. | MIT-WPU, Pune Department of Bioengineering Discover the mission, vision, and advanced research at MIT-WPU's Bioengineering Dept. Meet our expert faculty and explore our state-of-the-art labs. MIT-WPU / Schools / Faculty Of Engineering And Technology / School Of Engineering And Technology / Department Of Bioengineering / Department of Bioengineering Schools Department of Bioengineering Discover the mission, vision, and advanced research at MIT-WPU's Bioengineering Dept. Meet our expert faculty and explore our state-of-the-art labs. About The Department of Bioengineering at MIT-WPU, Pune offers an interdisciplinary programme that seamlessly integrates biology, engineering, and technology to address some of the most pressing global challenges. Situated within the vibrant ecosystem of the School of Engineering and Technology and the School of Computer Science and Engineering - both with a rich legacy of over 43 years - the department benefits from a strong foundation of academic excellence and innovation. Students are mentored by dedicated faculty with international research and industry experience and gain insights from experts across allied disciplines. The program emphasizes hands-on learning, offering access to 20+ advanced laboratories, including the Central Instrument Facility, to nurture innovation and research capabilities. With a GATE-aligned curriculum and a diverse range of professional elective tracks such as Biomedical Engineering, Biomechanics, Bioinformatics, Bioprocess Engineering, and Genetic Engineering, the department equips students with both technical expertise and industry readiness. Integrating Artificial Intelligence and sustainable technologies, it prepares graduates for impactful careers in biotechnology, healthcare

entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU's Department of Biosciences & Technology signs an MOU with Haystack Analytics - IIT Bombay's clinical genomics leader - unlocking research and internship access for biotech students.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Biosciences Signs MOU with Haystack Analytics Department of Biosciences and Technology Signs MoU with Haystack Analytics MIT-WPU's Department of Biosciences & Technology signs an MOU with Haystack Analytics - IIT Bombay's clinical genomics leader - unlocking research and internship access for biotech students. MIT-WPU / News / Department of Biosciences and Technology Signs MoU with Haystack Analytics News Department of Biosciences and Technology Signs MoU with Haystack Analytics MIT-WPU's Department of Biosciences & Technology signs an MOU with Haystack Analytics - IIT Bombay's clinical genomics leader - unlocking research and internship access for biotech students. Happenings Happenings News The Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT World Peace University, Pune, has recently signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Haystack Analytics, a genomics-driven diagnostics company focused on delivering clinically actionable insights through advanced sequencing technologies and automated analysis. Department of Biosciences and Technology Signs MoU with Haystack Analytics Haystack Analytics is dedicated to developing integrated solutions for real-world healthcare settings, enabling faster, standardized, and scalable genomic decision-making. This collaboration aims to create a strong academic-industry interface, opening avenues for joint workshops, collaborative research projects, and knowledge exchange initiatives. These efforts will provide students and faculty with meaningful opportunities to engage with emerging advancements in genomics and healthcare technologies. The partnership reflects MIT-WPU’s continued commitment to fostering industry-aligned learning, promoting research excellence, and enhancing the overall academic experience. The University l

About The Department of Business at MIT-WPU provides a comprehensive curriculum encompassing core, interdisciplinary, and elective courses, bolstered by skill-based certifications. The pedagogical approach combines innovative teaching methods, immersive hands-on training, live projects, industry site visits, capstone projects, structured summer internships, enlightening seminars, hands-on workshops, and access to international credit programmes. Esteemed academicians and industry stalwarts collaborate to deliver industry-aligned training, equipping students with the prowess to tackle the dynamic corporate landscape. Discover our vision, mission, academic excellence, and dedication to provide, via innovative education and research, industry-ready chemical engineers. About The Department of Chemical Engineering at MIT-WPU offers a comprehensive blend of academics, experiential research, industrial training, and practical projects to prepare students for diverse careers. Graduates are equipped for roles in industries spanning petroleum refining, petrochemicals, polymers, biochemicals, biomedical devices, materials, drugs, fertilizers, dyes, textiles, ceramics, and foods. The department also prepares students for emerging fields such as data analytics for predictive process solutions, smart manufacturing technologies, process automation and control, process modelling simulation and optimisation, green energy, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and more. Notably, the department has secured numerous research grants from national funding agencies, fostering a conducive environment for cutting-edge research and innovation. Our Objectives To develop globally recognised ethical, responsible chemical engineering professionals contributing to nation-building. To promote higher education, research, innovation, and entrepreneurship amon

About The Department of Civil Engineering fosters a deep understanding of the engineering field, emphasising creativity and razor-sharp analytical skills. The department inspires and instil in students the values of discipline and teamwork. It places a strong emphasis on cultivating a robust research ecosystem, equipping students with the ethical values of integrity and social responsibility as well as technical skills essential for thriving careers in civil engineering. In its pursuit of excellence, the department has crystallized its educational objectives to encompass a holistic understanding of all aspects of civil engineering. The educational objectives of the Department of Civil Engineering underscore the importance of practical knowledge, ensuring programme-specific outcomes. Graduates become adept at effectively addressing engineering challenges and taking on roles that are both innovative and result-driven. The department fosters a well-rounded education and comprehensive professional development for aspirants, preparing them for excellence in the field of civil engineering. Learn about our vision, mission, and dedication to clinical education, research, and innovative healthcare. About In response to the pressing demand for proficient Clinical Psychologists amidst the COVID-19 pandemic, the Department of Clinical Sciences inaugurated the esteemed M.Sc. Clinical Psychology programme in 2021. This two-year, full-time course was strategically designed to cater to the burgeoning interest among students, positioning Clinical Psychology as a highly sought-after field of study. Through this comprehensive programme, students gain deep insights into the intricacies and expansiveness of this domain, acquiring essential skills pivotal for a thriving clinical practice. The department is dedicated to fostering equal opportunities for all and nurturing a culture of inclusivity within the academic fraternity. Our Objectives icon To equip students with knowledge, skills, and research aptitude to complete an entry-level position in the industry or pursue higher education. entries.text: About - Department Of Design At MIT-WPU Department of Design MIT-WPU's Department of Design works to inspire in its design students technical capabilities, creativity, and innovation. Emphasizing practical learning heavily, the department uses specialized courses and professional advice from seasoned instructors to equip students for the changing design field. MIT-WPU / Schools / Faculty Of Design Humanities And Liberal Arts / School Of Design / Department Of Design / Department of Design Schools Department of Design MIT-WPU's Department of Design works to inspire in its design students technical capabilities, creativity, and innovation. Emphasizing practical learning heavily, the department uses specialized courses and professional advice from seasoned instructors to equip students for the changing design field. About The Department of Design at MIT-WPU stands as a premier design institution in India, offering a spectrum of undergraduate and postgraduate programmes designed to equip students for diverse careers. The programmes encompass fields like visual communication, user experience, interior design, fashion, and more. Students’ contextual understanding of design, creative thinking, artistic prowess, and technical skills are enhanced. Students learn to craft prototypes, interfaces, products, interiors, and user experiences that align with current market trends. Complementing classroom learning, our co-curricular activities encompass entrepreneurship training, interdisciplinary collaborative innovation and research projects, peer learning, and engaging industry visits. Additionally, study tours to renowned institutions such as the National Institute of Design in Ahmedabad, and IIT Gandhinagar, and international expos like Dubai Expo provide students with a well-round

About The Department of Education at WPU School of Education, MIT-WPU, is dedicated to moulding students into conscientious, mindful, and empathetic educators for the future. The forward-looking curriculum of the B.Ed. and M.Ed. degree programmes, curated by esteemed academicians integrates the most effective pedagogical techniques and theories. A blend of regular faculty and guest lectures, along with focused classroom management sessions, is instrumental in training candidates to comprehend student psychology effectively & overcome the challenges of the academic environment. These programmes are designed to empower future educators with the ability to amalgamate the best practices from Western pedagogy with the ideals of India's Gurukul system, fostering a more student-centric learning environment. People → Academic programmes → Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action. MIT-WPU / Schools / Faculty Of Business And Leadership / School Of Business / Department Of Hospitality Management / Department of Hospitality Management Schools Department of Hospitality Management MIT-WPU's Department of Hospitality Management seeks to produce experts in customer service, operations, and hotel management. The program gives students professional exposure and practical experience to equip them for prosperous futures in the exciting hotel sector. About The Department of Hospitality Management at MIT-WPU offers industry-curated BBA degree and certificate programmes to equip students for success in the hospitality industry. These practical courses, featuring project-based experiential learning, are led by industry experts and esteemed academicians. The department boasts modern laboratories featuring cutting-edge equipment and facilities, ensuring every student gains a comprehensive understanding of the hospitality industry through hands-on learning and real-world exposure. entries.text: Department of Mathematics and Statistics Department of Mathematics and Statistics The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Mathematics and Statistics Structure, uncertainty, modelling, and data. 23 faculty 4 research themes 5 programmes Overview The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. People → Academic programmes → Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets. Discover our goal to produce talented engineers helping to propel world technology forward. About The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-WPU covers a wide range of disciplines, including design, thermodynamics, materials science, manufacturing processes, automation, and robotics. Students explore areas like e-mobility, bio-medical applications, aircraft, energy systems, and modern industrial processes, gaining a holistic view of mechanical engineering. Well-equipped laboratories support hands-on learning, allowing students to apply theoretical knowledge practically. The department collaborates with government entities such as DRDO and CME, involving faculty in research initiatives. Partnerships with top organisations provide academic structure, guest lectures, industry visits, and internships. The education offered is comprehensive, preparing students for diverse career opportunities in the evolving field of mechanical engineering. entries.excerpt: MIT‑WPU Mechanical Engineering Department partners with Regal Rexnord to enhance research, internships, and industry projects.
entries.text: Mechanical Engg Dept Signs MoU with Regal Rexnord : News Department of Mechanical Engineering MOU with Regal Rexnord Company MIT‑WPU Mechanical Engineering Department partners with Regal Rexnord to enhance research, internships, and industry projects. MIT-WPU / News / Department of Mechanical Engineering MOU with Regal Rexnord Company News Department of Mechanical Engineering MOU with Regal Rexnord Company MIT‑WPU Mechanical Engineering Department partners with Regal Rexnord to enhance research, internships, and industry projects. Happenings Happenings News We are thrilled to share that the Department of Mechanical Engineering recently signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Regal Rexnord, a renowned company located in Hinjewadi, Pune. This collaboration signifies a strong bond between academia and industry, offering our students invaluable opportunities for real-world learning and professional development. Department of Mechanical Engineering MOU with Regal Rexnord Company Key to this partnership was Prof. (Dr.) Pankaj Dhatrak, our Single Point of Contact (SPOC) coordinated and implemented the MOU. His dedication and expertise ensured the successful alignment of our academic goals with industry requirements. The signing ceremony took place with great enthusiasm and pride. Prof. (Dr.) Deepak Hujare, Programme Coordinator from Robotics and Automation, proudly represented our institution during the signing of the MOU between MITWPU and Regal Rexnord, Pune. This collaboration paves the way for a mutually beneficial relationship that fosters advancements in both education and industry practices. Discover our dedication to the development of future communication leaders, programs, and vision. About The Department of Media and Communication at MIT-WPU stands as a premier institution for media studies and journalism in the country. Offering both undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the department places a strong emphasis on cultivating critical and analytical skills in students while imparting knowledge about the intricate interplay between society, media, and communication. Its pedagogy approach employs a blend of theoretical lectures, tutorials, classroom presentations, and industry-oriented assignments to equip students for diverse roles in the media, broadcasting, journalism, and communication sectors, with a specific focus on emerging technologies and online media channels. Beyond conventional classroom learning, the institution’s multifaceted approach ensures that students not only grasp theoretical concepts but also gain practical insights into the dynamic field of media and communication. Interactive sessions with industry experts and academicians play a pivotal role in shaping students’ perspectives, enabling them to delve deeper into the nuances of the ever-evolving media industry. Discover our purpose, academic offerings, dedication to industry-driven innovation and sustainable energy solutions. About The Department of Petroleum Engineering, the second oldest in India & the only one in Maharashtra, offers a distinctive B.Tech Petroleum Engineering with a specialised, industry-focused curriculum. This programme prepares students to tackle challenges & contribute to the nation’s energy security. The curriculum covers reservoir, production, drilling engineering, & petroleum exploration, drawing from various engineering disciplines such as mechanical, electrical, electronics, & chemical engineering. Students also gain exposure to data sciences, machine learning, computer modelling & simulation, & alternate energy, ensuring their readiness for the industry. The department hosts four prominent professional students’ chapters, facilitating direct interaction between students & the professional petroleum engineering community. The MIT-WPU SPE student chapter has consistently received the “Student Chapter Excellence Award” for the past five years. Our Objectives To be a center of academ

The department places a strong emphasis on practical learning through diverse projects, MOOCs (Massive Open Online Courses), immersive experiences, research-focused modules, industry excursions, and a comprehensive six-month full-time industry or research-oriented project. These initiatives sharpen students’ abilities in problem-solving, analytical thinking, logical reasoning, and research, equipping them with the confidence to excel in research, academia, and industry, leaving a lasting impact. Our Objectives To develop minds inquisitive about Physics and the world around. icon To train students to think in a scientific manner and carry out scientific investigations to draw verifiable conclusions. About The MIT-WPU Department of Polytechnic is dedicated to delivering value-based education and fostering entrepreneurial skills, ensuring that students are well prepared for both employment and entrepreneur-ship. Since its inception in 1996, the department (formerly Sri Savitribai Phule Polytechnic) has been a prominent institution in Maharashtra, featuring state-of-the-art infrastructure and a distinguished faculty. The department promotes a problem-solving mindset and emphasises social innovation, aiming to develop well-rounded individuals with the confidence and skills necessary for real-world challenges. Through its comprehensive academic programmes, the department focuses on holistic development, preparing students to excel in their professional and entrepreneurial endeavours. Our Objectives To impart industry-oriented technical and skill-based education in emerging engineering domains. icon To develop innovative and entrepreneurial professionals capable of solving real-world problems. About The Department of Public Health at MIT-WPU is dedicated to advancing knowledge and innovation in public health with a clear mission: ‘Health for all.’ The department focuses on educating students about the socio-economic disparities and their impact on the well-being of populations, as well as addressing public health sector needs in a sustainable manner. Students receive comprehensive exposure to various public health issues and are equipped with research and analytical skills to analyse and address these challenges. They learn to understand the social, economic, and environmental factors influencing public health and gain the skills to develop and implement public health policies and programmes tailored to diverse communities. Students are well-prepared for careers in health

entries.title: Design and Development of Valve Stem Seal Test Rig
entries.excerpt: Explore the Valve Stem Seal Test Rig project developed by MIT-WPU students, showcasing engineering innovation, practical design expertise, and industry-focused problem-solving skills.
entries.text: Valve Stem Seal Test Rig Project by MIT-WPU Students Design and Development of Valve Stem Seal Test Rig Explore the Valve Stem Seal Test Rig project developed by MIT-WPU students, showcasing engineering innovation, practical design expertise, and industry-focused problem-solving skills. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Student Projects / Design and Development of Valve Stem Seal Test Rig Life Wpu Design and Development of Valve Stem Seal Test Rig Explore the Valve Stem Seal Test Rig project developed by MIT-WPU students, showcasing engineering innovation, practical design expertise, and industry-focused problem-solving skills. Student Projects Student Projects Project Details This project focuses on the design and development of a specialized test rig for evaluating valve stem seals used in internal combustion engines. The rig incorporates advanced technologies such as Arduino Uno, servo motors, a two-bar linkage mechanism, and an oil pump to simulate real-world operating conditions. This Project outlines the design and development of a Valve Stem Seal Test Rig, which integrates cutting-edge technologies like Arduino Uno, servo motors, a two-bar linkage mechanism, and an oil pump for lubrication. By addressing the need for precise testing methodologies in the automotive industry, particularly concerning the evaluation of valve stem seals in internal combustion engines, this critical apparatus fills a significant gap in the field. The systematic design process, guided by engineering principles and advanced solutions, yielded a versatile and accurate testing platform. The incorporation of Arduino Uno allows for seamless control and automation, while servo motors and the two-bar linkage mechanism enable precise manipulation of operational conditions. entries.title: Designing the Future: The Growing Importance of Semiconductor and VLSI Skills
entries.excerpt: Learn why semiconductor and VLSI skills are in high demand. Explore career scope, industry growth, and how to build expertise in chip design today.
entries.text: Semiconductor & VLSI Skills: Designing the Future Designing the Future: The Growing Importance of Semiconductor and VLSI Skills Learn why semiconductor and VLSI skills are in high demand. Explore career scope, industry growth, and how to build expertise in chip design today. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Designing the Future: The Growing Importance of Semiconductor and VLSI Skills Faculty Articles Designing the Future: The Growing Importance of Semiconductor and VLSI Skills Learn why semiconductor and VLSI skills are in high demand. Explore career scope, industry growth, and how to build expertise in chip design today. 978 words 4 min read Semiconductor chips are silent passengers in nearly every modern advance, yet they power almost anything with the potential to change our future. Artificial intelligence. Electric vehicles. IoT. 5G and 6G. Robotics. Smart healthcare. High-performance computing. All of these need a strong semiconductor design to provide. This is precisely the reason why Semiconductor and VLSI Design has lost its so-called niche status. It has an emerging field of engineering for the next generation. India is at a significant and pivotal juncture in its developmental trajectory. The Government of India claims that the value of the semiconductor market in the country was about $38 billion as of 2023, grew massively to an estimated range of approximately $45 to 50 billion over the period between 2024 and 2025, and will continue to grow significantly with expectations reaching a valuation of anywhere from $100-$110 billion by 2030. Moreover, in terms of policy initiative, this is a strategic policy direction which further establishes that India is progressively and systematically emerging as a major global semiconductor hub internationally against the backg

entries.title: Dr. Aachal Taywade
entries.excerpt: Personality, Smartphone Addiction, Parenting, youth mental health, and interpersonal relationships.
entries.text: Dr. Aachal Taywade Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Personality, Smartphone Addiction, Parenting, youth mental health, and interpersonal relationships. Dr. Aachal is a distinguished academician and researcher, holding a PhD in Psychology with a specialization in Personality Factors, Psychopathology, Intelligence, and Smartphone Usage Behavior of Adolescents. A gold medalist in her MA Psychology program, she has in depth understanding of areas like Developmental Psychology, Educational Psychology, Personality Psychology, and Smartphone Addiction. Dr. Aachal has worked with renowned institutions, including Bharatiya Vidya Bhavan, Podar International School, Matru Sewa Sangh Institute of Social Work, Amity University, giving her a very broad perspective in her teaching practices. Her expertise has been pivotal in designing syllabi and developing course structures for some private universities in Maharashtra. Her counseling practice is grounded in humanistic and cognitive approaches, with a strong focus on the work of eminent psychologists such as Viktor Frankl, Abraham Maslow, Aaron Beck, Robert McCrae, Albert Ellis, Alfred Adler, and Karen Horney. She has received professional training and certifications in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Rational Emotive Behavior Therapy (REBT), and her practice reflects her deep belief that every individual has a unique pot

entries.title: Dr. Aaditi Khare
entries.excerpt: Mrs. Aaditi A Khare is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Liberal Arts having a Teaching experience of 10 years in the field of Psychology at undergraduate and postgraduate level. Her teaching philosophy focuses on clear understanding, practical…
entries.text: Dr. Aaditi Khare Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Mrs. Aaditi A Khare is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Liberal Arts having a Teaching experience of 10 years in the field of Psychology at undergraduate and postgraduate level. Her teaching philosophy focuses on clear understanding, practical learning, and active student participation. She is motivated by the desire to help students develop strong subject knowledge, critical thinking, and good values. She believes in creating a supportive and engaging classroom environment that encourages curiosity and continuous learning. In line with MIT-WPU’s vision, She aims to contribute through effective teaching, innovative methods, and academic commitment, helping students grow into skilled professionals and responsible individuals who can positively contribute to society. Her PhD is in the domain of undergraduate college students’ well-being.

entries.title: Dr. Aarti Renukadas Shastri
entries.excerpt: Natural Products
entries.text: Dr. Aarti Renukadas Shastri Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Natural Products Dr. Aarti Shastri is an Assistant Professor in the field of Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry, specializing in the extraction, isolation and separation of chemical constituents from herbal drugs. She has a PhD in the same field and has extensive experience in pharmacological and phytochemical evaluation of herbal drugs/formulations for new therapeutic indications. Her research work is focused on natural products and their therapeutic uses. Dr. Shastri has published several research papers and articles in national and international journals and has presented her work at various conferences and seminars. Her expertise in natural products has been recognized by her peers and she is often invited as a speaker at conferences and workshops. As an academic, Dr. Shastri is passionate about teaching and mentoring students. She uses her research work to enrich her teaching and provide real-world examples to students. Her enthusiasm and dedication have earned her the respect and admiration of her students and colleagues.

entries.title: Dr. Aavishkar Chittaranjan Katti
entries.excerpt: Theoretical nonlinear optics, nonlinear dynamics, optical solitons, and photonic systems.
entries.text: Dr. Aavishkar Chittaranjan Katti Assistant Professor Department of Physics Photonics Research Group Theoretical nonlinear optics, nonlinear dynamics, optical solitons, and photonic systems. Dr. Aavishkar Katti obtained his Ph.D. in Physics from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, and his Master’s degree in Physics from the University of Delhi. His doctoral research specialized in theoretical nonlinear optics, with a focus on optical solitons and photonic systems. He has qualified several prestigious national-level examinations, including CSIR- NET (All India Rank 15), GATE, and SET (Gujarat), demonstrating strong academic excellence and foundational expertise in physics. Dr. Katti has nearly a decade of teaching experience. He is currently serving as Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, and has previously taught at Banasthali Vidyapith, Rajasthan. His teaching spans core physics courses alongwith specialized courses across the spectrum of optics and photonics. He has secured competitive research funding from government agencies. As Principal Investigator, he was awarded a Core Research Grant from the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB-DST), Government of India. He is also Co- Principal Investigator on an ongoing A2K+ Studies grant from the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR), Gov

He brings over twelve years of teaching experience and a strong background in legal research, policy development, and public safety initiatives. Dr. Dhere has designed and delivered courses in law, legal policy, and governance, emphasizing the connection between academic learning and practical societal impact. He actively engages students through workshops, seminars, and outreach programs that simplify complex legal and regulatory issues, fostering critical thinking and civic responsibility. A proud alumnus of the University of Pune, he completed his undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral studies in Law, and is also an alumnus of the Hague Academy of International Law (2024). He has been actively involved with the WHO Legal Development Programme on Road Safety (India) from 2016 to 2019 and is a distinguished alumnus of the Global Road Safety Leadership Course conducted by the Global Road Safety Policy and Johns Hopkins International Injury Research Unit, USA. entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhijeet Subhash Sutar is a distinguished academician and researcher in the domain of Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Pharmaceutical Analysis, currently serving as Program Director) at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over 20…
entries.text: Dr. Abhijeet Subhash Sutar Associate Professor and Program Director Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Abhijeet Subhash Sutar is a distinguished academician and researcher in the domain of Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Pharmaceutical Analysis, currently serving as Program Director) at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over 20 years of extensive teaching and research experience, he has consistently contributed to academic excellence, research innovation, and student mentorship. He earned his Bachelor of Pharmacy degree from Pune University in 2002, followed by a Master of Pharmacy in Pharmaceutical Chemistry from Shivaji University in 2005, and was awarded a Ph.D. in Pharmacy by Shivaji University in 2021. His doctoral research focused on advanced analytical methodologies and pharmaceutical quality evaluation. Dr. Sutar has successfully guided more than 15 undergraduate and 12 postgraduate and currently 3 research scholars registered for PhD research projects, fostering a strong research culture among students. He has been recognized for the quality and relevance of his research through competitive research grants totalling ₹4.2 lakhs from BCUD, Savitribai Phule Pune University. He is also the author of a book in Pharmaceutical Analysis and has published multiple research articles in reputed national and international journals. His work is particu

entries.title: Dr. Abhijit Bendre
entries.excerpt: Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos.
entries.text: Dr. Abhijit Bendre Assistant Professor Department of Physics Astrophysics Research Group Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos. Abhijit B. Bendre earned his PhD in Astrophysics from the Leibniz Institute for Astrophysics in Potsdam. He has been a researcher at several institutions, including the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune, India, the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL) in Switzerland and Scuola Normale Superiore (SNS) in Pisa in Italy. His research interests are cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear dynamo theory, galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium (ISM), and cosmic ray dynamos. His past research focuses on exploring the mechanisms behind the generation of magnetic fields in various celestial objects and their connection to turbulence. A list of his publications can be found here :

entries.title: Dr. Abhijit Nair
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhijit Nair has over 25 years of experience and held prominent academic and administrative positions, including Dean-Online Programs, Pan-Campus Area Chair for Liberal Arts and Business Communication, Head Placements and Head Admissions at Jaipuria…
entries.text: Dr. Abhijit Nair Associate Professor & Dean Department of Liberal Arts Dr. Abhijit Nair has over 25 years of experience and held prominent academic and administrative positions, including Dean-Online Programs, Pan-Campus Area Chair for Liberal Arts and Business Communication, Head Placements and Head Admissions at Jaipuria Institute of Management Noida. He has dabbled in entrepreneurship running an ad-agency, a travel magazine and an agriculture-venture. His contributions involve being part of academic quality enhancement, institutional accreditation (NBA, NAAC), AACSB, curriculum building and designing courses and programs. He has been involved in building curricula based on Objective Based Education (OBE). He has also designed learner engagement differentiating based on pedagogy, andragogy and Heutagogy. He has also taken part in improving industry- academia engagements, student training and development, and student affairs to improve their life at campus. Dr. Nair holds a Ph.D. in English language and literature, an Executive Program in HR Leadership from SP Jain, and MA and BA in English language and literature with Sociology and Anthropology as minors. He has been involved in Management Development programs for SME's, Public Sector undertakings and consulting assignments as a communication consultant with marketing sales boutiques and advertising agencies

entries.title: Dr. Abhijith B
entries.excerpt: Abhijith B serves as an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. He holds a PhD in Cultural Studies from The English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad. His study concentrates on contemporary Malayalam…
entries.text: Dr. Abhijith B Assistant Professor Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Abhijith B serves as an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. He holds a PhD in Cultural Studies from The English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad. His study concentrates on contemporary Malayalam cinema, particularly addressing issues of form, space, and subjectivity. By prioritising the ideology of form as a primary theoretical framework, his work analyses the historical transformations influencing Indian cinema in a broader context. His study examines the intertwining of geography and story as a crucial indicator of cinema's social component in the contemporary era, especially in the absence of concrete social markers under the neoliberal context. His study has been published in peer-reviewed journals, and he is presently examining the correlation between contemporary genre forms and the issue of political federalism in Indian cinema.

entries.title: Dr. Abhishek Madhukar Thote
entries.excerpt: Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Design and Finite Element Analysis
entries.text: Dr. Abhishek Madhukar Thote Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Design and Finite Element Analysis Dr. Abhishek M. Thote is an accomplished researcher and assistant professor in Mechanical Engineering at Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology (VNIT), Nagpur. He has a strong academic background, having completed his graduation in Mechanical Engineering in 2011 from St. Vincent Pallotti College of Engineering and Technology, Nagpur. He later pursued post-graduation in CAD-CAM specialization in 2014 from VNIT, Nagpur, and completed his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the same institute in 2019. His Ph.D. research, titled ""Biomechanics of orthodontic tooth movement in invisible braces: mathematical and experimental approach,"" has gained widespread recognition in the field of orthodontics. Dr. Thote's core areas of research include Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Design, and Finite Element Analysis. He has published seven papers in reputed international journals, all of which are either SCIE or Scopus indexed. He has also been granted two patents named ""Loop device for a plurality of teeth"" and ""A Nail Rubbing Exercise Machine."" Additionally, he has the copyright of developed computer software named TRAC (Tooth Retraction Activity Calculator). Dr. Thote's achievements also include receiving a Travel Grant b

entries.text: Dr. Ajit Baburao Deore Assistant Professor Department of Physics Corrosion science, including anti-corrosive films on steel. Dr. Ajit B Deore is an Assistant Professor of Physics with expertise in Corrosion Techniques, particularly anti-corrosive films on steel. He completed his post-graduation in Physics with a specialization in Electronics. Dr. Deore's research interests focus on the development and application of various corrosion techniques to prevent or mitigate the damaging effects of corrosion on different materials. His research has a specific emphasis on developing anti-corrosive films on steel to increase the material's durability and lifespan. Dr. Deore is committed to advancing the field of corrosion techniques and has contributed significantly to this area through his research and publications.

He holds a B.Pharm from North Maharashtra University, M.Pharm in Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry from Savitribai Phule Pune University, and a Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Sciences from JNTU, Hyderabad, and is a Registered Patent Agent (IN/PA 5393). He has published over 30 peer-reviewed research papers, contributed to multiple book chapters, and has 14 patents filed/granted to his credit. He has guided several M.Pharm dissertations, successfully supervised one Ph.D. scholar to completion, and is currently supervising seven Ph.D. scholars in the areas of Pharmaceutical Sciences. Dr. Baheti has been recognized with several awards, including the MIT Foundation Day Award (2014) and the Best Innovative Teacher Award by Savitribai Phule Pune University (2017). entries.title: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik
entries.excerpt: Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology.
entries.text: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology. Dr. Amruta Naik is working as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a goal-driven researcher and academician with a research experience of around 12 years in the field of Stem Cell Biology and Bone Biology. Dr. Amruta has received Ph.D. in Biotechnology from the University of Pune. Her research interests mainly include invention of new strategies for the application of Mesenchymal Stem Cells for the treatment of bone degenerative disorders to get better therapeutic effects. Dr. Amruta has received two research grants from DBT and DST, Govt. of India for perusing research in the field of stem cell biology and regenerative medicine. During her post-doctoral research work, she was also involved in collaborative research projects that studied the in vitro biocompatibility and osteointegration of scaffolds for bone and cartilage regeneration. She has published 14 research and review articles in peer-reviewed journals of international repute. Currently Dr. Amruta Naik is serving as a Guest Editor for Cancer Reports (Wiley) and JoVE.

Kulkarni is an expert in biodiesel production and optimization, with extensive experience in comparing different methods for controlling wax deposition in crude oil transportation, including magnetic field and pour point depressants. His current research is centered on the valorization of biomass through chemical and biological pathways. Specifically, he is exploring ways to upgrade waste glycerol - a byproduct of biodiesel production - into high-value products such as polyglycerol and polyhydroxy alkynoates (PHA).

Happenings Happenings Dr. Anand J Kulkarni Featured in Exclusive LIVE Interview on DD Sahyadri News MIT-WPU is proud to share that Dr. Anand J Kulkarni, Professor & Associate Director, Research Institute of Artificial Intelligence (Center of Excellence of AI) was recently featured in an exclusive LIVE interview on DD Sahyadri as part of the special discussion series 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' on 07 February 2025. The discussion focused on India’s progress in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the India AI Mission. During the LIVE session, Dr. Kulkarni shared his expert insights on how AI is shaping India’s future, driving self-reliance, and positioning the country as a global leader in AI. He elaborated on the transformative impact of Artificial Intelligence across industries, the role of AI in national development, and the emerging opportunities in this

Kulkarni, Professor & Associate Director of the Research Institute of Artificial Intelligence (Center of Excellence of AI), has been appointed as an Adjunct Professor in the Faculty of Information Technology at the Victorian Institute of Technology (VIT), Melbourne, Australia. This prestigious two-year appointment is a testament to Dr. Kulkarni’s outstanding contributions to research and academia, particularly in the domains of optimisation, artificial intelligence, and computational intelligence. His expertise and leadership in these fields will further strengthen international academic collaborations and cutting-edge research initiatives between institutions. entries.title: Dr. Anik Karan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anik Karan, Ph.D., MRSC is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans across multiple fields, including biomaterials science, neuroscience, cancer biology,…
entries.text: Dr. Anik Karan Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Dr. Anik Karan, Ph.D., MRSC is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans across multiple fields, including biomaterials science, neuroscience, cancer biology, tissue engineering and biomedical imaging. Dr. Karan previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Surgery and Transplant at the University of Nebraska Medical Center, Omaha, Nebraska, USA and the Department of Radiation Oncology and Department of Bioengineering at the University of Kansas Medical School and University of Kansas, contributing to studies on tissue engineering related to chronic wound healing and bone regeneration. He also has more than two years of experience in the biotechnology industry under L’Oreal, USA and Cl Lab LLC, Maryland, USA. In L’Oreal, he has worked on developing sustainable natural hair dye delivery systems through clay-based nano-carriers and in Cl Lab LLC., generating defined disease models for preclinical studies in the areas of type 2 diabetes treatment and Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF) treatments. He has done his Master of Science and Doctor of Philosophy in Biomedical Engineering from Louisiana Tech University, Louisiana, USA, on the topic of the development of novel biodeg

entries.title: Dr. Anil Satishrao Hiwale
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Communication, Healthcare, Signal Processing
entries.text: Dr. Anil Satishrao Hiwale Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Communication, Healthcare, Signal Processing Dr. Anil S. Hiwale is a renowned professor with a Ph.D. in Electronics Engineering, specialized in Wireless Communication, from SGB Amravati University. With over 33 years of teaching experience, he is an expert in Wireless Communication, Signal Processing, Healthcare, and AI/ML. Dr. Hiwale has published numerous papers in reputed journals and conferences, and he has successfully supervised three Ph.D. scholars, with five more under his guidance currently. He is currently serving as the Associate Dean (Students Development) at the Faculty of Engineering & Technology, MITWPU, and has previously held positions such as Professor, Dean R & D, and Principal of MIT College of Engineering. Dr. Hiwale is a Fellow of IETE (The Institution of Electronics & Telecommunication Engineers) and IE (Institution of Engineers, India), and he is also the Chairman of The IET Pune Local Network.

entries.title: Dr. Anil Tukaram Pawar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anil Pawar is an Associate Professor at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 17 years of experience in academic teaching, research, and industry. He holds a B. Pharm from…
entries.text: Dr. Anil Tukaram Pawar Associate Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Anil Pawar is an Associate Professor at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 17 years of experience in academic teaching, research, and industry. He holds a B. Pharm from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M. Pharm from Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Karnataka, and a Ph.D. in Pharmacology. He has published more than 40 research articles and several review papers, authored books and book chapters, and holds five patents. He has guided M. Pharm students and is currently supervising Ph.D. scholars. Dr. Pawar has also received consultancy grants, reflecting his engagement in applied research and industry collaboration, and has established strong national and international research collaborations. He is a member of several prestigious professional associations, including the Association of Pharmaceutical Teachers of India (APTI), Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA), Indian Pharmacological Society (IPS), Society of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (SPER), International Association for the Study of Traditional Asian Medicine (IASTAM-India), and the Laboratory Animal Science Association of India (LASAI). He has received multiple awards for excellence in teaching and research, including the Ideal Teacher Aw

entries.title: Dr. Anita Kumari
entries.excerpt: Behavioural Finance, Financial Management
entries.text: Dr. Anita Kumari Assistant Professor Department of Business Behavioural Finance, Financial Management Dr. Anita Kumari is a dedicated academician and finance professional with a strong background in both research and teaching. She holds a PhD in Finance, an MBA in Finance, with extensive academic experience in the field of financial management and analysis. With deep expertise in statistical analysis tools such as SPSS, SEM, PLS, SEM, and AMOS. Her academic interests span financial markets, investment analysis, and corporate finance. She is actively engaged in research and has contributed to various national and international journals. Dr. Kumari is committed to developing industry- relevant financial acumen among her students and promoting ethical financial practices.

entries.title: Dr. Anjali Ajit Sane
entries.excerpt: Financial Literacy, Banking
entries.text: Dr. Anjali Ajit Sane Dean - School of Economics and Commerce Department of Commerce & Accounting Financial Literacy, Banking An accomplished teacher with over 20 years of experience in the areas of Economics and Banking, Dr. Anjali Sane is also a capable administrator. In addition to academic administration, her research areas include Managerial Economics, Micro and Macro Economics and various areas in international business. A dedicated and sincere teacher, she is known for use of practical examples and case studies to supplement classroom learning. Apart from pursuing research in her domain areas, she is also involved in interdisciplinary areas of research such as higher education, financial literacy and so on. She has completed Minor Research Project in the area of Financial Literacy with grant from Board of College and University Development (BCUD), Savitribai Phule Pune University. Dr. Anjali Sane prefers to use a combination of quantitative and qualitative research tools while using exploratory research methodology. She has published more than 20 research papers in peer reviewed management journals of repute as well as in conference proceedings of national and international seminars and conferences. She has co-authored 3 books on Business Economics (Micro), 1 book each on Analysis of Financial Statements and Business Economics (Macro). Dr. Anjali Sane has been awarded wit

entries.title: Dr. Anjali Milind Purohit
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics
entries.text: Dr. Anjali Milind Purohit Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics Prof. Anjali Purohit is an accomplished academic and researcher in the field of Electrical Engineering. She completed her graduation in Electrical Engineering from Pune University in 1992 and later received her M.E. (Power Systems) from the same university in 2000. Currently, she is pursuing her PhD in power system protection from Amaravati University. Anjali has over 20 years of teaching experience and two years of industry experience. She is an Associate Professor in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Prof. Purohit is a member of IE (I) and Life Member of ISTE. She has organized numerous STTP and conferences for the benefit of faculty and students. Prof. Purohit has also undertaken significant administrative responsibilities throughout her career. Her research interests include power system protection, power electronics, and artificial intelligence, among others.

entries.title: Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya is a distinguished Political Scientist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), where she served over four decades in pivotal roles including Senior Professor and Chair of Political Science, Dean of Social Sciences, Director of the…
entries.text: Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya Distinguish Professor & Senior Advisor Department of Liberal Arts Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya is a distinguished Political Scientist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), where she served over four decades in pivotal roles including Senior Professor and Chair of Political Science, Dean of Social Sciences, Director of the Centre for Nepal Studies, Director of the Centre for Integrated Rural Development, and Adjunct Professor at the Malaviya Centre for Peace Research. Her academic journey includes a PhD from BHU (1981), MA from Allahabad University (1974), post-doctoral research at the London School of Economics, Woodrow Wilson International Centre for Scholars, Brown University, and an Advanced International Diploma in Conflict Resolution from Uppsala University. She has held prestigious positions such as Research Director at the UN University Institute on Conflict Resolution and Ethnicity (UK), Scholar-in-Residence at Woodrow Wilson Centre (USA), foundational ICCR Chair at Tribhuvan University (Nepal), India Chair by ICSSR New Delhi, and visiting professorships at institutions like Wellesley College, Concordia University, Stanford University, PRIO Oslo, and universities across Sweden, Germany, Australia, Canada, and Poland. Dr. Upadhyaya, a prolific scholar, has authored groundbreaking publications with leading presses such as Manchester University Pres

entries.title: Dr. Ankita Agarwal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ankita Agarwal is an experienced academician and researcher who holds a PhD in Computational Biology from IIT Kharagpur and Gold medal in M. Tech. (Bioinformatics) from West Bengal University of Technology. With a background in Chemistry and…
entries.text: Dr. Ankita Agarwal Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Dr. Ankita Agarwal is an experienced academician and researcher who holds a PhD in Computational Biology from IIT Kharagpur and Gold medal in M. Tech. (Bioinformatics) from West Bengal University of Technology. With a background in Chemistry and Bioinformatics, she has transitioned into the field of Bioengineering and Chemical Engineering, and joined as faculty at MIT-WPU. She brings a unique blend of expertise in both experimental and computational techniques, and has published research articles in leading scientific journals. Dr. Agarwal teaches a professional elective course of Bioinformatics and regular courses such as Data Science for engineers, Python for Bioinformatics and Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Biology to undergraduate students. With more than six year of previous research experience in DBT and CSIR-funded projects on computational analysis and prediction of protein-RNA interactions in human RNA-binding proteins, Dr. Agarwal’s current interest of research is to understand the sequence-structure-function paradigm of protein-RNA complexes especially in human RNA-binding proteins. With demonstrated expertise in computational biology, Dr. Agarwal is committed to fostering innovative approaches in teaching and research, and mentoring students towards achieving their full potential.

entries.title: Dr. Anu Nair
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anu Nair is a dedicated academician with over 13 years of experience in media and communication education, academic administration, and curriculum development. She joined as an Assistant Professor and is currently serving as Program Director at the…
entries.text: Dr. Anu Nair Programme Director & Assistant Professor Department of Media and Communication Dr. Anu Nair is a dedicated academician with over 13 years of experience in media and communication education, academic administration, and curriculum development. She joined as an Assistant Professor and is currently serving as Program Director at the Department of Media and Communication at MIT World Peace University, Pune. Her areas of specialization include Advertising, Branding, Consumer Psychology, Consumer Behaviour, Media Psychology and Strategic Communication. She has held several key academic and administrative responsibilities, including academic planning and coordination, internship and placement coordination, curriculum development, and industry engagement initiatives. She has played an instrumental role in strengthening experiential learning opportunities, fostering collaborations with media organizations, and facilitating industry-academia partnerships to enhance student employability. Her academic interests focus on the integration of Artificial Intelligence into media education and innovative teaching-learning practices. She has presented and published research in the areas of media, communication, advertising,psychology and neuro marketing . Through her teaching, research, and institutional service, Dr. Nair remains committed to preparing future media professionals with

entries.title: Dr. Anu Singha
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anu Singha is a seasoned academician and researcher, currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Computer Science & Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. Prior to this, he served as a faculty…
entries.text: Dr. Anu Singha Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Dr. Anu Singha is a seasoned academician and researcher, currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Computer Science & Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. Prior to this, he served as a faculty member at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Delhi-NCR Campus, and Sri Ramachandra Institute of Higher Education and Research, Chennai. He obtained his Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering from Tripura Central University in 2021, following his M.Tech. completion in 2015. His educational background also includes an MCA from South Asian University, New Delhi. He initiated his research career as a Junior/Senior Research Fellow (JRF/SRF) under a DRDO-sponsored project at the Computer Vision Laboratory, Tripura University. Dr. Singha’s research expertise lies in artificial intelligence, with a strong focus on machine learning and deep learning techniques, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs). His work includes object detection in adverse weather conditions and several impactful healthcare applications, such as histopathology-based breast cancer detection, MRI-based brain tumor classification, knee joint inflammation detection, and fetus detection using medical imaging. His broader research interests cover computer vision, image

entries.title: Dr. Anugamini P. Srivastava
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anugamini P. Srivastava is an accomplished academic, researcher, and management educator with over a decade of experience in teaching, research, and doctoral supervision. She currently serves as Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad's MIT World…
entries.text: Dr. Anugamini P. Srivastava Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Anugamini P. Srivastava is an accomplished academic, researcher, and management educator with over a decade of experience in teaching, research, and doctoral supervision. She currently serves as Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad's MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, India, and is also associated with INTI International University, Nilai, Malaysia, as a Research Fellow, contributing to international research collaborations and interdisciplinary scholarship. She earned her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee, India, as a UGC-NET Junior Research Fellow and completed her higher education at the University of Allahabad (Prayagraj), India. She has further strengthened her academic expertise through professional learning and certifications from globally renowned institutions, including Rice University, USA, and Johns Hopkins University, USA. Dr. Srivastava has published extensively in leading international journals indexed in Scopus, Web of Science, and ABDC, including the Journal of Cleaner Production, Business Ethics, the Environment & Responsibility, Journal of Global Information Management, Tourism Management Perspectives, and the International Journal of Productivity and Performance Management. entries.text: Dr. Anuradha Shantanu Kanade Program Director & Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Dr. Anuradha Kanade is the Program Director of the Department of Computer Science and Applications at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M.Phil., MCA, MBA (Business Analytics), and a B.Sc. (Physics). With over 24 years of academic and industry experience, she has made significant contributions to teaching, research, academic leadership, and curriculum development. She is starving for the excellence to ensure the overall growth of the students, department and faculty members. Her teaching philosophy emphasizes learner-centric, value-based, and outcome-oriented education that nurtures technical competence, ethical values, innovation, and lifelong learning. She is passionate about motivating students through research, industry interaction, internships, and project-based learning Through her contributions in research, patents, publications, academic leadership, and industry collaborations, she actively supports MIT-WPU’s vision of promoting excellence in education, innovation, research, and holistic development of socially responsible global professionals. She is an approved Ph.D. supervisor and has guided research scholars in emerging areas of computer science. Dr. Kanade has authored 50+

entries.title: Dr. Anuruddha Rajaram Chabukswar
entries.excerpt: Dr. A. R. Chabukswar is an esteemed Professor in Pharmaceutical & Medicinal Chemistry at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, with a rich academic and research experience of 25 years. He has made significant contributions to the field, having…
entries.text: Dr. Anuruddha Rajaram Chabukswar Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. A. R. Chabukswar is an esteemed Professor in Pharmaceutical & Medicinal Chemistry at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, with a rich academic and research experience of 25 years. He has made significant contributions to the field, having published over 100 research papers in various National & International Journals. His remarkable achievements include securing 2 Major Research Grants (DST RS. 31.60 Lacs & AICTE - Rs. 11.33 Lacs) and being a Co-PI for CSIR Funded Project (Amount Rs 21.31 Lacs). Additionally, he has completed 2 Minor Research projects worth Rs. 4.9 Lacs and has 2 granted patents to his credit. Dr. Chabukswar has successfully guided 05 Ph.D. students, and 07 Ph.D. students are currently working under his guidance. He has also mentored more than 65 M. Pharm students, helping them achieve their academic and professional goals. Furthermore, he has authored 5 books and 10 book chapters, showcasing his expertise in the field of Pharmaceutical & Medicinal Chemistry. Dr. Chabukswar has been an active participant in various National & International Conferences, where he has presented his research papers. He is a well-respected member of the academic community, and his significant contributions have been acknowledged through various awards and accolades.

She is an Associate Professor and currently serves as the Program Director at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. Her area of specialization is Human Resource Management, and her area of expertise includes Employer Branding, Organizational Behaviour, Training and Development and Strategic Management. Dr. Dixit has held several key positions, including Head of the School of Management (PG). She is an approved Ph.D. guide in the area of Human Resource Management. She has also published numerous research papers in reputed national and international journals. Over the years, Dr. Dixit has contributed extensively to curriculum development, innovative pedagogy, and strategic academic planning.

entries.title: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka
entries.excerpt: Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics.
entries.text: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka Assistant Professor Department of Physics Quantum Science & Technology Group Photonics Research Group Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics. Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka received his Integrated M.Sc. in Physics from Amrita University in 2012. He qualified for several entrance tests, including JAM, GATE, JEST, PRL (Ahmedabad), IPR (Gandhinagar), and IISc (Bangalore), before joining the Physical Research Laboratory, Ahmedabad, as a Ph.D. scholar in 2012. He earned his Ph.D. in 2016 from the Indian Institute of Technology, Gandhinagar, India. After graduation, he was a postdoctoral investigator at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Mexico, and gained research experience as a guest scientist at the Max Planck Institute for the Science of Light (Germany), Max Planck – uOttawa Centre for Extreme Quantum Photonics (Canada), and The Institute of Photonic Sciences (Spain). Before taking up his current position at MIT- WPU, he was a research professor at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Puebla, Mexico. Dr. Nellikka specializes in experimental optics, focusing on light-matter interactions using structured optical beams and quantum optics. His research includes generating and characterizing single photon sources, studying entangled photons, and designing nanoplasmonic devices that exhibit quantum behaviors. With her expertise in Computer Vision, Embedded Systems and IoT, Cubesat Development, and Biomedical Engineering, she has made remarkable contributions to her field. Dr. Naik has an impressive track record of research, with 29 published research papers in renowned international conferences and journals such as IEEE, IARIA, and Elsevier, along with three book chapters to her credit. She has also been granted an Indian patent for her innovative work. Dr. Naik's dedication and hard work have earned her recognition in the form of two best paper awards at international conferences. She has also served as a session chair at various international conferences, showcasing her proficiency in the field. Overall, Dr. Apurva Naik is a respected academician who has made a significant contribution to her field and continues to inspire her students and colleagues to strive for excellence.

entries.title: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan believes that education in media and communication should effectively bridge theory, practice, and critical inquiry to foster students' intellectual and professional growth. He strives to create an interactive, research-driven learning…
entries.text: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan Program Coordinator & Assistant Professor Department of Media and Communication Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan believes that education in media and communication should effectively bridge theory, practice, and critical inquiry to foster students' intellectual and professional growth. He strives to create an interactive, research-driven learning environment that connects academic concepts with real-world media practices. His teaching approach combines conceptual clarity with experiential learning, enabling students to engage in newsroom simulations, fieldwork, and collaborative projects through courses such as Television Journalism, Digital Journalism, and Media Production. Drawing on his industry experience, he enriches classroom discussions with practical insights and contemporary perspectives. Aligned with MIT World Peace University's philosophy of life transformation, Dr. Khan emphasizes ethical responsibility, social awareness, and value-based education, nurturing skilled, reflective, and socially responsible media professionals.

entries.text: Dr. Arti Girish Swami Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Arti Swami is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Pharmaceutical Chemistry at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad, MIT World Peace University, Kothrud, Pune. She has over 15 years of academic experience with a strong emphasis on pharmaceutical research and advanced analytical sciences. She completed her Ph.D. and Post-Graduation from Savitribai Phule Pune University and obtained her undergraduate degree from Shivaji University, Kolhapur. Dr. Swami has published several research articles in reputed national and international journals, contributing significantly to the field of pharmaceutical analysis and quality-by-design approaches. Her research interests primarily focus on impurity profiling, Analytical Quality by Design (AQbD), Design of Experiments (DoE), in vitro–in vivo correlation (IVIVC), in vitro permeation testing (IVPT), and stability studies, with an aim to enhance product quality, robustness, and regulatory compliance.

entries.text: Dr. Arun Prakash Program Director & Assistant Professor Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Films and New Media Dr. Arun Prakash is a distinguished academician and accomplished professional, currently serving as a Program Director & Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With expertise in Photography, Cinematography, Qualitative Research, and Online Content Creation, Dr. Prakash seamlessly integrates theoretical knowledge with practical application, equipping students for successful careers in the dynamic fields of media and film. A UGC NET and Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) qualified scholar, Dr. Prakash earned his PhD in Film Studies from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), Varanasi. He also holds a BA (Hons.) and MA in Mass Communication from BHU, specialising in Television, Radio, and Films. As a testament to his leadership and global engagement, he represented India as part of the official delegation at the 2017 World Festival for Youth & Sports (WFYS) in Russia, under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India. Dr. Prakash’s professional journey spans more than seven years, including three years in the media industry and more than four years in academia after PhD. Dr. Ashish Andhale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Economics and Commerce, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University. With a Ph.D. in Economics from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, his research dives into the spatial dynamics of India's automobile component industry. His research, published in prestigious journals, explores themes from regional economics to international trade, reflecting his deep interest in the location of economic activity and spatial economics. Also, he is a tech-savvy analyst, skilled in QGIS and SPSS, always integrating cutting-edge tools into his work. His journey is a testament to his commitment to advancing economic thought and fostering a new generation of thinkers.

entries.text: Dr. Ashish Haribhau Pawar Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Prof. Ashish Pawar is an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has 9 years of experience in teaching and research, as well as 1 year of industry experience. Prof. Pawar is currently pursuing his PhD in "Experimental study on Hybrid ECM for machining of High Temperature Titanium alloys" at Savitribai Phule Pune University. He has published more than 10 papers in the field of Robotics, Non-Conventional Machining, and Design and Optimization. Prof. Pawar has also been involved in organizing workshops and conferences, such as SMF2022. He is in charge of admissions-related activities for the School of Mechanical Engineering and is currently guiding the Electric Two Wheeler Team of MITWPU, which won the overall 1st Rank in a competition organized by IIT GOA and ATHER.

entries.title: Dr. Asim Samanta
entries.excerpt: Dr. Asim Samanta is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. He holds an M. Pharm (Pharmaceutics) degree from Jadavpur University, Kolkata,…
entries.text: Dr. Asim Samanta Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Asim Samanta is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. He holds an M. Pharm (Pharmaceutics) degree from Jadavpur University, Kolkata, India, and a PhD from the Department of Pharmacy at the National University of Singapore (NUS). He qualified GATE 2005 with an All India Rank of 46. Dr. Samanta has over two years of postdoctoral research experience at NUS and the University of the Pacific, California, USA, along with nine years of extensive industrial experience at Johnson & Johnson and Encube Ethicals. His expertise lies in Novel Drug Delivery System, Process Analytical Technology (PAT), NIR and Raman spectroscopy, chemometrics, multivariate data analysis, and Quality by Design (QbD). He has published widely in reputed international journals and has presented his research at numerous national and international conferences.

entries.title: Dr. Atanu Hazarika
entries.excerpt: Dr. Atanu Hazarika is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He is a Post-Doctoral Fellow from the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore. The title of the Post Doctoral research is ‘Developing…
entries.text: Dr. Atanu Hazarika Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Atanu Hazarika is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He is a Post-Doctoral Fellow from the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore. The title of the Post Doctoral research is ‘Developing sustainability-related knowledge and capability for farmers, Case Study of an Irrigation Tank project’. The project is sponsored by the Hanns Seidel Stiftung foundation (HSS), Germany. His PhD has been awarded by the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur. His doctoral research work has focused on the issues of productivity and efficiency of paddy farming in Assam. His attempts to develop an interdependence (multivariate) framework for measuring the adoption rate of multiple modern technology practices. Additionally, the study attempts to find the technical efficiency and allocative efficiency level of the farmers. In addition, he worked on several projects sponsored by national and international agencies like NOVOD, SANDEE, ICSSR, and NABARD during his PhD tenure. The projects experienced give an exposure to work in different domains like the adoption and expansion behaviour of the Jatropha farmers, the economic valuation of biodiesel, the evaluation of watershed programs, and the financial risk attitude of the handloom micro-entrepreneur. He has worked as a Guest Fac

entries.text: Dr. Basant Tiwari Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Dr. Basant Tiwari is currently an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. He brings extensive experience in teaching undergraduate and postgraduate programs across national and international universities. Dr. Tiwari has authored numerous research papers published in national and international journals and conferences, with over 25 publications indexed in SCI and Scopus. He has also contributed to edited handbooks published by Springer, Taylor & Francis, and IGI Global. Additionally, he has actively participated in various national and international conferences, workshops, seminars, and symposiums. His current research focuses on Cyber Security in Pervasive Computing, with special emphasis on applications in Healthcare, Internet of Things (IoT), and Data Analysis. Dr. Tiwari is a Senior Member of IEEE and ACM, as well as a Life Member of CSI and IACSIT. He has been honored with leadership roles such as Bhopal Representative in the IEEE M.P. Sub-section, Secretary of CSI Bhopal Chapter, and Secretary of ACM Indore Chapter. entries.excerpt: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra is presently working as Assistant Professor in School of Liberal Arts, Dr. Viswanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune and involved in Teaching and Research in Political Science. Dr. Mohapatra has about 25 years of…
entries.text: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra is presently working as Assistant Professor in School of Liberal Arts, Dr. Viswanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune and involved in Teaching and Research in Political Science. Dr. Mohapatra has about 25 years of experience in Teaching and Research in Political Science and peace studies. Specialised in governance, public policy, decentralisation, local democracy and economic development. He has obtained M.Phil in Political Science from Berhampur University, Odisha, India and completed his PhD from Centre for Economic and Social Studies (CESS) Hyderabad in Political Science in 2018. Dr. Mohapatra has been extensively worked in Research Studies in various parts in India. He has published 28 numbers of Journal Articles in various reputed journals apart from 2 Books and 9 Chapters in Edited Books. He has also published 3 Research Monographs and associated with many global professional bodies, policy think-tanks and Journals as Mentor, Editorial Board Member, Advisor and Expert Members.
