entries.title: Dr. Brijesh Kumar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Brijesh Kumar is a developmental biologist with extensive research experience in genome engineering, including CRISPR/Cas, animal models (chicken and zebrafish), and Drug/Target Discovery technologies. After completing his master's thesis on…
entries.text: Dr. Brijesh Kumar Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Dr. Brijesh Kumar is a developmental biologist with extensive research experience in genome engineering, including CRISPR/Cas, animal models (chicken and zebrafish), and Drug/Target Discovery technologies. After completing his master's thesis on developmental metabolomics in chicken embryos at IIT Kanpur, Dr. Kumar obtained his PhD on pancreatic beta cell function and regeneration, mitigating diabetes using a zebrafish model from the Max Planck Institute for Heart and Lung Research, Bad Nauheim, Germany. His work led to the identification of several targets and drugs holding potential for therapeutic interventions. Scholastically, Dr. Kumar was selected for developmental biology training at the Marine Biological Laboratory, Woods Hole, Massachusetts, USA, where he witnessed the Nobel laureates and learnt state-of-the-art molecular tools and techniques from world leaders in the field. For his postdoctoral training in disease modelling for Congenital Disorder of Glycosylation and Heart Failure, he worked at Greenwood Genetic Center, South Carolina, USA, Heidelberg University Hospital, Germany, and Duke University, USA, where he contributed to projects focused on molecular genetics, functional genomics, and prime editing. Dr. Kumar has actively supervised laboratory courses, led journal clubs, and mentored research

entries.text: Dr. Chandrashekhar Digambarrao Bobade Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Biomaterials, thin polymeric films, bioengineering, nanotechnology, and advanced drug delivery systems Dr. Chandrashekhar D. Bobade is an accomplished academician, researcher, and academic leader and career blog writer with over 17 years of experience in pharmaceutical education and research. He is a DHR–ICMR International Fellow (Government of India, 2025–26) and a DST-SERB & INSA Travel Grant Awardee (Government of India, 2023, USA). Currently, he serves as the President of the Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA), Pune (2024–26). Dr. Bobade is an active member of the Biopolymers and Drug Delivery Research Group, with a strong research focus on advanced drug delivery systems and biomaterials. His scholarly work has been published in high-impact international journals, including RSC, ACS, and Springer Nature. He has received multiple research and travel grants from SPPU, DST-SERB, and INSA, Pharmacy council of India including support for presenting his research at ACS Fall 2023, USA. His leadership journey spans key academic and administrative roles such as UG Program Head and Research Coordinator, contributing significantly to institutional growth and academic governance. entries.excerpt: AI, Power System, IoT, Machine Learning, Smart Grid
entries.text: Dr. Chetan Bhimrao Khadse Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering AI, Power System, IoT, Machine Learning, Smart Grid Chetan B Khadse is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Electrical Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. He holds a PhD in "AI applications in Electrical Engineering" from Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology Nagpur, India, an MTech in Power System from Shivaji University, India, and a Bachelor's from Amravati University, India. With a research focus on artificial intelligence, power systems, power quality, optimization algorithms, and intelligent systems, he has published over 18 research papers in peer-reviewed reputed journals, chapters, and conferences. Dr. Khadse is an active participant in MITWPU's center of excellence for electric vehicles and uses AI techniques to improve EV performance. He also regularly reviews papers for IEEE, Elsevier, Taylor & Francis, and Willey journals.

Deshmukh is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Pharmacology in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud, Pune, India. He holds a B.Pharm degree from the Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research, Borgaon (Meghe), Wardha, Maharashtra, which he completed in 2005 with first division. Later, in 2007, he completed the Executive Master’s Program in Pharmacy Management (MBA) from Mitcon Institute of Management, Balewadi, Pune, Maharashtra. He also holds a Master of Pharmacy degree in Pharmacology, which he completed in 2009 with first division from Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College, Belgaum, Karnataka. He has been teaching for the last 14 years and completed his Ph.D. in Pharmacology in 2017 from Pacific Academy of Higher Education and Research (PAHER), Udaipur, Rajasthan. In addition to his academic achievements, Dr. Deshmukh has passed 30 and 40 words per minute typing exams and has participated in various extracurricular activities and social work. entries.title: Dr. Deepak Kumar
entries.excerpt: Vortex-induced Vibrations, Drag reduction and Flow Control, Fluid-Structure Interaction, Thermal management of systems
entries.text: Dr. Deepak Kumar Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Vortex-induced Vibrations, Drag reduction and Flow Control, Fluid-Structure Interaction, Thermal management of systems "Dr. Deepak Kumar is an accomplished Assistant Professor at the School of Mechanical Engineering, Dr. Vishwanath Karad, MIT World Peace University in Pune, Maharashtra. He is a highly regarded researcher with a passion for the numerical analysis of fluid-structure interactions, refrigeration and air-conditioning, and solar energy. Having earned his PhD in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (ISM) Dhanbad, Dr. Kumar's research has resulted in numerous peer-reviewed publications in top-tier international journals and conferences. His innovative and impactful contributions have received recognition and numerous awards. In addition to his research, Dr. Kumar is an active mentor and instructor. He has taught a range of mechanical engineering undergraduate and graduate courses, and has supervised multiple postgraduate students in their research projects. Dr. Kumar is committed to inspiring and guiding the next generation of engineers and researchers in India. To learn more about Dr. Kumar's research interests, publications, and teaching activities, visit his website at https://sites.google.com/view/drkumardeepak/home."

He is dealing with subjects Advanced Mechanical Vibrations, Dynamics of Machinery, NVH and Design Engineering at undergraduated and post graduate level.He has guided more than 25 under graduate academic Projects and almost 8 post graduate academic projects.

entries.title: Dr. Deepali Dhananjay Shahane
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Agile Methodology, Software Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Management, Digital Transformation, Analytical Study
entries.text: Dr. Deepali Dhananjay Shahane Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Agile Methodology, Software Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Management, Digital Transformation, Analytical Study Dr. Deepali Shahane is an academic professional with over 25 years of experience in teaching, research, and academic administration. She is currently serving as an Associate Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune. Her academic interests include agile methodology, software engineering, information systems, digital transformation, and computer management. She has guided undergraduate, postgraduate, and Ph.D. students, and has also conducted pre-Ph.D. coursework sessions. She has been actively involved in curriculum design, aligning programs such as BBA, MBA, and MCA with NEP 2020 guidelines. Dr. Shahane has contributed to quality assurance as IQAC Convener and Secretary of the Board of Studies at MITWPU, and has participated in NBA and NAAC accreditation processes. Dr. Shahane has a publication record that reflects her research interests in agile practices, educational informatics, healthcare IT, digital transformation, and management education.

entries.excerpt: Dr. Deependra Sharma is a visionary academic leader and the Dean of the School of Business at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With a dynamic career spanning over 25 years, he brings a future-focused approach to management education i.e. constantly…
entries.text: Dr. Deependra Sharma Dean - School of Business Department of Business Dr. Deependra Sharma is a visionary academic leader and the Dean of the School of Business at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With a dynamic career spanning over 25 years, he brings a future-focused approach to management education i.e. constantly evolving, refining, and adapting to the needs of an ever-changing global business landscape. An alumnus of the Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad (IIM-A), Dr. Sharma combines deep academic insight with practical industry experience. His early career in managerial roles across Indian industries shaped his pragmatic approach to education, blending theory with real-world relevance. Throughout his academic journey, Dr. Sharma has led curriculum transformation, fostered innovation, and championed collaboration between academia and industry. His leadership is rooted in a strong belief in empowering students, not just as learners, but as future entrepreneurs and changemakers. He has played a key role in nurturing a culture of innovation and enterprise, championing incubation initiatives, experiential learning, and industry-aligned pedagogy across his leadership roles. His research areas such as innovation diffusion, entrepreneurship, consumer behaviour, and university–industry partnerships, with publications in leading journals indexed in Scopus, Emerald, and Elsev

entries.title: Dr. Deobrat Singh
entries.excerpt: High-energy theory, gravitation, and fundamental interactions.
entries.text: Dr. Deobrat Singh Assistant Professor Department of Physics High-energy theory, gravitation, and fundamental interactions. Dr. Deobrat Singh is an Assistant Professor of Physics. He completed his Ph.D. from the University of Delhi in theoretical High Energy Physics. His research interests lie in the field of Theoretical High Energy Physics. Dr. Singh has published several papers in various international journals and conferences. Some of his notable research publications include: Quantum effects in topological and Schwarzschild de Sitter brane: Aspects of torsion on (DD)4-brane universe"" by Richa Kapoor, Supriya Kar, and Deobrat Singh, published in the International Journal of Modern Physics D24, No:2 (2015) 15 50015. CFT6 Bulk/Boundary AdS5 Q Correspondence and Emergent Gravity"" by R. Nitish, Deobrat Singh, and Supriya Kar, published in Physica Scripta. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1088/1402-4896/ab144e ArXiv:1612.01065 Torsion geometries in U (1) gauge theory on D5-brane"" by Deobrat Singh, Richa Kapoor, and Supriya Kar, published in Springer Proceedings in Physics Vol.174, Ch.77, pg.507-512, ISBN 978-3-319-25617-7. Dr. Singh's research contributions have been recognized in the academic community, and he has an impressive citation record on Google Scholar and Scopus. He also has an ORCID ID with publications listed in his profile.

entries.text: Dr. Devendra Joshi Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering My teaching philosophy focuses on fostering critical thinking, innovation, and practical problem-solving among students. I aim to integrate theoretical knowledge with real-world applications, particularly in areas such as artificial intelligence, data science, and optimization. I am motivated to create an engaging learning environment that encourages research, collaboration, and ethical values. Through effective teaching, mentoring, and research contributions, I strive to support MIT-WPU’s vision of developing socially responsible professionals and advancing knowledge that contributes to technological progress and societal well-being.

entries.title: Dr. Dhanashree Umesh Tharkude
entries.excerpt: Human Resource Management/ OB, Sustainability
entries.text: Dr. Dhanashree Umesh Tharkude Associate Professor Department of Business Human Resource Management/ OB, Sustainability Dr. Dhanashree Tharkude is an accomplished academic and industry professional with more than two decades of experience. She holds a doctoral degree in Human Resource Management from Savitribai Phule Pune University and has been recognized for her research work at several levels. Dr. Tharkude has published two patents for her research and was awarded ""Best PhD Thesis"" at the Jaipuria Institute of Management Conference. Currently serving as the Associate Dean for External Relations and HR Faculty at MIT World Peace University's Ramcharan School of Leadership, Dr. Tharkude is also a recognized PhD guide at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. Her areas of expertise include Organizational Behaviour, Human Resource Management, Talent Management, and Organizational Development. In addition to her academic and industry experience, Dr. Tharkude has also been involved in course design, student development, academic programming and planning, policies and procedures development, and designing syllabus frameworks. She has previously worked with Aptech Ltd. for five years and has conducted training sessions for organizations such as National Defence Academy, AXA-Abs, Daimler Chrysler, IBM, TCS, Airtel, and KPIT Cummins. entries.title: Dr. Dibyangana Chakraborty
entries.excerpt: Dr. Dibyangana Chakraborty is an accomplished academician and researcher with a Ph.D. in Economics from the renowned Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune. She joined MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) in July 2024, where she currently serves…
entries.text: Dr. Dibyangana Chakraborty Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Dibyangana Chakraborty is an accomplished academician and researcher with a Ph.D. in Economics from the renowned Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune. She joined MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) in July 2024, where she currently serves as a faculty member, teaching Managerial Economics, Statistics, and Research Methodology to undergraduate and postgraduate students in the BBA and MBA programs. With a strong foundation in applied economics, Dr. Chakraborty’s research interests lie at the intersection of Rural Economy, Livelihood Studies, and Development Economics. Her work is driven by a deep commitment to understanding grassroots economic challenges and contributing to policy- oriented solutions that support sustainable development. Dr. Chakraborty has presented her research at numerous prestigious national and international conferences and has several peer-reviewed publications to her name. Her academic contributions reflect a blend of rigorous analysis and real-world relevance, making her a valuable asset to both the academic community and the broader field of development economics.

entries.text: Dr. Dipak Abnave Assistant Professor Department of Public Health Health System Research, Health Governance, Human Rights and Health Dr. Dipak Abnave, PhD in Social Medicine and Community Health from JNU, New Delhi, is deeply committed to advancing the discourse on Health System Strengthening, health governance, diversity, equity, inclusion, and sustainability. Currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU Pune, Dr. Abnave brings a wealth of experience from previous roles as an Assistant Professor at Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Tuljapur Campus, and as a Scientist-B for ICMR-NIRRCH, Mumbai. Dr. Abnave's professional journey has equipped them with extensive project implementation experience in health and development across various organizations, positioning them as a dedicated advocate for the right to health and health security.

entries.title: Dr. Divyanshi Pal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Divyanshi Pal is an Assistant Professor at MIT-World Peace University with a Ph.D. in Marketing from RGIPT. She specializes in consumer behavior, retail experiences, and brand management. She has published in reputed ABDC-listed journals such as…
entries.text: Dr. Divyanshi Pal Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Divyanshi Pal is an Assistant Professor at MIT-World Peace University with a Ph.D. in Marketing from RGIPT. She specializes in consumer behavior, retail experiences, and brand management. She has published in reputed ABDC-listed journals such as International Journal of Retail & Distribution Management and Asia Pacific Journal of Marketing and Logistics. Dr. Pal has designed and taught several BBA and MBA courses and actively mentors students. She is UGC-NET qualified and has presented at conferences hosted by IIMs and IITs. Her research explores sensory marketing, consumer intention, and experiences in retail.

entries.title: Dr. Dnyaneshwar Ramchandra Waghole
entries.excerpt: heat transfer equipments, nanofluidics/nanomaterial, EV (BTMS/Fuel cell),Healthcare ,energy ,Operations research
entries.text: Dr. Dnyaneshwar Ramchandra Waghole Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering heat transfer equipments, nanofluidics/nanomaterial, EV (BTMS/Fuel cell),Healthcare ,energy ,Operations research Dr. Dnyaneshwar R Waghole holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering, along with an M.E. in Mechanical Engineering with a specialization in Heat Power and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering. He has a total of 21.5 years of experience, including 20 years in teaching and 1.5 years in the industry. His research areas include NanoFluids, Nanomaterials & Allied Nano Materials, Biomedical Healthcare Technology, Heat Transfer Equipment, and Energy Harvesting Technology. Dr. Waghole has published 10 research papers in national and international journals and has filed a patent for "Heat Transfer Enhancement using Flow divider type Inserts." He is a member of the Solar Energy Society of India (SESI) and the Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE).

entries.excerpt: Geotechnical Engineering, Pavement Engineering
entries.text: Dr. Ganesh Shivaji Ingle Associate Professor Department of Civil Engineering Geotechnical Engineering, Pavement Engineering Prf. Dr. Ganesh Ingle is an Associate Professor in Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has completed his Ph.D. in Transportation Engineering from College of Engineering Pune (COEP) in the year 2018, received his B. E. in Civil Engineering from Govt. College of Engineering, Amravati in the year 2000 and Masters (M. E. Civil) in technical Engineering in the year of 2003, from College of Engineering Pune (COEP). He is working with MIT-WPU since August 2008 and has been teaching various subjects like soil Mechanics, Foundation Engineering, soil structure and interaction, concrete technology, construction materials and advanced transportation Engineering. As a part of Ph. D work, he has indigenously designed and developed a Laboratory Full scale Accelerated Pavement Testing Set Up (APT) in COEP campus through an R&D Grant of 13.5 Lac. This is a unique facility which is not available in India where the performance of any pavement can be tested within a short period. It was also applied for Indian Patent. He has published more than 15 research papers in Scopus and SCI Journals and also published three Indian Patent and one Australian Patent granted on stabilization of soil. entries.title: Dr. Ganesh Suresh Jadhav
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ganesh Jadhav is the Associate Professor & Program Director, School of Design at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, and a design academician with a rare blend of deep research, industry practice, and academic leadership.…
entries.text: Dr. Ganesh Suresh Jadhav Program Director Department of Design Dr. Ganesh Jadhav is the Associate Professor & Program Director, School of Design at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, and a design academician with a rare blend of deep research, industry practice, and academic leadership. He holds a PhD in Design from IIT Guwahati, with specialized expertise in Ergonomics, Product Design, and Industrial Design, and over 16 years of combined academic and industrial experience. Dr. Jadhav has worked with leading organizations such as Philips India Pvt. Ltd., bringing strong industry relevance into design education through practice-driven pedagogy and innovation-led mentoring. An active researcher and innovator, Dr. Jadhav has multiple national and international patents, over fifteen design registrations, and SCI/Scopus-indexed publications in high-impact journals. His research focuses on human-centred design, ergonomic interventions, indigenous craft innovation, and sustainable product development, bridging traditional knowledge with contemporary design solutions. At MIT-WPU, he has played a pivotal role in curriculum development, program leadership, PhD program establishment, accreditation processes (NAAC/NIRF), and industry-academia integration. entries.text: Dr. Gautam Surendra Bapat Associate Professor and Program Director Department of Business Dr. Gautam Bapat is Associate Dean – Faculty Affairs and Associate Professor of Marketing at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over a decade of experience, he has held key academic leadership roles, contributing to program design, faculty development, and institutional advancement. His academic philosophy integrates industry relevance with scholarly rigor. Dr. Bapat holds a Ph.D. in Management, focusing on digital marketing for student recruitment, along with qualifications in marketing, computer applications, and zoology. His interdisciplinary approach informs his teaching in branding, digital transformation, and marketing strategy. A prolific researcher, he has published over 38 papers, presented at international forums, authored textbooks, and holds five patents in areas like IoT and social service systems. His work explores AI in marketing, higher education strategies, and consumer behavior. Recognized with awards from IIM Ahmedabad and IRSSM, he is a sought-after speaker and an advocate for innovation in management education.

Skilled in Sales & Marketing, Training, Consultancy, Market Research. Has 18 years of work experience in Teaching and Industry. He holds a Ph.D. in Management, an MBA, and a PGDFT from the University of Pune, and is UGC-NET (twice) and SET qualified. He has completed a PGP in Data Science from KPMG and a Design Thinking course from IIT Madras. His research has been presented at esteemed institutions like IIMs and IITs. Dr. Mude has facilitated the International Winter School Program at the University of Hull, UK, and has visited London, Italy, Switzerland, France, and the UK for academic assignments and institutional collaborations. Recipient “Research Scholarship” from University of Vermont, USA; “Research Fellowship from EURAC, Italy” and “Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam MIT- WPU Research and Innovation Promising Prize 2019”. With a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (Aerospace-Scramjet) from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has 27 years of professional experience, out of which 24 years are in teaching. His areas of interest and expertise include engineering design, design thinking, computational fluid dynamics, tribology, and total quality management. He has authored 11 research papers in various international journals and conferences. He founded and headed the SUPRA SAEINDIA team at MIT since 2011 with an impressive track record at national and international levels.

Additionally, he is an esteemed life member of ISTE (Indian Society for Technical Education). His expertise lies in the domains of Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Data Science. He has contributed significantly to the academic and research community with over 50 publications in various esteemed international journals and conferences, collaborating with researchers from around the world, including professors from IITs and NITs. entries.text: Dr. Harshali Digambar Zodpe Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering VLSI, Cryptography & Embedded Systems Dr. Harshali Zodpe is a highly experienced academic, currently serving as an Associate Professor in the School of ECE. With an impressive 16 years of teaching experience, she is widely regarded as a leading authority in the field of electronics and communication engineering. Dr. Zodpe's academic background is characterized by a deep understanding of the fundamental principles of electronics and communication engineering. She completed her B.E. in Electronics Engineering from VNIT Nagpur in 2004 and went on to earn an M.Tech in VLSI Design from Nagpur University in 2006. She later obtained her Doctor of Philosophy (Electronics & Telecommunication) degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2019, further solidifying her expertise in the field. Dr. Zodpe's research interests are focused on VLSI, Cryptography, and Embedded Systems. Her work in these areas has been instrumental in advancing the field of electronics and communication engineering, with her research being published in numerous top-tier scientific journals. Additionally, she has presented her work at conferences and symposia around the world. She has extensive experience in studying the relationship between brain function, personality, and behavior, and is committed to improving the lives of individuals with mental health disorders. Some of her research has been published in psychology and multidisciplinary journals, and she has presented her findings at numerous national and international conferences. As an Assistant Professor, Dr. Bhansali continues to mentor and inspire young researchers in their dissertation research.

entries.title: Dr. Jehath Syed
entries.excerpt: Dr. Jehath Syed is a clinical pharmacist and researcher currently serving as Assistant Professor in the Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With specialized expertise in geriatric care, pharmacoepidemiology, and…
entries.text: Dr. Jehath Syed Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Jehath Syed is a clinical pharmacist and researcher currently serving as Assistant Professor in the Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With specialized expertise in geriatric care, pharmacoepidemiology, and digital health solutions, Dr. Syed integrates evidence-based pharmaceutical care with innovative healthcare technology to optimize patient outcomes. A Registered Pharmacist with the Maharashtra State Pharmacy Council, Dr. Syed earned her PhD from JSS Academy of Higher Education and Research, Mysuru, focusing on "Implementation and Evaluation of Clinical Pharmacist's Comprehensive Geriatric Care Model." She also holds a Doctor of Pharmacy (PharmD) and is certified as an Age-Friendly Pharmacist by the American Society of Consultant Pharmacists. Dr. Syed's professional journey includes experience as Clinical Research Coordinator at JSS Hospital, where she coordinated multiple COVID-19 clinical trials. Her notable contributions include developing EldenCare (a comprehensive geriatric care application in collaboration with L&T Technologies), De-Med webpage for identifying potentially inappropriate medications, and "Dose 4 You" dose calculator. Dr. Syed has published over 25 peer-reviewed articles in prestigious international journals. She is an active member of ISP

entries.text: Dr. Jivan Pandharinath Biradar Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting General area of research interest is in Development Economics. Specific area interest in Agriculture and Rural Development, Banking and Finance, Insurance Dr. Jivan Biradar is an experienced assistant professor of economics in the School of Economics and Commerce at MIT-WPU. He holds a Ph.D. and M.Phil. in economics from Pune University, and has over 10 years of teaching experience. Prior to joining MIT, he worked at the Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, where he conducted research on various projects sponsored by NABARD and the Ministry of Agriculture, Government of India. His areas of research include banking, health insurance, rural development, agriculture finance, and public policy. He teaches microeconomics, macroeconomics, and managerial economics to undergraduate and postgraduate students.

entries.excerpt: Peace and Conflict Studies, Migration Studies, Gender Studies, Masculinity, mobility and aspirations, gendered geography, and public spaces.
entries.text: Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava Assistant Professor Department of Peace Studies Peace and Conflict Studies, Migration Studies, Gender Studies, Masculinity, mobility and aspirations, gendered geography, and public spaces. Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava holds a Ph.D. in Peace Research from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi. Her Ph.D. thesis asses existing forms of masculinities producing differential treatment for women migrants. The empirical part explores migrant women’s experiences with masculinity, division of labor, hierarchies, and the social conflict it produces. It was designed to convey the diverse experiences of women migrants in the varying masculinities present, building an alternative experiential knowledge system, stimulating the prevailing framework of gendered migration, and the role of masculinities in social-behavioral patterns during migration. She has qualified for UGC JRF-NET. She was awarded the Linnaeus Palme Scholarship issued by the Swedish International Development Agency during her research. She holds a master's degree in Conflict Management and Development. She has also attended the Summer Intensive Learning Course on: "Religion and Foreign Policy: Investigating Religious Minorities and Human Rights" at McGill University, Montreal, Canada. She has published her research work in the UGC care list and peer-reviewed national and international journals. She has attended I

Dr. Kausik Bhattacharyya is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Biosciences and Technology (DoBT) and Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation. Serving additionally as a Departmental Placement coordinator for the department of Biosciences and Technology, Dr Kausik has more than 12 years of research experience in Infectious Disease biology, Microbiology, Genomics and Bioinformatics and 7 years of teaching experience. He has completed his Ph.D. from Department of Biomedical Sciences (ACBR), University of Delhi. During his Ph.D., he has demonstrated how genomic sequence and novel genomic variations can have functional significance in Multi-Drug Resistant (MDR) clinical isolates of M. tuberculosis through Bioinformatics and Experimental approaches, including altering protein 3D structure, causing drug resistance, altering host-pathogen interactions, Molecular mimicry, and Evasion of Immune responses. Before joining Ph.D., he worked as a junior Team Mentor and Project Fellow in the area of drug discovery against Tuberculosis (OSDD), a CSIR-led Team India Consortium with global participation. entries.text: Dr. Kaustubh Arvind Sontakke Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Kaustubh Arvind Sontakke is the Associate Professor at The School of Business, MIT World Peace University. He is a teacher and researcher with more than 22 years’ rich experience in the field of management and finance. He has completed two doctorate / Ph.D. degrees. He holds Ph.D. (Business Management) and Ph.D. (Accountancy) from University of Mumbai. He is M. Phil (Finance) and Gold Medallist for M. Com (Accounts and Finance) from the University of Mumbai. Also, he has completed an MBA (Finance) and MA (Economics) and MBA (HRM). He has authored twelve books and various research papers in the field of accounting and finance. He has attended and chaired many technical sessions in national and international conferences. He is Life Member of Indian Accounting Association and a Life Member of Higher Education Forum. He has his special devotional interest in Bhagavad Gita studies. He is Elite Life Member of International Society for Krsna Consciousness (ISKCON).

entries.text: Dr. Ketaki Hanamant Kulkarni Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Ketaki Kulkarni is an esteemed Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune. She graduated from Government College of Engineering, Aurangabad in Civil Engineering in the year 2003, and then completed her M.E in Civil-Water Resources Engineering from Government Engineering College, Aurangabad in 2005. She was selected for a research fellowship in Indian Institute of Technology, Mumbai, and was a research associate there until 2007. Prof. Dr. Kulkarni has been associated with MIT since 2007 and has taught a variety of subjects like Basic Civil and Environmental Engineering, Fluid Mechanics I, II, Momentum Transfer, Hydrology and Water Resources Engineering, Dams and Hydraulic Structures. She earned her doctorate in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, specializing in Hydraulic Engineering under the guidance of Dr. G. A. Hinge. She has published approximately 4 international journals and 8 international conference papers, including a BEST PAPER AWARD. She is a life member of the Indian Society of Hydraulics (ISH) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE). She is also serving on the editorial board of LARHYSS journal, which is a Scopus-indexed international journal. Additionally, she is a reviewer for Water Science and Technology - Water

entries.title: Dr. Kiran Nagare
entries.excerpt: "Education is not just about acquiring knowledge; it's also about empowering minds, igniting passions, and shaping futures." As an experienced Assistant Professor specializing in Commerce and Management, she has a proven track record of success and…
entries.text: Dr. Kiran Nagare Assistant Professor Department of Business "Education is not just about acquiring knowledge; it's also about empowering minds, igniting passions, and shaping futures." As an experienced Assistant Professor specializing in Commerce and Management, she has a proven track record of success and expertise in her field. She holds a PhD in Business Management, as well as a Master's in Business Administration (MBA) and a Master of Commerce (M.Com) with a specialization in Finance and Marketing from RTM Nagpur University. She qualified her MH- SET and UGC- NET in Commerce & Management. Her areas of expertise include digital marketing, research proficiency, teaching excellence, a student-centred approach, effective communication, technological proficiency, time management, and administrative skills. She has few remarkable research publications in her credit. With a demonstrated track record of success in academia, she is passionate about fostering student success and driving innovative learning experiences.

entries.title: Dr. Kiran Prakash Shejale
entries.excerpt: 1. Energy Storage & Conversion – Development of high-performance lithium-ion and solid-state batteries, supercapacitors, and fuel cells with advanced electrode materials and electrocatalysts. 2. Nanomaterials & Functional Coatings – Synthesis of functional…
entries.text: Dr. Kiran Prakash Shejale Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering 1. Energy Storage & Conversion – Development of high-performance lithium-ion and solid-state batteries, supercapacitors, and fuel cells with advanced electrode materials and electrocatalysts. 2. Nanomaterials & Functional Coatings – Synthesis of functional nanostructures, carbon nano-assemblies, quantum dots, and ionic polymer films for energy, catalysis, and environmental applications. 3. Solar Energy & Photocatalysis – Design of dye-sensitized (DSSC) and perovskite solar cells, along with nanostructured catalysts for solar-driven hydrogen production and water purification. 4. Electrochemical Systems & Smart Interfaces – Development of bioelectrochemical interfaces, conductive polymers, and heterostructured materials for sensors, energy devices and electrocatalysis. 5. Water Treatment & Environmental Sustainability – Advanced membrane-integrated nanomaterials, activated carbon and photocatalytic processes for water purification, CO₂ capture and pollution control. Dr. Kiran P. Shejale is a renowned researcher, innovator, and educator in Chemical Engineering, Materials Science and Bioengineering with a Ph.D. from IIT Jodhpur, he also holds an M.Tech. in Nanotechnology from IIT Roorkee and a B.E. in Biotechnology from Shivaji University. entries.text: Dr. Kishanprasad Gangadhar Gunale Associate Professor and Director, Career Development Centre (CDC) Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Automotive Electronics, Computer Vision "Dr. Kishanprasad Gunale is a highly motivated academic professional and a dedicated career counsellor. He received his Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2019 and completed his B.Tech. and M.Tech. degrees with First Class with Distinction from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Technological University, Lonere, in 2001 and 2006, respectively. Since joining MAEERs, MIT Group of Institutions, Kothrud, Pune, in 2012, Dr. Gunale has contributed significantly to the organization's growth in academic processes, industry collaboration, research, and industry-based competitions such as Smart India Hackathon, IIT-B e-Yantra, Texas Instruments, KPIT Sparkle, TCS Code Vita, and L&T TECHgium, among others. He played an instrumental role in establishing an IIT Bombay Remote Center and running Faculty/student Development Programs under National Mission on Education through ICT for the overall development of students and faculty. As a workshop coordinator, he organized FDPs on ""Foundation Program in ICT for Education"" and ""Pedagogy for Online and Blended Teaching-Learning Process,"" initiated IIT Bombay Spoken Tutorial activity, and mentored over 1000 students. entries.text: Dr. Kishor Rambhau Kolhe Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Kishor Kolhe has more than 28.6 years of teaching and industry experience. His areas of interest are Network and Security, Cyber Security, Blockchain Technology, and Internet of Things. He has published more than 79 research papers in reputed journals and conferences. He has also guided more than 76 undergraduate students, 18 post graduate students. 1 Research Scholar: completed her PhD under his supervision and is presently guiding. 2 Ph.D. research scholars. He has also worked as Head, I.T. Department, System In-charge of Center for Professional Courses and Member of various committees. He has reviewed research papers in national and international conferences and journals. He is a life member of CSI, ISTE and Fellow member of IETE etc. He has organized several conferences, workshops, seminars, Graduation Ceremony programs and short term training programmes etc. He had been invited as session chair of National and International conferences. He participated in various innovative workshops on Education and Training.

entries.title: Dr. Krishna Keshavrao Warhade
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Signal Processing, Communication
entries.text: Dr. Krishna Keshavrao Warhade Professor and Director Doctoral Programme Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Signal Processing, Communication Dr. Krishna Warhade is an accomplished educator and researcher, with over 27 years of experience in teaching and research. He completed his PhD with specialization in communications and signal processing from IIT Bombay and currently serves as a Professor at School of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. Dr. Warhade has held various administrative positions, including the Dean of Research and Doctoral Program at MITWPU, Pune, and Vice President of Institutions Innovation Council (Part of MHRD’s Innovation Cell) at MITWPU. He was also the SPOC from MITWPU for Smart India Hackathon organized by MHRD, AICTE, i4C and Persistent from 2017 to 2021. Dr. Warhade has also served as the Remote Center Coordinator for MHRD-IIT Bombay National Mission on Education through ICT from MITWPU. He is the author of the book titled “Video Shot Boundary Detection,” co-authored by Dr. S. N. Merchant (Professor IIT Bombay), and Dr. U. B. Desai, Director IIT Hyderabad, published by River Publishers, Denmark. Dr. Warhade has also contributed a chapter to the book titled “Research Methodology, A practical and scientific-based approach,” published by CRC Press, Taylor and Francis.

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Krishna Kumar Saini is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. He has over 10 years of academic and research experience in the field of Electrical Engineering. He…
entries.text: Dr. Krishna Kumar Saini Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Krishna Kumar Saini is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. He has over 10 years of academic and research experience in the field of Electrical Engineering. He submitted his Ph.D. titled “Techno-Economic Assessment and Development of Microgrid Operation Strategies with Their Digital Replica Validation” in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at Birla Institute of Technology and Science (BITS), Pilani. He holds M.Tech. degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from Maharishi Dayanand University, Rohtak, and B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering from Rajasthan Technical University, Kota. His research interests include renewable energy-integrated power systems, microgrid operation and control, demand-side management, forecasting techniques, energy management strategies, and digital twin applications for smart energy systems. He has significant experience in the design and deployment of solar photovoltaic microgrids with battery energy storage systems and IoT-based monitoring. He is the inventor of a published patent on microgrid management using digital twin technology and has authored 11 research articles in reputed international journals, book chapters, and conference p

entries.title: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj obtained his Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. His doctoral research focused on the development of Viscoelastic Surfactant (VES) fracturing fluids for high- temperature reservoirs, integrating key parameters to…
entries.text: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj Assistant Professor Department of Petroleum Engineering Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj obtained his Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. His doctoral research focused on the development of Viscoelastic Surfactant (VES) fracturing fluids for high- temperature reservoirs, integrating key parameters to enhance temperature stability and proppant- carrying capacity. As part of his study, he designed and conducted minifrac tests using specially formulated fracturing fluids and custom-built experimental setups to interpret critical fracturing parameters. He completed both his B.Tech and M.Tech also in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. Dr. Abhijeet has also worked extensively on the synthesis, characterization, and application of natural bio-based surfactants for VES fracturing fluids, exploring their potential as environmentally sustainable alternatives in hydraulic fracturing. His research area includes Hydraulic Fracturing optimization, enhanced oil recovery (EOR), reservoir simulation and Carbon Capture Utilization and Storage Prior to joining MIT-WPU Dr. Abhijeet has worked in Presidency University and DIT University where He took core Petroleum Engineering subjects and mentored many students in accomplishing their goals. Currently he is teaching advance courses for post graduate students at MIT-WPU. He is also mentoring students for th

entries.title: Dr. Kunal Gaurav
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kunal Gaurav is a Professor of Marketing & Strategy at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over two decades of experience in academia, research, and academic leadership, he has played a pivotal role in enhancing the quality…
entries.text: Dr. Kunal Gaurav Professor Department of Business Dr. Kunal Gaurav is a Professor of Marketing & Strategy at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over two decades of experience in academia, research, and academic leadership, he has played a pivotal role in enhancing the quality and relevance of business education across diverse institutions. He holds a PhD in Business Administration and an MBA earned with top academic honours. His teaching and research interests span Strategic Management, Digital Transformation, Digital Marketing, and Customer Relationship Management (CRM). He is widely recognized for integrating emerging industry trends with pedagogical innovation, particularly in the areas of blended learning and faculty development. A prolific academic author, Dr. Gaurav has published several books on general management, brand management, and social entrepreneurship. His research is extensively published in reputed ABDC and Scopus indexed journals and reflects a strong focus on applied business insights and interdisciplinary inquiry. Dr. Gaurav is also actively engaged in editorial and advisory roles and has contributed to institutional development through research mentoring, curriculum design, and leadership of national and international conferences. His work exemplifies a deep commitment to academic excellence, innovation, and impactful education.

entries.title: Dr. Kunal Modak
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kunal Modak holds a Ph.D. in Structural Geology from IIT ISM Dhanbad, where he conducted research on the structural geometry, structural mapping, deformation, paleopeizometry, and metamorphism of the Mesoproterozoic Kaladgi Basin. His work provided…
entries.text: Dr. Kunal Modak Assistant Professor Department of Petroleum Engineering Dr. Kunal Modak holds a Ph.D. in Structural Geology from IIT ISM Dhanbad, where he conducted research on the structural geometry, structural mapping, deformation, paleopeizometry, and metamorphism of the Mesoproterozoic Kaladgi Basin. His work provided critical insights into basin evolution and its implications for hydrocarbon and mineral exploration. He also holds an MSc in Geology and an M.Tech in Petroleum Exploration from IIT ISM Dhanbad, equipping him with a strong foundation in both fundamental geological processes and their applications in petroleum systems. He has over 6 years of experience as a scientist under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, where he worked on the prestigious "Scientific Deep Drilling in Koyna Intraplate Seismic Zone" project. His research in this project focused on understanding the mechanisms of reservoir-triggered seismicity through core logging, petrography, microstructural analysis, SEM, and EDS. His research contributions include both single-author and leading-author publications, including a 2022 paper proposing a hypothetical 5 km deep borehole in the Koyna region to study reservoir-triggered seismicity. He also serves as a reviewer for several reputed peer-reviewed journals, contributing to the advancement of geoscientific research. Basic concept of Geology, Petroleum Geol

entries.text: Dr. Kundlik Vitthal Mali Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Dr. Kundlik Mali is a Professor and AHoS in the field of Mechanical Engineering. He earned his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from SPPU Pune in 2014 and completed his M.Tech. from COEP Pune. His expertise lies in the field of Thermal Sciences - HVACR, technology, and he has worked with leading companies such as Godrej and Daikin AC Ltd. to improve the energy efficiency of air conditioners. Dr. Mali has organized several workshops and training programs for students and faculty members in the area of Thermal Engineering. In the last three years, he has taught courses such as RAC, Heat transfer, Fluid Machinery, Numerical Methods, and CFD.

entries.text: Dr. Lipsa Mishra Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Lipsa Mishra is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from September 2024. She completed her B. Tech in Civil Engineering from KIIT Deemed to be University, Bhubaneswar in 2013, followed by an M.Tech in Environmental Science and Engineering from VSSUT, Burla in 2015 and PhD. in Environmental Engineering from NIT Rourkela, Odisha in 2023. Her research interests include water and wastewater treatment, Industrial wastewater treatment, Adsorption and biological treatment methods, optimization study, usage of natural/biobased materials as a coagulant. Dr. Lipsa Mishra has worked as an Assistant Professor at MITS, Andhra Pradesh for 2 years and 6years of research experience. Dr. Mishra has published 3 research papers (SCI) and 2 International conference papers (Scopus).

entries.title: Dr. Madhumita Mukherjee
entries.excerpt: Dr. Madhumita Mukherjee is currently serving as an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune in School of Business (SOB). Previously, she held the esteemed position of Director-Principal at the University School of…
entries.text: Dr. Madhumita Mukherjee Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Madhumita Mukherjee is currently serving as an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune in School of Business (SOB). Previously, she held the esteemed position of Director-Principal at the University School of Tourism, Airlines, and Hotel Management, Rayat Bahra University, Chandigarh, for Three and half years. With a Ph.D. in Tourism Management and certification from the International Air Transport Association (Canada), Dr. Mukherjee boasts over 22 years of comprehensive experience in academia and the corporate sector. Her professional journey includes associations with renowned universities such as Jamia Millia Islamia (Delhi), Lovely Professional University (Punjab), and Amity University (Kolkata), and aviation sector like Air Sahara. Dr. Mukherjee has made significant contributions to the fields of tourism, airlines, and hotel management through her publications in prestigious national and international journals including Scopus indexed Journals, as well as authorship of books. She has actively participated in numerous international conferences and summits, earning accolades and recognition, including awards at platforms like Edu Summit and NEP forums. entries.title: Dr. Mahesh Balwant Sonawane
entries.excerpt: Building Energy Simulation, Building energy analysis, GIS applications.
entries.text: Dr. Mahesh Balwant Sonawane Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Building Energy Simulation, Building energy analysis, GIS applications. Dr. Mahesh B. Sonawane has been working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, since July 2017. He received his Ph.D. from the prestigious Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute (VJTI), Mumbai, in March 2021 for his work "GIS-based building energy simulation studies". He obtained his BE (Civil Eng.) and M.E. (Civil- Building Science & Technology) degrees from North Maharashtra University, Jalgaon, in 1998 and 2008, respectively. Dr. Sonawane has been associated with the Maharashtra Institute of Technology (MIT), Pune, since December 2006 and has been teaching various subjects such as Building Design & Drawing, Green building & Infrastructure, Smart City Planning & Development, Building Control Procedure & Legislation. He had presented research papers in reputable international conferences in India and abroad. In 2016, he presented a research paper at the International Conference held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. He has published 8 research papers in international journals and presented 8 research papers in international conferences. His research interests include energy simulation of buildings, building physics, and GIS-based energy simulation st

entries.title: Dr. Mahesh Maganlal Makwana
entries.excerpt: Structural Engineering, Precast concrete, Structural Health Monitoring
entries.text: Dr. Mahesh Maganlal Makwana Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Structural Engineering, Precast concrete, Structural Health Monitoring Dr. Mahesh Maganlal Makwana is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune. He completed his master’s in civil-Structures from the College of Engineering Pune and PhD (Structural Engineering) from Maharashtra Institute of Technology Pune. Since joining MIT in 2008, he has taught a range of subjects, including Applied Mechanics, Strength of Materials, Structural Analysis, and Structural Design. He has more than 21 years of teaching and 6 years of industrial experience. Presently he is working as Program coordinator of Civil Smart Infrastructure and construction program, Departmental Budget and Purchase coordinator. Dr. Makwana's specific areas of interest include Structural Engineering, Precast Concrete, Durability of Concrete, and Structural Health Monitoring. He has published around 17 research papers in international journals and presented 1 research paper at an international conference. He has received Research grant of Rs 13 Lacs from AICTE. Dr. Makwana guides undergraduate and postgraduate students and is a Member of the Institute of Engineers, as well as a life member of the Indian Society of Structural Engineering and the Indian Society of Technical Education.

entries.excerpt: Dr. Mahesh Vasantrao Kulkarni is a postgraduate in Mechanical (Design) Engineering and a Doctorate in Mechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University. He is currently working as an Assistant Professor in Department of Mechanical Engineering at…
entries.text: Dr. Mahesh Vasantrao Kulkarni Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Dr. Mahesh Vasantrao Kulkarni is a postgraduate in Mechanical (Design) Engineering and a Doctorate in Mechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University. He is currently working as an Assistant Professor in Department of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. He has 27 years of experience. He has published 44 peer-reviewed papers in reputed international journals. He has 07 International Patents, 05 Book chapters and 01 International Book to his credit. He has successfully completed various research funding projects. His Research interests include Acoustics, Noise control and its applications, Mechanical System Design, Composite materials, Vibration and condition monitoring, Sustainability and environment. He is also keenly involved in multidisciplinary research. Research Area: Acoustics, Noise control and its applications, Mechanical System Design, Composite materials, Vibration and condition monitoring, Sustainability and environment.

entries.title: Dr. Makrand Mahadev Wagale
entries.excerpt: Dr. Makrand Wagale is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University in Pune. He completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering from Walchand College of Engineering, Sangli, and his M.E. and Ph. D in Transportation…
entries.text: Dr. Makrand Mahadev Wagale Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Makrand Wagale is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University in Pune. He completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering from Walchand College of Engineering, Sangli, and his M.E. and Ph. D in Transportation Engineering from Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani. Dr. Wagale specializes in Transportation Planning, Public Transportation, Rural Road Assessment and Design, Artificial Intelligence, soft computing, Computational Intelligence, Augmented Reality Applications for Civil Engineering Design, and Transportation Safety. He has authored several research papers in international journals and conferences and actively reviews journals of international repute, such as the Journal of Traffic and Transportation Engineering and the Transportation Research Board. Dr. Wagale is proficient in software such as HDM-4, VISSIM, MX ROAD, GIS, and computing tools like SPPS and R-Programming. He has over three years of combined experience in teaching and industry. Dr. Wagale also worked as a road safety auditor for the Bikaner to Phalodi section of NH-15 for four-laning from km 4.200 to 55.250 and two-laning with paved shoulder from km 55.250 to 163.500 in the state of Rajasthan, India.

He holds a B.E in Chemical Engineering and a Ph.D in Polymer Engineering & Technology. He is currently involved in teaching both Undergraduate and Postgraduate courses in Chemical and Polymer Engineering. Dr. Kulkarni's research is focused on the development of Sustainable Eco-friendly materials in the field of Chemical and Polymer Engineering. He is a Life Member of AICER (Associate, International Congress of Environmental Research), Indian Plastics Institute (PUN-231-LIF), and Asian Polymer Association (L-604).

entries.title: Dr. Manali Joshi
entries.excerpt: Dr. Manali Joshi is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Kothrud, Pune. She has over 5+ years of total experience, including 3 years of experience in teaching and 2+ years of experience in industry, which comprises MEPF…
entries.text: Dr. Manali Joshi Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Manali Joshi is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Kothrud, Pune. She has over 5+ years of total experience, including 3 years of experience in teaching and 2+ years of experience in industry, which comprises MEPF design, and active involvement in R and D department of a slope erosion control company. She completed her PhD at the Symbiosis Institute of Technology, Symbiosis International (Deemed) University in 2024. She has pursued her M.E. in Environmental Engineering in 2019 from The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda and B.E. in Civil Engineering in 2016, from L.D. College of Engineering (Gujarat Technological University), Ahmedabad, with a high merit percentage. Her key research areas include wastewater remediation, water supply and distribution, plumbing and firefighting design, design of STP and ETP, integrated treatment methods primarily novel techniques of photocatalysis, and phytoremediation along with conventional techniques for complete remediation of wastewater targeting the possible reuse, recycle and recovery, zero waste generation, zero liquid discharge technologies especially for industrial wastewater, green building and zero energy building design as per IGBC and USGBC standards. She is a member of the Institute of Engineers (India), Gujarat Inst

entries.title: Dr. Manasi Mishra
entries.excerpt: Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution.
entries.text: Dr. Manasi Mishra Associate Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution. Dr. Manasi Mishra is working as Associate Professor in Department of Biosciences & Technology, MIT-World Peace University. She has been actively working in the area of plant immunity proteins and peptides involved in defense against insect pest infestation and those with relevance in the context of crop improvement. Her research has been focused on structure-function correlation of diverse plant defense proteins, molecular diversity and their functional significance. She is also working extensively towards understanding the defense peptides distribution within individual plants and their tissues, factors influencing their expression, gene architecture, post-translational mechanisms, bioactivities and designing synthetic peptides directed towards therapeutic molecular targets. Her research Interests include Plant immunity proteins and peptides, Plant bioactive molecules, Botanical therapeutics, Protein engineering, Molecular diversity and evolution. She is a recipient of short-term DAAD fellowship and conducted collaborative research at EMBL Hamburg, Germany. She is also the recipient of DST-INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship award from Department of Science & Technology a

entries.title: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar
entries.excerpt: Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models.
entries.text: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models. Neurodegenerative disorders, characterized by the accumulation of protein aggregates in the human brain, represent a significant threat to human well-being for which no therapeutic treatment is available. Dr. Mandar's research attempts to address this condition by exploring the effect of herbal extracts on protein aggregation. During his doctoral research, he discovered an Ayurvedic extract known as Triphala to be capable of inhibiting the formation of protein aggregates in vitro. Further, the very challenging task of identification of the active ingredient of Triphala was successfully achieved and the mechanistic insights into the inhibition process were obtained. After his PhD, he worked as an industrial R&D scientist on the development of generic equivalent of the following: (a) Peptide hormones involved in controlling blood glucose levels (b) Peptides to treat short bowel syndrome (c) Vitamin supplements. During his short tenure at IISER Berhampur, he was in the pursuit of unravelling the structure and dynamics of complex protein enzymes by NMR spectroscopy. Going ahead, he is interested in targeting three interesting research directions: (i) Investigating

Lele is a distinguished academic scholar who earned his Ph.D. in 2006 from I.I.T. Bombay, Powai, Mumbai-76, India in the field of Mech. Engg. (Cryogenic Engineering). He completed his M.E. (Mech) with Power Engineering as Specialization in 1997 from Walchand College of Engineering, Sangli, which is one of the leading institutes in India. He earned his B.E. (Mech) in 1992 from Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, which is one of the most reputed institutes in India. With over 28 years of experience in the field, Dr. Lele has guided 17 PG and 6 PhD students, with one student submitting a thesis, and more than 25 UG projects to his credit. He has published more than 75 papers and holds 4 patents. Dr. Lele has been recognized for his outstanding contributions and awarded as a Fellow & Chartered Engineer of the Institution of Engineers, India (Membership No: F-1271411). He is also an ASHRAE member from 2006 onwards, a Life Member of ISHRAE, Indian Cryogenic Council, ISHMT, IIT Bombay Alumni Association, and ISTE. Dr. Lele has served as a member of the Board of Studies, Mechanical Engineering, under Savitribai Phule Pune University (Formerly University of Pune)

In his outreach role, Dr. Nigadkar has coordinated multiple national academic and rural immersion programs, facilitating experiential learning for hundreds of students across Maharashtra and beyond. She brings over 17 years of combined teaching and industry experience. She has successfully completed a DST-funded research project worth INR 50 lakhs under the Young Scientist Scheme, along with a research project funded by BCUD, Savitribai Phule Pune University. She has also undertaken several consultancy assignments based on her domain expertise. Dr. Deshmukh has published 35 international research papers, attended numerous national and international conferences, and holds 7 patents to her credit. Her consultancy and research work focuses on biofuels, carbon capture, oil spill removal, water treatment, and solid waste management. Dr. Deshmukh completed her Ph.D. in Bioenergy and M.Tech in Chemical Engineering from Pune University. Her key research areas include bioenergy, drilling operations and drilling fluids, enhanced oil recovery, eco-friendly solutions for oil spill remediation, and carbon sequestration. Her research has been published in reputed journals such as Renewable and Sustainable Energy Reviews, Energy & Fuels, and Environmental Science and Pollution Research. entries.text: Dr. Mithila Arvind Biniwale Program Coordinator & Assistant Professor Department of Media and Communication Dr. Mithila Biniwale believes in integrating critical inquiry with practice-based learning, encouraging students to engage with media as both a field of scholarly investigation and creative practice. She is committed to fostering research orientation, critical thinking, and reflective engagement while equipping students with the analytical and production skills required to navigate the contemporary media landscape. Through experiential pedagogies, including documentary filmmaking, field-based learning, podcast production, workshops, and industry interactions, she bridges the gap between theory and practice. By nurturing informed, socially conscious, and innovative media professionals, Dr. Biniwale contributes to MIT World Peace University's vision of progressive transformation through education, innovation, and culture.

Before joining academia, Dr. Killemsetty gained industry experience in the civil engineering sector, working on major projects with organizations such as Mumbai Metro and Jindal Steel and Power Limited. entries.title: Dr. Narendra Laxman Mathakari
entries.excerpt: Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures.
entries.text: Dr. Narendra Laxman Mathakari Professor of Practice Department of Physics Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures. Dr. Narendra Mathakari obtained his M.Sc. (Physics) degree from Department of Physics, SPPU in 1989 and Ph.D degree from the same department and university in 2009, He has a vast teaching experience of over 30 years and research experience of over 15 years. He has published 11 research papers in international journals. and 4 papers in international conference proceedings. He has also presented papers at 9 national and international conferences. He has also published 10 science popularization articles in newspapers and delivered science popularization talks at various places. His research interests include radiation processing of polymers and composites and viscoelastic and acoustic properties of binary and ternary liquids. He currently has research facilities, including an Abbe refractometer, a density measurement device, a viscometry system, and an ultrasonic interferometer all with constant temperature baths. He has also published a book on “Experiments in Engineering Physics”. Currently his book on “Concepts of Engineering Physics” is in the process of publication. During his career in MIT WPU, he received the MIT Foundation Day Award in 2008 and MIT Ideal Teacher Award in 2009.

Dr. Agashe has completed her M.Phil and Ph.D from University of Pune in the area of International Relations. Her Ph.D is in Failed States and Issue of Maritime Security : A Case study of Somalia. Dr. Agashe has completed a Diploma in Human rights from NLSIU, Bangalore and a certificate course in Geo-politics from JRVGTI. She has previously worked as the Head of the Department, Political Science in Symbiosis College of Arts and Commerce, as a Coordinator of PG Department in S.P College, Pune and as a visiting faculty in several reputed colleges such as ILS Law College, Symbiosis Law College among others. Dr.Agashe has also carried out surveys in collaboration with CDSA and UNICEF -Disaster Risk Reduction unit and for specialists working on FAO ( United Nations) project. She has presented

entries.title: Dr. Neha Bokey
entries.excerpt: Human gut microbiome, metagenomics and metabolomics, molecular biology, biochemistry, and phylogenetic analysis.
entries.text: Dr. Neha Bokey Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Human gut microbiome, metagenomics and metabolomics, molecular biology, biochemistry, and phylogenetic analysis. Dr. Neha Bokey is Assistant Professor in the School of Biosciences and Technology, She completed her doctorate degree from Department of Biotechnology SGBAU Amravati University. Her Ph.D. topic was DNA barcoding and phylogenetic analysis of Dioscorea species. She also completed 6 Weeks Technology Entrepreneurship Development Program (TEDP) sponsored by Department of Science and Technology (DST) conducted by IITM HTIC MedTech Incubator virtually in 2021. She completed NIDHI-EIR fellowship from HTIC med tech incubator IITM in 2023. During her fellowship period she was working on gutmicrobiome study for better health. Her research interest include, human gut microbiome (with focus on role of microbiome in pathological metabolic conditions like, IBS and IBD) and molecular biology. Since 2021, she has been actively working on comparative analysis metabolomics and metagenomics of gut microbiome. Her research group is working on how gut health is related to different dietary pattern and health parameters.

entries.excerpt: Image Processing, Power Systems, IoT, Robotics
entries.text: Dr. Netra Manoj Lokhande Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Image Processing, Power Systems, IoT, Robotics Dr. Netra Lokhande is an Associate Professor in the School of School of Engineering and Technology at MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a PhD in Electronics, a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, and a Bachelor's degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering. With 25 years of experience in teaching, Dr. Lokhande has expertise in Image Processing, Power Systems, Robotics, and Power Electronics. She has published 30 research papers and is a member of various professional bodies such as ISTE, IEI, IAENG, and IAEME.

entries.title: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar, M.Sc., Ph.D. is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans parasitology, host–pathogen interactions, immunology, and antiparasitic drug…
entries.text: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar, M.Sc., Ph.D. is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans parasitology, host–pathogen interactions, immunology, and antiparasitic drug development. Dr. Joardar previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Immunology & Microbiology, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Department of Medicine (Division of Infectious Diseases) Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis, contributing to studies on bacterial, parasitic infections and biomarker discovery. He did his PhD on antifilarial drug development and host-pathogen interaction from the Department of Zoology, Visva Bharati (An Institute of national importance). He has published extensively in leading journals, received multiple national research fellowships, and serves as an Associate Editor for Frontiers in Neglected Tropical Diseases and review editor in Frontiers in Parasitic Diseases. He is a life member of National Environmental Science Academy (NESA), New Delhi and member of American Society for Tropical Medicine and Hygiene (ASTMH). His research interest is focused on exploring redox biology for novel therapeutic interventions in parasitic and infectious diseases and host-pathogen in

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Industrial Internet of Things, Networking
entries.text: Dr. Nilam Mahesh Pradhan Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Industrial Internet of Things, Networking Prof. Nilam M. Pradhan is a highly qualified and experienced educator with a passion for research in the field of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering. She completed her Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2002, followed by a Master's degree in VLSI & Embedded System from Pune University in 2012. Currently pursuing her Ph.D. degree with the Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Pune, her research interests lie in the area of Internet of Things and wireless sensor networks. As a member of IEEE, SAE, and a Lifetime Member of ISTE, Prof. Pradhan has published papers in both national and international journals and conferences. With over 12 years of teaching experience, she has taught a wide range of subjects such as Computer Network, Digital Electronics, Object Oriented Programming, and Analog Communication, among others.

entries.title: Dr. Nilesh Bajad
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nilesh Bajad completed his Doctoral degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (BHU), Varanasi, under the Prime Minister’s Research Fellowship (PMRF), India’s most prestigious doctoral fellowship awarded by the Ministry of Education, Government of…
entries.text: Dr. Nilesh Bajad Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Nilesh Bajad completed his Doctoral degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (BHU), Varanasi, under the Prime Minister’s Research Fellowship (PMRF), India’s most prestigious doctoral fellowship awarded by the Ministry of Education, Government of India. He earned his Master’s degree from IIT (BHU), where he developed a strong foundation in Pharmaceutical Chemistry, and completed his B Pharm from the Government College of Pharmacy, Amravati. He is an active Member of the Royal Society of Chemistry (MRSC) (2025–Present). He has qualified several national-level competitive examinations, including GPAT (AIR 260), GATE (Life Sciences), GAT-B/JNU-CEEB (AIR 29), and NIPER-JEE in 2019. His Ph.D. research, titled “Exploring the diagnostic and therapeutic potential of rationally designed and synthesized novel multifunctional ligands for Alzheimer’s disease,” focused on the design, synthesis, and biological evaluation of multifunctional small molecules integrating therapeutic and diagnostic (theranostic) functionalities. The work aimed to develop novel agents capable of crossing the blood–brain barrier, detecting amyloid aggregates, and modulating key enzymatic and oxidative pathways implicated in Alzheimer’s disease pathogenesis. Dr. Bajad possesses extensive experience in organic synthesis, biophysical a

entries.title: Dr. Nithya N Kutty
entries.excerpt: Plant specialised metabolism, plant–microbe interactions, plant volatiles, metabolomics, and green chemistry.
entries.text: Dr. Nithya N Kutty Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant specialised metabolism, plant–microbe interactions, plant volatiles, metabolomics, and green chemistry. Dr. Nithya N. Kutty has completed her doctoral study from IIT Kharagpur. Her doctoral study was focused on dynamics of scent volatiles synthesis, storage and emission in flowers of tuberose, a widely cultivated ornamental plant in Asian countries. During her doctoral study (through CSIR JRF/SRF fellowship), she has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of metabolite profiling from plant endophytes, hairy roots of carrots and studied the effect of gamma rays on floral volatiles. She has also presented her research work in several national and international conferences. She pursued her post-graduation (M Tech) in Agricultural biotechnology from IIT Kharagpur and completed her graduation (B Tech Biotechnology) from JNTU Hyderabad. Her research area of interests includes plant specialized metabolism, plant-microbe interactions, plant volatiles and metabolomics. Presently she is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral levels. She is a recognized PhD supervisor of the university. She also mentors students for their research project. Recently, she could secure "DST Young scientist and Technologist" grant under which she is working on vaporiz

entries.excerpt: Dr. Nivedita Gogate is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from 2018. She joined the MIT-WPU in 1996 and since then she has been associated with teaching,…
entries.text: Dr. Nivedita Gunesh Gogate Associate Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Nivedita Gogate is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from 2018. She joined the MIT-WPU in 1996 and since then she has been associated with teaching, administrative, research and examination work of the university. She completed her BE (Civil Engineering) from COEP followed by M.Env.Eng.Sc. (MS in Environmental Engineering) from University of New South Wales (UNSW), Sydney, Australia. She developed training manuals for Environmental Awareness in Industry for managers and workers in association with an Australian consulting firm. She has an industrial experience of 2 years and teaching experience of 26 years. She completed her PhD in Civil-Environmental Engineering from COEP. She has published 5 research papers in international journals of good repute and presented papers in international conferences. She, along with her colleagues has published an Indian patent. Presently, she is working as a PhD Coordinator of Department of Civil Engineering.
