entries.text: Dr. Omkar Kulkarni Received Teaching Excellence Award : MIT-WPU News Dr. Omkar Kulkarni Honored with the Prestigious Teaching Excellence Award MIT World Peace University proudly congratulates Dr. Omkar Kulkarni, Assistant Professor [Department of Mechanical Engineering], for being honored with the IEI NMLC–FCRIT Excellence Award 2025 . MIT-WPU / News / Dr. Omkar Kulkarni Honored with the Prestigious Teaching Excellence Award News Dr. Omkar Kulkarni Honored with the Prestigious Teaching Excellence Award MIT World Peace University proudly congratulates Dr. Omkar Kulkarni, Assistant Professor [Department of Mechanical Engineering], for being honored with the IEI NMLC–FCRIT Excellence Award 2025 . Happenings Happenings News MIT World Peace University proudly congratulates Dr. Omkar Kulkarni, Assistant Professor [Department of Mechanical Engineering], for being honored with the IEI NMLC–FCRIT Excellence Award 2025 (Teaching Excellence Award 2025), conferred by The Institution of Engineers (India), Navi Mumbai Local Centre and Fr. C. Rodrigues Institute of Technology. Dr. Omkar Kulkarni Honored with the Prestigious Teaching Excellence Award What makes this accolade truly special is that Dr.Omkar Kulkarni received the award from the distinguished hands of Shri. Kishan Narayanrao Jawale (IAS), District Collector of Raigad. This achievement is not just a recognition of his unwavering commitment to teaching and research excellence, but also a reminder of the vital role educators play in shaping future leaders, fostering innovation, and driving societal progress. His milestone inspires the entire MIT-WPU fraternity to continue striving for academic brilliance and advancing engineering education with purpose and passion. Related public links Happenings

entries.text: Dr. Pallavi Prashant Tatpate Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Adsorption, membrane separation, polymer composite, green composite, waste management, sustainable processes and renewable energy Dr. Pallavi Tatpate is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Chemical and Polymer Engineering. She has more than 10 years of academic experience and 1 year of industrial experience. She teaches courses such as Momentum Transfer, Process Heat Transfer, Mass Transfer, Process Calculations, and Process Instrumentation. Dr. Tatpate’s research work is focused on the membrane formation, characterization and application for wastewater and effluent treatment. Also her research interests include adsorption, membrane separation, polymer composite, green composite, waste management, sustainable processes, and renewable energy.

entries.text: Dr. Pankaj Nivrutti Dhatrak Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Bio-mechanical Engineering, Medical Devices, Vibration and Fatigue, FEA Dr. Pankaj Dhatrak is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-World Peace University, Pune, India. With a PhD in Biomechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has a strong background in Finite Element Analysis of Automotive components, Biomechanics, Biomaterials, and their applications. He has over 5 years of industrial experience in vibration and fatigue analysis of various automotive components using different CAE tools, along with more than 15 years of academic experience teaching UG/PG students. Dr. Dhatrak is an author of over 45+ peer-reviewed research articles and book chapters in reputed Journal and Conference Proceedings. Recently, he has been actively involved in designing and developing biomedical devices. He has served as a Technical Advisory Committee Member for 5+ International conferences and has reviewed manuscripts for over 5+ reputable peer-reviewed journals. entries.text: Dr. Parshant Kumar Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Composite Materials, Tribology, Experimental Mechanics, Mechanical Metallurgy Dr. Parshant Kumar is currently working as Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering. His research interests are mainly focused on the development of composites for mechanical and tribological applications. Please refer to his personal webpage for more information. (https://sites.google.com/view/parshantkumar"Dr. Parshant Kumar is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering, where he brings extensive expertise and knowledge to his work. His research interests are centered on the design and development of high-performance composites for mechanical and tribological applications. As an expert in his field, Dr. Kumar is highly skilled in the study of materials science and engineering, with a focus on the mechanical and tribological properties of composites. His work is characterized by a deep understanding of the fundamental principles governing the behavior of these materials, as well as a practical approach to their design and development. Dr. Kumar's contributions to the field of mechanical engineering have been widely recognized, both nationally and internationally. His research has been published in numerous top-tier scientific journals, and he has presented his work at confer

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Signal Processing, AI-ML, Computer Vision
entries.text: Dr. Parul Madhuraj Jadhav Program Director Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Signal Processing, AI-ML, Computer Vision Dr. Parul Jadhav is an associate professor with almost 30 years of experience at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. She has held various roles in academics, restructuring, research, operations, and accreditation, and has successfully led several portfolios at the Faculty of Engineering and Technology (FoET). Dr. Jadhav's research interests include Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Deep Learning Architecture, Video and Image Analytics, Compression Techniques, and Signal Processing. She has published over 30 papers in international journals/conferences, holds three patents, and has multiple datasets/copyrights. Her research has been recognized through grants from organizations such as the Department of Science & Technology, BCUD, ASPIRE, and KIRAN Division - Women Scientists Scheme. She designed the Post Graduate Diploma in AI&ML, which was created in collaboration with Infosys, and has established many Labs across universities, where she is regarded as an expert in embedded signal processing. She has also conducted many faculty development programs and is a reviewer for Elsevier and IEEE journals. entries.text: Dr. Parveen Kumar Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Parveen Kumar is a passionate teacher and researcher with over one decade of experience. He holds PhD in Wastewater Management from prestigious Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee (IITR). He has qualified NET examination in 2005 and 2015. He has supervised 2 students for PhD work in area of Agro-waste Management and Wastewater Management. He has authored 25 research papers and 3 book chapters in various referred journals and conferences. He has given presentations in various national and international conferences and has been resource person in webinars. He has made professional visit to Malaysia. Dr. Kumar has worked with GD Goenka University and Amity University Haryana before joining as Assistant Professor at School of Business, MIT WPU. Research Areas: Circular Economy, Sustainable Waste Management, CSR and Sustainability, Water Resources Management

She graduated from SGGS College of Engineering, Marathwada University in 1992, and went on to complete her master's degree in M.E. (Instrumentation) from Govt. College of Engineering, Pune, Pune University in 2005. She has since pursued her Ph.D. from VIT Research Centre, SPPU, Pune, which she successfully completed in 2021. Pooja has been a dedicated faculty member at MITCOE since August 2005. She has published over 34 research papers in various conferences and journals and authored the book “Instrumentation and Control” in Oct 2000. Pooja has also mentored both undergraduate and postgraduate students in their projects and has successfully completed three BCUD-funded research Projects. In recognition of her contribution to the institute, Pooja was conferred the Ideal Teacher Award in 2014. She is highly skilled in industry-oriented projects, and under the Industry-Academia Immersion Program, she worked with Forbes Marshall, Chakan, for three months in 2022. With her vast knowledge and experience, Pooja is a valuable member of the engineering faculty

entries.title: Dr. Poonam Inamdar
entries.excerpt: Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling
entries.text: Dr. Poonam Inamdar Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling Dr. Poonam R. Inamdar works in the field of synthetic inorganic chemistry. Her research area interests are the synthesis of the metal complexes and evaluation of their biological studies and biomolecular interactions. She is also working on projects which include protein interaction studies of the organic ligands for the design of potent pharmacophores using spectrometric techniques. Her research focus is mainly an exploration of biomolecular interaction studies of the compounds using UV, FTIR, Conductometry, Cyclic voltammetry, Fluorescence spectroscopy, Viscosity and Circular dichroism studies as well as gel electrophoresis studies of the metal complexes for the assessment of DNA cleavage studies.

entries.title: Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari is an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. He has previously worked at Birla Global University and Manipal University Jaipur as an Associate Professor and Assistant Professor. Dr. Tiwari holds a…
entries.text: Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari is an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. He has previously worked at Birla Global University and Manipal University Jaipur as an Associate Professor and Assistant Professor. Dr. Tiwari holds a Ph.D. in the Faculty of Engineering, along with M.Tech in CSE and M.Phil in Computer Science. Currently he is pursuing a Postdoctoral. His research areas cloud computing, distributed systems, AI, and IoT. With over fifteen years of experience in teaching and research, Dr. Tiwari has authored more than fifty publications in Web of Science and Scopus indexed journals, conferences, and book chapters in the field of computer science. Additionally, he has received three patent grants and published three patents. One scholar has completed and five scholars are currently pursuing their Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Tiwari. Dr. Tiwari serves as the editor of two books indexed in Scopus and has actively contributed as the Organizing Chair and TPC member for numerous international conferences. Recognizing his outstanding contributions, Dr. Tiwari has been honored with the "Best Researcher" award by a reputed organization. He is also serving as a reviewer for several SCI and Scopus journals.

Kulkarni is an Associate Professor at MIT-WPU, whose teaching philosophy focuses on bridging the gap between theoretical computer engineering… MIT-WPU / Faculty / Dr. Pradnya S. Kulkarni Faculty Dr. Pradnya S. Kulkarni MIT-WPU Faculty Faculty Associate Professor Bio Dr. Pradnya S. Kulkarni is an Associate Professor at MIT-WPU, whose teaching philosophy focuses on bridging the gap between theoretical computer engineering and industrial application. Guided by the university’s vision of holistic, research-driven education, she brings valuable international academic and industry experience from Australia, Canada, and India to create a collaborative learning environment. Her contributions include leading high-impact research in medical image processing. Through guiding industry-sponsored projects and coordinating academic curricula, she strives to empower students with the technical expertise and ethical grounding required for global leadership. entries.excerpt: Synthetic Chemistry,Pharmaceutical Organic Chemistry,Computer Aided Drug Design,Green Chemistry,Microwave Synthesis
entries.text: Dr. Prajakta Vishal Adsule Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Synthetic Chemistry,Pharmaceutical Organic Chemistry,Computer Aided Drug Design,Green Chemistry,Microwave Synthesis Dr. Prajakta Adsule is an accomplished academician and researcher in the field of Pharmaceutical Chemistry. She graduated with a First Class degree in Pharmacy from Shivaji University, Kolhapur in 2003, and subsequently completed her M.Pharm and Ph.D. degrees with First Class from Shivaji University Kolhapur and Savitribai Phule Pune University respectively. Dr. Adsule has 16 years of experience in teaching and 6 years of research experience. She is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, School of Pharmacy, Pune. Dr. Adsule has published numerous research papers in national and international journals and is a life member of APTI and IPA. She has also received research grants and awards for her contributions to the field.

entries.title: Dr. Prajkta Bhide
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prajkta Bhide is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Public Health at MIT-WPU. She completed her Masters in Health Science degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University (formerly known as University of Pune). She holds a doctoral degree (PhD in…
entries.text: Dr. Prajkta Bhide Assistant Professor Department of Public Health Dr. Prajkta Bhide is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Public Health at MIT-WPU. She completed her Masters in Health Science degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University (formerly known as University of Pune). She holds a doctoral degree (PhD in Health Science) from Savitribai Phule Pune University. Her doctoral work explored the epidemiology of birth defects / congenital disorders in India and their larger public health impact. She areas of expertise include conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses, and quantitative data analyses. Her areas of interest include birth defects, maternal and child health, epidemiology and health economics.

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prakash Mohan Mainkar obtained his Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2001, followed by a Master's degree in Electronics (VLSI Design) in 2007. He later completed his Doctor of Philosophy (Engineering) from RTM…
entries.text: Dr. Prakash Mohan Mainkar Director - Admissions Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Dr. Prakash Mohan Mainkar obtained his Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2001, followed by a Master's degree in Electronics (VLSI Design) in 2007. He later completed his Doctor of Philosophy (Engineering) from RTM Nagpur University, Nagpur, India, in 2019. Currently, he serves as the Director of Admissions and an Associate Professor in Electronics & Communication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Along with his teaching responsibilities, he actively participates in various institute committees and contributes to the development of laboratory infrastructure and curriculum. Dr. Mainkar regularly engages with industry professionals to explore employment opportunities for students and facilitate international university collaborations for MIT-WPU. He has undertaken several international study tours to universities in China, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Nepal, in his capacity as an International Study Tour Coordinator. Over the past 8 years, he has also served as a National Study Tour Coordinator and organized visits to numerous industries throughout India to provide students with exposure to real-world applications of their coursework.

entries.text: Dr. Pranali K. Kosamkar Dr. Pranali K. Kosamkar Dr. Pranali Kosamkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University… MIT-WPU / Faculty / Dr. Pranali K. Kosamkar Faculty Dr. Pranali K. Kosamkar MIT-WPU Faculty Faculty Associate Professor Bio Dr. Pranali Kosamkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. Prof. Pranali is well known for her fundamental and impactful contribution to the field of computing education, elevating every student she has mentored through a consistent, selfless teaching service for more than 20 years. Her research interests are in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Information Retrieval, and Quantum Computing. Prof. Pranali is also a well-respected technologist. She has delivered sessions on Big Data Analytics with Python to selected officers of the Indian Armed Forces at Military Institute of Training, Girinagar Pune. She has guided many undergraduate and graduate projects and has national and international paper publications to her credit. Her innovative teaching of basic courses like compiler design, database management system, data mining and principles of programming languages has built a high recognition among the student community in the institute MIT-WPU, Pune. Faculty Faculty Associate Professor Bio Dr. Pranav Kayande is an academic professional with extensive experience in academic administration, quality assurance, and institutional initiatives. During his career, he has held several key roles including IQAC Coordinator, NAAC Coordinator, MBATech Program Coordinator, Vivekananda Study Circle Coordinator, and NBA Coordinator, contributing significantly to academic governance and quality enhancement processes. At MIT World Peace University, within the RSoL (Ramcharan School of Leadership), Dr. Kayande has actively organised and coordinated various academic and national-level initiatives. He has led the Future Fridays lecture series featuring keynote sessions by senior university leadership, including the Registrar, Controller of Examinations, and Chief Belief Officer of MIT-WPU. He also served as Co-Convener for the National Visioning Conference held at Babasaheb Bhimrao Ambedkar University on 9 September 2025 and contributed backend support for the National Visioning Conference held in Goa in January 2026. entries.title: Dr. Prasad Pathak
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prasad Pathak is an Associate Professor at the School of Business, MIT-WPU, with over two decades of academic experience in management education. He holds a Ph.D. in Human Resource Management and has successfully guided doctoral research at MIT-WPU.…
entries.text: Dr. Prasad Pathak Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Prasad Pathak is an Associate Professor at the School of Business, MIT-WPU, with over two decades of academic experience in management education. He holds a Ph.D. in Human Resource Management and has successfully guided doctoral research at MIT-WPU. His academic interests span strategic management, HR practices, peace studies, and socio-cultural dimensions of leadership. He has published extensively in Scopus, Web of Science, and ABDC-indexed journals, with notable work on topics such as Dasbodh philosophy, agile project management, and ethical leadership. Dr. Pathak has presented papers at prestigious international conferences, earning multiple Best Paper Awards. He has held key academic and administrative roles, including Ph.D. Course Trainer, NAAC Criteria Head, Peace Courses Coordinator, and Member Secretary of the Board of Studies for BBA. Deeply influenced by philosophical and spiritual thought, Dr. Pathak brings a values-based approach to education, fostering holistic growth among students.

entries.title: Dr. PrasantaKumar Sudarshan Ghosh
entries.excerpt: Organic and inorganic functional nanomaterials, organic field-effect transistors, hybrid materials, chemical sensors, and research instrumentation.
entries.text: Dr. PrasantaKumar Sudarshan Ghosh Assistant Professor Department of Physics Organic and inorganic functional nanomaterials, organic field-effect transistors, hybrid materials, chemical sensors, and research instrumentation. Dr. Prasanta Ghosh is an applied physicist with 16 years of research experience and 9 years of teaching experience. He conducts research in organic/inorganic functional nanomaterials, including organic Field Effect Transistors, inorganic/organic hybrids, and advanced sensors. Dr. Ghosh has published 47 research papers in SCI journals, conferences, and books, and has been granted 3 patents by South African Innovation Patent. He is an advisory board member of Materials Research Express and Nanotechnology, both peer-reviewed SCOPUS indexed journals. Dr. Ghosh has also received several awards, including the Top Peer Reviewer in Materials Science by Publons. Apart from his scientific work, he has also developed indigenous instruments and provides low-cost automation and instrumentation solutions for research and industry.

entries.title: Dr. Prashant Joshi
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prashant Joshi is an accomplished researcher and academician in the field of medicinal chemistry, specializing in natural product-based drug discovery. He earned his B. Pharm. from Mahakal Institute of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Ujjain (India) in 2006,…
entries.text: Dr. Prashant Joshi Associate Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Prashant Joshi is an accomplished researcher and academician in the field of medicinal chemistry, specializing in natural product-based drug discovery. He earned his B. Pharm. from Mahakal Institute of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Ujjain (India) in 2006, followed by an M. Pharm. from the School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, RGPV Bhopal (India) in 2009. In 2018, he obtained his Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Sandip B. Bharate at CSIR-Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine, Jammu (India), through the Academy of Scientific and Innovative Research (AcSIR), New Delhi, as a CSIR-Senior Research Fellow. Dr. Joshi has held academic positions in pharmaceutical and natural product chemistry departments at SPTM, NMIMS Shirpur. Currently, he serves as an Associate Professor at the School of Health Science and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. His research interests focus on medicinal chemistry and natural products, aiming to discover new therapeutics for multi-drug-resistant (MDR) cancers and bacterial infections. He has an impressive record of 32 research publications and 9 US patents granted in the field of natural source-based drug discovery.

With a solid background in Thermal Engineering and extensive experience in Engine Simulation software such as GT-Power, Matlab-Simulink & CFD, he plays a vital role in shaping the minds of the future generation of engineers. He completed his M.Tech in Thermal Engg. with CGPA 8.11 from the College of Engg. Pune, and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the College of Engineering, Pune. Prior to his academic career, he worked with SKF India Ltd, Pune.

entries.title: Dr. Pratiksha Deepak Khurpade
entries.excerpt: Nanomaterials, Catalysis, Biofuels, Flow through Porous Media, Energy
entries.text: Dr. Pratiksha Deepak Khurpade Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Nanomaterials, Catalysis, Biofuels, Flow through Porous Media, Energy Dr. Pratiksha Khurpade is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Chemical and Petroleum Engineering. She holds an M. Tech in Chemical Engineering from LIT Nagpur and a Ph. D in Petroleum Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University. With over 14 years of academic experience and 2 years of industrial experience, she teaches courses such as Momentum Transfer, Process Heat Transfer, Process Instrumentation at the undergraduate level, and Catalysis Technology for Masters level students. Dr. Khurpade's previous research work focused on the synthesis of biodiesel from various feedstocks and its blending with petroleum diesel, along with the prediction of properties of diesel-biodiesel blends. Her present research interests include flow through porous media, nanomaterial synthesis and its applications, and green corrosion inhibitors.

entries.title: Dr. Preethi Sunkara
entries.excerpt: Plant developmental biology, plant–pathogen interactions, combined biotic and abiotic stress, plant genetics, and molecular biology.
entries.text: Dr. Preethi Sunkara Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant developmental biology, plant–pathogen interactions, combined biotic and abiotic stress, plant genetics, and molecular biology. Dr. Preethi Sunkara is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU. She is a Molecular and Developmental Plant Biologist who earned her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. Her doctoral research focused on the biochemical and genetic characterization of TCP3 and TCP4 transcription factors in Arabidopsis thaliana, specifically investigating their antagonistic roles in leaf development and growth. Her expertise spans advanced plant genetics, including EMS mutagenesis, ChIP assays, and confocal microscopy, Protein interactions, Molecular biology. An accomplished researcher, Preethi has published in journals such as PLoS Genetics and Plant Physiology. She received the second prize for best poster at the 29th International Conference on Arabidopsis Research (ICAR-2018) in Finland. Beyond her primary research, she has supervised numerous Masters and undergraduate student projects and possesses a strong background in biotechnology and Plant developmental biology. Her teaching interests include Molecular Biology, Genetic Engineering, Genetics and applied Biotechnology .

entries.text: Dr. Radhakrishnan Subramaniam Professor Emeritus and Director Research Department of Materials Science & Engineering Dr. Radhakrishnan Subramaniam is dedicated to cultivating strong foundational knowledge and fostering a deep interest in the subject among students. He strives to create an academic environment that encourages innovative thinking and promotes the application of knowledge through research and development activities. His teaching and mentoring approach emphasises project-based learning, guiding both faculty members and students toward practical and research-oriented education. He actively promotes and leads R&D initiatives while mentoring postgraduate and Ph.D. projects. Prof. Dr. Radhakrishnan Subramaniam also believes in strengthening academic and research collaborations with other institutes to encourage collaborative research and the development of advanced materials for specialised applications.

entries.title: Dr. Raghunath Subhanrao Bhadade
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: RF, Microwave and Antennas, Wireless Communications, Software Defined and Cognitive Radio, Television Engineering
entries.text: Dr. Raghunath Subhanrao Bhadade Associate Professor and Program Coordinator (ECE) Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering RF, Microwave and Antennas, Wireless Communications, Software Defined and Cognitive Radio, Television Engineering Dr. Raghunath S. Bhadade is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Kard MIT World Peace University, Pune. With a Ph.D degree from the Savitribai Phule Pune University, he is an approved Ph.D guide and holds two patents in his name. Dr. Bhadade has presented and published numerous research papers in National and International conferences and journals indexed in SCI/SCOPUS. He specializes in RF, Microwave and Antennas, Wireless Communications, Software Defined and Cognitive Radio, and Television Engineering. He also provides Testing and Calibration services, and serves as a faculty mentor for Vishwashanti CanSat Team. As a life member of the Indian Society for Technical Education, Dr. Bhadade has been invited as a resource person in FDP/STTPs in his research areas and as a guest speaker across India in the field of Electronics and Communication Engineering. He has organized various social events at his birthplace to aid the overall development of rural people. Dr. Bhadade also has a passion for trekking and has actively participated in various treks across India

entries.title: Dr. Rahul Ashokrao Aher
entries.excerpt: Semiconductor nanostructures, two-dimensional materials, thin films, sensors, and optoelectronic devices.
entries.text: Dr. Rahul Ashokrao Aher Assistant Professor Department of Physics Semiconductor nanostructures, two-dimensional materials, thin films, sensors, and optoelectronic devices. Dr. Rahul A. Aher obtained his Ph.D. in Advanced 2D Materials and their applications from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. He also holds a Master's degree from the same university and is qualified in SET and GATE. During his Ph.D. tenure, he received the Bharatratn J.R.D.Tata Gunwant Sanshodhak Vidyarthi Award. He previously served as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at Fergusson College, Pune. His research interests include Advanced 2D Materials, Quantum Materials, Opto-magneto-electronics, Field emission, Electrochemistry, Photodetector, and Photo-catalysis materials. He has published more than 25 research articles in reputed indexed journals.

entries.text: Dr. Rahul Kisan Jagtap Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering "Incremental Sheet Forming, Additive Manufacturing, CAD/CAM, Manufacturing Engineering" Dr. Rahul Jagtap is an Assistant Professor in the School of Engineering and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He completed his M. Tech. and Ph.D. from Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Surat. With over seven years of experience in teaching and research, Dr. Jagtap has also worked as a Junior Research Fellow on a SERB-DST funded project for more than 2.5 years. He has published and presented more than 24 research papers in reputed international journals and conferences.

Coordinator (Business Administration) at the Ramcharan School of Leadership, MIT World Peace University, Pune. Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Bio Dr. Rahul Roy is an Assistant Professor of Finance, Area Chair – Finance, and Ph.D. Coordinator (Business Administration) at the Ramcharan School of Leadership, MIT World Peace University, Pune. He holds a Ph.D. in Finance from Pondicherry University and is currently pursuing a second Ph.D. in Economics. A recipient of prestigious fellowships and international research grants, his research and teaching expertise span Asset Pricing, Capital Markets, FinTech, and Machine Learning applications in finance. Before academia, he worked as a Senior Manager at Solytics Partners, leading advanced analytics and model risk management initiatives. Dr. Roy has published in reputed international journals and serves as a reviewer for several top tier, internationally recognized academic publications. Educational Qualifications: Ph.D. in Finance – Pondicherry Central University, India M.Phil. in Finance – Pondicherry Central University, India MBA (Finance), M.Com (Financial Economics), MSc (Financial Economics) B.Com (Finance), BA (Economics) Areas of Expertise: Asset pricing, Big Data, Capital Markets, Energy Economics, Human Capital, ML models, Macroeconomics, FinTech, and International Business. Teaching Experience: 3 years and 5 months Industry / Research

His main goal is to equip pupils with the tools required for good leadership and government. MIT School of Government Executive President Message icon Realizing a Magnificent Dream! In India, the word ‘Politics’ connotes different meanings to different people. For some, it is the study of the political process and the functioning of the government, some view it as a power game riddled with considerations of selfish gains and objectives, while others see it as a noble profession devoted to social welfare and the progress of the country. Whatever the perception, the fact remains that politics is one of the most potent tools to bring a qualitative change in society and the greater the participation of educated and dedicated professionals in it, the better will be the outcome. Politics is in fact a full-time career now. All the political parties are embracing this reality and are looking forward to active participation from young and educated individuals. (UGC-CARE Approved) Orchid Link https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7413-0244 Dr. Rahul Verma is a trained and qualified sociologist with a passion for Pedagogy and Social Research. He possesses an in-depth understanding and interpretation of empirical reality, which has been acquired through the collection and analysis of primary data from various themes such as the use of Social Media, Social Change and Modernity, Religious Pluralism, and Inter-Religious Interaction. Driven by a natural curiosity towards investigating social phenomena, he employs both Qualitative and Quantitative research methods.

Dr. Dandage has also authored 2 books and 8 book chapters and has delivered 25+ expert sessions as a resource person in online and offline modes. He holds Master of Technology in Biomechanical Engineering and a PhD in 3D printing of bone screws from Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology, Patiala. With over 3 years of Teaching and 4.5 years of research experience, his expertise is in 3D Printing, Biomaterials, Polymer matrix composites, Tissue Engineering, Metal matrix composites, Machine Learning, Patient-Specific Implants and Medical Devices. His teaching philosophy emphasizes interdisciplinary learning, ethical responsibility, and innovation-driven research contributes to MITWPU’s vision of shaping socially responsible leaders and innovators who bridge academia, industry, and healthcare for transformative impact.

entries.text: Dr. Rajashri Amit Kadam Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Rajashri Kadam is dedicated Associate Professor in the domain of Marketing and currently handling additional responsibility as the Associate Dean – Academics at the School of Business since January 2022. With over 22 years of combined industry and academic experience, she has been a passionate educator for the past 18 years, known for her interactive and learner- centric pedagogy. Dr. Kadam holds a Ph.D. in Marketing Management and an Executive Diploma in Digital Marketing from IIM Jammu. Her teaching interests include Advertising, Services Marketing, Integrated Marketing Communication, and Strategic Marketing. She has contributed significantly to academic research with publications in reputed national and international journals, Scopus-indexed book chapters, Case studies and numerous conference papers. She has also guided student-led case studies and research publications. She is also a Ph.D. supervisor and certified trainer, with prior experience in consulting and content development. Known for her strong rapport with students and alumni, Dr. Kadam continues to inspire learners with her dynamic teaching, academic leadership, and commitment to educational excellence. In her current leadership role, Dr. Kadam oversees academic operations for multiple undergraduate and postgraduate programs across the School

entries.text: Dr. Rajeev Sengupta Associate Professor & Program Director Department of Business Dr. Rajeev Sengupta brings over two decades of industry experience in the BFSI sector across leadership roles in insurance, banking, and financial services. Currently serving as Associate Professor and Program Director for Banking & Finance at MIT-WPU, he leads academic delivery for BBA-BFI, MBA Finance, and MBA-BFS programs. A PhD from KIIT University, Dr. Sengupta holds qualifications in Finance, Insurance (FIII), Law, and Education Management. He has published extensively in Scopus, Springer, Routledge, and ABDC-indexed journals, with research interests spanning financial literacy, bancassurance, behavioural finance, and sustainable finance. His teaching portfolio includes Managerial Economics, Financial Management, Corporate Finance, Financial Markets, Financial Planning & Wealth Management and Legal Aspects of Business.

entries.title: Dr. Rajendra Singh
entries.excerpt: Dr. Rajendra Singh, known as the
entries.text: Dr. Rajendra Singh : MIT-WPU Awardee Dr. Rajendra Singh Dr. Rajendra Singh, known as the MIT-WPU / Awardee Profiles / Dr. Rajendra Singh Awardee Profiles Dr. Rajendra Singh Dr. Rajendra Singh, known as the Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Dr. Rajendra Singh Dr. Rajendra Singh Magsaysay Awardee, Well Known Water Conservationist (Waterman of India) & Chairman, Tarun Bharat Sangh Dr. Rajendra Singh, known as the "Waterman of India," has dedicated his life to the cause of water conservation and rural development. As the Chairman of the Tarun Bharat Sangh, an NGO based in Rajasthan, he has spearheaded efforts to revive traditional water management systems, leading to the rejuvenation of over 10,000 water bodies and the revival of five rivers. His work has transformed the lives of countless rural communities, earning him international acclaim. Dr. Singh’s illustrious career has been marked by his innovative approaches to sustainable development and his unwavering commitment to environmental stewardship. He is a recipient of numerous awards, including the Magsaysay Award in 2001 and the Stockholm Water Prize in 2015, often referred to as the “Nobel Prize for Water.” His academic contributions include extensive research on water conservation, rural livelihoods, and environmental sustainability, which have informed policy frameworks at both national and international levels. Dr. Singh holds a Master’s degree in Ayurvedic Medicine from the National Institute of Ayurveda, Jaipur, and has dedicated over three decades to empowering rural India through sustainable water practices. Related public links About Us Social Initiatives BANA

He completed his ME(Electronics) in 2005 and is currently pursuing his PhD from SPPU Pune. His subjects of interest include Basic Electronics, Digital Electronics, Digital VLSI design, Biomedical Electronics, and Image and Video processing, with a particular focus on the latter.

entries.title: Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale
entries.excerpt: Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing.
entries.text: Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale Assistant Professor Department of Physics Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing. Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale has over 15 years of experience as an academician, including 11 years as an Assistant Professor of Physics at MIT-WPU, Pune. He holds a Ph.D. in Nanotechnology from the Department of Physics, PSGVPM ACS College Shahada, Kavayitri Bahinabai Chaudhari North Maharashtra University, Jalgaon, and M.Sc. in Physics from prestigious Fergusson College Pune, affiliated with Savitribai Phule Pune University. Dr. Ingale has qualified both the SET and GATE examinations established himself as a dedicated researcher and academician. His expertise lies in the fabrication, characterization, and design of cost-effective doped metal oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing applications. He has authored 7 international research papers published in reputed journals and holds two granted international patents underscoring his innovative approach to research. Beyond academia, he is actively involved in creating impactful technological solutions, such as a women’s safety device, showcasing his commitment to addressing societal challenges through innovation.

entries.text: Dr. Rakhi Khedkar Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Dr. Rakhi Khedkar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University, Pune, with nearly 23 years of academic and research experience. She holds a Ph.D. and M.Tech. in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, completed at the College of Engineering Pune (COEP). She has contributed extensively to academic administration, research and development, and accreditation activities. She currently serves as the Vice Chair of the SIG Smart City Cell, IEEE Pune Section (since January 2025), and previously held the position of Secretary of the Smart City Cell, IEEE Pune Section (January 2023 – December 2024). She is a Life Member of IETE and a Member of IEEE. Dr. Khedkar has published 11 research papers in reputed national and international journals and has presented five papers at national and international conferences. She is a co-author of the book “Solid State Devices and Circuits” and holds five registered copyrights. She has actively participated in and coordinated numerous national and international workshops, seminars, and faculty development programs, and has guided over 100 UG and PG student projects. entries.text: Dr. Ranjana Anup Agrawal Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering My teaching philosophy is centered on experiential, research-driven learning that bridges theory with real-world application. I believe in nurturing curiosity, critical thinking, and ethical responsibility among students. By integrating emerging technologies such as AI and data-driven methods into the curriculum, I strive to create an engaging and future-ready learning environment. My motivation lies in mentoring students to become innovative problem-solvers and socially responsible professionals. Through interdisciplinary research, industry collaboration, and student-centric pedagogy, I actively contribute to MIT-WPU’s vision of academic excellence, innovation, and societal impact.

entries.text: Dr. Ranjeet Vinayak Gadhave Associate Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Ranjit Vinayak Gadhave is an experienced academician and researcher in Pharmaceutical Sciences with over 20 years of experience in teaching and research. He completed his post-graduation and doctoral studies in Pharmaceutical Sciences from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. Currently, he is working as an Associate Professor at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. His primary research interests include the synthesis and biological evaluation of rationally designed heterocyclic molecules. Dr. Gadhave has made significant contributions to pharmaceutical research through publications in reputed national and international journals and by presenting his work at various academic and scientific forums. He has successfully guided more than 20 postgraduate students and continues to actively contribute to academics, research, and student mentorship.

entries.text: Dr. Rashmi Suhas Phalnikar Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering I am guided by strong work ethics rooted in integrity, accountability, and methodological rigor, maintaining high ethical standards throughout research and development. With a proactive and collaborative approach, I emphasize attention to detail, continuous learning, and timely delivery of high-quality outcomes. This integrated perspective allows me to bridge technical excellence with responsible and impactful practice. My work encompasses software engineering practices, insightful data analysis, and user-centered interface design, with a focus on developing scalable, reliable, and efficient systems. I employ data-driven methodologies to extract meaningful insights while ensuring that software solutions remain intuitive, accessible, and aligned with user needs.

entries.title: Dr. Rehan Deshmukh
entries.excerpt: Electrochemical and optical biosensors for pathogen detection, surface functionalisation, nanomaterials, PCR-based sensing, and enzyme technology.
entries.text: Dr. Rehan Deshmukh Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Electrochemical and optical biosensors for pathogen detection, surface functionalisation, nanomaterials, PCR-based sensing, and enzyme technology. Dr. Rehan Deshmukh is a Mentor and Assistant Professor at the Dept. of Biosciences and Technology, MIT WPU. He earned his Ph.D. from BITS Pilani in 2020, bolstered by the ICMR Senior Research Fellowship awarded in 2018. During his postdoctoral tenure at IIT Madras, Dr. Rehan worked on the development of an optical assay for amplification-free detection of SARS-CoV-2 using fiber-optic U-bent probes. Dr. Rehan's research spans electrochemical and optical biosensors, with a focus on pathogen detection, disease diagnostics, nanoelectrochemical/optical applications, and surface modifications for sensors. His scholarly output includes 15 publications, a patent, and three book chapters, with key contributions published in Chemical Society Reviews, Bioresource Technology, and Analyst. In addition to his research, Dr. Rehan serves as a reviewer for prestigious journals like PLOS One, Natural Product Research, and Microbial Cell Factories, etc. He is currently editing a special issue on "Biosensors for Detection of Waterborne Pathogens: Recent Advances and Future Horizons" for Frontiers in Water Journal. entries.excerpt: Dr. Ritu Karidhal is a Senior Scientist in the Indian Space Research Organisation with a key role in realizing the Mars Orbiter Mission, which created history of being the first mission to reach Mars in its first attempt.
entries.text: Dr. Ritu Karidhal : MIT-WPU Awardee Dr. Ritu Karidhal Dr. Ritu Karidhal is a Senior Scientist in the Indian Space Research Organisation with a key role in realizing the Mars Orbiter Mission, which created history of being the first mission to reach Mars in its first attempt. MIT-WPU / Awardee Profiles / Dr. Ritu Karidhal Awardee Profiles Dr. Ritu Karidhal Dr. Ritu Karidhal is a Senior Scientist in the Indian Space Research Organisation with a key role in realizing the Mars Orbiter Mission, which created history of being the first mission to reach Mars in its first attempt. Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Dr. Ritu Karidhal Dr. Ritu Karidhal Rocket Woman, Senior Scientist, Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Dr. Ritu Karidhal is a Senior Scientist in the Indian Space Research Organisation with a key role in realizing the Mars Orbiter Mission, which created history of being the first mission to reach Mars in its first attempt. Being the Project Manager and Deputy Operations Director for MOM, she and her team were responsible for executing the critical operations of Leaving Earth and Capturing Mars , which could be realized flawlessly. Dr. Ritu Karidhal has published more than 20 papers both in International and National Publications. She has been the recipient of multiple awards including “Young Scientist Award in 2007 by Shri Abdul Kalam, the then President of India, “, “ISRO Team Award for MOM in 2015”, “ASI Team Award “, “Women Achievers in Aerospace, 2017” by the Society of Indian Aerospace Technologies & Industries, “Birla Sun Achievement Award “by the Bank of Baroda. Presently, Dr. Ritu Karidhal is working on ISRO’s Moon landing mission, i.e., the Chandrayaan-2. In recognition of Dr. Karidhal’s contribution to science in Bhara

entries.text: Dr. Rohit Kumar Sharma Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Rohit Kumar Sharma is currently working as Associate Professor at School of Business, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. Previously he was associated with Centre for Management Studies Dibrugarh University, The Assam Kaziranga University, Amity University Mumbai campus, Adamas University, Kolkata and Christ University Pune Lavasa campus. He has a total of 17 years of teaching and academic administration experience. He has completed his master’s in finance and Ph.D. from Dibrugarh University. He has published 1 book and published many research papers including Scopus indexed research papers and have keen interest in academic administration. He has also headed the Planning and Monitoring Department at Adamas University as Director. His research interest is in the area of Behavioural Finance.

Dr. Ghadge is a faculty member at the School of Mechanical Engineering, where he brings his rich experience and knowledge to educate and inspire the next generation of mechanical engineers. Dr. Ghadge is a seasoned professional with a well-rounded career that spans over two decades. He has honed his skills and expertise in Mechanical Engineering through a combination of industrial and academic experience. With 3.5 years of industry experience and around 20 years of teaching experience, Dr. Ghadge is a well-respected figure in the field. As an educator, Dr. Ghadge has dedicated himself to imparting knowledge and skills to students, nurturing their potential and helping them grow as engineers. He is passionate about creating a stimulating learning environment that encourages students to explore and innovate, equipping them with the tools they need to excel in their careers. entries.title: Dr. Rohit Rajabhau Salgude
entries.excerpt: Dr. Rohit Salgude is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, with over 14 years of academic experience. He is actively engaged in teaching, research, and academic administration. He…
entries.text: Dr. Rohit Rajabhau Salgude Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Rohit Salgude is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, with over 14 years of academic experience. He is actively engaged in teaching, research, and academic administration. He completed his Master’s degree in Construction and Management in 2011 and earned his Ph.D. in Transportation Engineering in 2022 from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. Dr. Salgude teaches both undergraduate and postgraduate courses and has published several research papers in reputed international journals and conference proceedings. He is a recipient of two Best Paper Awards at international conferences and has served as a reviewer for various journals and conferences. During his academic career, he has guided more than 35 postgraduate dissertations and is currently supervising two Ph.D. research scholars. Presently, he serves as the Alumni Coordinator and B. Tech Coordinator and is actively contributing to NAAC-related activities at the departmental level. He has also worked as a Co-Convener for the national-level NIRMITEE event and has coordinated several national-level immersion programs and industrial visits.

entries.title: Dr. Rupali Sandip Kute
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Computer Vision, AI ML
entries.text: Dr. Rupali Sandip Kute Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Computer Vision, AI ML Dr. Rupali Kute is an accomplished assistant professor at the School of Electronics and Communication. She completed her undergraduate studies at the Maharashtra Institute of Technology and earned her master's and doctorate degrees in electronics and communication from the College of Engineering Pune and SPPU Pune, respectively. Dr. Kute has published numerous papers in prestigious journals and book chapters, and presented several research papers at national and international conferences. She also holds two Austrian patents and has recently completed a consulting project on "AI-based retail product detection and identification for unmanned vending machines." Her research interests are primarily in image reconstruction, machine learning, and object detection using deep learning. At the school level, she has held various administrative responsibilities, including project coordinator and final year coordinator. She is currently in charge of managing research activities and has also conducted a workshop on data science and visualization for students. Her teaching expertise includes machine learning, data science, data structures and algorithms, digital signal processing, Python programming, and R programming.

Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Bio Dr. Rushabh Trivedi’s teaching approach emphasizes conceptual clarity, analytical rigor, and the application of management theories to contemporary organizational contexts. His pedagogy fosters a structured and inclusive learning environment that encourages critical inquiry and reflective thinking. At MIT World Peace University, he contributes through research-informed teaching, curriculum development, and student mentoring, with a focus on organizational behaviour, leadership, and emerging technologies. His academic engagement aligns with the University’s vision of academic excellence, responsible leadership development, and the integration of research, innovation and social relevance. Educational Qualifications: Post-doc, PhD, FDP (IIM-K), UGC-NET, MBA, PGPM, BBM (IB) Areas of Expertise: Organisational Behaviour and Human Resource Management Teaching Experience: 2 Years. entries.excerpt: Nanomaterials and nanocomposites: synthesis, characterisation, and applications.
entries.text: Dr. Sachin Arun Kulkarni Associate Professor & Head of Department Department of Physics Nanomaterials and nanocomposites: synthesis, characterisation, and applications. Dr. Sachin Kulkarni is a distinguished academician with a Ph.D. in Physics, specializing in nanomaterials. With an impressive career spanning over 24 years in teaching and 5 years in industry, he brings a rare blend of academic excellence and practical insight to his profession. Currently serving as an Associate Professor and Head of the Department of Physics, he has played a pivotal role in shaping both curriculum and research culture. He has authored more than 15 research articles published in reputed peer-reviewed journals and conference proceedings, reflecting his strong commitment to scholarly work. At present, he is actively leading a seed-funded research project, contributing to advancements in his field of expertise. Dr. Kulkarni’s dedication, integrity, and passion for teaching have earned him the prestigious Ideal Teacher Award. He has also demonstrated exceptional leadership on the global stage by serving as Chairman of an international conference jointly organized by IOP (U.K.) and SPIE (U.S.A.). His contributions to academia, research, and academic administration make him a respected figure in the scientific community and an inspiration to students and colleagues alike. entries.text: Dr. Sachin Bhimrao Bhoite Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Dr. Sachin B. Bhoite is an accomplished academician and researcher with over 25 years of experience in teaching and research. He serves as an Associate Professor and Ph.D. Coordinator at MIT-WPU University, Pune, and holds a SET qualification and Ph.D. in Computer Science, specializing in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Edge Computing. He has published over 70 papers in Scopus/WoS-indexed journals and a Springer book chapter, and holds patents in IoT-based systems. As a recognized Ph.D. guide, he mentors scholars and reviews for leading journals, contributing actively as a keynote speaker and conference chair.

entries.title: Dr. Sachin Harle
entries.excerpt: Next-generation sequencing, metagenomics and microbiome analysis, whole-genome and transcriptome analysis, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics.
entries.text: Dr. Sachin Harle Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Next-generation sequencing, metagenomics and microbiome analysis, whole-genome and transcriptome analysis, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics. Dr. Sachin Harle is working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. He did his Masters in Bioinformatics from Bioinformatics Centre, Savitribai Phule Pune University. Doctorate in Biotechnology from Department of Biotechnology, Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University. The Study involved Molecular Docking of plant-based compounds against cancer related targets; Molecular Dynamic Simulation of complexes and Pathway Simulations. He has experience in NGS Sequence analysis in both- industry and academic settings, including, NIPL (Nucleome Informatics Pvt. Ltd.), Hyderabad; ICAR-NIHSAD (National Institute of High Security Animal Diseases), Bhopal; SCMS (Department of Scientific Computing Modeling and Simulations), Savitribai Phule Pune University; BRIC-THSTI (Translational Health Science and Technology Institute), Faridabad. He has worked on Whole Genome Analysis, Whole Transcriptome Analysis, and Metagenome Analysis of data generated using pacbio, nanopore and illumina platforms.

His research interests encompass areas like machine learning, speech recognition, mathematical expression recognition, and image processing, with multiple publications in reputed journals and international conferences. Dr. Naik has guided 2 Ph.D. candidates successfully. He has received awards for his research and has been an active resource person for workshops and academic programs.

entries.text: Dr. Saloni Kumar Assistant Professor Department of Clinical Sciences Dr. Saloni Kumar is an Assistant Professor of Psychology with a strong commitment to teaching, research, counselling, and student development. Her academic interests focus on integrating psychological theory with practical applications to foster holistic learning and personal growth. Passionate about mentoring students, she strives to bridge classroom learning with real-world experiences through innovative teaching approaches and experiential learning. Through her work, Dr. Kumar aims to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary for professional success, emotional well-being, and social responsibility.

entries.title: Dr. Samarth Dilip Patwardhan
entries.excerpt: Samarth D. Patwardhan serves as Head of Centre for Subsea Engineering Research (CSER), and Professor in the Department of Petroleum Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. His research interests include production and reservoir engineering, enhanced oil…
entries.text: Dr. Samarth Dilip Patwardhan Professor Department of Petroleum Engineering Samarth D. Patwardhan serves as Head of Centre for Subsea Engineering Research (CSER), and Professor in the Department of Petroleum Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. His research interests include production and reservoir engineering, enhanced oil recovery, HSE, as well as applying data analytics to the oil and gas domain. Through research grants he has procured; he has been instrumental in revamping the reservoir characterization laboratory and upgrading the simulation lab with industry-benchmarked software tools at the department. He is involved in collaborating with industry to further the academic and research partnerships for the recently commissioned CSER lab, a one-of-its-kind lab established through a common shared vision with Aker Solutions. Further, his current efforts are also directed towards setting up a stateof-the-art research lab for testing various characteristics of Nano-dusts, which span across industries. The lab is jointly funded by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India (GoI), and Techniche Engineering Private Limited (TEPL), an HSE company based out of Pune, India. entries.title: Dr. Samidha Anand Jawade
entries.excerpt: Material Science, Additive manufacturing, Engineering Metallurgy
entries.text: Dr. Samidha Anand Jawade Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Material Science, Additive manufacturing, Engineering Metallurgy Dr. Samidha Jawade is an accomplished mechanical engineer and researcher with a specialization in Additive Manufacturing. She has a PhD in Mechanical Engineering and several years of experience in the fields of Material Science, Additive Manufacturing, and Engineering Metallurgy. Dr. Jawade currently serves as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU, where she is responsible for teaching and mentoring students in mechanical engineering and related disciplines. As a researcher, she has published several research papers and has been actively involved in various projects exploring the use of additive manufacturing technologies to produce novel materials and structures with unique properties. Dr. Jawade is passionate about advancing the field of mechanical engineering and material science through research, teaching, and industry collaborations.

entries.title: Dr. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla
entries.excerpt: Politics of the Middle EAST, International Relations, Political Theory, Indian Foreign Policy
entries.text: Dr. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla Associate Professor Department of Liberal Arts Politics of the Middle EAST, International Relations, Political Theory, Indian Foreign Policy Dr. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla is an Associate Professor at the MIT-WPU School of Liberal Arts. He completed his BA, majoring in Indian History (with minors in Economics & Political Science) from CMS College, Kottayam, followed by an MA in Politics & International Relations from the School of International Relations & Politics, Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam. He pursued his M.Phil in Diplomacy at Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi, and obtained his Ph.D. in Religion & Politics from the University of Exeter, United Kingdom. Previously, he served as a Post-Doctoral Fellow at the School of International Relations and Politics of Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam, Kerala. Additionally, he received his Doctorate in Politics and Theology from the University of Exeter in the United Kingdom. Dr. Kuruvilla's research interests primarily focus on West Asian Politics, the Israel-Palestine Conflict, and Indian Foreign Policy. His teaching experience encompasses Political Thought, Theory & International Relations, as well as various Area studies courses at both the Undergraduate & Postgraduate levels. He held the position of Visiting Fellow at the Manohar Parikkar Centre for Defense Studies and Analyses in New De

entries.title: Dr. Sandeep Chandrakant Potnis
entries.excerpt: Prof. Sandeep Potnis has more than 35 years of teaching and industrial experience. He is Head of Tunnel Engineering program at MIT WPU. Seven research scholars are pursuing Ph.D. in Tunnel Engineering under his guidance. Three scholars have completed their…
entries.text: Dr. Sandeep Chandrakant Potnis Professor Department of Civil Engineering Prof. Sandeep Potnis has more than 35 years of teaching and industrial experience. He is Head of Tunnel Engineering program at MIT WPU. Seven research scholars are pursuing Ph.D. in Tunnel Engineering under his guidance. Three scholars have completed their Ph.D. under the guidance of Dr. Potnis. More than 50 Research papers are published in reputed journals. Dr. Potnis conducted six months ‘Certificate Programs in Tunnel Engineering’ in collaboration with Montan University, Leoben, Austria. (First of its kind in India) since 2017. He was instrumental in developing the unique M.Tech.(Tunnel Engineering) program at MITWPU, Pune. This program is endorsed by International Tunnelling Association (ITA). Only five programs in the world are presently endorsed by ITA. (https://about.ita-aites.org/wg-committees/ita-cet/master-courses). Dr. Potnis has been providing consultancy services to various Tunnel Projects such as Pune Metro TBM and NATM Tunnel, NATM Tunnel and Road Projectat Package XVII, Vadodara Mumbai Express Way, Bhoj and Morbe. He has provided satisfactory solutions to control the overcut and also for improving the shotcrete quality. Dr. Potnis is Member of Governing Council of Tunnelling Association of India (TAI), New Delhi. Dr. Potnis is Member of Executive Council of Indian Society for Rock Mechanics

entries.title: Dr. Sandeep Namdev Dhikale
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sandeep Dhikale, also known as Sandeepsagar, embodies a distinctive confluence of the traditional Gurukul system and modern educational paradigms. His academic pursuits span a spectrum of disciplines, including Sanskrit, Peace Studies, Indology, and…
entries.text: Dr. Sandeep Namdev Dhikale Assistant Professor & Peace Studies Coordinator Department of Business Dr. Sandeep Dhikale, also known as Sandeepsagar, embodies a distinctive confluence of the traditional Gurukul system and modern educational paradigms. His academic pursuits span a spectrum of disciplines, including Sanskrit, Peace Studies, Indology, and Meditation. Dr. Sandeep has actively contributed to academia by presenting over 22 research papers at various international, national, and state-level conferences, reflecting his interdisciplinary approach and commitment to scholarly excellence. He is also the author of three acclaimed books: ‘Understanding Peace’; ‘Dimensions of Peace’; ‘Indian Knowledge Systems: A Treasure Trove of the Way of Holistic and Sustainable Life’ These works reflect his deep engagement with the themes of peace, holistic living, and the rich heritage of Indian knowledge traditions. He is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in Peace Studies at the School of Business and Management, MIT World Peace University, Pune.

entries.title: Dr. Sandhya Mathapati
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sandhya Mathapati is working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune. She has over 19 + years’ academic & industrial experience. She completed her Ph.D. in the Civil Structural Engineering in 2020. She…
entries.text: Dr. Sandhya Mathapati Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Sandhya Mathapati is working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune. She has over 19 + years’ academic & industrial experience. She completed her Ph.D. in the Civil Structural Engineering in 2020. She completed her M.E. in Structural Engineering in 2007 and B.E. in Civil Engineering in 2004, from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University Aurangabad Her key research interests include Structural Engineering, analysis of plates and shells, sustainable construction materials, and non-destructive testing methods. She has served as Subject Chairman for UG and PG courses in Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University Aurangabad, and is a Life Member of ISTE, IGS and IEI. She published 16+ research papers in SCI/Scopus/WoS/ Google scholar -indexed journals and conferences.

entries.title: Dr. Santosh Jaybhay
entries.excerpt: r. Jaybhay Santosh holds a BSc, LLM, and PhD, bringing over 27 years of rich teaching experience primarily at ILS Law College and JSPM University
entries.text: Dr. Santosh Jaybhay : Faculty At MIT-WPU Dr. Santosh Jaybhay r. Jaybhay Santosh holds a BSc, LLM, and PhD, bringing over 27 years of rich teaching experience primarily at ILS Law College and JSPM University MIT-WPU / Faculty / Dr. Santosh Jaybhay Faculty Dr. Santosh Jaybhay r. Jaybhay Santosh holds a BSc, LLM, and PhD, bringing over 27 years of rich teaching experience primarily at ILS Law College and JSPM University Faculty Faculty Dr. Santosh Jaybhay - faculty At MIT-WPU, Pune Associate Professor & Legal Advisor Bio Dr. Santosh Jaybhay holds a BSc, LLM, and PhD, bringing over 27 years of rich teaching experience primarily at ILS Law College and JSPM University. Recognized as a PhD guide and member of the Board of Studies at Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU), he has significantly contributed to legal academia and governance. Dr. Santosh has served as Rector of the Indian Law Society Pune for 27 years and held leadership roles including Vice Principal of a Law College and Director at JSPM University Pune. He has played an important role in various academic committees at ILS Law College such as the College Development Committee, Anti-Ragging Committee, and Quality Assurance Cell. As an Appellate Authority under the Right to Information Act, he has also coordinated key legal courses and organized national and international conferences and moot court competitions. Honored as the "Most Popular Teacher in Pune" by Times of India in 2009, Dr. Santosh has presented over 30 papers at national and international forums, contributed to faculty development programs, and served as a paper setter and examiner for over 27 years at SPPU. His dedication to legal education and student development is exemplary.

entries.title: Dr. Satish Arunrao Polshettiwar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Satish A. Polshettiwar is a highly accomplished Researcher Associate Professor and Academic Co-ordinator PG Program, at Dept of Pharmaceutics, School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune-India. With…
entries.text: Dr. Satish Arunrao Polshettiwar Associate Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Dr. Satish A. Polshettiwar is a highly accomplished Researcher Associate Professor and Academic Co-ordinator PG Program, at Dept of Pharmaceutics, School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune-India. With over 21 years of teaching and research experience, Dr. Polshettiwar has been recognized for his excellence in the field, having been awarded the Ideal Teacher Award 2021 from the MIT Group of Institutions in Pune and the Prestigious ICT Educator Award 2020 from the Association of Pharmaceutical Teachers of India (APTI), New Delhi. Dr. Polshettiwar is an active researcher who has presented numerous research papers at international and national conferences. He has also authored over 60 research and review articles in peer-reviewed, Scopus and WoS indexed journals, and written 4 textbooks on Pharm Microbiology, Pharmaceutical engineering, pharmacotherapeutics, and more. Additionally, he has contributed 30 book chapters to Elsevier’s, Sprinter Nature, Bentham Science, Wiley, IGI, CRS Press Publication, been granted 2 international patents, one Indian design patent, and filed 10 Indian patents. He is also NPTEL-SWAYAM speaker and AI Expert in Pharmaceutical Science contributed for designing FormulationAI web-based prediction tool. Dr. Polshettiw

entries.title: Dr. Saurabh Ardra
entries.excerpt: Dr. Saurabh Ardra has earned his Ph.D. in Operations and Supply Chain Management from the Department of Management Studies, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee. His doctoral research was titled "Analysis and Design of Framework for Circular Food…
entries.text: Dr. Saurabh Ardra Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Saurabh Ardra has earned his Ph.D. in Operations and Supply Chain Management from the Department of Management Studies, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee. His doctoral research was titled "Analysis and Design of Framework for Circular Food Supply Chain in India". He holds a B. Tech in Mechanical Engineering and an M. Tech in Industrial Engineering from Lovely Professional University, Punjab, where he was honoured with the Academic Excellence Award. Dr. Ardra has contributed to high-impact research, with publications in reputed A-category and Q1 journals. He presented his research at the Society of Operations Management (SOM) Conference held at IIT Kanpur in December 2019. He also serves as a reviewer for esteemed international journals, including the Journal of Cleaner Production and Environment, Development and Sustainability. His teaching interests span across core and applied areas such as Operations Management, Supply Chain Management, Supply Chain Analytics, Inventory Management, Project Management, and Operations Research. Dr. Ardra brings academic rigour combined with practical insight to his teaching and research in the field of operations and supply chain management.

entries.title: Dr. Savitri Nathrao Jadhav
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Savitri N. Jadhav is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering with over 18+ years of academic and research experience. She completed her Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2022. Handwritten MODI…
entries.text: Dr. Savitri Nathrao Jadhav Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Dr. Savitri N. Jadhav is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering with over 18+ years of academic and research experience. She completed her Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2022. Handwritten MODI character recognition was her topic of research. Her research interests span Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AIML), Embedded AI, Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, and Edge AI. She has contributed extensively to interdisciplinary domains including UAV-based flood detection, anomaly detection using embedded AI, and intelligent agricultural systems. She has published numerous research papers in reputed journals and conferences and holds multiple Indian patents in areas such as crop damage assessment, non-invasive glucose monitoring, and UV-C disinfection systems. She is actively involved in consultancy projects with industry partners like KPIT Technologies and Bharat Flow Analytics, focusing on real-world AI and embedded system applications. A professional member of IEEE and associated with the Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society, she is committed to advancing research, innovation, and student mentorship in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Dr. Shahbazmubarak Abdulkhadar Dandin
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shahbaz Dandin is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune, India. He completed his B.E in Civil Engineering from VTU, Belgaum in 2012, M.Tech in Geotechnical Engineering from Govt. Basaveshwar Engineering College,…
entries.text: Dr. Shahbazmubarak Abdulkhadar Dandin Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Shahbaz Dandin is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune, India. He completed his B.E in Civil Engineering from VTU, Belgaum in 2012, M.Tech in Geotechnical Engineering from Govt. Basaveshwar Engineering College, Bagalkot in 2014 and Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India in 2023. He is a renowned geotechnical consultant, and his primary research interests include mechanical soil stabilization, cellular reinforcement, geophysical testing, and numerical modeling of structural fills. His research journal publicatin_ link https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=9jy9wA4AAAAJ&hl=en . Currently, he is engaged in geophysical subsurface investigations, including GPR and tomographical studies on cementitious soils. At present, three PhD scholars are pursuing their doctoral research under his supervision. Prof. Dandin is the recipient of a Ph.D. fellowship (tenure of 30 months) from DoM, Government of India. He also holds an Patent (Granted)- Multi-utility Instant Dryer.

entries.title: Dr. Shailendra Kumar Kale
entries.excerpt: Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Operations, Entrepreneurship
entries.text: Dr. Shailendra Kumar Kale Professor Department of Business Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Operations, Entrepreneurship Dr. Shailendra Kumar Kale is a proficient teacher with over 23 years of experience in the faculty of management. He is passionate about work. Being an academic leader, he has proficiency in handling the institutional academic activities and administrative activities as well. As a Ph.D. research guide, he worked to develop a research attitude among his scholars. Four research scholars completed Ph.D. under his guidance. He has worked for developing the curriculum for MBA in academic autonomy structure. Dr. Kale has served as a member of the Board of Studies and Academic Council at a few autonomous colleges and universities. In his research domain, he has published 2 books, 2 book chapters and his 13 research articles are indexed in Scopus database. He has completed two minor research projects. Dr. Kale holds a BE, MBA, and a PhD in Management. Dr. Kale has undergone Faculty Development Program from IIM-A. He is currently working as a Professor in the School of Business (SoB), MIT WPU, Pune

entries.title: Dr. Shailesh Varade
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shailesh Varade serves as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemical Engineering at MIT-WPU, where he focuses on bridging rigorous scientific thinking with practical application. Drawing from his research in soft matter, postdoctoral work at…
entries.text: Dr. Shailesh Varade Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Dr. Shailesh Varade serves as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemical Engineering at MIT-WPU, where he focuses on bridging rigorous scientific thinking with practical application. Drawing from his research in soft matter, postdoctoral work at Paris- Saclay, and industry experience in cosmetic formulations, he emphasizes curiosity-driven learning that equips students with both analytical depth and industry readiness. Beyond the classroom, Dr. Varade brings insights from international postdoctoral research, space-based experiments, and industry formulation work to foster a culture of curiosity, rigour, and applied thinking among students. His efforts align with MIT-WPU’s vision of sustainable and inclusive transformation, nurturing professionals who lead by example and create meaningful societal impact.

entries.title: Dr. Shalaka Patil
entries.excerpt: Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools.
entries.text: Dr. Shalaka Patil Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools. Dr. Shalaka Patil is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a dedicated and passionate researcher with a PhD in Biology from the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Pune, under the supervision of Prof. Kundan Sengupta. She holds university rank 1 for her M.Sc. in microbiology from SPPU. She has qualified CSIR-UGC NET-JRF with AIR 22. Her research area is genome biology and chromosomal instability. Her doctoral work focused on understanding the role of the nuclear envelope in maintaining the genomic stability of cancer cells. After her PhD, she worked as a Senior Project Associate at IISER Pune on a project to develop a cost-effective FISH kit for detecting HER2 amplification in breast cancer patient samples. Later, she joined the CNRS Institute of Human Genetics, Montpellier, France, for her postdoctoral research with Dr. Marcel Mechali. Her post-doctoral work was focused on developing an in vivo system to examine the phase separation of the initiation of DNA replication.
