entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU embarked on a transformative journey towards a greener future with its eco-friendly campus. Through the installation of solar panels and other environmentally conscious technologies, not only are we reducing our carbon footprint and energy costs,…
entries.text: MIT-WPU : Eco Friendly Campus Eco-Friendly Campus MIT-WPU embarked on a transformative journey towards a greener future with its eco-friendly campus. Through the installation of solar panels and other environmentally conscious technologies, not only are we reducing our carbon footprint and energy costs, but we are also nurturing an educational environment that instills environmental awareness and responsibility in our students. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Eco-Friendly Campus Life Wpu Eco-Friendly Campus MIT-WPU embarked on a transformative journey towards a greener future with its eco-friendly campus. Through the installation of solar panels and other environmentally conscious technologies, not only are we reducing our carbon footprint and energy costs, but we are also nurturing an educational environment that instills environmental awareness and responsibility in our students. Green Campus Eco-Friendly Campus Green Campus 01 MIT-WPU embarked on a transformative journey towards a greener future with its eco-friendly campus. Through the installation of solar panels and other environmentally conscious technologies, not only are we reducing our carbon footprint and energy costs, but we are also nurturing an educational environment that instills environmental awareness and responsibility in our students. Our commitment to eco-friendliness extends beyond mere power generation. MIT-WPU boasts of a meticulously designed eco park, green vehicles, and waste management. We at MIT-WPU believe that by leading the way in an eco-friendly environment, we are not only providing a cleaner, more sustainable campus but also inspiring the next generation of environmental stewards. Together, we illuminate our path toward a brighter, more sustainable future.

WPU Goa / Academics / Educational Model Academic model Educational Model Discover WPU Goa’s educational model blending academics, real-world learning, global exposure, and transdisciplinary skills for future-ready careers. At WPU GŌA, learning goes beyond classrooms to blend real world experiences. Our model blends powerful academic foundations with real-world exposure, global immersion, and human-centred development, shaping students who can think boldly, lead responsibly, and create meaningful impact. Why Today’s World Needs a New Way of Learning Students, whether they have studied Mechanical Engineering, Psychology or Mathematics, need to be trained comprehensively to be able to navigate and address 21st century situations, to respond to questions of innovation, technological advancement, and societal conflicts, with agility, curiosity and specific skills to collaborate across domains. This way, at WPU GŌA you are trained to flexibly adjust to various conditions, to help steer complex decision making processes at leadership positions, and to be a unique asset in any room where new ideas and innovations are created, implemented and valorised. Our Core Educational Pillars At the heart of this transdisciplinary university ecosystem lie five interconnected dimensions, each designed to nurture a transdisciplinary mindset, holistic learning, and human flourishing. The Academic Core A rigorous foundation that interweaves knowledge domains, integrates real-world challenges, and equips students with analytical depth and conceptual clarity. entries.title: Electric Pulse Secures 3rd Place at Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU's Team Electric Pulse secures 3rd place in the prestigious Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024. Read about their innovative project.
entries.text: MIT-WPU's Electric Pulse Wins at AI Challenge 2024 Electric Pulse Secures 3rd Place at Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024 MIT-WPU's Team Electric Pulse secures 3rd place in the prestigious Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024. Read about their innovative project. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Electric Pulse Secures 3rd Place at Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024 Achievements Electric Pulse Secures 3rd Place at Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024 MIT-WPU's Team Electric Pulse secures 3rd place in the prestigious Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024. Read about their innovative project. Achievement Achievements We are thrilled to announce that Team Electric Pulse has achieved a remarkable 3rd place in the final stage (Stage 3) of the Edge AI Innovation Challenge 2024! Hosted by Digi Toad Technologies in partnership with STMicroelectronics, the competition featured India’s top 10 teams, selected from an initial 180 entries. On October 4th, the team presented their prototype and pitch on the "Integrated Active and Passive Cell Equalization System," implementing AI in SOC calculation, at the grand finale in Bangalore. electric-pulse-banner Team Members Vipin Jain : Third-Year Electrical and Computer Engineering Omkar Kolhe : Third-Year Electrical and Computer Engineering Samyak Bakliwal : Third-Year Electrical and Computer Engineering A special appreciation goes to their mentor, Dr. Netra Lokhande, for her invaluable guidance, and to the management, Dean, Programme Director, and Programme Coordinator for their unwavering support.

Events Empowering Future Petroleum Engineers Through Industry Engagement Happenings AIIIP-1 Events AIIIP-2 At MIT World Peace University, Pune, experiences extend beyond the classroom to create meaningful industry exposure and deeper understanding. On February 14, 2026, the Flagship SPE MIT-WPU Student Chapter’s Annual Industry–Institute Interaction Program (AIIIP) successfully brought together accomplished women leaders, young professionals, and industry veterans, offering students a rare and immersive perspective into the global oil and gas ecosystem. AIIIP-3 Through powerful and authentic conversations, industry professionals shared remarkable stories from the field from working on oilfields in Iraq under extreme security conditions to operating in 50°C desert environments and icy offshore rigs. They spoke insightfully about balancing leadership responsibilities with personal life, navigating complex global energy landscapes, and driving innovation across technical and AI domains. These firsthand experiences gave students insights that extend far beyond textbooks, helping them better understand the realities and opportunities within the industry. entries.excerpt: Recognize the standards by which academic performance is evaluated at MIT-WPU. Examine our clear assessment procedures, grading scheme, and student performance review procedure.
entries.text: Evaluation Criteria At MIT-WPU Evaluation Criteria Recognize the standards by which academic performance is evaluated at MIT-WPU. Examine our clear assessment procedures, grading scheme, and student performance review procedure. MIT-WPU / Academics / Evaluation Criteria Academics Evaluation Criteria Recognize the standards by which academic performance is evaluated at MIT-WPU. Examine our clear assessment procedures, grading scheme, and student performance review procedure. Examinations Evaluation Criteria The evaluation of a student for each course shall be based on his/her performance in formative (internal assessment) and summative (Term End Examination) assessments, taken together. Guidelines for the weightage of Formative and Summative Assessment. Assessment Formative Summative Total Type FAT 1 MID TERM FAT 2 Weightage 15% bharat-asmita-img 30% 40% 100% The faculties shall be empowered to design and define the assessment method as per the course requirement and CO attainment. Unique assessment treatment to each course shall further strengthen the University assessment. Every course shall have credits assigned. Any course when assigned a grade S (Satisfactory) or NS (Not Satisfactory) shall not be considered for CGPA. Courses with 1 credit shall have only formative assessments. Summative examination is not recommended. For MOOC, the learning can be imparted through the platform. The assessment of MOOC shall be carried out as per the MOOC Policy of the University. The assessment to be mapped with CO. The CO attainment analysis shall be available through Examination Management System. Central Assessment Program (CAP) On-screen evaluation of answer sheets. The digital record of all the answer sheets shall be maintained at the Examination Management System. Passing Crit

entries.excerpt: At MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), every milestone is more than an achievement—it is a reaffirmation of our vision to shape values, nurture talent, and create lasting impact.
entries.text: Excellence Awards 2025: Events At MIT-WPU Excellence Awards 2025: Celebrating Leadership on the Birthday of Our Visionary Executive President At MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), every milestone is more than an achievement—it is a reaffirmation of our vision to shape values, nurture talent, and create lasting impact. MIT-WPU / Events / Excellence Awards 2025: Celebrating Leadership on the Birthday of Our Visionary Executive President Events Excellence Awards 2025: Celebrating Leadership on the Birthday of Our Visionary Executive President At MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), every milestone is more than an achievement—it is a reaffirmation of our vision to shape values, nurture talent, and create lasting impact. Events Happenings Excellence Awards 2025: Celebrating Leadership on the Birthday of Our Visionary Executive President Events At MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), every milestone is more than an achievement—it is a reaffirmation of our vision to shape values, nurture talent, and create lasting impact. Leadership-event-1 This year, on the birthday of our Hon. Executive President, Dr. Rahul V. Karad, we celebrated his inspiring journey of leadership and service through the Excellence Awards 2025. The ceremony reflected his belief in education with purpose, rooted in responsibility toward society. Leadership-event-2 Honouring Trailblazers of Excellence The Excellence Awards 2025 recognised individuals whose contributions exemplify leadership, innovation, research, and loyalty—pillars that strengthen the foundation of MIT-WPU. Leadership-event-3 Leadership (Academics): Dr. Siraj Ahmed Bhatkar Leadership (Administration): Mr. Rhishikesh Jagdish Shrikhande Leadership-event-4 Innovation – Process/Product (Academics): Dr. Sumedha Dhananjay Sirsikar Leadership

entries.title: Executive Director
entries.excerpt: Dr. Aditi Karad.
entries.text: Dr. Aditi Karad - Executive Director : MIT-WPU Executive Director Dr. Aditi Karad. MIT-WPU / About Us / Executive Director Executive Director Her educational vision extends beyond higher education through her stewardship of World Peace Schools in Alandi, Latur, and Indore, where she is transforming school education by integrating value-based learning, modern pedagogy, and character building, thereby nurturing future-ready leaders from an early age. In addition to her role in education, Dr. Aditi Karad, an MBBS graduate with a Master’s in Hospital Management from the University of Leeds, England, UK, is a respected leader in the healthcare and service sectors. She serves as the Executive Director of VishwaRaj Hospital, known for its quality and affordable care, and founded the nationally acclaimed Vishwaraj Institute of Nursing. A staunch advocate for women’s empowerment, Dr. Karad has been at the forefront of initiatives that position women from diverse backgrounds into leadership roles. Her efforts go beyond professional development—she actively supports community-driven programmes that promote education, economic independence, and social well-being. Her leadership is deeply rooted in peacebuilding and social impact, ensuring that every initiative she spearheads aligns with the values of integrity, service, and empowerment. By fostering a culture of inclusion and progress, she continues to inspire individuals and communities to work towards meaningful change for the nation. Dr. Aditi Karad is more than a leader—she is a force of transformation, shaping the future of healthcare, education, and social reform with passion, purpose, and an unyielding commitment to excellence.

entries.title: Executive President
entries.excerpt: Dr. Rahul V. Karad.
entries.text: Rahul V Karad: Executive President of MIT-WPU Executive President Dr. Rahul V. Karad. MIT-WPU / About Us / Executive President Executive President 373 words 2 min read Carrying forward a glorious legacy of Revered Prof (Dr.) Vishwanath D Karad, Dr. Rahul Vishwanath Karad serves as the Managing Trustee & Executive President of MAEER’S MIT Group of Institutions, which encompasses 70+ institutes and nurtures over 80,000 students. An engineering graduate, a postgraduate in management from Cardiff University, UK, and an alumnus of Harvard Business School, USA, Dr. Rahul Karad is dedicated to nation-building through youth empowerment. His vision is to help young minds realise their full potential through holistic, value-based education. Dr. Rahul V. Karad Dr. Rahul V. Karad Managing Trustee & Executive President - MAEER's MIT Group of Institutions Executive President - MIT-WPU MIT-WPU School of Government MIT-WPU School of Government In 2005, Dr. Rahul Karad founded the MIT-WPU School of Government (MIT-SOG) with the mission of shaping future political leaders who embody ethics, spirituality, and leadership. The institute offers India’s first-of-its-kind two-year full-time Master’s Programme in Political Leadership & Governance, equipping students with expertise in political strategy, electoral processes, governance, international relations, media, and NGO work. Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad A pioneering youth platform, Bharatiya Chhatra Sansad (BCS) aims to integrate young voices into India’s political and social democracy. It provides practical leadership training to groom students into future leaders. Every year, the BCS Summit hosts over 12,000 students from 400+ colleges across India, fostering discussions on nation-building, governance, and civic res

entries.text: Facilities · Department of Biosciences and Technology Facilities Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Facilities 01 Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Confocal microscopy, flow cytometry, live-animal imaging, microfluidics, gene extraction, and advanced imaging software. 02 Cell culture and tissue engineering Animal-cell culture, tissue engineering, disease models, and regenerative-biology research. 03 Molecular biology, bioanalytics and bioinformatics Molecular and bioanalytical workflows, genomics, and computational bioscience. 04 Microbial and plant biotechnology Microbial culture, bioprocessing, plant biotechnology, and applied sustainable bioscience. Shared infrastructure MIT-WPU Research Facilities Significant shared research instruments and faculty contacts are maintained separately from routine teaching equipment. Central facilities

Balki,” featuring renowned filmmaker Shri. R. Balki and veteran Documentary maker and TV presenter Shri. Siddharth Kak On 13th November 2024. The session was part of the university’s initiative to foster collaboration between the creative industries and academia, and it provided a great opportunity for the students to interact with a renowned personality. The session began with a welcome note addressed by Prof. Dheeraj Singh, Associate Dean, Department of Media and Communication, MIT - WPU. Balki_fireside_chat During the session, Shri. Siddharth Kak brought Shri. Balki’s ability in storytelling and media communication to the fore. He asked insightful questions, which allowed Shri. Balki to delve deeper into his individual experiences, including how he transitioned from advertising to filmmaking. Shri. entries.excerpt: Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) held from 9–13 March 2026 at MIT World Peace University, Pune.
entries.text: 5-Day FDP on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) | MIT-WPU Pune Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) held from 9–13 March 2026 at MIT World Peace University, Pune. MIT-WPU / Events / Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) Events Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) held from 9–13 March 2026 at MIT World Peace University, Pune. Events Happenings Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) Events five-day-1 Five-Day Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Indian Knowledge Systems (IKS) held from 9–13 March 2026 at MIT World Peace University, Pune. five-day-2 Organized jointly by the School of Engineering and Technology (SoET) and the School of Computer Science and Engineering (SoCSE), sponsored by the IET Pune Local Chapter, and conducted in hybrid mode, the FDP brought together eminent experts from premier institutions across the country and abroad. The program witnessed enthusiastic participation from 85 faculty members and research scholars representing 20+ institutions across multiple states. It highlighted the contemporary relevance of traditional Indian knowledge across pedagogy, philosophy, science, ethics, heritage, sustainability, AI, and technology particularly in engineering education. Related public links Happenings

entries.title: Flavours of the World: A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU's Atri Lawns came alive with the vibrant International Food Festival ‘Flavours of the World’, a culinary celebration that brought together sixteen countries in a spectacular showcase of global cuisine.
entries.text: Flavours of the World - A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community : MIT-WPU Events Flavours of the World: A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community MIT-WPU's Atri Lawns came alive with the vibrant International Food Festival ‘Flavours of the World’, a culinary celebration that brought together sixteen countries in a spectacular showcase of global cuisine. MIT-WPU / Events / Flavours of the World: A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community Events Flavours of the World: A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community MIT-WPU's Atri Lawns came alive with the vibrant International Food Festival ‘Flavours of the World’, a culinary celebration that brought together sixteen countries in a spectacular showcase of global cuisine. Events Flavours of the World: A Taste of MIT-WPU's Global Community Happenings International-Food-Festival Events International-Food-Festival On 24th January 2025, MIT-WPU's Atri Lawns came alive with the vibrant International Food Festival ‘Flavours of the World’, a culinary celebration that brought together sixteen countries in a spectacular showcase of global cuisine. Inaugurated by Vice Chancellor Dr.R.M Chitnis, Pro-Vice Chancellor Mr. Milind Pande, and Registrar Ganesh Pokale the event was organised by the Department of International Affairs led by Dr.Nirupama Kocherlakota supported by Trinity Study Abroad, Imperial Overseas Education Consultants, and Joyful Safari creating a remarkable platform for cultural exchange. International-Food-Festival The International Food Festival was a sensory journey that transported visitors across continents through an incredible array of vegetarian delicacies. International students became culinary ambassadors, preparing and serving authentic dishes from Bangladesh, Nepal, South Sudan, Kenya, Iran, Nigeria,Tanzania, Uganda, and beyond.

entries.title: Fostering Global Connections: Aalen University's Inspiring Visit to MIT-WPU
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU strengthens its global connections by hosting an inspiring visit from a delegation from Aalen University, Germany.
entries.text: Aalen University, Germany, Visits MIT-WPU Campus : MIT-WPU News Fostering Global Connections: Aalen University's Inspiring Visit to MIT-WPU MIT-WPU strengthens its global connections by hosting an inspiring visit from a delegation from Aalen University, Germany. MIT-WPU / News / Fostering Global Connections: Aalen University's Inspiring Visit to MIT-WPU News Fostering Global Connections: Aalen University's Inspiring Visit to MIT-WPU MIT-WPU strengthens its global connections by hosting an inspiring visit from a delegation from Aalen University, Germany. Happenings Happenings Fostering Global Connections: Aalen University's Inspiring Visit to MIT-WPU News MIT-WPU welcomed distinguished guests from Germany's Aalen University on 11 March 2025. Dr. Anja Dieckmann, Vice President of Aalen University, and Ms. Miriam captivated our campus community with their presence and vision for international academic collaboration. The day kicked off with Dr. Prasad Kulkarni, Director and Assistant Professor, Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU, hosting an enlightening session about higher education opportunities in Germany, giving students valuable insights into global academic pathways. Later, an engaging faculty interaction led by Dr. Nirupama Kocherlakota, Associate Director-International Collaboration, and Prof. Khare, Head of the School of Sciences, explored exciting collaborative possibilities. This groundbreaking meeting established foundations for joint research initiatives between faculty members, diverse student exchange programmes including research internships and semester-abroad opportunities, and innovative cross-institutional mentorship for student projects. The energy and enthusiasm from both institutions created a vibrant atmosphere of possibility. This partne

entries.title: Fostering Global Insights: Guest Lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, Queen Mary University of London at RSOL
entries.excerpt: The Ramcharan School of Leadership hosted a guest lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick from Queen Mary University of London for global insights.
entries.text: Guest Lecture by Prof. from Queen Mary University: MIT-WPU News Fostering Global Insights: Guest Lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, Queen Mary University of London at RSOL The Ramcharan School of Leadership hosted a guest lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick from Queen Mary University of London for global insights. MIT-WPU / News / Fostering Global Insights: Guest Lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, Queen Mary University of London at RSOL News Fostering Global Insights: Guest Lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, Queen Mary University of London at RSOL The Ramcharan School of Leadership hosted a guest lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick from Queen Mary University of London for global insights. Happenings Happenings News MIT World Peace University's Ramcharan School of Leadership (RSOL) recently hosted a guest lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, International Finance, Queen Mary University, London, accompanied by Abhishek Boral, Senior Recruitment Adviser, South Asia. The lecture focused on the intriguing topic, "What happens to listed firms after they obtain costly shadow loans?" This event not only provided valuable insights into international finance but also explored opportunities to strengthen bilateral strategic relationships between MIT-WPU and Queen Mary University of London. Fostering Global Insights: Guest Lecture by Professor Sushanta Mallick, Queen Mary University of London at RSOL Key Highlights Engaging Discussion : The lecture offered a deep dive into the financial implications of shadow loans on listed firms. Global Collaboration : The event highlighted potential partnerships between MIT-WPU and QMUL, fostering a stronger academic and research network. Student Engagement : Second-year BBA students pursuing International Business at RSOL acti

entries.title: Founder President
entries.excerpt: Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad.
entries.text: Founder President Founder President Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad. MIT-WPU / About Us / Founder President Founder President 264 words 1 min read At MIT-WPU, we groom generations who will be the architects of tomorrow, drivers of positive global change and ambassadors of world peace. Over the past 4 decades, students of MIT-WPU have created their footprints of excellence all over the world that inspire the spirit of transformation, change, and perseverance. Here, we encourage our students to curate their own stories, carve their own trajectories, and build their own paths. The success of our students guides our methodologies and builds bonds with them that are everlasting. The MIT-WPU academia is coherent with what Swami Vivekanand once said and I quote, “Union of Science and Religion/ Spirituality alone will bring Peace and Harmony to the entire Humanity". With a world class contingent of academicians, scientists, engineers, and technologists here to guide our students through a plethora of academic programs segmented across 150+ academic disciplines, our students are in for one of the most enriching curricular experiences that also empower them to be physically strong, mentally alert, intellectually sharp, and spiritually elevated. Our walk is long, and the journey a continuous one, with students joining the caravan and marching ahead as they accomplish their goals and realize their dreams. Revered Prof (Dr.) Vishwanath D Karad Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad Founder & Chief Patron - MAEER's MIT Group of Institutions Founder President - MIT-WPU Chairman (Governing Body) - MIT-WPU Creator of the World Peace Dome "Conceptualised, designed, planned and created under the guidance and supervision of the visionary educationist and proponent of peace, Prof. Dr. Vishwanath Karad

MIT-WPU / News / Global Collaborations: MIT-WPU partners with University of Buffalo for Capstone Projects News Global Collaborations: MIT-WPU partners with University of Buffalo for Capstone Projects MIT‑WPU partners with the University at Buffalo to drive research, innovation, and international academic exchange. Happenings Happenings News We are thrilled to announce a groundbreaking international collaboration between MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) and the University of Buffalo for the Capstone Project. This pioneering partnership is transforming the educational landscape by bringing the best minds together. Our students are at the forefront of this exciting journey, bringing together the best for cutting-edge Capstone Projects. Project 1 : Pick and Place Using Robot Arm Guided by Prof. Mahesh N. Pradhan (MIT-WPU) and Prof. Dr. Karthik Dantu (University of Buffalo), this project involves students Bhate Isha Dinesh Kshirsagar Pratik Prasad Dhapte Aditi Ram Kokate Vaidehi Ganesh. Role context This record documents a public event, conference or speaker appearance. Participation does not establish employment at WPU Goa. The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave was conceptualised as a strategic platform to align Goa’s long-term development vision with the national goal of *Viksit Bharat@2047. The conclave brought together policymakers, industry leaders, academicians, and thought leaders to deliberate on building a future-ready Goa focusing on sustainable industrial growth, innovation-driven development, and strengthening industry-academia collaboration. Key Messages : Hon’ble CM of Goa Dr. Pramod Sawant sharing thoughts on the establishment of WPU GŌA Dr. Raghunath A. Mashelkar on receiving Gomant Gaurav Sanman Mr. Mauvin Godinho, Hon Minister for Transport and Industries, Gov. of Goa Session 1: Reimagining Goa’s Future: Industrial Growth with Ecological Balance Mr. Pravimal Abhishek Polumatla, IAS Secretary (Science, Technology & Waste Management), Government of Goa Managing Director, Goa Industrial Development Corporation (GIDC) Prof. Abhishek Ranjan Global ESG & Sustainability Leader | Chairman, ASSOCHAM Karnataka (CSR & Sustainability) Joint Secretary, UN Global Compact Network India Mr. Anirudh Dempo Director, Goa Paints & Alli

entries.text: Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave · WPU Goa Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave is a landmark initiative that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and society to shape a progressive and sustainable future for Goa. WPU Goa / Events / Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave Event Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave is a landmark initiative that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and society to shape a progressive and sustainable future for Goa. Role context This record documents a public event, conference or speaker appearance. Participation does not establish employment at WPU Goa. 16 April 2026 Overview: The Goa 2037: Knowledge, Industry & Sustainability Conclave is a landmark initiative that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and society to shape a progressive and sustainable future for Goa. Organised by the Goa Chamber of Commerce & Industry (GCCI) in collaboration with MIT World Peace University, Goa , the conclave aims to foster meaningful dialogue, encourage collaboration, and create actionable pathways for growth that balance economic development with ecological responsibility. Key Highlight Gomant Gaurav Sanman A special highlight of the conclave is the prestigious Gomant Gaurav Sanman , honouring Prof. Dr. Raghunath Mashelkar for his exceptional contributions to science, innovation, and nation-building. The award will be presented in the august presence of Shri. Pramod Sawant , Hon’ble Chief Minister of Goa. Conclave Themes Reimagining Goa’s Future: Industrial Growth with Ecological Balance Ex

They live, learn, collaborate, reflect, and grow within one connected campus environment. WPU Goa / About / Goa Residential Campus About WPU Goa Goa Residential Campus WPU GŌA is designed as a fully residential university in India , where students do not simply attend classes and return to a hostel. They live, learn, collaborate, reflect, and grow within one connected campus environment. Live Where Learning Never Stops WPU GŌA is designed as a fully residential university in India , where students do not simply attend classes and return to a hostel. They live, learn, collaborate, reflect, and grow within one connected campus environment. Set in Goa, the residential campus brings together academic life, peer learning, wellbeing, sport, dining, cultural activity, and community living. Each space is designed to help facilitate a deeper university experience where learning happens outside the classroom and in everyday life. A Fully Residential Learning Experience Learning that continues beyond the classroom. At WPU GŌA, residential life is not an add-on. It is central to the university’s academic model. Students live on campus and interact daily with peers from different programmes, including engineering, design, psychology, and management. This creates a learning environment where ideas move across disciplines and students begin to understand how the real world actually works. A conversation in class can continue in a common space. A project can become a collaboration across domains. A student can learn from faculty, mentors, peers, and campus experiences throughout the day. This makes W

Explore our green initiatives, social projects, and education for a better world. MIT-WPU / Green Campus Sustainability Green Campus Discover how MIT-WPU integrates sustainability into its core mission. Explore our green initiatives, social projects, and education for a better world. About MIT-WPU MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), located in Pune, India, is one of the country’s premier institutions of higher education, known for combining academic excellence with values of peace, sustainability, and innovation. Established under the umbrella of the MIT Group of Institutions (founded in 1983), MIT-WPU stands out for its unique blend of modern education and ancient Indian wisdom, committed to nurturing not only skilled professionals but also responsible global citizens—a strong emphasis on Peace Studies, Value-based Learning, and Social Impact. MIT-Campus-img At MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), sustainability is not just a concept—it's a core philosophy woven into our academic, research, and operational frameworks. Guided by the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), MIT-WPU is committed to nurturing environmentally conscious global citizens who contribute to a more equitable, peaceful, and sustainable world. SDG Our Vision for Sustainability Rooted in the principles of Peace, Innovation, and Social Responsibility, MIT-WPU strives to be a leader in higher education that actively contributes to global sustainability challenges through education, research, community engagement, and institutional practices. Sustainable Courses Overview CDC RIDE Cocreation National Immersion International Immersion Overview The institution integrates sustainability and environmental awareness into its academic and research framework. Several courses related to environment and sustainability are offered across disciplines to promote green learning. Faculty and students contribute scholarly publications addressing sustainability challenges. Events such as RIDE and Co-Creation foster innovation and dialogue on sustainable development. The institution supports Swayam/NPTEL(National Program on Technology Enhanced Learning) MOOCs local chapter and remote center for sustainable learning. The institution also conducts international collaborative programs and community service projects focused on environmental impact. Sustainability-driven startups are encouraged and supported through incubation initiatives. Education is further strengthened through the effective use of ICT tools, and dedicated research funds are allocated to advance sustainability-focused research and innovation. Social Empowerment Women Empowerment SLDP Rural Immersion Community Engagement Women Empowerment Women’s Day Event The Women’s Day Event held on 10th March 2025 at MIT-WPU aligned with SDG 5 (Gender Equality) and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities), celebrating women’s achievements in STEM and promoting gender equity. Through engaging panel discussions and interactive showcases, the event highlighted the contributions of women leaders, inspired young minds, and reinforced the university’s commitment to fostering inclusivity, empowerment, and equal opportunities across all domains. Pratibha Excellence Award from Eaton MIT World Peace University is proud to highlight the achievement of Ananya Shimpi, who received the prestigious Pratibha Excellence Award from Eaton. The national-level competition saw participation from over 100 students across India, including 40 from MIT-WPU’s School of Engineering. After multiple selection rounds, only 16 students were declared winners. Ananya’s recognition underscores the university’s commitment to empowering women in engineering and fostering excellence, innovation, and leadership among its students. pdf report attached in drive Events folder named Women Women Entrepreneur Track – HackMIT-WPU At MIT World Peace University, women-led teams are empowered to drive innovation and leadership in technology and entrepreneurship. Sustainable Initiatives Air & Climate Water Management Bio Gas Energy Conservation Air-Quality-Monitaring-Station Sustainable Transportation Waste Management Bird Nesting Air & Climate MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) spans over 3 lakh sq. ft. and is envisioned as a living ecosystem that seamlessly integrates nature with learning. The campus is home to over 3,000 mature trees representing 100+ species and 23,000 shrubs across 70+ varieties, collectively sequestering more than 12.2 tonnes of CO₂ annually. This multi-layered green cover enhances biodiversity, prevents soil erosion, supports rainwater recharge, and maintains ecological balance. Planned around native and climate-resilient species, the landscape ensures year-round greenery while naturally cooling the environment and enriching the urban ecosystem. An Air & Climate Meter installed on campus further supports continuous monitoring of environmental parameters, reinforcing MIT-WPU’s commitment to sustainability. Air-Quality-Monitaring-Station Designed for both functionality and aesthetics, the gardens feature shaded walkways, open lawns, and natural study zones that double as recreational and cultural spaces. entries.title: HackMIT-WPU 2025 : A Leap Towards Innovation and Entrepreneurship
entries.excerpt: HackMIT‑WPU 2025 brings together innovators, founders, and students to co‑create impactful tech solutions and startups.
entries.text: HackMIT-WPU 2025 : Events At MIT-WPU HackMIT-WPU 2025 : A Leap Towards Innovation and Entrepreneurship HackMIT‑WPU 2025 brings together innovators, founders, and students to co‑create impactful tech solutions and startups. MIT-WPU / Events / HackMIT-WPU 2025 : A Leap Towards Innovation and Entrepreneurship Events HackMIT-WPU 2025 : A Leap Towards Innovation and Entrepreneurship HackMIT‑WPU 2025 brings together innovators, founders, and students to co‑create impactful tech solutions and startups. Events Happenings HackMIT-WPU 2025 : A Leap Towards Innovation and Entrepreneurship Events hackthon-1 MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) hosted HackMIT-WPU 2025, a three-day innovation challenge held from 26 to 28 March 2025, under the theme “Le Chhalang” (The Leap of Faith). The event brought together over 7,000 participants from undergraduate, postgraduate, and Ph.D. programmes across disciplines, fostering creativity, collaboration, and impactful solutions to real-world problems. The event exemplifies MIT-WPU’s philosophy of “Mind to Market” and “Paper to Product”, empowering students to transition from job seekers to job creators. Vice Chancellor, MIT-WPU, Dr. R.M. Chitnis emphasised the hackathon’s role in addressing global challenges through teamwork and innovation. Pro Vice Chancellor and Chief Convenor, Dr. Milind Pande highlighted the event's alignment with India’s Atmanirbhar Bharat and Startup India missions, nurturing entrepreneurial talent for the future. hackthon-2 This year's event featured 14 diverse tracks, including Ideathon, Workathon, Hack-AI-thon, Bio-thon, Designathon, and more. entries.text: About Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture | MIT-WPU Pune Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture Learn about MIT-WPU's Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture in Pune - its vision, faculty, and focus on sustainable, innovative, and socially responsible design. MIT-WPU / Schools / Faculty Of Engineering And Technology / Hafeez Contractor School Of Architecture / Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture Schools Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture Learn about MIT-WPU's Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture in Pune - its vision, faculty, and focus on sustainable, innovative, and socially responsible design. About The Hafeez Contractor School of Architecture is committed to nurturing socially responsible, ethical, and globally competent architects who can shape the built environment through creative design, technological innovation, and sustainable practices. The School offers a rigorous, studio-based learning experience that integrates advanced digital technologies, research, and interdisciplinary collaboration to prepare students for the evolving demands of the architectural profession. Emphasizing ethical practice and adherence to the standards of the Council of Architecture (CoA), the School fosters strong industry and community partnerships to create meaningful societal impact. With a focus on design innovation and research, the School encourages students to address contemporary challenges in urbanism, climate change, digital fabrication, and sustainable development, empowering them to become future-ready professionals and leaders in architecture and the built environment. icon Vision To develop globally recognized ethical, socially conscious, and creative architectural professionals contributing to the art and science of nation-building. entries.title: High-Speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) in a LASER illuminated window bomb
entries.excerpt: An award-winning MIT-WPU project on high-speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) to understand combustion at a micro-scale.
entries.text: High-Speed Flame Imaging of SRP | MIT-WPU Project High-Speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) in a LASER illuminated window bomb An award-winning MIT-WPU project on high-speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) to understand combustion at a micro-scale. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Student Projects / High-Speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) in a LASER illuminated window bomb Life Wpu High-Speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) in a LASER illuminated window bomb An award-winning MIT-WPU project on high-speed flame imaging of Solid Rocket Propellant (SRP) to understand combustion at a micro-scale. Student Projects Student Projects krish_certificate Project Details This award winning project aimed to investigate the combustion behavior of Solid Rocket Propellants (SRPs), specifically focusing on the micro-scale phenomena occurring on the burning surface. By using a window bomb and high-speed cameras, the researchers were able to capture detailed images of the flame structure and determine the temperature profile of the burning surface. shrikant_certificate This project has made significant contributions to the understanding of SRP combustion. The insights gained from this research can be applied to improve the design and performance of solid rocket propulsion systems. Krish Raibole Prof. Shrikant Nimbalkar [Faculty Guide]

A pioneering institution of higher education with world-class academic standards, MIT-WPU’s rich legacy dates back more than 40 years. Visionaries of Education: The Birth of MIT-WPU image Between 1981 and 1983, MIT was conceived as an inspiration, a visionary institution that stood decades ahead of its time. It was born from the creative zeal to redefine education, embracing innovation and excellence. During this pivotal period, the seeds of a remarkable educational journey were sown, laying the foundation for an institution that would continuously inspire and shape the future of education, pushing the boundaries of what was thought possible in academia. entries.title: How MIT-WPU Pune’s B.Com in International Accounting and Finance Prepares Students for Global Success
entries.excerpt: See how MIT-WPU Pune's B.Com in International Accounting & Finance prepares students for global careers with industry-ready skills and expertise.
entries.text: B.Com International Accounting & Finance How MIT-WPU Pune’s B.Com in International Accounting and Finance Prepares Students for Global Success See how MIT-WPU Pune's B.Com in International Accounting & Finance prepares students for global careers with industry-ready skills and expertise. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / How MIT-WPU Pune’s B.Com in International Accounting and Finance Prepares Students for Global Success Faculty Articles How MIT-WPU Pune’s B.Com in International Accounting and Finance Prepares Students for Global Success See how MIT-WPU Pune's B.Com in International Accounting & Finance prepares students for global careers with industry-ready skills and expertise. 1,184 words 5 min read Accounting and finance are not just localised and specialised textbook domains. Students who wish to have a career in audit, taxation, financial reporting, management accounting or financial analysis now need more than just the basic pieces of commerce. From the beginning, they need to have international standards, real-world knowledge, analytical thinking, and be career-ready. That’s why the undergraduate track is so essential. The B.Com in International Accounting and Finance at MIT-WPU, Pune, is designed to suit the broad aspects. It is designed on international accounting principles and financial practice and is clearly oriented towards careers globally. The right degree can shape the direction of a finance career For the students aspiring to take up ACCA and work in the global finance ecosystem, the undergraduate degree is more than just a stepping stone. It’s the framework upon which everything else is built. entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU follows UGC-specified eligibility norms while reviewing international students’ admission applications.
entries.text: How To Apply For Study In India| India Application Process For Study How to Apply MIT-WPU follows UGC-specified eligibility norms while reviewing international students’ admission applications. MIT-WPU / Study In India / How to Apply Study In India How to Apply MIT-WPU follows UGC-specified eligibility norms while reviewing international students’ admission applications. Study in India International Admission Categories Foreign Nationals Students A student is eligible to apply as a Foreign National (FN) if he/she is a citizen of any country other than India and holds a foreign passport (FN - SAARC countries / FN – Non-SAARC Countries) CIWGC/CIWSEA Students Children of Indian Working in Gulf Countries & Southeast Asian countries: The candidates whose parents are working at Gulf countries or Southeast Asian countries only are eligible under this category OCI Students A student is eligible to apply as OCI if he/she is granted with Overseas Citizenship of India and has an OCI card NRI Students A student is eligible for admission under the NRI category if his/her parents (Father/ Mother or legal guardian) are holding an NRI status and working outside India in countries other than Gulf or Southeast Asia. Application Process Step arrow Apply online on admissions.mitwpu.edu.in and submit registration form Receive Login ID and Password (Auto redirect to Student Dashboard) Fill Application Form (Pay application form Fees - Rs.1800) Receive provisional offer letter Complete Fee Payment (Pay Processing Fee & 1st Instalment of Programme Fee) Verification (Payment/Personal/Education/Documents) Receive Final Offer Letter and Offer Guide with Student PRN Number Original Document Submission Please Note: On submission of application IEC department will share email with programme details,

entries.title: ICSSR Sanctions Rs. 16 Lakhs for Research on Elderly Well-being in Rural India
entries.excerpt: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) has provisionally sanctioned Rs. 16 lakhs under its Special Call for Vision Viksit Bharat@2047.
entries.text: ICSSR Sanctions Rs. 16 Lakhs on Well-being in Rural India : MIT-WPU Researcher ICSSR Sanctions Rs. 16 Lakhs for Research on Elderly Well-being in Rural India The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) has provisionally sanctioned Rs. 16 lakhs under its Special Call for Vision Viksit Bharat@2047. MIT-WPU / Researcher / ICSSR Sanctions Rs. 16 Lakhs for Research on Elderly Well-being in Rural India Researcher ICSSR Sanctions Rs. 16 Lakhs for Research on Elderly Well-being in Rural India The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) has provisionally sanctioned Rs. 16 lakhs under its Special Call for Vision Viksit Bharat@2047. 450 words 2 min read Researcher Research Researcher The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR) has provisionally sanctioned Rs. 16 lakhs under its Special Call for Vision Viksit Bharat@2047 initiative for a research project focused on the elderly population in rural India. The project, titled “Preserving the Dignity of the Aging Population: An Impact Evaluation of Social Security on Financial and Psychological Well-being in Rural India,” seeks to evaluate the social security measures in place for the aging demographic and their effectiveness in ensuring both financial stability and psychological well-being. krishna warhade Overview of the Research With the rapid growth of India's elderly population, especially in rural areas, the need to safeguard their dignity and enhance their quality of life during old age has become increasingly urgent. The study will explore various aspects of social security that contribute to the well-being of the elderly, aiming to ensure that they live with dignity and fulfillment. The research aligns with key global frameworks such as the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), part

Register now for an unforgettable experience Events Ignisia 2026 Happenings Events Ignisia2 Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University successfully hosted IGNISIA – a 24-hour National-Level AI Hackathon on 3rd & 4th April 2026, organized by the Department of Polytechnic and Skill Development and the Department of Computer Engineering and Technology. Ignisia3 With the theme Think. Evolve. Build., IGNISIA challenged students to develop AI-driven solutions for real-world problems within just 24 hours. Problem statements across domains like Healthcare, FinTech, Smart Cities, Education, Sustainability, and SME tools were released only 18 hours prior, pushing teams to innovate from scratch. The event witnessed an overwhelming response, with 450+ teams and over 1600+ participants registering from various states including Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Goa, Gujarat, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Madhya Pradesh. Participants came from a wide range of institutions, After a rigorous screening process, 78 teams comprising 280 students advanced to the Grand Finale, where they presented innovative solutions across diverse domains. Ignisia4 The event was graced by esteemed dignitaries. The Chief Guest was Swati Sadineni, Senior Director – Software Engineering, NVIDIA. The Guests of Honor were Aneesh Nat

Page Content Section 1 Section 2 Section 3 E-Cell Section 1 The Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) of MIT-WPU, founded in April 2024, is a rapidly growing student-led platform that drives innovation, venture creation, and entrepreneurial learning on campus. As the only space on campus where students can implement their startup ideas, validate concepts, and experience real startup culture, E-Cell provides hands-on exposure through pitch sessions, incubation pathways, and mentor guidance. In its first year, the cell engaged 1,500+ students, supported 40+ early-stage ideas, and represented MIT- WPU at national ecosystems including Startup Mahakumbh, MahaSummit, and key Women Entrepreneurship forums. With high-impact workshops, founder talks, design sprints, and industry collaborations, E-Cell MIT-WPU has quickly become a catalyst for empowering young innovators to build, pitch, and scale meaningful ventures. Link: https://www.linkedin.com/company/e-cell_mitwpu Section 2 Startup Mahakumbh 2025 Startup Mahakumbh 2025 in Delhi brought together 3,000+ startups across AI, Deeptech, Cybersecurity, HealthTech, AgriTech, Climate Tech, FinTech, D2C, Defence, Mobility, and more, making it India’s largest startup platform. 30 delegates from E-Cell MIT-WPU were selected to represent the university, immersing themselves in national-level innovation, policy, and investor conve

entries.text: KAPILA IIC | MIT-WPU IIC Initiative Institution Innovation Council KAPILA (Kalam Program for IP Literacy and Awareness) is a flagship initiative by the Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell (MIC) and AICTE, designed to promote intellectual property (IP) awareness and facilitate patent filing . MIT-WPU / Institution Innovation Council / Institution Innovation Council Institution Innovation Council KAPILA (Kalam Program for IP Literacy and Awareness) is a flagship initiative by the Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell (MIC) and AICTE, designed to promote intellectual property (IP) awareness and facilitate patent filing . About KAPILA Scheme KAPILA (Kalam Program for IP Literacy and Awareness) is a flagship initiative by the Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell (MIC) and AICTE, designed to promote intellectual property (IP) awareness and facilitate patent filing in Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) across India. The scheme is named in honour of Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam, reflecting his vision of fostering a culture of innovation and protecting creative ideas among students and faculty. Objective To create widespread awareness about the importance of intellectual property rights (IPR) and patent filing among students and faculty in HEIs. To recognize and support innovations and best practices in educational institutions. To establish a robust IP filing ecosystem, encouraging systematic protection of new ideas, research, and innovations with national and global relevance. Role of IIC in KAPILA The Institution’s Innovation Council (IIC) at MIT-WPU plays a crucial role in implementing the KAPILA scheme by: Raising Awareness: Organizing workshops, seminars, and training sessions on IP literacy and patent filing processes for students and faculty. Facilitating Patent Filing

MIT-WPU / Programme / Integrated Business Management (BBA+MBA) Programme Integrated Business Management (BBA+MBA) Earn your BBA and MBA together at MIT WPU Pune. Graduate faster at a highly rated private business university with 3500+ industry hiring partners. Apply now. Programme Overview The Integrated Business Management [BBA+MBA] programme by MIT World Peace University is the integration of undergraduate and postgraduate business management studies. The five-year degree programme is specifically designed to equip students with social, managerial, and entrepreneurial capabilities to effectively handle the requirements of today's global business environment. The curriculum incorporates ten semesters, offering student exchange opportunities, national and international immersion experiences, and internships. Careers in a variety of industries, including sales, marketing, finance, human resources, operations, supply chain management, business analytics, and information systems management, are made possible by Integrated Business Management (BBA+MBA). Students can also focus on particular fields like corporate social responsibility, sports management, hospitality, or healthcare. The programme is open to 12th standard graduates and culminates in an MBA degree upon successful completion. Alternatively, students have the option to exit after four years with a BBA (Hons) degree. Major Tracks Marketing and Branding Financial Accounting Human Resource Management Supply Chain Management International Entrepreneurship Duration & Fees Inte

Programme Overview A five-year Integrated M.Sc.biotechnology programme at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) is specifically designed to foster the next generation of biotechnologists that are well-equipped with theoretical and practical knowledge. The programme is characterised by a hands-on learning process. Through laboratory work, field studies, and research internships, the students develop practical skills and prepare them to be equipped to meet the challenges of the biotechnology industry. Such an approach to learning solidifies theoretical knowledge and equips students with the competencies required for successful careers. The graduates of Integrated M.Sc. Biotechnology programme would come out as rounded professionals equipped to handle real-world biotechnology challenges . The curriculum equips students for both higher studies and for lucrative careers in this sector. With a challenging curriculum, practical training, and research opportunities, this programme represents an ideal choice for ambitious biotechnologists who wish to take their passion for biotechnology to meaningful ends. entries.title: Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership
entries.excerpt: Learn how connected thinking across disciplines is shaping innovation, leadership, and future-ready problem solving.
entries.text: Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership · WPU Goa Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership Learn how connected thinking across disciplines is shaping innovation, leadership, and future-ready problem solving. WPU Goa / News / Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership News and ideas Integration Is the New Intelligence Thought Leadership Learn how connected thinking across disciplines is shaping innovation, leadership, and future-ready problem solving. The future belongs to those who can integrate knowledge across boundaries. The future will reward those who can connect disciplines, technologies, cultures, and ideas. The most significant opportunities of the coming decades will emerge not within narrow silos, but at the intersections of engineering and design, business and technology, psychology and data, law and innovation, health and life sciences, sustainability and enterprise. At WPU Goa, we are building a university for this new era. We are intentionally designing a university where technology, creativity, human insight, systems thinking, and real-world impact converge to solve the most complex challenges of our time. Our academic vision is rooted in transdisciplinary learning, global relevance, applied research, entrepreneurial thinking, and human-centred innovation. Students will be educated not only to succeed in existing careers, but to create new pathways in fields that are rapidly evolving. Over the coming years, WPU Goa will continue to develop a future-facing portfolio of programmes across high-growth sectors. Each domain is designed to combine academic rigour with practical relevance, enabling graduates to become leaders, builders, innovators and changemakers in a connected world. Explore our initiatives, resources, and support for patents, copyrights, and design registrations MIT-WPU / Research / Intellectual Property Rights Research Intellectual Property Rights Learn about MIT-WPU's commitment to innovation and intellectual property rights (IPR). Explore our initiatives, resources, and support for patents, copyrights, and design registrations IPR Cell About The IPR Cell at MIT World Peace University is committed to fostering a culture of innovation, creativity, and legal awareness regarding intellectual property among students, researchers, and faculty members. The Cell facilitates the protection of intellectual creations and aims to support the commercialization and technology transfer of innovations done by faculty, students, and alumni through patents, copyrights, trademarks, and other forms of IP. Vision To become a leading centre for promoting, protecting, and commercializing intellectual property, contributing to academic excellence and societal development. Mission To educate and create awareness about IPR among all stakeholders. To guide and assist inventors in the process of IP protection. To encourage innovation and facilitate patent filing and technology transfer. To foster collaboration with industry and government bodies in IPR-related activities. Objectives Promote IP awareness through seminars, workshops, and training programs. Identify and nurture innovative ideas from students and faculty. Provide assistance in patent drafting, filing, and prosecution. Facilitate partnerships with IP professionals and patent attorneys. Guide researchers through IP policy and regulatory frame

entries.title: International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology of Nanomaterials
entries.excerpt: International conference at MIT‑WPU bridges traditional knowledge and advanced nanomaterials research for future-ready innovations.
entries.text: Nanomaterials Conference: Ancient to Advanced Tech @ MIT‑WPU International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology of Nanomaterials International conference at MIT‑WPU bridges traditional knowledge and advanced nanomaterials research for future-ready innovations. MIT-WPU / Events / International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology of Nanomaterials Events International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology of Nanomaterials International conference at MIT‑WPU bridges traditional knowledge and advanced nanomaterials research for future-ready innovations. Events Happenings International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology of Nanomaterials Events ICON3 In an insightful gathering of scholars and scientists, the International Conference on Bridging Ancient and Advanced Technology delved into the fascinating connection between ancient practices and modern technological innovations. A key focus of the conference was exploring how traditional methods described in ancient texts, such as the Vedas, can be applied to create advanced materials like nanomaterials and oxides for various applications. Several distinguished speakers highlighted the processes outlined in the Vedas, particularly those related to the making of metals and ceramics. These ancient techniques, revered for their precision and effectiveness, are now being reconsidered for modern applications in nanotechnology. A prominent discussion centered around Raja Bhoj, who was said to have possessed an aircraft fueled by mercury, indicating the advanced nature of ancient technologies. ICON5 At MIT-WPU, researchers are deeply engaged in studying nanomaterials, drawing comparisons with ancient materials and analyzing their properties. Dr. Bharat Kale, a ke

Organising Committee Advisory Committee Technical Program Committee Program Schedule Call for Papers Submission Guidelines Important Dates Call for Workshops / Tutorials Proposals are invited from groups or individuals interested to host workshops and tutorials on conference themes and relevant fields during the pre/conference period. The purpose of workshops and tutorials is to provide an opportunity for participants from academia, industry, government and other related parties to present and discuss novel ideas on current and emerging topics relevant to the conference theme. We encourage workshops that bring together people working on new and developing research topics that favour highly interactive discussions. Tutorial proposals are encouraged that go in-depth into topics of interest to the fields of conference. Papers are to be submitted in both, MS Word and PDF formats. entries.excerpt: See how MIT-WPU celebrated the International Day of Yoga. Read highlights from our campus-wide yoga programme promoting wellness, peace, and holistic health.
entries.text: International Day of Yoga Programme at MIT-WPU | Event Highlights International Day of Yoga Programme See how MIT-WPU celebrated the International Day of Yoga. Read highlights from our campus-wide yoga programme promoting wellness, peace, and holistic health. MIT-WPU / Events / International Day of Yoga Programme Events International Day of Yoga Programme See how MIT-WPU celebrated the International Day of Yoga. Read highlights from our campus-wide yoga programme promoting wellness, peace, and holistic health. Events International Day of Yoga Programme Happenings Events International-Yoga-Day-img The 10th International Yoga Day was celebrated with great enthusiasm and reverence at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), marking a landmark event in the university's calendar. This year's theme, "Yoga for Women Empowerment," resonated profoundly with the attendees, highlighting the role of yoga in fostering strength, resilience, and inner peace among women. International-Yoga-Day-img Event Highlights The celebration commenced with the arrival of the Special Guest and yoga instructor for the session, Yogacharya Shri. Maruti Padekar Guruji. Accompanied by his skilled team of demonstrators, Guruji led the participants through a series of invigorating yoga exercises. This session was meticulously designed to ensure that everyone experienced the true essence of yoga practice, showcasing its transformative potential for both mind and body. International-Yoga-Day-img Special Sessions International-Yoga-Day-img Following the yoga session, the event featured an inspiring address by the Guest of Honour, Yogacharya Shri. Manoj Patwardhan Ji, Founder of “Manovardhan Yog Sanstha.” His speech emphasized the profound benefits of yoga in achieving mental and physical well-being. Shri. Patwa

Teaching across boundaries: Transdisciplinary Learning and Teaching Organised by WPU GŌA In collaboration with Athena Institute, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam (The Netherlands) 24 - 26 August 2026 Panjim, Goa, India Preparing educators for complexity and transformative learning Higher education is increasingly called upon to prepare graduates to address complex societal challenges such as climate change, public health, technological transformation, sustainable development, and social inequality. These challenges rarely fit neatly within the boundaries of a single discipline. Instead, they require collaboration across academic fields and meaningful engagement with stakeholders beyond the university. Transdisciplinary Learning and Teaching (TDL) equips educators with the knowledge, skills, and pedagogical approaches needed to create learning environments where students integrate diverse perspectives, collaborate across disciplines, and work on real-world challenges. This three-day international Faculty Development Programme introduces participants to the foundations of transdisciplinary learning and provides practical methods for integrating these approaches into teaching, curriculum design, research supervision, and community-engaged education. Happenings International Immersion Program-Thailand Happenings International Immersion Program Thailand-1 News International Immersion Program Thailand-2 MIT-WPU paved the way for 15 students from the School of Business and School of Engineering & Technology to participate in the International Immersion Program in Thailand. In this 2-week program, students immersed themselves in intellectually stimulating & fun-filled learning experiences. They explored trending technologies like Drone technology, GIS Mapping, and business topics like Fintech and Digital Marketing to name a few. A visit to Bangkok, Safari Tour, and a visit to Science Museum enabled them to understand the rich culture of Thailand as well. Related public links Happenings School of Business

entries.title: International Immersion Programme in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London (UK)
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU partners with Bloomsbury Institute, London, for a unique International Immersion Programme, offering students global exposure.
entries.text: Int'l Immersion Programme with Bloomsbury London : Events At MIT-WPU International Immersion Programme in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London (UK) MIT-WPU partners with Bloomsbury Institute, London, for a unique International Immersion Programme, offering students global exposure. MIT-WPU / Events / International Immersion Programme in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London (UK) Events International Immersion Programme in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London (UK) MIT-WPU partners with Bloomsbury Institute, London, for a unique International Immersion Programme, offering students global exposure. Events Happenings Events Students of the School of Law at MIT World Peace University, in collaboration with the Department of Internationalization and in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London, successfully concluded its 14-day International Academic Immersion Programme in London, UK, from 9th to 20th June 2025. This certified immersion programme was meticulously designed to offer students with hands-on exposure to the UK legal system and its professional environment. The curriculum seamlessly blended formal legal instruction, experiential training, and cultural exploration. Participants engaged in guided visits to leading legal institutions, attended sessions led by seasoned UK legal professionals, and took part in structured learning activities such as legal simulations and networking events. Informal interactions with legal practitioners from the Indian diaspora further enriched their professional and cultural perspectives. Day-by-Day Programme Highlights Day 1 – Orientation & Escape Hunt (9 June 2025) International Immersion Programme in association with Bloomsbury Institute, London (UK) Welcome at Bloomsbury Institute by coordinators John, G

Studying abroad will allow students to experience different cultures and perspectives that includes meeting new people. Students get exposure to step out of their comfort zone and challenge unfamiliar situations. MIT-WPU’s Student Exchange Programmes enable students to study abroad at one of our international partner institutions/universities. Student Exchange Programme MIT-WPU ensures to provide enriching experience and global exposure to students for at least two weeks without compromising on their academics. Criteria for continued Scholarship Year on Year: Academic Performance: To retain the 100% scholarship, students must maintain a minimum CGPA of 6, have no live backlogs Attendance: The student must maintain a minimum attendance of 80% per semester after catering for all exigencies including illness, sports, and any other requirements. Discipline: The student must maintain good conduct, behavior, and discipline as per the Code of Conduct of the University. entries.title: International Yoga Day: Embodying Balance, Wellness, and Inner Peace
entries.excerpt: MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) commemorated International Yoga Day 2025 with an inspiring event that brought the university community together in the spirit of unity and holistic well-being.
entries.text: International Yoga Day : MIT-WPU Events International Yoga Day: Embodying Balance, Wellness, and Inner Peace MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) commemorated International Yoga Day 2025 with an inspiring event that brought the university community together in the spirit of unity and holistic well-being. MIT-WPU / Events / International Yoga Day: Embodying Balance, Wellness, and Inner Peace Events International Yoga Day: Embodying Balance, Wellness, and Inner Peace MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) commemorated International Yoga Day 2025 with an inspiring event that brought the university community together in the spirit of unity and holistic well-being. Events International Yoga Day: Embodying Balance, Wellness, and Inner Peace Happenings International-Yoga-Day1 Events International-Yoga-Day2 MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) commemorated International Yoga Day 2025 with an inspiring event that brought the university community together in the spirit of unity and holistic well-being. The event was graced by Acharya Shri Shivam, spiritual guide, motivational speaker, and Dean at VVSS Viswasanthi Foundation, who led a transformative guided meditation that resonated deeply with all attendees. International-Yoga-Day3 The event was graced by Rev. Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad, Founder-President of MIT-WPU, whose visionary leadership continues to guide MIT-WPU’s commitment to holistic education and the advancement of peace through learning. International-Yoga-Day4 Faculty, and staff participated enthusiastically in yoga asanas, pranayama, and mindful reflection, embracing yoga as a way of life that nurtures body, mind, and soul. The event underscored the importance of integrating yoga into daily routines to promote physical health, mental clarity, and spiritual harmony

entries.title: Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU School of Economics and Commerce
entries.excerpt: MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) is proud to unveil the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy, a unique and pioneering initiative dedicated to celebrating the evolution and significance of accountancy through the ages.
entries.text: Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU | News Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU School of Economics and Commerce MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) is proud to unveil the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy, a unique and pioneering initiative dedicated to celebrating the evolution and significance of accountancy through the ages. MIT-WPU / News / Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU School of Economics and Commerce News Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU School of Economics and Commerce MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) is proud to unveil the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy, a unique and pioneering initiative dedicated to celebrating the evolution and significance of accountancy through the ages. 361 words 2 min read Happenings Happenings News Introducing the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy at MIT-WPU School of Economics and Commerce MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) is proud to unveil the Kautilya Museum of Accountancy, a unique and pioneering initiative dedicated to celebrating the evolution and significance of accountancy through the ages. This first-of-its-kind museum offers visitors a fascinating journey into the history, culture, and impact of accounting practices, making it a must-visit destination for scholars, students, and enthusiasts alike. A Journey Through Time The Kautilya Museum of Accountancy takes visitors on a captivating exploration of the accounting world, from its ancient origins to its modern applications. Rare artefacts such as historical khata-bahis (traditional Indian ledgers), priceless manuscripts, and exclusive photographs from the British Museum showcase the evolution of financial recording systems over centuries. entries.title: IQAC
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU IQAC: Fostering academic excellence through quality initiatives, ensuring student success, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Internal Quality Assurance Cell: Promoting Academic Excellence IQAC MIT-WPU IQAC: Fostering academic excellence through quality initiatives, ensuring student success, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement. MIT-WPU / About Us / IQAC About Us IQAC MIT-WPU IQAC: Fostering academic excellence through quality initiatives, ensuring student success, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement. The Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) has been established in alignment with the directives of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), Bangalore. As a vital component of our university, IQAC serves as a dedicated body committed to the continuous enhancement and sustenance of academic quality. Having evolved from its proposal stage, IQAC has become an integral part of our institution's fabric, working diligently to realize our goals of academic excellence and improvement. Its primary role is to develop a comprehensive framework for deliberate and consistent enhancements across all university aspects, drawing inspiration from peer committee recommendations. IQAC operates as a dynamic, participative system, striving to internalize and institutionalize quality enhancement initiatives, much like the "Quality Circles" in industries. From the Desk of the Director Dr. Rohini Bhalerao-Pannajkar Dear Faculty, Staff and Students, It is a pleasure for me to welcome you and inform you that your choice of seeking Higher Education in India and at MIT-WPU will prove to bring a positive transformation in your life. At IQAC, our commitment is to foster a culture of continuous quality enhancement, across all academic and administrative domains. As we strive for excellence, our focus remains on accreditation readiness,

A confident and bubbly fashionista, Janhavi Vinod Shrungare proves that all you need to follow your dreams is the courage to take that first step. Now inclined to foray into acting, MIT-WPU wishes her all the best in her future journey towards achieving even greater milestones! Janhavi is one of the many students at MIT-WPU who inspire us to take a chance and live our lives to the fullest. Immerse yourself in a rich collection of books spanning Engineering, Humanities, Science, Health Sciences, Law, And Business. Life@MIT-WPU Knowledge Resource Centre (KRC) The Knowledge Resource Centre (KRC) is a separate building, named ‘Agastya’, which has the area of 3056 sq.m (Basement + 6 Floors). The library supports teaching, learning, research and the vision of the university. The library is operational for twelve hours and this facility is extended during the exam period. It has an enormous collection of resources to support all programs under the university. It has a dedicated reading space, discussion rooms, and faculty space, all together with a total seating capacity of more than 2000. The library practices open access system which facilitates users to visit, browse the collection, read, refer and borrow the books. KRC follows Dewey decimal classification system and books are arranged according to subject for easy access. The library housekeeping operations are carried out with the help of open source Integrated Library Management Software ‘KOHA’. The library is equipped with Wi-Fi facility and CCTV for monitoring. The OPAC facility to browse the catalogue is provided on each floor. The library has an automated circulation system for tra

The Academic Council at MIT World Peace University holds the paramount authority in all academic matters and is entrusted with the vital responsibility of overseeing, regulating, and upholding the highest standards in teaching, research, and evaluation throughout the university. The council is also vested with the pivotal duty of formulating and guiding academic policies aimed at enhancing and sustaining the quality of teaching, research, extension activities, collaborative programmes, and the equitable assessment of faculty workloads.
