entries.title: Leadership That Delivers Conclave with BNI Goa Region
entries.excerpt: Read key insights from a one-day conclave focused on effective leadership, collaboration, and organizational success.
entries.text: Leadership That Delivers Conclave with BNI Goa Region · WPU Goa Leadership That Delivers Conclave with BNI Goa Region Read key insights from a one-day conclave focused on effective leadership, collaboration, and organizational success. WPU Goa / Events / Leadership That Delivers Conclave with BNI Goa Region Event Leadership That Delivers Conclave with BNI Goa Region Read key insights from a one-day conclave focused on effective leadership, collaboration, and organizational success. Role context This record documents a public event, conference or speaker appearance. Participation does not establish employment at WPU Goa. WPU Goa × BNI Goa Region One-Day Leadership Conclave The WPU Goa One-Day Leadership Conclave on “Leadership That Delivers” marked a landmark moment in the institutional journey of WPU Goa campus. Co-organised in partnership with BNI Goa Region, the conclave convened over 250 participants for an immersive evening of world-class leadership dialogue, institutional recognition, and community investment. The gathering brought together industry leaders, corporate professionals, MSME owners, startup founders, entrepreneurs, and academics — united by a shared conviction that leadership has never been more consequential than it is today. Conceptualised under the institutional vision of Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President, WPU Goa, and convened by Dr. Urvashi Makkar, Dean of the Ramcharan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU Pune, the conclave established WPU Goa as a genuine hub of leadership thought and professional community in Goa. “WPU Goa is being built not merely as a place of education, but as an ecosystem that connects leadership, industry, recognition, and transformation. Every event like this conclave is a demonstration — not just a promise — of that visio

entries.title: Learnings from International Immersion Programme - 2023-24, Multimedia University - Malacca Campus, Malaysia
entries.excerpt: Discover the key learnings and student experiences from our 2023-24 International Immersion Programme at Multimedia University in Malaysia.
entries.text: Learnings from our Malaysia Immersion Programme : MIT-WPU Events Learnings from International Immersion Programme - 2023-24, Multimedia University - Malacca Campus, Malaysia Discover the key learnings and student experiences from our 2023-24 International Immersion Programme at Multimedia University in Malaysia. MIT-WPU / Events / Learnings from International Immersion Programme - 2023-24, Multimedia University - Malacca Campus, Malaysia Events Learnings from International Immersion Programme - 2023-24, Multimedia University - Malacca Campus, Malaysia Discover the key learnings and student experiences from our 2023-24 International Immersion Programme at Multimedia University in Malaysia. Events Happenings Events Learnings from International Immersion Programme - 2023-24, Multimedia University - Malacca Campus, Malaysia The International Immersion Programme at Multimedia University's Malacca campus, Malaysia, took place from February 26 to March 3, 2024 (6 nights, 7 days). Organised for students in the Department of Computer Science and Applications, the programme attracted 17 participants (16 students and 1 faculty member) demonstrating a keen interest in the experience. This opportunity enriched their educational experience and contributed to their personal and professional development. Students learned about digital image processing, ethical hacking and penetration testing, and inventive problem-solving through TRIZ at Multimedia University. They also explored the historical city of Melaka, Malaysia. Students spent 3 days in Kuala Lumpur, the capital of Malaysia, exploring the beautiful city, including industry visits, Batu Caves religious site, and various towers. They thoroughly enjoyed the academic trip. Related public links Happenings

entries.title: Life Transformation Centre (LTC)
entries.excerpt: Explore the Life Transformation Centre at MIT-WPU, fostering personal growth, emotional well-being, leadership skills, and holistic development.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Life Transformation Centre | Holistic Student Development Life Transformation Centre (LTC) Explore the Life Transformation Centre at MIT-WPU, fostering personal growth, emotional well-being, leadership skills, and holistic development. MIT-WPU / Life Transformation Centre (LTC) Life Transformation Centre Life Transformation Centre (LTC) Explore the Life Transformation Centre at MIT-WPU, fostering personal growth, emotional well-being, leadership skills, and holistic development. Life Transformation Centre (LTC) The Life Transformation Centre (LTC) is dedicated to nurturing purpose-driven, resilient, and socially conscious individuals. Inspired by the philosophy of Vijigīṣū Jīvanavādī Yuvān (विजिगीषु जीवनवादी युवा), LTC promotes holistic development through five core pillars: Agriculture and Nature, Physical Fitness and Sports, Patriotism and Nation Building, Teamwork and Leadership, and Spirituality. SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Guided by the principle of "Connecting the Soul to the Soil," LTC blends experiential learning with Bharatiya values, encouraging students to develop self-awareness, leadership, discipline, and a deeper sense of purpose. Through immersive experiences, students are empowered to become compassionate leaders and responsible contributors to society. Events & News SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Related public links Jun 01, 2026 SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit

Programme Overview The LLB (Bachelor of Laws) is a three-year programme that provides students with a thorough understanding of the legal system, including business laws, intellectual property rights, alternative dispute resolution, and the Indian Constitution. Students gain insight into various acts and laws through seminars with academicians, industry experts, and practicing lawyers. entries.title: LL.M.
entries.excerpt: Advance your legal career with LLM at MIT WPU Pune. BCI approved 1 year master of laws with specialisation options and strong placement record. Apply now
entries.text: LLM Master of Laws in Pune India | BCI Approved | MIT WPU LL.M. Advance your legal career with LLM at MIT WPU Pune. BCI approved 1 year master of laws with specialisation options and strong placement record. Apply now MIT-WPU / Programme / LL.M. Programme LL.M. Advance your legal career with LLM at MIT WPU Pune. BCI approved 1 year master of laws with specialisation options and strong placement record. Apply now Programme Overview The LLM programme at the School of Law aims to develop strong legal research skills among the students and the ability to apply key concepts of substantive and procedural law to specific situations. Through a thorough study of society, laws, and judicial procedures, students learn to analyse and solve legal problems through logical reasoning. The programme also teaches the theories and practices of legislative enactment and interpretation, and trains students to apply these skills in simulated court sessions. Students have the option to specialise in one of four areas: constitutional law, criminal law, international law, or business law. Duration & Fees Duration 1 Year Last Date to Apply : 7 August 2026 Fee Per Year ₹ 1,50,000 Download Brochure Download Course Structure Terms & Conditions Apply: All Scholarships are awarded on a First Come First Serve basis. All Scholarships are awarded as fee adjustments. To continue the scholarship for the entire duration of the programme, a minimum level of the academic score has to be maintained at an 8 CGPA across all semesters, attendance is to be maintained at a minimum of 80 percent, with no live backlogs in any subject/programme and no semester break, and there should be no disciplinary action against the student. entries.excerpt: Master’s in Strategic Product Design at MIT-WPU Pune is a full-time two-year degree programme developed to bridge the gap between businesses and design
entries.text: M.Des. Strategic Product Design From MIT-WPU | Syllabus, Fees & Admission M.Des. Strategic Product Design Programme M.Des. Strategic Product Design Master’s in Strategic Product Design at MIT-WPU Pune is a full-time two-year degree programme developed to bridge the gap between businesses and design Programme Overview Master’s in Strategic Product Design at MIT-WPU Pune is a full-time two-year degree programme developed to bridge the gap between businesses and design. This programme addresses the pressing need for designers who are technologically adept, socially conscious, empathetic, market-savvy, globally minded, and capable of providing design leadership. The programme is meant to develop skills, knowledge and aptitude among students to become creative problem solvers who can bring about innovation in the hardware and software industry, be they product or service-based. The curriculum encompasses everything from user experience and brand management to platform-based technologies, futures foresight, service design, product visualisation, scenarios planning and leadership in new product development. Major Tracks Design Research and User Experience (UX) Design. Brand Management and Strategy. Service Design and Product Management. Corporate Development and Organisational Strategy. Social Impact and Corporate Equity. Strategic Design Interventions. Duration & Fees Duration 2 Years Last Date to Apply : 7 August 2026 Fee Per Year ₹ 4,25,000 Download Brochure Terms & Conditions Apply: All Scholarships are awarded on a First Come First Serve basis. Programme Overview The MIT-WPU’s M.Pharm in Pharmaceutical Chemistry programme is for students who want a profound understanding of the pharmaceutical industry and want to pursue a career in drug design and discovery research and development. The curriculum includes the most recent methods and advancements in the area and is designed with a focus for project-based learning. Graduates of this programme have seen considerable success, landing jobs with excellent packages at well-known firms like Sun Pharma, Lupin, Dr. Reddy's Lab, Emcure, Sanofi, Indico, FDC, Unichem Laboratories, Cadila Pharmaceuticals, and Micro Labs. Students pursuing this programme are prepared for significant roles in the pharmaceutical business, helping to advance the sector. Economics: A Strong Choice for Students Who Want Depth, Direction, and Real-World Relevance Explore why M.Sc Economics is a smart choice for students seeking depth, direction, and real-world relevance. Discover career paths and scope. 1,599 words 7 min read Economics is more than a theoretical text. Economics starts out as an issue for many students. It is much more than that at the postgraduate level. It becomes a lens for how the world works, how decisions are made, markets move, policy shapes everyday life, and data is leveraged to make sense of change. That is the hallmark of a good M.Sc. Economics programme and why it is so important. It has to be a little beyond concept teaching. This will help students comprehend and then analyse, to apply ideas to the world around them. In fact, that larger purpose is what has shaped the M.Sc. Economics programme at MIT-WPU, Pune. Combining academic rigour with applied learning, technical tools and a future-oriented perspective, it provides students with a postgraduate experience that is genuinely robust and relevant. A strong foundation still matters The fundamental principles are the starting point for any important economic expedition. Those basics need to be taken seriously at the postgraduate level. The programme helps students to stay up-to-date with the rapidly changing media landscape. This programme provides a broad understanding of the various social, political, ethical, and economic issues that influence the creation of news and content on various platforms, such as social media, newspapers, and online news channels. This will help students to develop a critical understanding of the media industry and the factors that shape it. The programme also offers a range of modules on professional photography, corporate communications, brand management, new media, graphic design, film studies, and video editing. These modules help students to develop the skills and knowledge that are necessary to succeed in a variety of media-related careers. In addition to focusing on professional development, the programme also emphasises the holistic development of students through personality development and peace studies modules. entries.title: Malhar Sameer Atre
entries.excerpt: On behalf of MIT-WPU, we extend our heartiest congratulations to Malhar Sameer Atre, a TY BBA student and our national champion swimmer, for his exceptional performance and remarkable achievements!
entries.text: Malhar Sameer Atre : MIT-WPU Achivements Malhar Sameer Atre On behalf of MIT-WPU, we extend our heartiest congratulations to Malhar Sameer Atre, a TY BBA student and our national champion swimmer, for his exceptional performance and remarkable achievements! MIT-WPU / Achievements / Malhar Sameer Atre Achievements Malhar Sameer Atre On behalf of MIT-WPU, we extend our heartiest congratulations to Malhar Sameer Atre, a TY BBA student and our national champion swimmer, for his exceptional performance and remarkable achievements! Achievement malhar-atre Achievements atre On behalf of MIT-WPU, we extend our heartiest congratulations to Malhar Sameer Atre, a TY BBA student and our national champion swimmer, for his exceptional performance and remarkable achievements! Malhar's dominance at the VSM State-Level Tournament, where he secured 4 Gold and 3 Silver medals in swimming, along with a Gold in Water Polo, and the Overall Individual Championship & Best Player Award, is truly inspiring. His winning streak continued at Flame University, where he added 2 Gold and 1 Bronze medal in swimming to his impressive record. atre-award These outstanding achievements are a testament to Malhar's dedication, hard work, and talent. We are incredibly proud of his accomplishments and the pride he brings to MIT-WPU. swimming Malhar has expressed his sincere gratitude to our esteemed founder, Respected Dr. Vishwanath Karad Sir, Executive President Rahul Karad Sir, Pro VC Dr. Milind Pande Sir, and the entire MIT-WPU faculty for their unwavering support. We are delighted to have played a part in his journey and wish him continued success in all his future endeavours. Related public links MIT-WPU

entries.text: Manjiri Dhawade : Faculty At MIT-WPU Manjiri Dhawade Manjiri Dhawade currently serves as a Research Associate at MIT World Peace University, bringing a strong academic foundation with a postgraduate degree in Public Administration. Her expertise and research interests lie in Public Policy and Governance, with a special focus on Public Service, Social Welfare, Rural Development, Environment, and Sustainability. MIT-WPU / Faculty / Manjiri Dhawade Faculty Manjiri Dhawade Manjiri Dhawade currently serves as a Research Associate at MIT World Peace University, bringing a strong academic foundation with a postgraduate degree in Public Administration. Her expertise and research interests lie in Public Policy and Governance, with a special focus on Public Service, Social Welfare, Rural Development, Environment, and Sustainability. Faculty Faculty Research Associate Bio Manjiri Dhawade is associated with the MIT School of Government, where she works in the areas of public policy and governance. Her work focuses on research-driven policy analysis and the development of academic initiatives that bridge theory with real-world policy challenges, particularly in the context of social equity and inclusion. She has been actively engaged in designing and facilitating academic programmes, workshops, and policy discussions on issues such as human rights and labour, climate change, and democratic governance. Her approach emphasizes critical thinking, evidence-based analysis, and meaningful engagement with contemporary public policy debates, enabling students to better understand complex governance structures and societal challenges. Programme Overview The MBA in Healthcare Management is a dynamic two-year degree programme designed to equip students with the specialised skills and knowledge needed to thrive in the rapidly evolving healthcare sector. As the field of healthcare continues to expand at an unprecedented pace, this programme prepares candidates to navigate and excel in various domains beyond traditional hospital management. These include healthcare insurance, healthcare IT, pharmaceutical management, clinical research, home healthcare, consultancies, public health, medical devices and equipment, diagnostic services, and more. With a curriculum that blends management principles with healthcare-specific expertise, this programme offers a comprehensive education that opens doors to diverse career opportunities in one of the most critical and fast-growing industries globally. Incorporating the methodologies of computer science and engineering, the programme illustrates real-world applications while fostering a research mindset. entries.text: MIT-WPU : Micro forming Research Micro forming Research Under the guidance of Dr. Kakandikar Ganesh, the Micro forming Research COE was established. The Center of Excellence is generously gifted with equipment from Aeronautics Research and Development Board, DRDO, New Delhi. The centre houses formality testing tools and equipment to facilitate micro structural study and mechanical testing analysis for the researchers. It also provides adequate heat treatment facilities for enhancement in micro structures. MIT-WPU / Micro forming Research Micro forming Research Under the guidance of Dr. Kakandikar Ganesh, the Micro forming Research COE was established. The Center of Excellence is generously gifted with equipment from Aeronautics Research and Development Board, DRDO, New Delhi. The centre houses formality testing tools and equipment to facilitate micro structural study and mechanical testing analysis for the researchers. It also provides adequate heat treatment facilities for enhancement in micro structures. Micro forming Research Under the guidance of Dr. Kakandikar Ganesh, the Micro forming Research COE was established. The Center of Excellence is generously gifted with equipment from Aeronautics Research and Development Board, DRDO, New Delhi. The centre houses formality testing tools and equipment to facilitate micro structural study and mechanical testing analysis for the researchers. It also provides adequate heat treatment facilities for enhancement in micro structures. Established in the year 2021, this centre of excellence has swiftly garnered prominence as an intellectual crucible for erudite researchers, offering an environment of heightened discernment to propel pioneering inquiries across a spectrum of academic frontiers.

It provides courses meant to give students the public sector's required leadership, policy, and managerial tools. The school stresses a strong intellectual basis together with useful, real-world application. About The MIT School of Government (MIT-SOG) was created by Shri. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, with the aim of producing ethical, passionate, and dedicated political leaders for the country from diverse backgrounds. Good political leadership from Panchayat to Parliament can only ensure good governance and vibrant democracy in a country with this thought. Established in 2005, MIT-SOG is the first institution of its kind to offer a two-year Master's Programme in Political Leadership & Government (MPG). The Programme is designed to provide hands-on learning experiences for its students, with the first year consisting of classroom lectures delivered by core faculty members, visiting professors, and eminent personalities from the fields of politics, social work, bureaucracy, judiciary, media, and business. Acceleracers competes in various national and international events. A group of 20 students from various engineering streams who do continual research and development in the field of automobile industry, we design and build a formula style race car. Each of our team members drive for something greater, that’s why we are Team Acceleracers. Team Acceleracers aims to provide a platform to students, where they can design and fabricate formula style car to participate in events like SUPRA and Formula Student India (FSI) and others. We also work towards cultivating innovative approach among the students by designing and manufacturing innovative race cars. Our workshop is home to innovation of various things. In our workshop, all the team members year after year, design and manufacture their formula type race car. Each time trying to make the fastest, the smartest and the best car. entries.text: MIT-WPU and SRH University, Germany | MIT-WPU News MIT-WPU and SRH University, Germany, Establish Strategic Academic Partnership MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) has taken a significant step forward in fostering international academic cooperation by formalising a partnership with SRH University, Germany. This strategic alliance was solidified through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed by Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President of MIT-WPU MIT-WPU / News / MIT-WPU and SRH University, Germany, Establish Strategic Academic Partnership News MIT-WPU and SRH University, Germany, Establish Strategic Academic Partnership MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) has taken a significant step forward in fostering international academic cooperation by formalising a partnership with SRH University, Germany. This strategic alliance was solidified through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed by Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President of MIT-WPU Happenings Happenings News MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) has taken a significant step forward in fostering international academic cooperation by formalising a partnership with SRH University, Germany. This strategic alliance was solidified through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed by Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President of MIT-WPU, and Dr. Thorsten Bagschik, Managing Director of SRH University. The delegation also included Prof. Dr. Gerd Moeckel, Vice Academic Director of the School of Technology & Architecture at SRH University. MIT-WPU at BSE: A Historic Celebration of Financial Awareness To commemorate this milestone, more than 1,000 students from the School of Business came together with BSE and Krisha Foundation to form a record-breaking Human Chain, spreading crucial awareness about preventing scams and financial frauds. This powerful initiative was telecast live from the BSE International Convention Hall in the presence of the Executive Director of SEBI, senior BSE officials, and leading figures from India’s financial community. This student-led campaign will be featured in the International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO) report, to be circulated across 182 countries — a proud milestone for the University. These strategic partnerships will enable student and faculty exchanges, joint research, immersive academic programmes, and cross-border learning opportunities, aligned with our vision of preparing globally competent, peace-driven leaders. Events Happenings MIT-WPU Celebrates World Environment Day with Renowned Climate Scientist Dr. Dev Niyogi Events In line with the global theme of "Restore Our Earth," MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) hosted a momentous celebration of World Environment Day on Tuesday, June 4, 2024. The event, titled "Our Land - Our Future," brought together the university community, environmental leaders, and a renowned climate scientist to highlight the critical need for environmental restoration efforts. The celebration commenced with the arrival of the esteemed guests, including the Chief Guest, Dr. Dev Niyogi, a prominent climate scientist and meteorologist from The University of Texas at Austin. Dr. Niyogi holds the William Stamps Farish Chair in the Department of Earth & Planetary Sciences and is a leading researcher in urban climate, extreme weather events, and sustainable development. DSC_9554 The occasion served as a double celebration for MIT-WPU. The university was recognised for its outstanding commitment to environmental action by Earthday Network, receiving the prestigious Star Campus Awards 2022-23 in the Energy Management

Student Clubs MIT-WPU Cultural Club The MIT-WPU Cultural Club, also known as the Theatre Club, has been an integral part of MIT World Peace University since 1983. Our goal is to nurture creativity, promote artistic expression, and foster a deep appreciation for the performing arts among our students. We offer a supportive and inclusive environment for budding actors, directors, playwrights, and all theatre enthusiasts. Through workshops, competitions, and events, we aim to help students understand the power of storytelling and theatre. We actively participate in state-level and national-level competitions and festivals, celebrating India's rich cultural heritage through theatre. These achievements reflect Team RoboVin’s dedication to innovation, collaboration, and real-world impact—championing MIT-WPU Pune’s leadership in defense technology and robotics for a safer, smarter future.

entries.title: MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Historic Achievement in ISRO's PSLV-C60 Launch
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU celebrates a proud moment as its contribution features in ISRO’s PSLV-C60 mission—highlighting innovation, research, and national progress.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Events At MIT-WPU MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Historic Achievement in ISRO's PSLV-C60 Launch MIT-WPU celebrates a proud moment as its contribution features in ISRO’s PSLV-C60 mission—highlighting innovation, research, and national progress. MIT-WPU / Events / MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Historic Achievement in ISRO's PSLV-C60 Launch Events MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Historic Achievement in ISRO's PSLV-C60 Launch MIT-WPU celebrates a proud moment as its contribution features in ISRO’s PSLV-C60 mission—highlighting innovation, research, and national progress. Events Happenings Events MIT-WPU Reaches for the Stars: Historic Achievement in ISRO's PSLV-C60 Launch In a remarkable demonstration of student innovation and scientific excellence, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune has etched its name in India's space history through successful participation in ISRO's PSLV-C60 launch that happened on 30th December 2024. We are proud to be one among the Top 4 Institutes across the country to have played a key role in this PSLV launch. The Space Technology Research Group (STeRG) of MIT-WPU has developed a payload under the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering (DoEEE) and the Center of Excellence in Space Technology. This groundbreaking POEM-4 payload, designated as STeRG-P1.0, aboard PSLV C-60, was successfully launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC)-SHAR in Sriharikota. This achievement marked a significant milestone in the institution's journey toward space exploration. The STeRG team's innovative payload, weighing just 0.380 kg and measuring 100 x 100 x 25 mm, has been indigenously designed to test the performance of MEMS-based IMU sensors and ARM-based microcontrollers for attitude and heading determinat

entries.title: MIT-WPU Researchers Make Significant Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery
entries.excerpt: A team of researchers from MIT-WPU identified a novel damselfly species in the ecologically rich Western Ghats region of Kerala.
entries.text: Researchers Make Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery : MIT-WPU Researchers MIT-WPU Researchers Make Significant Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery A team of researchers from MIT-WPU identified a novel damselfly species in the ecologically rich Western Ghats region of Kerala. MIT-WPU / Researcher / MIT-WPU Researchers Make Significant Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery Researcher MIT-WPU Researchers Make Significant Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery A team of researchers from MIT-WPU identified a novel damselfly species in the ecologically rich Western Ghats region of Kerala. Researcher Research MIT-WPU Researchers Make Significant Contribution to Biodiversity Discovery Researcher A team of researchers from MIT-WPU identified a novel damselfly species in the ecologically rich Western Ghats region of Kerala.This groundbreaking discovery, emerging from a collaboration between MIT-WPU and Christ College, Kerala, has augmented India's already remarkable biodiversity. damselfy Dr. Pankaj Koparde and Arajush Payra from MIT-WPU played a crucial role in this important research. The recently identified species, coined by the researchers as the "Agasthyamalai Bambootail" (Melanoneura agasthyamalaica), is a remarkable addition to the damselfly family, recognized for its vivid appearance and genetic distinctions from its nearest relative, the Malabar Bambootail, the only other known species in this genus, which is found in the Coorg-Wayanad region. The new species is part of the bambootail group, distinguished by its long cylindrical abdomen resembling a bamboo stalk—a feature that inspired its common name. damselfy_img The identification of this new species underscores the significance of non-reserve ecosystems in conserving biodiversity. The Agasthyamalai Bambootai

Happenings Happenings MIT-WPU School of Business Ranks 9th Nationally News We are proud to announce that the School of Business at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) has been ranked 9th in India in the prestigious TIMES BBA Education Ranking Survey 2024. This recognition is a testament to our unwavering commitment to excellence in business education. The ranking is based on the rigorous i3RC-BRM model developed by i3RC insights, which evaluates institutions through a comprehensive 360-degree perspective. This proprietary methodology assesses BBA programs across three critical dimensions: resources, effort, and outcomes. The resources dimension evaluates the quality and availability of facilities, faculty, and learning materials. Our inclusion in the top 10 of India reflects the outstanding quality of our academic programs, the dedication of our faculty, and the exceptional achievements of our students. The MoU was formalised during the visit of Dr. Parul Jadhav, Programme Director, Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering (DoEEE), to Michigan State University, where extensive discussions were held with the university's leadership to explore opportunities for long-term institutional collaboration. MIT-WPU / Events / MIT-WPU Successfully Hosts 90th Annual Conference of the Indian Mathematical Society Events MIT-WPU Successfully Hosts 90th Annual Conference of the Indian Mathematical Society In a groundbreaking achievement, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) became the first private university to host the prestigious 90th Annual Conference of the Indian Mathematical Society (IMS). The four-day international conference, held from December 23rd to 26th, 2024. Events Happenings Events In a groundbreaking achievement, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) became the first private university to host the prestigious 90th Annual Conference of the Indian Mathematical Society (IMS). The four-day international conference, held from December 23rd to 26th, 2024, transformed the Department of Mathematics and Statistics into a vibrant hub of mathematical innovation and discourse. MIT-WPU Successfully Hosts 90th Annual Conference of the Indian Mathematical Society Distinguished luminaries graced the event, including Prof. Rajat Moona, Director of IIT Gandhinagar and former Director General of C-DAC, who delivered an illuminating keynote address as the 'Guest of Honour'. His presentation expertly wove together mathematics' crucial role in pioneering fields - from artificial intelligence and space exploration to climate modelling and vaccine development

entries.title: MIT-WPU Team A.V.I.O.N: Soaring High at SAE Aerothon 2025
entries.excerpt: Team A.V.I.O.N from MIT-WPU, Pune, achieved an impressive milestone at the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Aerothon 2025, securing an All India Rank 5 out of 79 elite teams nationwide.
entries.text: Team Avion Soars at SAE Aerothon 2025 | MIT-WPU Achievement MIT-WPU Team A.V.I.O.N: Soaring High at SAE Aerothon 2025 Team A.V.I.O.N from MIT-WPU, Pune, achieved an impressive milestone at the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Aerothon 2025, securing an All India Rank 5 out of 79 elite teams nationwide. MIT-WPU / Achievements / MIT-WPU Team A.V.I.O.N: Soaring High at SAE Aerothon 2025 Achievements MIT-WPU Team A.V.I.O.N: Soaring High at SAE Aerothon 2025 Team A.V.I.O.N from MIT-WPU, Pune, achieved an impressive milestone at the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Aerothon 2025, securing an All India Rank 5 out of 79 elite teams nationwide. Achievement Achievements Team-AVION Team A.V.I.O.N from MIT-WPU, Pune, achieved an impressive milestone at the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Aerothon 2025, securing an All India Rank 5 out of 79 elite teams nationwide. Their distinguished performance highlighted cutting-edge innovation in the highly competitive UAV design and rescue domain. The team's winning pitch during the Tiger’s Cage challenge presented AEROXRESCUE — a sophisticated disaster-response UAV engineered for rapid, high-impact emergency support. This breakthrough design reflects rigorous technical expertise, inventive problem-solving, and strategic foresight. Guided by the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering's mentorship, Team A.V.I.O.N demonstrated outstanding collaboration and innovation. Their achievement underlines MIT-WPU Pune’s commitment to fostering technical excellence and real-world impact in emerging aerospace and rescue technologies.

This annual national-level robotics contest, organised by Doordarshan and hosted by IIT Delhi, brings together top engineering minds across India to showcase innovative skills and strategic thinking. By competing against over 750 students from more than 45 institutions, the MIT-WPU Tech Team has proven its ability to thrive in a highly competitive environment. This success highlights the team's commitment to innovation and excellence in robotics, positioning them as a force to be reckoned with in the field. Team-Piranha-Racing MIT-WPU Tech Team is now more determined than ever to dominate Robocon, aiming for international standards. 2024 was a journey of learning and teamwork, setting the stage for a stronger comeback in 2025. Happenings Happenings News MIT-WPU’s School of Law Honoured with Distinguished Award of Excellence MIT World Peace University’s School of Law has been awarded the esteemed ‘Award of Excellence’ at the International Legal Conference 2025, organised by the Services Export Promotion Council (SEPC) in collaboration with the International Association of Legal Professionals. The event was supported by the Ministry of Law and Justice, as well as the Department of Commerce, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India. The award was received on behalf of the School of Law by Dr. Santosh Aghav, Professor & Dean, and Dr. Pournima Inamdar, Associate Dean of External Relations. The honour was presented by Hon’ble Mr. Justice Manmohan, Judge of the Supreme Court of India, together with Adv. (Dr.) Lalit Bhasin, President of SILF and Founder Chairman-SEPC, and Adv. (Dr.) Anju Rathi Rana, Secretary, Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law & Justice. These collaborations bring together academic, industry, research and institutional partners to generate new… At WPU GŌA, MoUs and strategic partnerships are built to take transdisciplinary learning beyond the classroom, beyond a single discipline and beyond traditional modes of education. These collaborations bring together academic, industry, research and institutional partners to generate new opportunities for hands-on learning, innovation, mentoring, internships and real-world problem-solving. The purpose is to help students see the links between ideas from different subjects, learn from a wide range of experts and use knowledge in ways that make a difference. Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Mr. Aayush Mayank Mishra - faculty At MIT-WPU, Pune Bio Mr. Aayush Mishra specialises in Corporate and Constitutional Law. After completing his post graduation from National Law University Jodhpur in the field of Corporate Law, je joined academics. Before joining MIT WPU he worked as an Assistant Professor of Law in ILS Law College, Pune. Mr. Aayush is pursuing his Ph.D in the domain of Competition Law from Symbiosis International University, Pune. In Addition to this, he has done diploma courses in Intellectual Property Rights and Corporate Law. He has published more than 10 research papers in the reputed International and National Journals indexed in scopus, UGC Care. His are of Interest likes in corporate law, constitutional law, civil laws, property laws. He has participated and presented the paper in various reputed conferences and seminars. He has also published a book on Media Law which is part of the curriculum in one of the government universities. He was invited as a speaker in various universities to address the students in the seminars and workshops.

entries.title: Mr. Abhinay Kamble
entries.excerpt: Abhinay Kamble brings together academic depth and creative practice as a filmmaker and media professional, A UGC-NET Qualified with a strong academic foundation in Psychology (B.A.) and Media and Communication Studies (M.Sc. Savitribai Phule Pune…
entries.text: Mr. Abhinay Kamble Teaching Associate Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Abhinay Kamble brings together academic depth and creative practice as a filmmaker and media professional, A UGC-NET Qualified with a strong academic foundation in Psychology (B.A.) and Media and Communication Studies (M.Sc. Savitribai Phule Pune University). Specializing in video production, his work spans direction, cinematography, and documentary filmmaking. Abhinay Shot & co-directed Bhutacha Mala, inspired by the writings of Annabhau Sathe, and contributed as a camera assistant to Gana Gondhal, a feature film exploring subaltern art and politics. His latest documentary film project, The Bombay Philatelist, traces the journey of a retired PWD worker whose vast collection of rare postage stamps serves as a testament to cultural preservation. A 2024 fellow of PSBT’s Doc_Commune project, Abhinay’s practice lies at the intersection of creative storytelling and academic inquiry. With expertise in multimedia production, photojournalism, and visual design, he works across diverse mediums to engage with themes of culture, identity, and social consciousness.

He has research paper publications in prestigious international journals and conferences such as IEEE and Springer. His areas of interest include IoT, Information and Cyber Security, Social Media Privacy and Security, and AR/VR.

Faculty Faculty Assistant Manager Bio Mr. Amey Chavan - faculty At MIT-WPU, Pune Amey Chavan is associated with the MIT School of Government and NLC Bharat, where he works as an Assistant Manager. He is part of a team that designs and delivers academic and training programs to help legislators better understand parliamentary work, governance, and public discourse. He has been actively involved in planning and conducting policy discussions, forums, and workshops on topics like elections, legislative processes, and international relations. Amey has also contributed to academic writing, including a book chapter titled “Redefining Digital Democracy: Governance in the 21st Century.” He is interested in techno-governance, studying how technology and public policy work together. His work focuses on digital regulation, platform governance, and the responsible use of new technologies. He is especially interested in how governments can balance innovation with accountability in a data-driven world.

He is pursuing his Ph.D. in Law from the Vellore Institute of Technology School of Law (VITSOL), Chennai, Tamil Nadu, and also holds a Post-Graduate Diploma in Cyber Laws from New Law College, Bhartiya Vidyapeeth, Pune. He has completed his LL.M. with Distinction in Constitutional Law from the Faculty of Law, Aligarh Muslim University, and LL.B. from the Faculty of Law, University of Lucknow. An active scholar and researcher, he has authored the book Words of Hate: A Statutory Analysis of Hate Speech in India and has presented numerous research papers at national and international conferences and seminars. His works have been published in reputed national and international journals on diverse areas of law, which extend to well-regarded Scopus-indexed journals and book chapters with internationally reputed publishers. His academic interests primarily lie in Constitutional Law, Comparative Constitutional Law, Human Rights, Civil Liberties, Legal Philosophy, and Legal Reforms, through which he seeks to contribute to the empowerment of marginalized and underprivileged sections of society. Study Abroad Director’s Message Director - International Collaboration Dear Friends, Mr. Pradnesh Desai MIT-WPU stands at the forefront of transforming higher education in India into a global experience. In an era shaped by globalization, our commitment to internationalization is unwavering. Through strategic collaborations with renowned universities worldwide, we've woven a rich tapestry of intercultural and international dimensions into our student learning ecosystem. As the gateway to global learning in India, we've partnered with top-tier institutions, offering our staff, students, and the entire university unique advantages and cohesive growth opportunities. These partnerships serve as conduits for pioneering research and the seamless exchange of knowledge, propelling our academic progress. We've witnessed a surge in students' interest in studying abroad, a testament to the global mindset we cultivate. Each year, international scholars, faculty, and industry experts visit our campus, imparting diverse teaching methodologies and global insights, enriching our students' academic journeys. Lahane is an Associate Professor in the School of Computer Science Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune, with over 17 years of teaching and academic administration experience. He completed his Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering from VIT University, Vellore, and holds B.Tech. and M.Tech. degrees in Computer Science and Information Technology. His contributions include academic coordination, industry collaboration, NAIP training, and conducting major national examinations such as JEE, GATE, MHT-CET, and AIAPGET. entries.title: Mr. Prathamesh Sonawani
entries.excerpt: Prathamesh Sonawani believes teaching is about awakening perception, not just delivering information. In his classes, he encourages students to listen, observe, and think critically about sound, performance, and storytelling as lived experiences, not just…
entries.text: Mr. Prathamesh Sonawani Teaching Associate Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Prathamesh Sonawani believes teaching is about awakening perception, not just delivering information. In his classes, he encourages students to listen, observe, and think critically about sound, performance, and storytelling as lived experiences, not just technical subjects. His motivation comes from seeing learners discover their own artistic voice and confidence. He blends practical industry insight with creative exploration, so students are prepared for both professional realities and meaningful expression. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to contribute to an environment where artistic skill, cultural awareness, and emotional intelligence grow together in thoughtful, socially aware creators.

Faculty Faculty Research Associate Bio Mr. Samruddha Bhirad - faculty At MIT-WPU, Pune Samruddha S. Bhirad is a Research Associate at the MIT School of Government. He holds a bachelor’s degree in Electronics Engineering and a postgraduate degree in Masters in Political Leadership and Government (MPG). Prior to transitioning into politics and governance, he gained around three years of professional experience in the IT industry. Since joining the MIT School of Government in 2023, Samruddha has been actively engaged in research, teaching, content creation, social media management, digital marketing, institutional branding, and ground-level political initiatives. He has extensive on-ground campaign experience across multiple states, including Punjab, Assam, and Maharashtra, contributing to election research and strategic planning. His expertise lies in psephology, election studies, surveys, and data analysis. His research focuses on understanding how data shapes political strategies, the potential of data-driven campaigns, and the role of political research and communication in influencing voter behaviour and electoral outcomes. entries.text: Mrs. Gauri Rahul Dhongade Teaching Associate Department of Computer Science and Applications Gauri Dhongade has completed her M.Sc. in Computer Science and has qualified the State Eligibility Test (SET). She is currently pursuing her Ph.D. in the field of Cybersecurity and Machine Learning. She has a total of 14 years of teaching and industry experience. At present, she is working as a Teaching Associate in the School of Computer Science at MIT World Peace University, Pune. Her research areas include Cybersecurity, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Analysis, and Ransomware Detection. She has published research papers in National and International Conferences and Journals and has also contributed to patents in her domain. She has prior industry experience as a C#.NET Developer, which adds practical exposure to her teaching. She has actively participated in various FDP, Training Programs, workshops, seminars, and conferences at both National and International levels. She is actively involved in academic and administrative responsibilities, contributing to student development, research activities, and departmental coordination, thereby supporting a research-oriented and innovative learning environment.

Faculty Faculty Professor Bio An architect by profession and a teacher by passion, I believe that training young minds to stay rooted in the context, while imparting technical skills to keep up with the world, is the need of the hour. As rightly said by Sir Winston Churchill “We shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us.” I believe shaping young minds who will shape the world of the future is at the same time a challenging yet rewarding task - a responsibility that I accept with commitment and gratitude. Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Ms. Mithila Shelar - faculty At MIT-WPU, Pune Bio Ms. Mithila Shelar is an Assistant Professor at School of law, MIT- World Peace University.She is an alumnus of the University of Pune where she earned her LLM in Constitutional and Administrative law. She is currently enrolled as a Ph.D. scholar at Maharashtra National Law University, Nagpur. Prior to joining academia, Ms. Mithila amassed extensive experience working as a consultant in the field of Indirect taxes and Corporate law. During this period, she advised a diverse range of clients ranging from multinationals, real estate developers and emerging entities. Furthermore, she represented her clients at tax tribunals across India and at the Department of Revenue, Ministry of finance. She possesses a comprehensive understanding of the complex regulatory landscape and policy matters governing indirect taxation. In addition to her professional achievements, Ms. Mithila has served as a visiting lecturer offering her understanding of the Arbitration, Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code and the Labour laws. Additionally she has also presented research papers in various National and International conferences.

Today, she is certainly one of the best storytellers in Indian Cinema Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Ms. Nandita Das Ms. Nandita Das Eminent Actor, Director, Writer, Producer and Social Advocate She calls herself a “hesitant” actor and filmmaker. Her filmography certainly attests to that claim. There were years in which she did not act in a single film, and others in which she acted in five. She made films only when she felt compelled to tell stories. Today, she is certainly one of the best storytellers in Indian Cinema. Ms. Nandita Das has appeared in films like Fire, 1947 Earth, Bawandar, Kamli, and Before The Rains. Her directorial debut Firaaq, premiered at the Toronto Film Festival and travelled to more than 50 festivals, winning more than 20 awards. Her second film as a director was Manto. Ms. Nandita Das has acted in more than 40 feature films in 10 different languages. Her directorial debut in 2008 was Firaaq that brought her many accolades and appreciation, both in India and abroad. Prof. Pandit has led successful initiatives such as achieving water self-sufficiency for Village Naroli through a watershed conservation project and spearheading the 'Know Your Constitution' program, which has educated over 75,000 citizens on the Indian Constitution. Her expertise spans community organization, social justice, women’s political empowerment, and sustainable resource management, with influential publications on these topics, including Dr. B.R. Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Ms. S. Jothimani Ms. S. Jothimani Hon. Member of Parliament, Lok Sabha (INC) | Karur, Tamil Nadu Ms. Jothimani Sennimalai, widely known simply as Jothimani, is an Indian politician, writer, and social worker from Karur, Tamil Nadu. She came into public life at a remarkably early age, driven by a commitment to social justice and grassroots democratic participation. She graduated from Sri G.V.G. Visalakshi College for Women, Udumalaipet, and later earned an MA and an M.Phil. from Annamalai University. Ms. Jothimani's political career began at the panchayat level. She was elected Councillor of the K. Paramarthi Panchayat Union in 1996 and served for two consecutive terms until 2006. During this period, she simultaneously rose through the ranks of the Indian National Congress, serving as Vice President of Tamil Nadu Youth Congress (2006–2008), a member of the Tamil Nadu Censor Board (2006–2009), and General Secretary of the Indian Youth Congress (2009–2012). In 2019, she became the first-ever Congresswoman-parliamentarian elected from the Karur Lok Sabha constituency, defeating veteran AIADMK leader M. Thambidurai. She was re-elected in the 2024 General Elections with an even more resounding mandate, having recorded an attendance rate of 95% in Parliament, well above the national average. entries.text: National Academic Immersion Program (NAIP) : Events At MIT-WPU NAIP Organized by MIT-WPU in Collaboration with RV University The National Academic Immersion Program (NAIP) is a flagship initiative organised by MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, in collaboration with RV University (RVU), Bengaluru. This immersion programme took place from 18th to 27th December 2024 and was joined by 93 students and 5 faculty members. This programme aimed at combining academic orientation, industrial exposure, and cultural enrichment into a holistic learning experience for the students. The carefully curated schedule featured activities such as patent drafting workshops, industrial and cultural visits, and interactive sessions with industry experts, all designed to nurture innovation and interdisciplinary collaboration among students. Events National Academic Immersion Programme at IIT Jodhpur Happenings IIT-Jodhpur Events IIT-Jodhpur As part of the National Academic Immersion Programme (NAIP) 2026, 167 third-year students from the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, participated in a ten-day academic immersion programme at the Technology Innovation and Startup Centre (TISC), IIT Jodhpur, from 16th to 25th June 2026. IIT-Jodhpur Accompanied by four faculty members, the students attended expert-led lectures and interactive sessions on emerging areas such as analog and digital circuit design, IC fabrication, VLSI design flow, hardware verification, signal processing, wireless power transfer for electric vehicles, AI hardware accelerators, and the Internet of Things (IoT). IIT-Jodhpur The programme also included visits to the Jodhpur Centre for Knowledge and Innovation Foundation (JCKIF), Central Research Facility (CRF), and AIoT Laboratories, offering students valuable exposure to advanced research infrastructure, innovation ecosystems, and cutting-edge technological developments. entries.title: National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU students gained hands-on learning, research exposure, and technical insights through the National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa.
entries.text: NIT Goa Academic Immersion Programme | MIT-WPU Pune National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa MIT-WPU students gained hands-on learning, research exposure, and technical insights through the National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa. MIT-WPU / Events / National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa Events National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa MIT-WPU students gained hands-on learning, research exposure, and technical insights through the National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa. Events Happenings Events National Academic Immersion Programme at NIT Goa The Department of Computer Science and Applications at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, successfully conducted the National Academic Immersion Programme (NAIP) at NIT Goa for third-year BCA and B.Sc. students. Conducted across three batches, the six-day programme witnessed the participation of 489 students and 14 faculty members. Designed to bridge academic learning with practical exposure, the programme featured intensive sessions on Machine Learning and Deep Learning, covering key concepts such as regression, classification, deep neural networks, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), and Autoencoders through expert-led lectures and hands-on workshops. Beyond technical learning, the immersion programme encouraged cross-cultural learning, teamwork, and collaborative problem-solving through experiential activities and industry exposure. The initiative strengthened students' technical capabilities while enhancing their adaptability, communication skills, and overall professional readiness, reflecting MIT-WPU's commitment to experiential and industry-oriented education. The School of Business at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) focuses on developing future-ready business professionals through rigorous management education, industry engagement, and applied research. The school offers a comprehensive business education designed to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and perspectives required to succeed in the fast-paced and continuously evolving global business environment. The School of Business offers a diverse portfolio of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programmes in management, spanning key functional and emerging areas of business. With a vibrant academic community of nearly 6,000 students, the School of Business is among the most dynamic and prominent academic units of the University, fostering innovation, research, and industry engagement. Extended Abstract : Concise submission highlighting research objectives, methodology, key findings, and implications of ongoing or completed studies (1,000–1,500 words). Article : Analytical or practice-oriented articles discussing contemporary issues, challenges, and policy perspectives related to GST reforms and sectoral impact. Case Study: Original real-world case studies focusing on GST implementation, sectoral challenges, compliance issues, and decision-making insights. Original and unpublished research papers, conceptual papers, case studies, articles, and opinion pieces are invited from academicians, researchers, industry professionals, and practitioners. nctsrie-paper-submission-qr-code.webp Papers should be theoretical, empirical, or policy-oriented and aligned with the conference theme and sub-themes. entries.text: National Immersion : MIT-WPU National Immersion The National Immersion Programme provides engaging, innovative, and exemplary experiences to students in world-class National level companies & institutions. The programme shapes student learning experience and advances motivation and achievement through exposure in nationally reputed institutions. MIT-WPU / National Immersion National Immersion The National Immersion Programme provides engaging, innovative, and exemplary experiences to students in world-class National level companies & institutions. The programme shapes student learning experience and advances motivation and achievement through exposure in nationally reputed institutions. National Immersion national-immrsion-img The national immersion programmes at MIT-WPU are a part of the graduate and post graduate courses which gives the students opportunities to pay a visit to these institutes for a better understanding of the practices and research taking place in the various parts of the country. These six day tours comprise visits to venues of academic, national, historical, cultural and spiritual importance which sensitize the students to the diversity of the country. national-immrsion-img1 Students are trained to identify the problems faced by the community and come up with solutions for the same. These tours complement the traditional classroom based learning as students get to appreciate the history, geography, culture and lifestyle of Bharat as well as build team spirit and friendships amongst themselves. National Academic Immersion Programme at MCOPS, MAHE, Manipal The programme includes mentoring the students on three levels, namely pre-visit, on field and post-visit. It is designed to offer invaluable opportunities to the student youth of 11th and 12th grade at the +2 Board level, as well as the student community of technical institutions, graduate, and postgraduate levels in colleges and universities across India Student Clubs National Service Scheme The National Service Scheme (NSS) is a flagship programme under the purview of the Government of India, specifically administered by the Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports. It is designed to offer invaluable opportunities to the student youth of 11th and 12th grade at the +2 Board level, as well as the student community of technical institutions, graduate, and postgraduate levels in colleges and universities across India. The primary objective of NSS is to facilitate active participation in government-driven community service activities and programmes. Since its establishment in 1969, the NSS has witnessed remarkable growth, with the number of participating students soaring from 40,000 to an impressive 3.8 million. entries.title: National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon and CTF (Ciphathon 2026)
entries.excerpt: A 24-Hour National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon & CTF (Ciphathon 2026) on 27–28 March 2026, was jointly hosted by the School of Computer Science & Engineering and the School of Engineering & Technology at MIT World Peace University.
entries.text: Ciphathon 2026: National Cybersecurity Hackathon & CTF National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon and CTF (Ciphathon 2026) A 24-Hour National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon & CTF (Ciphathon 2026) on 27–28 March 2026, was jointly hosted by the School of Computer Science & Engineering and the School of Engineering & Technology at MIT World Peace University. MIT-WPU / Events / National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon and CTF (Ciphathon 2026) Events National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon and CTF (Ciphathon 2026) A 24-Hour National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon & CTF (Ciphathon 2026) on 27–28 March 2026, was jointly hosted by the School of Computer Science & Engineering and the School of Engineering & Technology at MIT World Peace University. Events National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon and CTF (Ciphathon 2026) Happenings Events sus-event-1 A 24-Hour National-Level Cybersecurity Hackathon & CTF (Ciphathon 2026) on 27–28 March 2026, was jointly hosted by the School of Computer Science & Engineering and the School of Engineering & Technology at MIT World Peace University. sus-event-2 This event marks a proud milestone for the university as it was the first-ever 24-hour hackathon conducted at MIT-WPU. The hackathon witnessed an incredible participation of 208 students from 15+ states across India, including Goa, Gujarat, Karnataka, Delhi, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and many more—making it truly a national celebration of talent, innovation, and cybersecurity excellence. We were privileged to host esteemed experts and leaders from organizations such as Quick Heal Technologies, C-DAC, Rivedix, SysTools, DeepCytes, and Tata Consultancy Services, along with 12 distinguished industry judges who enriched the event with their valuable insights. sus-event-3 The event received generous suppor

MIT-WPU / Ninox Nature Ninox Nature Discover Ninox Nature at MIT-WPU, a singular project advancing sustainability and biodiversity. Participate in events and initiatives meant to protect the environment and promote awareness of it. Student Clubs Ninox Nature Ninox Nature Club's mission is to connect students with nature and promote sustainable practices. Our objectives include organising field trips, hosting expert talks, conducting research using citizen science data, raising awareness through outreach, and exploring technology for wildlife research and conservation. Our club is dedicated to nurturing a deep appreciation for the complexity of life and its connection to nature. Through our diverse activities, we aim to inspire active participation in conservation efforts. Members Sr. No. Name Designation Anushka Gupta President Sharayu Jadhav Vice-President

MIT-WPU / Events / NLC Bharat 2025: Empowering Leaders, Strengthening Democracy Events NLC Bharat 2025: Empowering Leaders, Strengthening Democracy The National Legislators' Conference (NLC) Bharat Capacity Enhancement Program 2025 has successfully concluded, marking a milestone in strengthening India's democratic framework. This transformative two-day conference, held on 8-9 February 2025. Events Happenings Events The National Legislators' Conference (NLC) Bharat Capacity Enhancement Program 2025 has successfully concluded, marking a milestone in strengthening India's democratic framework. This transformative two-day conference, held on 8-9 February 2025, witnessed the convergence of more than 130 legislators, including Members of Legislative Assemblies (MLAs) and Members of Legislative Councils (MLCs) from across India. The conference established itself as a groundbreaking platform that transcends political boundaries, fostering meaningful collaboration and knowledge exchange among elected representatives. By bringing together legislators from diverse political backgrounds, NLC Bharat created an environment where shared governance challenges could be discussed openly, and innovative solutions could emerge through collective wisdom. This unique forum enabled elected representatives to connect beyond party lines, facilitating candid discussions and the exchange of best practices. The spirit of unity and common purpos

entries.title: NSRTC 2025
entries.excerpt: A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for India.
entries.text: NSRTC 2025 NSRTC 2025 A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for India. NSRTC 2025 Science, innovation, and national research priorities Date 18 July 2025 · three-day conference Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for India. The programme opened with plenary sessions on Nobel Prize-recognised work in medicine, chemistry, physics, and engineering, followed by specialist talks and a formal inaugural session. Technical sessions and round tables connected fundamental research, applied science, technology development, and questions of national capacity. Themes Artificial intelligence Advanced materials and processing Digital transformation, energy, and sustainability Healthcare, pharmaceutical science, and biotechnology Programme Special talks Ashutosh Sharma spoke on science and technology for national development; Arindam Ghosh on materials for quantum technologies; and Ajit Kembhavi on the life and scientific journey of Jayant Vishnu Narlikar. Scientific programme Parallel sessions covered artificial intelligence, materials and devices, energy and sustainability, healthcare, pharmaceuticals, and biotechnology, with posters and round-table discussions.

entries.title: NSRTC 2026
entries.excerpt: Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology.
entries.text: NSRTC 2026 NSRTC 2026 Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. NSRTC 2026 A three-day conclave advancing science, technology, and innovation Date 17–19 July 2026 Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. The programme combined invited talks and parallel technical sessions with round-table discussions, poster presentations, project exhibitions, and a Scholars-to-Scientists Connect initiative. The conference brought together contributors from universities, national laboratories, research institutes, industry, and public organisations. Themes Reimagining the future with artificial intelligence Materials, energy, and sustainability Emergent device technologies New biology and health sciences Programme Day 1 Opening sessions considered major scientific and engineering prizes, followed by the inaugural programme, research and entrepreneurship awards, keynote addresses, and a lecture on national wealth through science and technology. Days 2–3 Four parallel research tracks covered artificial intelligence, sustainable energy, advanced materials, semiconductors, health technologies, biotechnology, quantum technologies, and indigenous innovation.

Nummerates Club About the Club Nummerates is a premier student-driven club at MIT WPU dedicated to nurturing innovative ideas and fostering an environment of academic excellence. Our mission is to support students from their first year by providing a dynamic platform to explore and implement their innovative ideas. The club offers a collaborative environment where students can engage with experienced seniors, seek guidance on academic subjects, internships, and placements. Additionally, Nummerates serves as a platform to showcase one's talents and connect with some of the brightest minds in the field. Achievements Innovative Projects : Successfully mentored numerous student-led projects, many of which have received accolades in inter-collegiate competitions. Workshops and Seminars : Conducted several workshops on advanced mathematical concepts and innovative techniques, significantly enhancing the skill set of our members. Collaborations : Partnered with industry leaders for various events, offering students valuable networking opportunities and insights into real-world applications. Read the instructions given in the answer book and question paper carefully before attempting the examination. Inspired by the Ensō, it reflects mindfulness, wholeness, and the idea of becoming—never finished, always evolving. Blue stands for trust and global… WPU Goa / About / Our logo About WPU Goa Our logo The WPU GŌA symbol is rooted in a simple belief: education must serve humanity. The circle represents unity, balance, and continuity of learning. Inspired by the Ensō, it reflects mindfulness, wholeness, and the idea of becoming—never finished, always evolving. Blue stands for trust and global… The WPU GŌA symbol is rooted in a simple belief: education must serve humanity. The circle represents unity, balance, and continuity of learning. Inspired by the Ensō, it reflects mindfulness, wholeness, and the idea of becoming—never finished, always evolving. Blue stands for trust and global perspective. Maroon reflects courage, leadership, and purposeful action. Together, they form the Circle of Peace: A symbol that reflects what we believe, how we learn, and who we aim to become. Globally informed. Locally rooted. Our Vision Our Mission Ōur Vision “To be the world’s leading transdisciplinary university dedicated to peace and sustainability, designing and demonstrating regenerative futures, where human societies thrive in harmony within planetary boundaries.” Ōur Mission WPU GŌA exists to prepare leaders who think deeply, connect ideas across domains, and act with empathy. We educate leaders who can see the whole system, understand people, and design solutions with compassion. We generate research that pushes boundaries and enables real change, and we work to catalyse innovation and policy that shape the world

Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Padma Shri Dr. Mohan Agashe Padma Shri Dr. Mohan Agashe Veteran Actor & Psychiatrist Dr. Mohan Agashe is one of India's most distinguished figures at the intersection of medicine and the performing arts. A psychiatrist of national standing and a versatile actor of extraordinary range, he has spent over five decades enriching both the mental healthcare landscape and Indian cinema and theatre. His life exemplifies how intellectual rigour and artistic sensibility can coexist and reinforce each other at the highest level. Dr. Agashe completed his MBBS and MD in Psychiatry from B.J. Medical College, Pune. He served as Professor of Psychiatry at B.J. Medical College and Sassoon General Hospital, Pune (1981–1991), and was instrumental in establishing the Maharashtra Institute of Mental Health (MIMH) in Pune in 1991, a state-level institution focused on training, research, and rehabilitation. entries.title: Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan
entries.excerpt: Explore the inspiring journey of Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan, legendary sarod maestro and distinguished awardee featured by MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan | MIT-WPU Awardee Profile Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Explore the inspiring journey of Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan, legendary sarod maestro and distinguished awardee featured by MIT-WPU. MIT-WPU / Awardee Profiles / Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Awardee Profiles Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Explore the inspiring journey of Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan, legendary sarod maestro and distinguished awardee featured by MIT-WPU. Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Padma Vibhushan Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Indian Classical Musician, Sarod Virtuoso & Composer Ustad Amjad Ali Khan is highly revered as one of the greatest sarod players in the history of Indian classical music. The sixth generation of the illustrious Bangash lineage of the Senia Bangash Gharana, a family that claims to have invented the sarod, he was born Masoom Ali Khan to the legendary court musician Ustad Hafiz Ali Khan. His name was later changed to Amjad by a sadhu. He received his early training entirely under his father, performing publicly on the sarod from an age as young as six, and gave his first major solo recital at the age of twelve. Over a career spanning more than six decades, Ustad Amjad Ali Khan transformed the way the sarod is played and perceived globally. He pioneered the technique of using fingernails instead of fingertips to produce a distinctive, ringing clarity of tone, and composed numerous original ragas, including Kiran Ranjani, Shivanjali, Haripriya Kanada, Amiri Todi, and Bapukauns. He first performed in the United States in 1963 and has since taken Indian classical music to concert stages across the world, collaborating with the Hong Kong Philhar

He currently sits on the Boards of three reputed companies in India like Hero MotoCorp, Godrej etc. As an inspiring role model, Dr. Pritam Singh has spent his entire life tirelessly for awakening students, academia, corporate heads and policy makers to raise their excellence levels. He is a member of the Empowered Committee by Prime Minister for the selection of “Institute of Eminence”. He was also the Chairman of the Defence Acquisition Committee in the year 2016 for streamlining the process of Defence procurement. He has been on the board of several Public Sector Companies and has worked with more than 200 CEOs in India and abroad as a consultant. Programme Overview The Department of Computer Science and Applications offers a highly research-intensive full-time and part-time Doctorate programme and provides students with excellent facilities and expert guidance to support their research endeavors. The programme is designed to help postgraduate students develop research skills and prepare for careers in academia or research. The programme provides specialised training in research components such as hypothesis creation, research questions, literature review, research ethics, and the use of online tools and resources. The programme has a strong focus on interdisciplinary research and encourages students to pursue innovative and entrepreneurial ideas in their chosen areas of study. The students are encouraged to find the solution to the problems faced by society and different domains of technology in computer science and other fields. Programme Overview The Pharm.D (Post Baccalaureate) at MIT-WPU is a three-year programme offered to the graduates holding a B.Pharm degree where students are admitted to the fourth year of the Pharm.D programme. This programme emphasises on clinical pharmacy and pharmaceutical care, equipping students with the skills necessary to provide optimal drug therapy and patient care. The curriculum is divided into two phases: the first two years focus on theoretical knowledge across various subjects such as Pharmacotherapeutics and Clinical Pharmacy, while the final year is dedicated to a mandatory internship, allowing students to gain hands-on experience in clinical settings. Graduates are prepared to excel in diverse roles within healthcare, including hospitals, pharmacies, and pharmaceutical industries, ultimately enhancing their contributions to patient health and safety. With a strong emphasis on practical training and patient-centered care, this programme positions graduates for successful careers in the evolving field of pharmacy. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Student Projects / Plasma Based Ozone (O3) Generation and its Applications Life Wpu Plasma Based Ozone (O3) Generation and its Applications This award winning project focuses on the development of a plasma-based ozone generation system and its applications in various industries. Ozone is a proven oxidizing agent that leaves no harmful residue after oxidation. Student Projects Student Projects Project Details Plasma Based Ozone (O3) Generation and its Applications This award winning project focuses on the development of a plasma-based ozone generation system and its applications in various industries. Ozone is a proven oxidizing agent that leaves no harmful residue after oxidation. Due to its green and cost-effective attributes, it is widely used for various industrial and domestic treatment/disinfection applications. The first step in Ozone generation involves the decomposition of molecular Oxygen into atomic Oxygen that requires energy of a certain intensity. This requirement of energy is fulfilled using Platinum coated PbO2 electrodes, UV ray lamps with a certain range of wavelengths, and secondary streamers in plasma arc, respectively. In addition to the generation methods, various applications of Ozone in wastewater treatment, fish farming, the food and dairy industry, and odor treatment have also been reviewed.

entries.excerpt: Get basic pre- and post-arrival policies for overseas students attending MIT World Peace University. Discover travel, lodging, orientation, and settling to make a smooth process.
entries.text: Pre & Post Arrival Guidelines At MIT-WPU | Study In India Guidelines Pre-Arrival & Post-Arrival Guidelines Get basic pre- and post-arrival policies for overseas students attending MIT World Peace University. Discover travel, lodging, orientation, and settling to make a smooth process. MIT-WPU / Study In India / Pre-Arrival & Post-Arrival Guidelines Study In India Pre-Arrival & Post-Arrival Guidelines Get basic pre- and post-arrival policies for overseas students attending MIT World Peace University. Discover travel, lodging, orientation, and settling to make a smooth process. Study in India Pre-Arrival Information MIT-WPU is 17km an hour drive from Pune International Airport and you need to book your ticket to Pune International Airport after taking a Valid Student Visa from Indian Embassy/ High Commission. Make sure that you have taken all the required vaccinations like Yellow Fever, Typhoid Vaccine, Hepatitis Vaccine, etc. to avoid medical complications while you are in India. pre-arrival-information Pick up & Drop services can be arrange on chargeable basis. Make sure you inform MIT-WPU International Education Centre (IEC) for the Airport pickup at least two weeks prior to arrival and provide the complete details of flight. (OR you can easily get Uber & Ola cabs from Airport to Campus) Make sure that you bring the Admission Letter issued by the University along with all the Certificates/Degrees and Transcripts (in original) and 15 passport size photographs. You can carry all your money in form of Travellers Cheques, which can be easily redeemed at the foreign exchange offices and banks, or you can use International Debit / Credit Cards.
