entries.text: Privacy Policy · WPU Goa Privacy Policy Read WPU Goa privacy policy outlining data collection, usage, cookies, and measures to protect user information and ensure secure access. WPU Goa / About / Policies / Privacy Policy About WPU Goa Privacy Policy Read WPU Goa privacy policy outlining data collection, usage, cookies, and measures to protect user information and ensure secure access. Thank you for visiting the official WPU GŌA site and taking a moment to read our Privacy Policy. Your trust matters to us, and we take your privacy seriously. This policy explains how data is collected, stored, and used whenever you interact with our site. In brief, we gather personal details only when you choose to provide them. We never sell, trade, or rent your information to third parties. All data is used exclusively for legitimate university activities and managed with strict care. For specifics on technical logs and voluntary submissions, please see the sections that follow. Non-Personal Information That We Record When you browse our site without submitting personal details, our servers automatically capture certain technical metadata about your session, including: the Internet domain of your service provider; the make and version of the web browser employed; the operating system running on your device; the date and time of each visit; the specific pages accessed and the duration of each view; and web address that referred you here, when relevant. We process this information exclusively for statistical reporting and to refine site architecture, user flow, and overall performance. All data is reviewed only in aggregate form and is never used to identify individual visitors. entries.title: Prof. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake is a dedicated Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. With over ten years of teaching experience, he specializes in Construction…
entries.text: Prof. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake is a dedicated Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. With over ten years of teaching experience, he specializes in Construction Engineering and Management. He has completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering and M.E. in Construction and Management from Savitribai Phule Pune University. he earned his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune, titled "Development of a comprehensive framework in reducing delays for predictable risks." Dr. Shelake is actively engaged in teaching both undergraduate and postgraduate students. His teaching portfolio includes key subjects like Project Management, Project Scheduling & Material Management, Earned Value Management, Project Risk Management, and Surveying & Geomatics at UG and PG level. His pedagogical approach combines practical knowledge with software applications, making use of advanced tools such as Autodesk AutoCAD, Civil 3D, REVIT, NAVISWORK, Microsoft Project, and Primavera. He is certified by Autodesk and holds credentials including a Six Sigma Green Belt, ISO 9001:2015 QMS Internal Auditor, and a Project Management Professional Plus (PMP+) certification from Simplilearn Academy.

entries.text: Prof. Abhijeet Shrikrishna Kokare Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Mr.Abhijeet Kokare received his M.Sc (Computer Science) from Modern College of Arts Science and Commerce, Savitribai Phule University Pune in the year 2009. He has also qualified the SET (Maharashtra) exam. He has 14 years of experience teaching BCA, B.Sc(CS) M.Sc.(CS) students. He is a faculty member of the Department of Computer Science MIT-WPU, Pune. He has attended many conferences, workshops, and seminars.

entries.title: Prof. Abhijit Manikrao Bhagat
entries.excerpt: Abhijit Bhagat brings over two decades of industry experience, along with more than ten years of shaping young designers in the classroom. He understands the realities of the field, having worked on major projects with Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and…
entries.text: Prof. Abhijit Manikrao Bhagat Assistant Professor Department of Design Abhijit Bhagat brings over two decades of industry experience, along with more than ten years of shaping young designers in the classroom. He understands the realities of the field, having worked on major projects with Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and Tata Motors, before going on to establish his own architecture practice. Teaching, for him, is far more than a side pursuit. He has spent significant time mentoring students at D Y Patil Design School, PDEA College of Architecture, Anantrao Pawar College of Architecture, and Minerva College of Architecture. Rather than limiting himself to textbooks, he works closely with students in the studio—guiding them through design thinking, helping them tackle real-world challenges, and building their confidence for the profession. Abhijit’s approach moves beyond traditional lectures. He emphasizes hands-on learning and live projects, encouraging students to question, think critically, and explore solutions that are both creative and meaningful. He is deeply committed to helping aspiring designers discover their interests, stay curious, and continuously refine their skills. At the core of his teaching is a focus on creating inclusive spaces where every idea is valued and has the opportunity to grow. This philosophy aligns seamlessly with MIT-WPU, Pune’s vision. Abhij

entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhishek Prakash Chunawale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With 9.5 years of teaching experience, he received his B.E. in IT from Sant Gadge…
entries.text: Prof. Abhishek Prakash Chunawale Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Abhishek Prakash Chunawale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With 9.5 years of teaching experience, he received his B.E. in IT from Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University in 2011, where he was a Gold Medalist, and his M.E. in IT from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2014. He has completed his PhD in Computer Engineering, and his research area is Affective Computing. He has research paper publications in prestigious international journals and conferences such as IEEE and Springer. His areas of interest include IoT, Information and Cyber Security, Social Media Privacy and Security, and AR/VR.

entries.title: Prof. Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav
entries.excerpt: Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures
entries.text: Prof. Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures Prof. Adinath Yadhav, an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With 9 years of teaching experience under his belt, he completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University and his MTech in Marine Structures from National Institute of Technology, Surathkal. He has completed Ph.D. in Structural Engineering at MITWPU. Prof. Yadhav teaches postgraduate courses such as Advanced DEsign Design of Steel Structures, Plastic Design of Steel Structures and also undergraduate courses such as Design of Steel Structures, Design of RCC Structures, Theory of Structures, Strength of Materials, etc. He is proficient in software tools such as ETABS, STAAD PRO, ANSYS Auto CAD Civil 3D, and more. Rigidity Analysis of Floating Offshore Platforms is his area of interest, and he has published 7 research papers in international journals. He also published Indian patent on "A Precast Single Tee Girder with Glass Fibre Reinforced Polymer in Bridge Construction."

entries.excerpt: Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete
entries.text: Prof. Akashdeep Sharma Assistant Professor Department of Design Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete Akashdeep Sharma is an Assistant Professor with over Eight years of academic and professional experience in the fields of architecture and interior design. He holds a Master of Architecture (M.Arch) in Construction Management and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Design and Architecture at Symbiosis International University (SIU). He is a Certified Architect registered with the Council of Architecture, New Delhi. With extensive exposure to residential, commercial, and institutional projects, he brings strong practical insight into design education. His core competencies include a solid understanding of design principles, proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp, and other design tools, and effective verbal and written communication skills. Actively engaged with the industry, he facilitates student internships, collaborative projects, and placement opportunities, while demonstrating leadership in academic programs and design initiatives.

entries.title: Prof. Amir Shikalgar
entries.excerpt: Mr. Amir Shikalgar is a Practitioner turned Academician. He is a successful professional with 30+ years of multi-faceted experience across industries like FMCG, Retail, IT, Training and Education in India and abroad. Mr. Amir Shikalgar began his career as…
entries.text: Prof. Amir Shikalgar Assistant Professor Department of Business Mr. Amir Shikalgar is a Practitioner turned Academician. He is a successful professional with 30+ years of multi-faceted experience across industries like FMCG, Retail, IT, Training and Education in India and abroad. Mr. Amir Shikalgar began his career as a Management Trainee with Britannia Industries Ltd., where he rose to the position of Sales Operations Manager. Subsequently, he joined Frito Lay India as Regional Sales Manager – West. Over the years, he has held key roles at companies such as Conagra Foods and NIS Sparta, taking on diverse responsibilities across functions. He also served as Head – Sales Capability Building at PepsiCo (Beverages). His international assignment was with Ajmal Perfumes, where he led operations across Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, and Malaysia. In leadership roles, he has worked as Vice President at VitalSmarts India and as National Head at Pragati Leadership. He has worked with the School of Retail Management at Symbiosis Skills and Professional University, Pune. Mr. Amir Shikalgar was also a visiting faculty at Symbiosis Institute of Business Management, Pune. He has trained and guided 2000+ students, UG as well as PG, till date in various Engineering and Management Institutes. He is currently working with the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Kothrud, Pune Mr. Amir Shikal

entries.text: Prof. Amruta Pradeep Dixit Assistant Professor Department of Business Ms. Amruta Dixit brings over 16 years of rich experience in teaching and academic mentoring in the field of finance and accounting. She has taught diverse subjects including Financial Management, Cost & Management Accounting, Financial Accounting, and Business Simulation. Currently pursuing her Ph.D. in Financial Management under SPPU, she is also a certified Business Simulation trainer from Marketplace Simulations (USA). Apart from her teaching responsibilities, Ms. Dixit contributes actively to curriculum restructuring, student mentoring, project guidance, academic audits, and SOP design. She has two patents to her credit. Ms. Dixit is committed to supporting students not just in academics but also in their holistic professional and personal development.

entries.excerpt: Anant Srivastava is committed to creating a student-centric learning environment that encourages independent thinking, self-confidence, curiosity, and lifelong learning. His teaching philosophy focuses on helping students build strong fundamentals while…
entries.text: Prof. Anant Srivastava Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Anant Srivastava is committed to creating a student-centric learning environment that encourages independent thinking, self-confidence, curiosity, and lifelong learning. His teaching philosophy focuses on helping students build strong fundamentals while nurturing their distinct identities and individual potential. He encourages learners to ask questions freely, explore multiple perspectives, and connect theoretical concepts with real-world applications. Through his approach, he aims to inspire students to become self-reliant learners, ethical professionals, and confident individuals capable of contributing meaningfully to society. Anant Srivastava firmly believes in the holistic development of students through the harmonious integration of science and spirituality.

entries.text: Prof. Anita Mahesh Thengade Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Anita Thengade is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. With over 24 years of teaching experience, She earned her Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Swami Ramanand Teerth Marathwada University, Nanded. Her research interests include Machine Learning, Medical Image Processing, and Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality. She has published more than 30 research papers in reputed international journals and conferences and holds two granted patents. She was also selected through the Maharashtra Public Service Commission (MPSC) for the position of Assistant Professor early in her career.

entries.title: Prof. Anshul Gupta
entries.excerpt: Mr. Anshul Gupta is a designer with a strong understanding of human behaviour and user- centric design principles. His approach focuses on creating solutions that not only meet functional requirements but also establish meaningful emotional connections…
entries.text: Prof. Anshul Gupta Assistant Professor Department of Design Mr. Anshul Gupta is a designer with a strong understanding of human behaviour and user- centric design principles. His approach focuses on creating solutions that not only meet functional requirements but also establish meaningful emotional connections with users. He integrates experience design and gamification to develop innovative, practical, and engaging products. His work aims to address contemporary challenges while ensuring accessibility, inclusivity, and personalization. By blending usability with thoughtful interaction design, he strives to make every day experiences intuitive, impactful, and rewarding.

entries.title: Prof. Anuja Dilip Dhulekar
entries.excerpt: Indian Handicrafts, Pottery, Ergonomics
entries.text: Prof. Anuja Dilip Dhulekar Assistant Professor Department of Design Indian Handicrafts, Pottery, Ergonomics Ms. Anuja Dhulekar is an experienced design professional and academic with over 14 years of combined experience across industry and academia. She is a graduate of Sir J. J. School of Art, Mumbai, and holds a postgraduate degree in Industrial Design (Ceramics and Glass Design) from the National Institute of Design (NID), Ahmedabad. Her professional journey includes extensive work in product development, particularly in the fields of ceramics and lifestyle accessories. She has also successfully led projects under the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME), contributing to innovation and entrepreneurship in the design and craft sectors. Ms. Dhulekar has been associated with leading design institutes as both a full-time and visiting faculty member. Her academic interests include Systems Thinking, Design Thinking, Form Development, Pottery and Ceramics manufacturing, and in the handicraft sector. Currently, she is serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University and is pursuing her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Guwahati.

entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Prof. Aparna Kamble holds a BE degree from Shivaji University Kolhapur and an M.E degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Maharashtra, India in 2004 and 2012 respectively. Completed PhD in 2019 in the domain of WSN. Currently serving as an Assistant…
entries.text: Prof. Aparna Ashok Kamble Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Prof. Aparna Kamble holds a BE degree from Shivaji University Kolhapur and an M.E degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Maharashtra, India in 2004 and 2012 respectively. Completed PhD in 2019 in the domain of WSN. Currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the School of Computer Engineering & Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, Maharashtra, India, with over 19 years of teaching experience. Her research interests revolve around wireless sensor networks and distributed systems.

entries.title: Prof. Aparna Virendra Shete
entries.excerpt: Water Resources Engineering, Town planning
entries.text: Prof. Aparna Virendra Shete Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Water Resources Engineering, Town planning Prof. Aparna V. Shete is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering from MGM, Nanded, and a Master's degree in Civil- Town and Country Planning from the prestigious College of Engineering, Pune in 2010.She has been associated with MIT-WPU since 2014 and has taught various subjects such as Basics of Civil and Environmental Engineering, Applied Mechanics, Surveying, Advanced Surveying, Irrigation Engineering, Building Design and Drawing, Building Information Modelling, and Management Information System. Prof. Shete completed her Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, specializing in the area of Water Resources Engineering under the guidance of Dr. S. S. Shahapure. Her expertise in the field has earned her life membership of the prestigious Indian Society of Hydraulics (ISH) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE).

entries.text: Prof. Archana Pradip Varade Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Mrs. Archana Pradip Varade is committed to utilizing her academic knowledge and skills for the betterment of students and the institution, while continuously striving for excellence in teaching, learning, and professional growth. She focuses on integrating research and collaborative problem-solving into the classroom to enhance the overall learning experience. Aligned with MIT-WPU’s vision of providing value-based quality education, she aims to foster holistic student development, contributing to social transformation and institutional excellence.

entries.title: Prof. Arun Balaso Mali
entries.excerpt: Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring
entries.text: Prof. Arun Balaso Mali Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring Mr. Arun Mali is currently working as Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud Pune. He is having more than 12 years of teaching experiance. He holds Masters of Engineering in Design Engineering Domain and Currently pursuing Ph. D. in Mechanical Engineering. Master's work was associated with Design and Numerical Experiment on Compliant Mechanisms. Previously worked on Flexure mechanisms in Precision Applications. Research work was coined to achieve a specific objective of demonstration of dual use of Voice Coil Motor (i.e as actuator as well as sensor) which eliminates need of sensor in precision scanning applications . It was planned to design frictionless, backlash free XY flexural mechanism and experimentally demonstrate the dual use of VCM. His research areas are Compliant Mechanisms, Mechanical Design, Vibrations and acoustics, condition monitoring of Machines etc. A devoted educator, he focuses on inspiring students to engage critically with Engineering and Technology while contributing meaningfully to the study of diverse perspectives. His commitment to fostering intellectual growth reflects his belief in the transformative power of education.

entries.title: Prof. Asha Rajendra Sanap
entries.excerpt: Publication
entries.text: Prof. Asha Rajendra Sanap Lecturer Department of Polytechnic Publication Prof. Asha Sanap is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Polytechnic at MIT World Peace University, Pune, Maharashtra, India. She specializes in Electronics and Communication Engineering, with a focus on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. As an accomplished academician, she plays a vital role in shaping the minds of the future generation in this field.

entries.title: Prof. Ashish Singh Tomar
entries.excerpt: Ashish believes that design education should be hands-on, empathetic, and rooted in real human needs. His goal is not just to teach tools and techniques, but to help students observe deeply, think critically, and build solutions that truly matter. What…
entries.text: Prof. Ashish Singh Tomar Assistant Professor Department of Design Ashish believes that design education should be hands-on, empathetic, and rooted in real human needs. His goal is not just to teach tools and techniques, but to help students observe deeply, think critically, and build solutions that truly matter. What drives him is the joy of seeing young minds grow curious and confident. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to create a warm, collaborative learning space where students feel encouraged to experiment, fail, and learn. With his background in product development and UX, he would love to contribute to projects that blend technology with social impact, staying true to the institution's vision of nurturing responsible, innovative leaders.

entries.title: Prof. Ashok Shripati Deokar
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Mr. Ashok S. Deokar has successfully completed his MCA and MBA from Savitribai Phule Pune University. He is presently pursuing his Ph.D. at the same university, specializing in Big Data and Digital Marketing. With over 22 years of teaching experience & 1.5…
entries.text: Prof. Ashok Shripati Deokar Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Mr. Ashok S. Deokar has successfully completed his MCA and MBA from Savitribai Phule Pune University. He is presently pursuing his Ph.D. at the same university, specializing in Big Data and Digital Marketing. With over 22 years of teaching experience & 1.5 as Industry Experience, he has imparted knowledge across a wide range of subjects including Database Management Systems, Advanced DBMS, Web Technologies, Big Data Analytics, Cloud Computing, Cyber Security, Mobile Application Development, Operating Systems, Network Technologies, Data Structures, C, and C++. He has contributed extensively to academia through the publication of numerous research papers in reputed journals and conferences and holds two patents to his credit. In addition, he has actively participated in various STTPs and FDPs organized by AICTE, IITs, and leading universities. In 2025, he joined MIT World Peace University as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Applications, where he also serves as the CAP Coordinator. Previously, he worked as a College Examination Officer for Savitribai Phule Pune University. Beyond his academic responsibilities, he has successfully organized and conducted several co-curricular activities for students and professional organizations. He is also a certified I

entries.title: Prof. Asmita Gaikwad
entries.excerpt: Prof. Asmita Gaikwad is a passionate and experienced academician with over a decade of expertise in teaching, research, and professional training. She is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, where she…
entries.text: Prof. Asmita Gaikwad Assistant Professor Department of Business Prof. Asmita Gaikwad is a passionate and experienced academician with over a decade of expertise in teaching, research, and professional training. She is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, where she specializes in Business Analytics, Data Science, and Marketing. Prof. Gaikwad is a Lean Six Sigma Green Belt holder and a UGC-NET qualified educator. She is also a certified trainer under the Wadhwani Foundation's Ignite Entrepreneurship Development Programme, reflecting her dedication to fostering entrepreneurial thinking and data-driven decision-making. Currently pursuing her Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, she has contributed significantly to academic literature through publications in UGC-CARE, ABDC indexed journals, along with co-authoring nationally recognized books on SPSS and data analytics. Her hands-on training in tools like Power BI, Tableau, SPSS, Excel, and DBMS, combined with a deep understanding of Emotional Intelligence and SEM, makes her a valuable mentor and educator. Prof. Gaikwad is committed to creating engaging, impactful learning experiences and empowering students to thrive in the world of analytics and research.

entries.title: Prof. Balagangadhar Reddy Kandula
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: An academic and researcher dedicated to bridging the gap between theoretical computer science and practical, large-scale industrial application. My teaching philosophy centers on "learning by building," where students engage with real-world problems in…
entries.text: Prof. Balagangadhar Reddy Kandula Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications An academic and researcher dedicated to bridging the gap between theoretical computer science and practical, large-scale industrial application. My teaching philosophy centers on "learning by building," where students engage with real-world problems in data science and AI. I am deeply motivated by MIT-WPU’s vision of fostering research-led innovation and contributing by integrating advanced technologies such as LLM-powered recommendation systems and distributed computing into our institutional ecosystem to enhance academic outcomes and operational efficiency.

Complementing his academic career, Prof. Bhasker has extensive international experience through his numerous visiting appointments. entries.title: Prof. CA Bipin Vishvas Palande
entries.excerpt: Personal Finance, Behavioural Finance, FinTech
entries.text: Prof. CA Bipin Vishvas Palande Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting Personal Finance, Behavioural Finance, FinTech Bipin is an Assistant Professor with the Department of Commerce and Accounting. He is a qualified Chartered Accountant and an alumnus of IIM Tiruchirappalli. He has also cleared NET (with JRF) in Commerce. He has more than eight years of post-qualification experience in industry as well as academia. He is currently pursuing PhD from Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune in the area of Personal Finance. His teaching interests include Financial Management, Cost and Management Accounting, Taxation, Business Mathematics etc.

entries.title: Prof. CA Dhaarna Rathore
entries.excerpt: Accounting, E Commerce, E Business, ERP, Finance, Management Accounting, IOT.
entries.text: Prof. CA Dhaarna Rathore Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting Accounting, E Commerce, E Business, ERP, Finance, Management Accounting, IOT. Dr. CA. Dhaarna Singh Rathore is an accomplished academic and finance professional with over 18 years of diverse experience in educational administration and financial Consulting. Currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT WPU University , she has a distinguished track record in teaching, curriculum development, and research, particularly in the areas of ERP implementation and commerce. Dr. Rathore holds a Ph.D. in Commerce, an MBA in Finance, M. Com and is a Chartered Accountant, bringing a rich blend of academic knowledge and practical expertise to her role. She has made significant contributions, authoring two books, numerous scholarly papers, securing two patents, and obtaining one copyright for innovative teaching methodologies. Her career is marked by innovative leadership, successful program coordination, and a commitment to fostering student growth and success.

entries.title: Prof. CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar
entries.excerpt: Sustainability, CSR ,ESG ,Corporate Finance
entries.text: Prof. CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar Assistant Professor Department of Business Sustainability, CSR ,ESG ,Corporate Finance CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar is a highly accomplished Chartered Accountant and passionate educator, currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU. With a strong research acumen, she is also a research scholar focusing on Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives in India and their outcomes. Her expertise in this area makes her a sought-after speaker and consultant, and she has contributed to several publications on the topic. Driven by a desire to create positive change, Manjiri is dedicated to empowering her students and fellow professionals with the knowledge and skills they need to make a difference in the world.CA Manjiri has conducted several MDPs on “Finance for Non-Finance executives” for employees of several listed and private companies.

entries.title: Prof. CA Swapnil Mahesh Gupta
entries.excerpt: CA Swapnil Gupta focuses on clear, practical, and student-friendly teaching that helps learners understand concepts and apply them confidently. Her approach is exam-oriented yet concept- driven, employing simple explanations, real-world illustrations, and…
entries.text: Prof. CA Swapnil Mahesh Gupta Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting CA Swapnil Gupta focuses on clear, practical, and student-friendly teaching that helps learners understand concepts and apply them confidently. Her approach is exam-oriented yet concept- driven, employing simple explanations, real-world illustrations, and regular interaction to build strong fundamentals. With teaching experience in Law at the CA Foundation level, she breaks down complex legal provisions into easy and relatable examples, motivated to guide students through structured learning, discipline, and professional ethics while contributing to MIT-WPU via effective teaching, academic rigor, and outcome-based education.

entries.text: Prof. Deepali Sanjay Kulkarni Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering POLYMER MATERIALS ,NANOMATERIALS ,COMPOSITES Publication 1)Effect of egg shell powder and fish bone powder content on mechanical performance of PP 2)Synthesis of Unsaturated Polyester Resin Based on Pet Waste 3)Synthesis Of Bio-Polyurethane Foam Using Liquefied Pine Wood 4)Effect of nano silica on phase transformation from form-II to form –I of Polybutene-1 5)Polyfurfuryl based Polymer Concrete Google Scholar Link https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=uL5abvEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao Orcid Link https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0309-3519 Mrs. Deepali Kulkarni has a vast teaching experience of 28 years in the field of polymer materials, processing of composites, rubber technology, and design of equipment and machine elements. She has also conducted research on topics such as the effect of egg shell powder and fish bone powder content on the mechanical performance of PP, the effect of Nano Silica on phase transformation from Form-II to Form-I of Polybutene-1, and polyfurfuryl-based polymer concrete.

entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Deepali Sonawane is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Application, committed to fostering a learner-centric environment aligned with MIT-WPU’s vision of “Powering the Future.” Her teaching philosophy emphasizes innovation,…
entries.text: Prof. Deepali Sonawane Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Deepali Sonawane is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Application, committed to fostering a learner-centric environment aligned with MIT-WPU’s vision of “Powering the Future.” Her teaching philosophy emphasizes innovation, inclusivity, and practical knowledge, encouraging students to think critically and apply technology for societal impact. She leads with integrity and a commitment to continuous learning, while nurturing empathy and student growth. She actively promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and aims to empower students with the skills, values, and a progressive mindset needed to contribute meaningfully to education, innovation, and society.

entries.title: Prof. Devashish Shedge
entries.excerpt: Mr. Devashish Shedge is a Film Director and Senior Creative Director with over 35 years of experience across India and the UAE. With expertise in storytelling, advertising, and audio-visual production, he brings extensive industry knowledge into the…
entries.text: Prof. Devashish Shedge Assistant Professor Department of Media and Communication Mr. Devashish Shedge is a Film Director and Senior Creative Director with over 35 years of experience across India and the UAE. With expertise in storytelling, advertising, and audio-visual production, he brings extensive industry knowledge into the classroom through a practice-driven approach to learning. By integrating the disciplines of cinema, advertising, and media education, he mentors students to develop creative thinking, technical excellence, and industry-ready skills.

Prof. Shastri's ultimate objective is to equip students with the necessary skills to overcome any challenge they may face in the world.

entries.title: Prof. Diksha Dinar Bedekar
entries.excerpt: Sustainable Consumption, Consumer Behavior, Marketing Management
entries.text: Prof. Diksha Dinar Bedekar Assistant Professor Department of Business Sustainable Consumption, Consumer Behavior, Marketing Management Prof. Diksha Bedekar is an academician with over 15 years of teaching experience at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. and is currently pursuing her Ph.D. in the adoption of sustainable consumption behaviour under Marketing specialization. She has served as the Program Head of BBA (International Business) and has been actively engaged in administrative roles, curriculum design, and academic restructuring in line with the guidelines of NEP 2020. At present, she is a member of the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) at MIT-WPU, where she contributes to Communication and Documentation and has played an important role in institutional quality processes, including NAAC accreditation cycles. She is also a Board of Studies Member at Modern College, Pune, and her research contributions span Consumer Behavior pertaining to Sustainable Consumption. She has presented papers at several national and international conferences, including those hosted by IIMs and leading B-Schools in India. She has received a research grant for her doctoral work and has been awarded the Best Paper Award for her Ph.D. research. As an educator, she is motivated to guide students in building confidence, critical thinking, and problem-solving abilities. She actively engages in mentoring, academic coordination, and research to enhance the overall learning experience. Through her consistent efforts in teaching and professional development, she contributes to academic excellence. In alignment with the vision of MIT World Peace University, she strives to nurture responsible, skilled, and ethically aware graduates who can contribute positively to society.

MIT-WPU / Awardee Profiles / Prof. Dr. Gagandeep Kang Awardee Profiles Prof. Dr. Gagandeep Kang Dr. Gagandeep Kang FNA, FASc, FRS is an eminent clinician scientist and is currently a Professor at The Wellcome Trust Research Laboratory, Division of Gastrointestinal Sciences at the Christian Medical College, Vellore. Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Prof. Dr. Gagandeep Kang Prof. Dr. Gagandeep Kang Professor, The Wellcome Trust Research Laboratory, Division of Gastrointestinal Sciences, Christian Medical College, Vellore Dr. Gagandeep Kang FNA, FASc, FRS is an eminent clinician scientist and is currently a Professor at The Wellcome Trust Research Laboratory, Division of Gastrointestinal Sciences at the Christian Medical College, Vellore. She is the first Indian woman scientist to be elected as a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) in 359 years of history of this prestigious scientific academy. She is also the executive director of the Translational Health Science and Technology Institute, Faridabad, an autonomous institute of the Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India. Being very passionate from a very young age about science, today she is a leading researcher with a major research focus on viral infections in children, and the testing of rotaviral vaccines. She also works on other enteric infections and their consequences when children are infected in early life, sanitation and water safety. Dr. Gagandeep Kang

MIT-WPU / Faculty / Prof. Dr. Urvashi Makkar Faculty Prof. Dr. Urvashi Makkar Learn about Urvashi Makkar, faculty member at MIT-WPU Pune. Explore her academic expertise, teaching experience, research contributions, and role in shaping future-ready professionals. Faculty Faculty Dean & Professor Bio Prof. Dr. Urvashi Makkar has over 28 years of experience and held prominent academic and administrative positions, including Director & Professor - Marketing at IMS Ghaziabad and Director General at GL Bajaj Institute of Management & Research. Her contributions span across academic quality enhancement, institutional accreditation (NBA, NAAC), global collaborations, research mentoring, and fostering industry-academia partnerships. Dr. Makkar holds a Ph.D. in Management, an Executive Programme in HRM from IIM Calcutta, an MBA in Marketing, PGDCA, and a B.Sc. in Physics. She has driven institutional growth through strategic academic initiatives, impactful placement efforts, and international academic alliances, including delivering a Global Executive MBA module at UCS, Brazil. Her commitment to academic leadership is further evidenced by her participation in international faculty development programs, including the UK-India Education and Research Initiative (UKIERI) in collaboration with AICTE, the AICTE-UKIERI Master Trainer Development Program, and the Higher Education Leadership Program at Harvard Graduate School of Education, USA. Raghuram is a distinguished academician and former Director of the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore (IIM-B). He served as the Director of IIM-B from 2017-2020, where he played a pivotal role in enhancing the institute's global reputation and fostering a culture of excellence in both, teaching and research. Prior to his tenure as Director, Dr. Raghuram was a Professor at IIM Ahmedabad, where he spent over three decades contributing significantly to the fields of infrastructure, transportation, and logistics. His work has been widely recognised for its impact on policy and practice in Bharat as well as internationally. He has also participated in numerous government policy-making and advisory committees. entries.title: Prof. Garima Chaudhary
entries.excerpt: Garima Chaudhary is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). She holds Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD degrees in Fine Arts from Aligarh Muslim University, reflecting a strong…
entries.text: Prof. Garima Chaudhary Assistant Professor Department of Visual Arts Garima Chaudhary is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). She holds Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD degrees in Fine Arts from Aligarh Muslim University, reflecting a strong academic foundation. With over two years of experience in Visual Arts education, Garima combines academic rigor with practical expertise. Her professional skills encompass research, drawing, painting, branding, print and digital media, semiotics, and photography, with a particular research interest in photography, art, and design. In her teaching, she integrates creative vision with structured project management, enabling her to mentor students effectively. This approach helps students develop critical thinking and robust art skills, preparing them for diverse creative challenges. Garima’s commitment to both academic excellence and hands-on learning fosters a dynamic and supportive environment for aspiring artists and practitioners.

entries.title: Prof. Gauri Bilaye
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: My philosophy as a teacher will be based on stimulating curiosity, critical thinking, and problem solving in the real world. My goal is to build a non-discriminatory, interactive learning classroom, in which students form an active group, challenge their…
entries.text: Prof. Gauri Bilaye Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering My philosophy as a teacher will be based on stimulating curiosity, critical thinking, and problem solving in the real world. My goal is to build a non-discriminatory, interactive learning classroom, in which students form an active group, challenge their thoughts and possibilities, and relate theory with practice. As a teacher, my driving power is to guide students towards achieving not only academic results by also teaching them how to achieve ethical values, creativity and life-long learning. In line with the vision of the holistic and value-based education at MIT-WPU, I would be able to make a contribution through creative teaching methods, interdisciplinary cooperation, and the learning process based on research that would help students become responsible professional beings and influential global citizens.

entries.title: Prof. Harshit Pratap Singh
entries.excerpt: Harshit is an educator and designer, specializing in areas of research, graphic design, and film-making. He holds a UGC-NET qualification, and has completed an M.A. in Education from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai and a Bachelor of Design from…
entries.text: Prof. Harshit Pratap Singh Assistant Professor Department of Design Harshit is an educator and designer, specializing in areas of research, graphic design, and film-making. He holds a UGC-NET qualification, and has completed an M.A. in Education from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai and a Bachelor of Design from Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. Before joining the Department of Design at MIT World Peace University as an Assistant Professor, he has worked with multiple NGOs including Mantra4Change, Simple Education Foundation, Myna Mahila Foundation in varying capacities of researcher, designer, and educator. He excels in weaving individual narratives with contextual research to design visual communication solutions that are thought-provoking and insightful. Having a keen interest in storytelling, his projects include multiple short films and books along with published papers and articles. He tries to encourage his students to think critically and analytically to design solutions that contribute to the upliftment of the marginalized, and the society at large.

entries.title: Prof. Hemlata Sandip Ohal
entries.excerpt: : AI in Health Care
entries.text: Prof. Hemlata Sandip Ohal Lecturer (Sr. Scale) Department of Polytechnic : AI in Health Care Mrs. Hemlata Sandip Ohal is an accomplished academician with over 26 years of teaching experience and is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at the School of Polytechnic, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds an M.E. in Information Technology from the University of Pune and is currently pursuing her Ph.D. at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. Mrs. Ohal has made notable contributions to technical education through research, innovation, and authorship. She has published 14 research papers in reputed Scopus-indexed journals and conference proceedings and has three Indian patents to her credit. She has also authored several textbooks for Diploma Engineering students, published by Nirali Prakashan. Her areas of expertise include Object-Oriented Programming, Computer Graphics, and Machine Learning. A dedicated mentor, she actively guides students in project-based learning and innovation activities. Under her mentorship, students have secured several prizes and awards at various technical competitions and events. Mrs. Ohal is a Life Member of ISTE and is actively involved in promoting holistic well-being through yoga. entries.title: Prof. Heramb Chintamani Phadake
entries.excerpt: Air conditioning, Energy optimization
entries.text: Prof. Heramb Chintamani Phadake Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Air conditioning, Energy optimization Prof. Heramb Phadake is a highly accomplished Assistant Professor with a diverse background in Mechanical Engineering. With a 20+ years of experience, he has spent 20 years working in the industry and 5 years teaching. Prof. Phadake holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (currently pursuing), an M.Tech. in Energy Engineering - Design, and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering. He specializes in Energy Engineering, Thermal Engineering, and Automotive Technology, and is a member of the Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE). Prof. Phadake's strengths lie in process planning and execution, and he has gained extensive coordination experience while working on infrastructure projects for over 20 years.

entries.title: Prof. Hossein Martin Fazeli
entries.excerpt: Hossein Martin Fazeli is a distinguished Iranian writer, producer, and director who has been an A-list filmmaker in North America for many years and currently serves as a Professor of Practice at Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School. With over two…
entries.text: Prof. Hossein Martin Fazeli Professor of Practice Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Hossein Martin Fazeli is a distinguished Iranian writer, producer, and director who has been an A-list filmmaker in North America for many years and currently serves as a Professor of Practice at Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School. With over two decades of experience in producing, directing, and writing fiction and non-fiction television and films, Fazeli has built an illustrious career marked by his extensive international collaborations across more than a dozen countries. His works have been broadcast globally on leading networks such as CBC, BBC, ARTE, and Canal+, earning 37 international awards and critical acclaim. Fazeli’s films often explore themes of human rights, women’s rights, and minority rights, underscoring his commitment to social justice. He has collaborated with prominent organizations, including the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the European Commission, and Nonviolence International, contributing to impactful film and television projects and national and regional campaigns. One of his landmark productions, the 2007 documentary “The Tale of Two Nazanins”, spotlighted the plight of a teenage girl imprisoned on death row in Iran. Broadcast on major international networks such as BBC and CNN, the film ignited a global campaign that ultimately save

entries.text: Prof. Jagruti Divakar Patil Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Jagruti Patil is a distinguished Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She has earned a Ph.D. in Structural Engineering with a specialization in Earthquake Engineering from MIT-ATD University, Pune. Additionally, she holds an M.E. in Structural Engineering and a B.E. in Civil Engineering from Pune University. Apart from teaching responsibilities, Dr. Patil is also responsible for coordinating social media and admissions activities at MIT-WPU.

entries.title: Prof. Jayshree Ajit Aher
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Jayshree Aher(Ghorpade) is committed to a student-centric teaching philosophy that blends conceptual clarity, innovation, and real-world application. Her motivation lies in nurturing curious, responsible learners who can contribute meaningfully to…
entries.text: Prof. Jayshree Ajit Aher Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Jayshree Aher(Ghorpade) is committed to a student-centric teaching philosophy that blends conceptual clarity, innovation, and real-world application. Her motivation lies in nurturing curious, responsible learners who can contribute meaningfully to society and the evolving knowledge economy. She emphasizes interdisciplinary learning, research orientation, and industry collaboration to prepare students for future challenges and strives to foster creativity, critical thinking, and ethical values. She has mentored students at SIH, IEEE, various national/international Hackathons/Projects, research scholarships for students, etc. She also holds granted patents, copyrights and actively contributes as a reviewer, mentor, and research leader, committed to developing scalable, explainable, and transformative AI-driven systems. Jayshree actively contributes to the vision of MIT-WPU by promoting excellence in education, research, and societal impact while cultivating globally competent professionals.

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Music Signal Analysis and Synthesis, Speech Processing, Pattern Recognition, Computer Vision, Machine Learning
entries.text: Prof. Jyoti Anirudha Lele Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Music Signal Analysis and Synthesis, Speech Processing, Pattern Recognition, Computer Vision, Machine Learning Jyoti Lele is an Assistant Professor at the School of Electronics and Communication, with a B.E. degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2000, and an M.E. in Electronics (Digital Systems) from Pune University in 2009. Currently, she is pursuing her Ph.D. from the Department of Technology at SP Pune University, focusing on music signal analysis and synthesis. Her research interests span a variety of areas, including pattern recognition, machine learning, speech processing, computer vision, and artificial intelligence. With over 20 years of teaching experience, she has published more than 15 technical papers in peer-reviewed journals and conference proceedings.

Her research areas are Blockchain, AI and ML. With over five years of experience in teaching and research, she authored three books on Internet of Things, Business Analytics and Statistical Methods using R, Big Data Analytics.

entries.text: Prof. Kiran Bidua Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Prof. Kiran Bidua brings over a decade of experience in teaching B.Tech students, primarily in the areas of networking and cybersecurity. He combines strong academic expertise with industry-aligned knowledge to create a relevant and engaging learning environment. A certified professional in Palo Alto Networks technologies, OPSWAT Network Security, and OSINT, he continuously upskills to deliver industry-relevant insights that enhance student employability in a competitive landscape. He is passionate about translating theoretical concepts into real-world applications and actively contributes to technical initiatives and student clubs. Prof. Bidua remains committed to contributing meaningfully to the sustained excellence and innovation of MIT World Peace University.

entries.title: Prof. Mahesh Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Assistant Professor and 3D Modeler/Animator Mahesh M. Kulkarni holds 20 plus years of Industry and Academic experience. Graduation and Post Graduation from Pune University and CDAC, Mahesh holds industry experience in Architectural and Environment design…
entries.text: Prof. Mahesh Kulkarni Assistant Professor Department of Design Assistant Professor and 3D Modeler/Animator Mahesh M. Kulkarni holds 20 plus years of Industry and Academic experience. Graduation and Post Graduation from Pune University and CDAC, Mahesh holds industry experience in Architectural and Environment design for games and animation. His broad experience includes Game Level Design, Game Assets creation, Digital Sculpting, 3D Modeling & Animation, optimization for games and Architectural/ Interior designing. He is enthusiastic in teaching and sharing industry standard design techniques and aesthetically stunning visual imagery. His wide range of portfolio includes Architectural Visualization for European Interior designing firms/ Architectural companies, Game asset visualization and production for domestic firms and Consultancy on Animation and game designing start-ups.

entries.title: Prof. Mahesh Nana Pradhan
entries.excerpt: Design, Composite Material, Robotics, Mechatronics
entries.text: Prof. Mahesh Nana Pradhan Associate Professor and Program Coordinator (R&A) Department of Mechanical Engineering Design, Composite Material, Robotics, Mechatronics With over 25 years of teaching experience in the prestigious MIT-WPU, Prof. Mahesh Pradhan is a renowned figure in the field of mechanical engineering. His two-year industrial experience at Bajaj Tempo Ltd has contributed to his vast knowledge in the industry. Prof. Pradhan completed his M.E in Mechanical from COEP, Pune and has authored several research papers in the field of design and composite materials. As the Dept. Coordinator of the Centre for Industry Academia Partnership (CIAP) and Internship Coordinator, he has played a key role in shaping the academic curriculum of MIT-WPU. He is also the coordinator of the Rickshaw and Taxi Fare Meter Testing Centre and has served as a committee member for the implementation of electronic Rickshaw and Taxi fare meters in Maharashtra State. Prof. Pradhan is also credited with setting up the Mechatronics and Robotics laboratory in MIT-WPU and is currently serving as the laboratory incharge.

entries.title: Prof. Maisam Ali
entries.excerpt: Maisam Ali is an independent filmmaker with expertise in both short and feature films. He is a graduate of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), majoring in Film Direction. His short films have been exhibited and honoured in France, the United…
entries.text: Prof. Maisam Ali Assistant Professor Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Maisam Ali is an independent filmmaker with expertise in both short and feature films. He is a graduate of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), majoring in Film Direction. His short films have been exhibited and honoured in France, the United States, the United Arab Emirates, and India. His feature film In Retreat was the first Indian film to be included in the ACID sidebar section at the Cannes Film Festival 2024, marking a 32-year milestone. The film was screened at the Busan International Film Festival, Sao Paulo Film Festival, Galway Film Festival, Moscow International Film Festival, and Mumbai Film Festival, among others. In addition, he has been teaching filmmaking at several institutions in India. He is presently overseeing Direction practices at Dadasaheb International Film School in the position of Assistant Professor.

About The Prof. Makarand Hastak School of Construction Engineering & Management at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University is envisioned as a nationally and globally eminent academic unit dedicated to advancing the science, engineering, and management of the built environment. At a time when India is undergoing an unprecedented infrastructure revolution—expressways, metros, tunnels, airports, smart cities, green buildings, and industrial corridors—the construction industry demands highly skilled, techno-managerially proficient, and ethically grounded professionals. The School addresses this challenge by integrating engineering precision, management intelligence, technological advancement, and value-based education into a unified academic ecosystem. The School functions under the strategic guidance of global leader Prof. Makarand (Mark) Hastak, Chairman of the Academic and Industry Advisory Board. entries.title: Prof. Mangesh Raghvendra Mahajan
entries.excerpt: Energy, HVAC
entries.text: Prof. Mangesh Raghvendra Mahajan Lecturer Department of Polytechnic Energy, HVAC Prof. Mangesh R. Mahajan is a hardcore, committed teacher and has teaching experience of 28 years. He has also undergone various training programmes, completed MOOC courses, and NITTTR's teacher training programmes. He has completed post graduation in Heat Power engineering from Pune University (Now SPPU). He is pursuing doctoral programme from Sandip University with research work in Diffuser Augmented Wind Turbines. Apart from this HVAC is also his key research interest area. He has published 05 papers till date mostly in SCOPUS journals. He has 03 books to his credit. He has a passion for teaching and developed extensive learning material for the benefit of students. He had been actively involved in the curriculum making process of different courses. He has also developed a lab manual for MSBTE students. He executed successfully a task of creating digital learning resources for MSBTE for a course Emerging trends in Mechanical Engineering. He had given expert lectures on AutoCAD to chemical engineering students. He has been instrumental in organizing various Faculty Development Programmes on campus. He is actively involved in maintaining rapport with industry for various co-curricular activities like Industry Visits, Expert Lectures, Industry oriented workshops, Internships activities. entries.excerpt: Prof. Manish has around 5.5 years of working experience in Industry and academia. His PhD is from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur (QS World-Ranked 215, 2026). He has earlier worked with the Centre for Digital Transformation at the Indian…
entries.text: Prof. Manish Kumar Sharma Assistant Professor Department of Business Prof. Manish has around 5.5 years of working experience in Industry and academia. His PhD is from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur (QS World-Ranked 215, 2026). He has earlier worked with the Centre for Digital Transformation at the Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad (QS World-ranked 27th in Management, 2026), where he contributed to a research project on Responsible AI and FinTech analysis. He has qualified UGC-NET in Management. He has done his post-graduation with MBA in Finance (University Gold Medallist) and B. Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering. His research interests lie in the areas of International Business and Economics, International Trade, Digital Economy, FinTech, Digital Transformation, Artificial Intelligence, Econometrics, Data Science, and Machine Learning. He has gained industry exposure for around four years, working both in India and internationally in East Africa. He earlier worked with the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance, Govt. of India, on a research project titled “Digital Economy and Fintech in India: Opportunities and Challenges” as a PhD research intern. He is a published author with three published articles in reputed journals and has many more in the pipeline. He is also a peer reviewer at Electronic Commerce Research, Springer

entries.title: Prof. Manisha Mohan Wani
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Electrical Power systems
entries.text: Prof. Manisha Mohan Wani Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Electrical Power systems Prof. Manisha Mohan Wani is an accomplished electrical engineer and educator with over 11 years of teaching experience. She graduated with a degree in Electrical Engineering in 2004 from Pune Vidyrthi Gruha’s College of Engineering, Pune University, and completed her ME in Electrical Power Systems in 2012 from the same institution. Since August 1, 2007, Prof. Wani has been working at MIT-WPU, where she has taken on a range of academic and administrative responsibilities. She is a permanent member of the Institution of Engineers, India and has served as a class teacher, ISO & NBA coordinator of CEES department, felicitation committee head of CEES department, and ARC officer for FE, direct second year, and ME admissions, among other duties. Additionally, Prof. Wani has been a paper setting committee member at SPPU and MIT-WPU and a university flying squad member at MIT-WPU. Her expertise in electrical engineering and her dedication to teaching have made her an asset to the academic community.

entries.text: Prof. Manisha Ravindra Ingle Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering VLSI Design Manisha Ingle is an Associate Professor at the School of Electronics and Communication Engineering. She earned her Bachelor's degree in Electronics from Nagpur University and her Master's degree in Electronics and Telecommunication/Control Systems from the University of Pune. With over 10 years of experience in academia, her area of specialization is VLSI Design. She is passionate about teaching and mentoring students, and has guided several undergraduate and graduate level projects in her field of expertise. Apart from teaching, Manisha has also been involved in research and has published numerous research papers in reputed international journals and conferences. Her research interests include low-power VLSI design, digital signal processing, and embedded systems. With her expertise and contributions to the field, Manisha is a valuable member of the School of Electronics and Communication Engineering at her university."

entries.title: Prof. Mrudul Chilmulwar
entries.excerpt: Mrudul Chilmulwar began his journey as a mechanical engineer before discovering that product design was where he truly belonged. He went on to pursue a Master’s in Product Design from the National Institute of Design (NID), Ahmedabad. His work spans…
entries.text: Prof. Mrudul Chilmulwar Assistant Professor Department of Design Mrudul Chilmulwar began his journey as a mechanical engineer before discovering that product design was where he truly belonged. He went on to pursue a Master’s in Product Design from the National Institute of Design (NID), Ahmedabad. His work spans creating livelihood opportunities for underserved communities in Delhi for the Somfy Foundation, designing products for people with visual impairments, and developing structural packaging for brands such as Ponds, Lifebuoy, Indulekha, and more. Over the past four years, he has been in academia, where he enjoys helping students frame better questions, think beyond tools, embrace ambiguity, and develop the depth needed to create meaningful design outcomes. He believes design education is less about teaching what to think and more about nurturing how to think. He has also facilitated design thinking workshops for corporate teams, school principals, and university students. When he isn’t teaching or designing, you’ll likely find him on a frisbee field, playing badminton, experimenting in the kitchen, travelling, or immersed in a good film, book, cartoon, or anime. He enjoys making things with his hands and is endlessly curious about how people think, create, and experience the world around them.

entries.text: Prof. Nikhil Vijaykumar Soniminde Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Prof. Nikhil Soniminde is an Assistant Professor committed to outcome-based education, experiential learning, and research-driven teaching. His teaching approach emphasizes conceptual clarity, hands-on learning, and the integration of emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and image processing to address real-world challenges. Actively engaged in research, publications, and innovation initiatives, he strives to create an inclusive, engaging, and student-centric learning environment. Aligned with the vision of MIT World Peace University, he focuses on fostering critical thinking, ethical values, innovation, and lifelong learning among students.

entries.text: Prof. Piyush Shaha Assistant Professor Department of Business Mr. Piyush Shaha is an accomplished academician with extensive experience in the fields of Statistics, Operations Management, and Business Administration. He holds multiple postgraduate degrees, including an M.Sc. in Statistics, M.B.A. in Operations, and M.Com. in Business Administration, along with a B.Sc. in Statistics. He has also qualified the SET (State Eligibility Test) in Mathematical Sciences and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Statistics. Mr. Shaha has been serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU since January 2024. Prior to this, he held key academic positions at several reputed institutions, including NMIMS University, St. John College of Humanities and Science, The New College – Kolhapur, and others. His teaching career spans over 15 years, covering diverse roles such as full-time faculty, visiting professor, academic coordinator, and secondary school mathematics teacher. He began his academic journey as a Visiting Professor (CHB) in 2005, and since then, has consistently contributed to higher education through teaching, academic administration, and assessment roles. Mr. Shaha has served as an invigilator, moderator, and paper setter across various reputed institutions affiliated to Shivaji University, Mumbai University, Bharati Vidyapeeth, Tilak Maharashtra Vidyapeeth, Mahatma Phule Krishi Vidyapee

I actively integrate emerging technologies such as Cloud, DevOps, and Blockchain into the curriculum, fostering innovation and research orientation among students. Currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Blockchain, I remain committed to advancing MIT-WPU’s vision of value-based, technology-driven education and holistic student development.

entries.excerpt: Prasantanu Mohapatra is a Professor of Practice at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School, MIT-WPU. He is the renowned Director of Photography for ‘Kerala Story’ and has been a part of the Indian film industry for over 35 years. Mohapatra grew up…
entries.text: Prof. Prasantanu Mohapatra Professor of Practice Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute Prasantanu Mohapatra is a Professor of Practice at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School, MIT-WPU. He is the renowned Director of Photography for ‘Kerala Story’ and has been a part of the Indian film industry for over 35 years. Mohapatra grew up toying with photography in a family-owned photo studio. Later he indulged in art and literature that drew him towards cinema that culminated in a degree from FTII (1992) with specialization in Cinematography. In between professional cinematography, he pursued teaching cinema as guest faculty across India, including at FTII.

entries.text: Prof. Pravin Raghunath Minde Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Construction Management, Ferrocement , Sustainable construction Dr. Pravin Minde is working as an Assistant Professor and PG Coordinator (CEM) in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune. He has over 12 years academic & administrative experience. He completed his Ph.D. in the domain of Construction Project Management from MIT-WPU in April 2025. He earned his M.E. in Construction Management in 2014 and B.E. in Civil Engineering in 2012, both from Pune University. His key research interests include project management, sustainable construction materials, and total quality management. He has served as Subject Chairman for UG and PG courses in Pune University, and is a Life Member of ISTE and IWWA, as well as an Associate Member of the Institute of Engineers. He has authored 10+ books and published 21+ research papers in SCI/Scopus/WoS-indexed journals and conferences. He has authored over 5 book chapters with reputed international publishers, including Elsevier, Taylor & Francis, and Wiley. He also holds 3 published patents. Dr. Minde is an ISO 9001:2015 QMS Auditor and Six Sigma Green Belt certified. He actively reviews papers for journals like Springer, Elsevier, and ASCE. Currently, she is pursuing her Ph.D. in Stress Management and Happiness Index of secondary School students from Savitribai Phule Pune University. Priya specializes in Pedagogy of Language, Reading and Reflecting on Text, Guidance and Counseling, Learning and Teaching, and Use of ICT in the Classroom. She is a State Level Certified Trainer from MSCERT for making audio-visual aids for Primary and Secondary School Teachers, and conducts workshops on ‘Making Traditional and Advanced Teaching Aids’ for school teachers. She is also an approved counselor for IGNOU Courses. Priya has presented 10 research papers at national and international conferences and journals and attended over 100 seminars, workshops, conferences, and symposiums at the state/national and international levels. entries.text: Prof. Priya Nagargoje Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering My teaching approach focuses on practical learning, curiosity, and industry relevance. I believe education should go beyond textbooks and help students develop the ability to think critically, solve real-world problems, and adapt to emerging technologies. I am motivated to create an engaging learning environment where students gain both strong conceptual understanding and hands-on experience, especially in areas like cloud computing, DevOps, and AI. Through mentorship, research, and industry collaboration, I aim to contribute to the vision of MIT World Peace University by nurturing skilled, ethical, and innovative professionals who can create meaningful impact in society.

entries.excerpt: Signal Processing
entries.text: Prof. Priyamwada Sachindra Mahajani Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Signal Processing Priyamwada Mahajani is an Assistant Professor in the School of Engineering and Technology at MIT World Peace University. She received her Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Telecommunication and Master of Engineering in Electronics and Telecommunication from Govt. College of Engineering, Pune, India. Ms. Mahajani's research interests lie in the areas of signal processing and error control coding for communication systems. She is passionate about developing innovative techniques to improve the performance of communication systems, and her research has focused on developing novel signal processing algorithms for communication applications. As an Assistant Professor, Ms. Mahajani is committed to teaching and mentoring students, sharing her knowledge and expertise with the next generation of engineers. Her dedication to her students and her contributions to the field of signal processing make her a valuable member of the academic community.

entries.text: Prof Rachit Agarwal Prof. Rachit Agarwal Prof. Rachit Agarwal is an Assistant Professor at the Ramcharan School of Leadership, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with 8 years of teaching and research experience across leading universities in India. MIT-WPU / Faculty / Prof. Rachit Agarwal Faculty Prof. Rachit Agarwal Prof. Rachit Agarwal is an Assistant Professor at the Ramcharan School of Leadership, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with 8 years of teaching and research experience across leading universities in India. Faculty Faculty Assistant Professor Bio Prof. Rachit Agarwal is an Assistant Professor at the Ramcharan School of Leadership, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with 8 years of teaching and research experience across leading universities in India. He is UGC-NET qualified and holds Ph.D. in FinTech, MBA (Finance), MA (Economics), and M. Com (Finance). He has also completed an Executive Development Programme in Financial Data Analytics from IIM Kashipur. Prof. Agarwal has authored and co-authored 30+ research papers and book chapters, including Scopus and ABDC indexed publications. He has presented research at many national and international conferences and received Best Paper Awards. He has served as Academic and Program Coordinator for multiple finance programs and led a Centre of Excellence focused on financial innovation and analytics. His expertise spans financial analysis, FinTech, investment behaviour, ESG, data analytics, and business intelligence, making him a strong academic professional. This aligns closely with MIT-WPU’s vision of developing value-driven, innovative leaders. He is deeply committed to mentoring students not only for career readiness but also to cultivate leadership, in

entries.title: Prof. Rahul Gaikwad
entries.excerpt: Rahul Gaikwad is an illustrator and design tutor with over 7 years of combined experience in the education and creative industries. He holds a Bachelor's degree in Fine Art from Sir J.J. School of Art and a Master's in Illustration and Animation from…
entries.text: Prof. Rahul Gaikwad Assistant Professor Department of Design Rahul Gaikwad is an illustrator and design tutor with over 7 years of combined experience in the education and creative industries. He holds a Bachelor's degree in Fine Art from Sir J.J. School of Art and a Master's in Illustration and Animation from Coventry University, UK. His professional work spans across diverse domains such as illustration, asset building, animation, character design, branding, graphic design and storyboarding. Showcasing his versatility and command over visual storytelling. Rahul's teaching approach blends industry insight with academic rigor, helping students build strong creative foundations. In 2012, he was honored as a young cartoonist by the legendary R.K. Laxman through a recognition by the Times of India, marking a significant early milestone in his career. Passionate about both craft and mentorship, Rahul continues to inspire and guide aspiring designers through his practice and pedagogy.

Kanzade is an Assistant Professor with over 25 years of extensive teaching experience in the field of Computer Science and Applications. Currently associated with MIT World Peace University, he is dedicated to fostering a dynamic and student-centric learning environment through effective pedagogy, research, and academic engagement. He holds an MBA and an M.Sc. in Computer Applications, and is currently pursuing his Ph.D., reflecting his commitment to continuous learning and academic advancement. His areas of expertise span a wide range of subjects, including programming languages (C, C++, Java, Python), Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Cyber Security, and Software Engineering. Mr. Kanzade has actively contributed to research, with multiple publications focusing on mobile marketing, digital transformation, and sustainable business strategies in the evolving digital landscape. His work has been recognized at national and international platforms, including award-winning research contributions. Beyond academics, he has held several key leadership and administrative roles, including Head of Department (BBA-CA), Placement Coordinator, IQAC Member, and Student Development Officer.
