entries.title: Prof. Rashmi Ashwinikumar Rane
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: I believe in fostering analytical thinking, practical problem-solving, and lifelong learning among engineering students. As a faculty member in Computer Engineering, my teaching philosophy emphasizes concept clarity, real-world application, and active…
entries.text: Prof. Rashmi Ashwinikumar Rane Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering I believe in fostering analytical thinking, practical problem-solving, and lifelong learning among engineering students. As a faculty member in Computer Engineering, my teaching philosophy emphasizes concept clarity, real-world application, and active learning through labs, case studies, and collaborative challenges. I am motivated to bridge the gap between theory and industry expectations, especially in core areas like Operating Systems and Systems Programming. Through innovative pedagogy and research engagement, I strive to contribute to MIT-WPU’s vision of developing globally competent, ethically grounded, and socially responsible technology professionals.Professional Affiliations: Life Membership of ISTE

entries.title: Prof. Rashmi Sridhara
entries.excerpt: Rashmi Sridhara is a multidisciplinary Textile and Surface Designer, Print Maker, DIY Content Developer, and Educator with over a decade of professional experience in the design industry. A postgraduate from the National Institute of Design (NID), Paldi,…
entries.text: Prof. Rashmi Sridhara Assistant Professor Department of Design Rashmi Sridhara is a multidisciplinary Textile and Surface Designer, Print Maker, DIY Content Developer, and Educator with over a decade of professional experience in the design industry. A postgraduate from the National Institute of Design (NID), Paldi, and an undergraduate from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), Bangalore, she has worked extensively across sectors including fashion, craft research, home furnishings, and education. Her areas of academic and creative expertise include surface ornamentation, functional fashion, traditional textile crafts, and game-based design learning. Prior to joining MIT-WPU, Rashmi held key roles at Pidilite Industries, where she led design strategy and product development for DIY and craft-based content. She was also part of the core team of the Association of Designers of India (ADI), Mumbai Chapter, and contributed to the Pune design community. As an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, she is deeply committed to fostering innovation, sustainability, and hands-on learning in design education.

entries.title: Prof. Rasika Ramendra Beohar
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: EnTC and Philosophy
entries.text: Prof. Rasika Ramendra Beohar Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering EnTC and Philosophy Meet Prof Rasika Beohar, an accomplished Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With a Master's degree in E&TC Engineering (VLSI and Embedded systems), she brings a wealth of knowledge and expertise from over 15 years of teaching and one year of industrial experience. Prof Beohar has published 9 research papers in reputed international journals and conferences and has attended over 15 conferences and workshops. Her outstanding contributions have been recognized by the Bharatratna Maulana Azad Social, Education and Sports Association, Pune, who awarded her the coveted "Ideal Teacher Award" on 5th Sept 2022. In addition to her academic pursuits, Prof Beohar has also been selected as a "Volunteer" for the UNESCO Body & Mind Wellness Club in Maryland, USA, where she will assist with social media, organize events, and deliver meditation sessions."

entries.title: Prof. Ravi Nikam
entries.excerpt: Mr. Ravi Nikam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Media & Communication at MIT-WPU, bringing extensive experience from both the media industry and academia. He believes that meaningful learning is achieved through a balance of theoretical…
entries.text: Prof. Ravi Nikam Assistant Professor Department of Media and Communication Mr. Ravi Nikam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Media & Communication at MIT-WPU, bringing extensive experience from both the media industry and academia. He believes that meaningful learning is achieved through a balance of theoretical understanding and hands-on practice. His teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, ethical storytelling, and industry relevance, empowering students to become creative, socially responsible media professionals. Passionate about documentary filmmaking and visual storytelling, he is committed to fostering innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and research-oriented learning, contributing to MIT-WPU's vision of developing globally competent, socially conscious, and future-ready students.

entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Renuka Mane is an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 10 years of experience in Computer Engineering. She specializes in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), IoT, Blockchain, and Network Security. She holds a PhD from Bharati…
entries.text: Prof. Renuka Amit Mane Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Renuka Mane is an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 10 years of experience in Computer Engineering. She specializes in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), IoT, Blockchain, and Network Security. She holds a PhD from Bharati Vidyapeeth (2023) and has published 20+ research papers in international journals and conferences. With multiple patents in BCI and network security, she actively mentors students and participates in faculty development programs. Her expertise includes machine learning, neural networks, and IoT frameworks, contributing significantly to research and innovation in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Prof. Rucha Deshpande
entries.excerpt: Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education
entries.text: Prof. Rucha Deshpande Assistant Professor Department of Design Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education Rucha Deshpande is a seasoned design educator with over 14 years of experience in design strategy, user experience, and creative thinking. She has worked with leading brands such as Lifestyles and Reliance Trends as a Concept Designer for retail store experiences before transitioning into academia with institutions including the Symbiosis Institute of Design and MIT-WPU, Pune. An alumna of NID Ahmedabad and a certified UX Designer from IIT Delhi, she brings a strong foundation in user-centred design and cross-functional collaboration. Rucha is currently pursuing her PhD at IIT Hyderabad, where she continues to thrive at the intersection of design, user experience, and innovation. Known for her adaptability, empathy, and strategic thinking, she consistently contributes to shaping meaningful learning experiences and future-ready design frameworks.

Sachin is currently pursuing his Ph.D. and holds an MTech degree. His expertise lies in the fields of Additive Manufacturing and Hybrid Manufacturing. He is passionate about sharing his knowledge with his students and helping them excel in their careers.

entries.title: Prof. Sagar Sunil Kolapkar
entries.excerpt: Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies
entries.text: Prof. Sagar Sunil Kolapkar Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies Prof. Sagar Kolapkar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University, Pune. He received his B.E. in Civil Engineering (2010) from Pune University and M.Tech (Transportation Systems Engineering) in 2013 from IIT Guwahati. His research focuses on Parking Demand Management and Supply Regulation and Warm Mix Asphalt. He is a certified Road Safety Auditor by the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways and Indian Roads Congress. He has actively worked with Pune Municipal Corporation on Parking Policy. He qualified GATE in 2011 and has published several research papers in top-ranked journals in Civil Engineering. He has been an active reviewer of international journal Materials Today (Elsevier). He has written a book on Advanced Transportation Engineering. He is actively working as Infrastructure Cordinator at Department of Civil Engineering.

entries.title: Prof. Sandeep Sampatrao Sathe
entries.excerpt: Corrosion in reinforced concrete, Fiber reinforced concrete, Steel Structures, Pervious Concrete, Ferrocement Technology, Recycled Aggregate Concrete, Concrete with Admixtures and Non-Destructive Testing
entries.text: Prof. Sandeep Sampatrao Sathe Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Corrosion in reinforced concrete, Fiber reinforced concrete, Steel Structures, Pervious Concrete, Ferrocement Technology, Recycled Aggregate Concrete, Concrete with Admixtures and Non-Destructive Testing Dr. Sandeep Sathe is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He earned his bachelor’s degree in civil engineering from the University of Pune in 2011, followed by a Master’s in Structural Engineering from MIT, Pune in 2013. He was awarded a Ph.D. in 2024, with a specialization in Corrosion in Reinforced Concrete. His research expertise spans a wide array of domains within Civil and Structural Engineering, including Corrosion in Reinforced Concrete Structures, Fiber Reinforced Concrete, Concrete Durability, Concrete with Admixtures, Lightweight and Pervious Concrete, Bituminous (Flexible) and Rigid Pavements, Recycled Aggregate Concrete, Non- Destructive Testing (NDT), Structural Health Monitoring, Retrofitting of Structures, Ferrocement Technology, Geopolymer Concrete, and Sustainable Construction Materials. With over 15 postgraduate research projects guided to completion and three Ph.D. candidates currently under supervision, he has made a consistent contribution to academic mentorship. He currently teaches UI/UX, Graphic Design, and Portfolio Development, guiding students to develop both conceptual clarity and practical skills. Actively engaged with the evolving landscape of visual communication, he encourages innovation, critical thinking, and industry readiness among aspiring designers.

Idhate is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. With 19 years of teaching experience, she is dedicated to nurturing analytical thinking, innovation, and research aptitude among students. Her expertise spans Digital Electronics, Algorithms, Computer Organization, Cloud Computing, Analog Communication, and MMA. She believes in outcome-based, student-centric learning aligned with industry and research needs. With notable publications and a patented innovation, she actively contributes to academic excellence, technological advancement, and the university’s vision of value-based, research-driven education.

entries.title: Prof. Seema Jitendra Patil
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Machine Learning
entries.text: Prof. Seema Jitendra Patil Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Machine Learning Prof. Mrs. Seema J. Patil is a highly accomplished computer engineer, who graduated with distinction from SSBT's COET, Jalgaon in 2006. She completed her Master's degree from MIT Pune and went on to work as a lecturer in SMIT College, Jalgaon for a year. With a successful software testing training program from STC Technologies, Pune and an IBM certification in DB2 fundamental, she joined MIT-WPU as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering, where she has been teaching for the past 15 years. Prof. Patil has a wide range of teaching experience and has guided many undergraduate level projects. She has also coordinated various subjects and organized industrial visits for students. Her research work has been published in several international journals. Prof. Patil's dedication to her students and passion for teaching has made her an inspiring mentor and guide.

He is having more than 24 years of teaching experiance. He holds Masters of Engineering in Design Engineering Domain and Currently pursuing Ph. D. in Mechanical Engineering. Master's work was associated with peeling strength of different Biopolymers. Research work is going on Peeling and Fatigue Behavior of Adhesively Joint Different Biopolymers. A dedicated educator, he focuses on inspiring students to engage critically with literature while contributing meaningfully to the study of diverse perspectives. His commitment to fostering intellectual growth reflects his belief in the transformative power of education.

She is known for guiding many graduate and postgraduate students, encouraging a culture of excellence in their work. Prof. Kinger has written many articles for both national and international journals, showcasing her dedication to tackling important issues in AI and explainable AI. Recognized for her innovative thinking, Professor Shakti Kinger remains at the forefront of shaping the future of technology through her expertise and leadership.

entries.excerpt: Dr. Sheetal Girase is an enthusiastic educator and dedicated researcher affiliated with Maharashtra Institute of Technology, World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, where she has been serving since January 2002. She has earned her Ph.D. from MIT World…
entries.text: Prof. Sheetal Pravin Girase Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Sheetal Girase is an enthusiastic educator and dedicated researcher affiliated with Maharashtra Institute of Technology, World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune, where she has been serving since January 2002. She has earned her Ph.D. from MIT World Peace University, Pune, India after completing her Post-graduation from Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune and Graduation from SSVP’s College of Engineering, Dhule. Over the years, she has taught a diverse range of subjects including: Theory of Computation, Database Management Systems, Software Engineering, Advanced Database Management Systems, Data Warehousing and Data Mining, Big Data Analytics. Her academic journey is driven by a passion for both teaching as well as research. She has published more than 20 papers in reputed International Journals with her research domains as recommendation Systems and Computer Vision, Artificial Intelligence. Throughout her career she has supervised numerous Bachelors and Masters students for projects.

entries.text: Prof. Sheetal Suresh Rajapurkar Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Applications Ms. Sheetal Rajapurkar has completed her M.Sc. Electronics from Shivaji University, Kolhapur and has qualified National Eligibility Test (UGC – NET) in December 2012. She is pursuing her Ph.D. from Shivaji University, Kolhapur. She has a total of 17 years of Teaching Experience. At present, she is working as an Assistant Professor in School of Computer Science , MIT WPU. Her research areas include Microcontroller, Microprocessor, Anettena, Fuzzy Logic, Digital Image Processing, etc. She has published 14 research papers in National and International Conferences. Her 6 patents are published. She authored two books namely Analog Systems and 8051 Microcontroller. She has attended many workshops, seminars and conferences at the National and International Level. She worked as Student Development Officer for Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. She worked as College Examination Officer for Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. She is presently working as an Assistant Controller of Examinations at MIT-WPU, Pune. She is also working as ERP Coordinator for Faculty of Science, MIT-WPU. She worked as a Secretory for Students Grievance Redressal Committee, MIT-WPU. She is working as UG Program Coordinator in Department of Computer Science and Applications, MIT WPU.

entries.title: Prof. Shiv Havgirao Sutar
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Wireless Sensor Networks, Internet of Things, and High-Performance Computing.
entries.text: Prof. Shiv Havgirao Sutar Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Wireless Sensor Networks, Internet of Things, and High-Performance Computing. Prof. Shiv H. Sutar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Engineering and Technology, MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. With over 15 years of experience in teaching, he is a flexible, dynamic, and enthusiastic professional in the field of wireless sensor networks, internet of things, and high-performance computing. He holds a technical background and has delivered lectures on topics such as compilers, operating systems, system software, and IoT. Prof. Sutar seeks challenging positions in technical institutes that require innovation, creativity, and dedication, enabling him to work in a fast-paced and challenging environment.

entries.title: Prof. Shraddha Salvi
entries.excerpt: Visual Analysis, Media and Communication Studies
entries.text: Prof. Shraddha Salvi Assistant Professor Department of Visual Arts Visual Analysis, Media and Communication Studies Ms. Shraddha Salvi is an Assistant Professor at the School of Visual Arts, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over two years of teaching experience, she brings a creative and research-driven approach to the classroom. Her academic and creative interests lie at the intersection of visual arts and cultural studies. Her areas of interest include typography, visual storytelling, semiotics, and the role of visual media in preserving cultural narratives. Ms. Salvi is passionate about teaching creatively to make learning more engaging and accessible for students. She designs interactive assignments that foster critical thinking, conceptual clarity, and visual experimentation. She has worked extensively on themes such as expressive typography, semiotic analysis, and cultural representation in cinema and print. Her approach to teaching emphasizes student involvement, encouraging them to explore ideas deeply while building strong foundations in visual communication and cultural understanding.

She has completed MCom with Costing specialisation, MBA in Financial Management, CMA Inter, UGC - NET in Commerce and currently pursuing PhD in Behavioral Finance from Symbiosis International (Deemed)University Pune. Working with Department of Commerce and Accounting as an Internship and Placement Coordinator and IQAC coordinator.

entries.excerpt: Dr. Shridevi Karande has been working as an assistant professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University since 2005. With over 21 years of experience, she has managed various academic responsibilities and placement-related tasks. She holds a PhD…
entries.text: Prof. Shridevi Chandraprakash Karande Associate Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Dr. Shridevi Karande has been working as an assistant professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University since 2005. With over 21 years of experience, she has managed various academic responsibilities and placement-related tasks. She holds a PhD in the AI and ML healthcare domain, and her research project, titled "Detection of Pre-Dementia," received funding from IBM. She has authored more than 20 research papers and articles in international journals and conferences. Her research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Big Data Analytics, Cloud Computing, and Healthcare Analytics.

entries.title: Prof. Shrikant Krishnanath Yadav

entries.text: Prof. Shrikant Krishnanath Yadav Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Automobile, Robotics, IoT Shrikant K. Yadav is a mechanical engineer with over 8 years of experience in the field. He received his Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering from Vidyapratishthan's College of Engineering, Baramati in 2010, and went on to complete his Master's degree in Mechanical Automotive Engineering from Sinhgad College of Engineering, Pune in 2013. Mr. Yadav has published 8 research papers in international journals and is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering. He is dedicated to advancing the field of Mechanical Engineering through his research and teaching.

entries.title: Prof. Shruti Shukla
entries.excerpt: Shruti Shukla is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). Shruti Shukla is a passionate Graphic Designer with over 8 years of experience in the design industry and 2+ years in…
entries.text: Prof. Shruti Shukla Assistant Professor Department of Visual Arts Shruti Shukla is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). Shruti Shukla is a passionate Graphic Designer with over 8 years of experience in the design industry and 2+ years in the teaching field. My specialization lies in Visualization, with a strong focus on conceptual thinking, aesthetic composition, and impactful communication through design. Throughout my career, I’ve worked extensively across various domains, including Outdoor Advertising, Publication Design, Illustration, and Graphic Design. These diverse experiences have helped me build a strong understanding of both print and digital mediums, allowing me to deliver practical, creative, and result-oriented design solutions. As an educator, I’m committed to bridging the gap between classroom learning and real-world industry practices. I believe in hands-on learning and strive to provide my students with an immersive and practical understanding of design workflows, industry expectations, and creative problem-solving.Whether working on client projects or mentoring students, I bring the same energy and enthusiasm to every opportunity—dedicated to shaping meaningful visual narratives and nurturing the next generation of creative thinkers.

entries.title: Prof. Shubhangi Rajesh Shekokar
entries.excerpt: Prof. Shubhangi Shekokar is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She received her B.E. in Civil Engineering from Government College of Engineering, Amravati in…
entries.text: Prof. Shubhangi Rajesh Shekokar Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Prof. Shubhangi Shekokar is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She received her B.E. in Civil Engineering from Government College of Engineering, Amravati in 2004 and her M. Tech in Geotechnical Engineering from College of Engineering Pune (COEP) in 2010. Presently, she has completed Ph.D. in Environmental Engineering from SGBAU, Amravati University under the guidance of Dr. Y.R.M. Rao. She is a life member of the Indian geotechnical Society (IGS), Institute of Public Health Engineers (IPHE) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE). She has been teaching at the institute since August 2008 and has experience teaching various subjects like BCE, Survey, Soil Mechanics, Foundation Engineering, Building Design and Drawing, Estimation and Costing, Valuation and Slope Stabilization Techniques. Dr. Shekokar has published 5 research papers in Scopus Indexed Journals, 2 in National Journals, and 6 in International Conferences.

entries.title: Prof. Shubhika Malara
entries.excerpt: Biophilia, Type Research, Indian Heritage and Visual Cultures.
entries.text: Prof. Shubhika Malara Assistant Professor Department of Design Biophilia, Type Research, Indian Heritage and Visual Cultures. Shubhika Malara is a passionate Visual Artist, Designer and Educator, with over 5 years of expertise in Communication and Outreach, and a strong interest in sustainability, public spaces, heritage and culture studies. As an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU School of Design in Visual Communication Design, she facilitates courses on Design History, Strategy and Research Methods, Branding, Illustration, Typography and Publication Design. She is a strong advocate of impact-driven design solutions, and attempts to nurture lateral thinking and interdisciplinary approach to problem-solving. Shubhika holds a M.Des. in Graphic Design from the National Institute of Design (Ahmedabad), and B.Des. in Public Space Design from Srishti Institute of Art Design and Technology, (Bengaluru), with an Exchange semester at Massachusetts College of Art and Design (Boston, USA). Highlights of her work include contributing creatives for Science Gallery Bengaluru’s exhibition-season ‘CARBON’ (2024), and designing Art Deco Mumbai’s ‘Map for the Victorian Gothic and Art Deco Ensembles of Mumbai - a UNESCO World Heritage Site’ (2019).

entries.excerpt: Shweta Gade is an Architect and Industrial Product Designer. She is an esteemed alumna of the National Institute of Design (NID) Ahmedabad, where she completed her advanced studies in Product Design (M.DES) in 2009 after her Bachelors in Architecture…
entries.text: Prof. Shweta Hitesh Gade Assistant Professor Department of Design Shweta Gade is an Architect and Industrial Product Designer. She is an esteemed alumna of the National Institute of Design (NID) Ahmedabad, where she completed her advanced studies in Product Design (M.DES) in 2009 after her Bachelors in Architecture (B.Arch.) from Savitribai Phule Pune University. Currently she is pursuing Ph.D. in Design. During her Master studies she has received awards for her projects and as a young designer she has gained recognition from design fraternity. Her expertise spans across various domains, including industrial design, design thinking, and design research. She has academic teaching experience over 15 years as she has taught at various premier Design and Architecture institutes in Pune. In her academic capacity, she has conducted specialized master courses as a visiting faculty in product design, product ergonomics, form Studies and Design thinking and interior space and furniture design. Before joining academia, she has worked in the industry as industrial designer and designed products in lighting and renewable energy domain. As an architect she has worked on residential and commercial interior design projects for more than 10 years as practicing freelance design consultant. Her career trajectory is marked by a continuous integration of academic theory and practical design applic

entries.excerpt: Research Interests: Marketing Management, Sustainability, Circular Economy
entries.text: Prof. Sonal Manohar Muluk Assistant Professor Department of Business Research Interests: Marketing Management, Sustainability, Circular Economy Dr. Sonal Muluk is an accomplished academician with over 16 years of experience in higher education. She currently serves as an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), Pune. Specializing in Marketing Management, she is widely recognized for her student-centric and application-oriented teaching methodology. Dr. Muluk is also a recognized Ph.D. Research Supervisor, mentoring doctoral scholars in the field of Marketing Management. In addition, she guides postgraduate students in fieldwork, research projects, and practical application of business concepts. Dr. Muluk has further enriched her teaching approach by completing the prestigious “Teaching with Cases: Engage, Energize, and Challenge Your Students” program at Harvard Business School, gaining global insights into effective case-based pedagogy and classroom engagement strategies. Her academic contributions include the publication of over 25 research papers in reputed national and international journals and conferences, including Scopus-indexed and ABDC-listed outlets. She has authored several book chapters and contributed to edited volumes published by leading international publishers such as Springer, Taylor & Francis,

He holds a Master of Science in Blockchain Technology from MIT World Peace University, where he was awarded a Gold Medal, and a B.Tech in Information Technology from IIIT Vadodara. With one year of teaching experience and over four years as a Software Engineer, he brings valuable industry perspective to academia. His expertise includes Blockchain Technology, Cryptography, and Mathematics. He teaches Blockchain Technology & Applications, Ethereum Technology, Smart Contracts Programming, and Blockchain & IoT Applications, and has a keen interest in the entertainment industry, with two years of experience as an Assistant Director.

entries.text: Prof. Swapnil Virendra Chalwadi Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts behavioral economics, monetary economics, gender, and development. Swapnil Chalwadi is an accomplished Assistant Professor and Program Head at the School of Liberal Arts in MIT WPU, Pune, India. With over 8 years of experience in teaching, he specializes in the macroeconomic and monetary aspects of economic development. He has earned an M.Phil degree in Economics from the University of Hyderabad and a Master's in Economics from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune. Swapnil's deep interest and expertise in macroeconomics have helped him publish research papers in reputed journals, making him a valuable asset to the academic community.

entries.text: Prof. Tejaswini Bharati Assistant Professor Department of Design https://www.behance.net/tejaswibharati Tejaswini Bharati is a multidisciplinary designer with three years of professional experience in London. Currently an Assistant Professor at MIT WPU, she blends her practical expertise with a passion for education. She specializes in creating visually captivating, user-centric designs, utilizing her proficiency in tools like Adobe Suite and Figma. Her detail-oriented approach, paired with exceptional communication skills, allows her to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and deliver top-notch design solutions, even under tight deadlines. Tejaswini thrives in fast-paced, dynamic environments, always eager to elevate projects to new heights. Whether working on a creative project or guiding students, her passion for design shines through in her ability to craft impactful visual experiences. Her commitment to innovation and design excellence makes her a valuable asset in any creative endeavor.

entries.title: Prof. Vandana Swapnil Jagtap
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science
entries.text: Prof. Vandana Swapnil Jagtap Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science Prof. Vandana Jagtap is an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. She has an M.Tech degree in Computer Science & Engineering from the University of Pune and is currently pursuing her Ph.D. in the same field. With 23 years of teaching experience, her areas of interest include Advanced Machine Learning, High-performance Computing, Distributed and Parallel computing, Cloud Computing, Algorithms, and Assistive Technology. Prof. Jagtap is the author of the book "Cloud Computing Policies, Outsourcing and Computing Keys: Cloud Computing policies" and has 50+ research publications in refereed international journals and conferences. She has also reviewed many refereed International journals and worked as an Advisory member and Editorial Board Member. Prof. Jagtap has delivered many expert lectures and is a member of various professional bodies such as ACM, CSI, ISTE, IEI. Under her guidance, 25 B.Tech (CSE) Project Groups and 13 M.Tech (CSE) Scholars have completed their projects.

Her areas of expertise include Signal Processing, Embedded Systems, and Digital Communication.

entries.title: Prof. Vidya Balaji Patil
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
entries.excerpt: In a variety of areas, including Python programming, machine learning, microprocessors, digital electronics, computer architecture, and network security, my teaching strategy is focused on conceptual clarity, hands-on learning, and problem-based learning.…
entries.text: Prof. Vidya Balaji Patil Assistant Professor Department of Computer Science and Engineering In a variety of areas, including Python programming, machine learning, microprocessors, digital electronics, computer architecture, and network security, my teaching strategy is focused on conceptual clarity, hands-on learning, and problem-based learning. Through projects, coding exercises, and using real world analogy, I encourage students to apply theoretical knowledge to practical issues. I want to develop technically proficient and socially conscious engineers by encouraging curiosity, creativity, and ethical responsibility. This strategy is consistent with MIT-WPU's objective to merge academics, technology, research, and social innovation for societal impact and global peace, as well as its vision of "Powering the Future" through education, innovation, and culture.

entries.title: Prof. Walter Leal Filho
entries.excerpt: Vice Chancellor
entries.text: Prof. Walter Leal Filho · WPU Goa Prof. Walter Leal Filho Vice Chancellor WPU Goa / People / Prof. Walter Leal Filho University leadership Prof. Walter Leal Filho Vice Chancellor A Message from the "Welcome to the World Peace University (WPU) GŌA - a visionary institution established to redefine the landscape of higher education. Our campus is not just a place of learning; it is a sanctuary for holistic development, where academic rigour meets the tranquility of nature." By breaking down traditional silos between subjects, we empower students to think critically, innovate freely, and develop a comprehensive understanding of the world-emerging as both experts and versatile thinkers. Three Pillars of Excellence Transdisciplinary Learning India's first university to fully embrace a transdisciplinary model, dissolving subject silos so students engage with the world's complexity as it truly is interconnected and multifaceted. Sustainability at the Core Sustainability guides our curriculum, operations, and campus design. Students don't just learn about environmental stewardship-they live it, on a campus that acts as a living laboratory. Holistic Development Fully residential and community-driven, WPU GŌA creates spaces for collaboration, creativity, and cultural engagement because education extends far beyond the classroom walls. entries.excerpt: Discover a learning journey in Goa that blends rigorous academics with hands-on projects, collaboration, and inspiration from nature
entries.text: Programme-wise Eligibility Criteria and Entrance Exam Requirements · WPU Goa Programme-wise Eligibility Criteria and Entrance Exam Requirements Discover a learning journey in Goa that blends rigorous academics with hands-on projects, collaboration, and inspiration from nature WPU Goa / Admissions / Programme-wise Eligibility Criteria and Entrance Exam Requirements Admissions Programme-wise Eligibility Criteria and Entrance Exam Requirements Discover a learning journey in Goa that blends rigorous academics with hands-on projects, collaboration, and inspiration from nature Note Candidates must have successfully completed the Class 12/ 10+2 examination (or equivalent) from a recognized board such as the International Baccalaureate (IB), Indian School Certificate (ISC), Cambridge International / CAIE, Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), or State Boards. Candidates with grades/GPA/credit points must submit an equivalence/conversion certificate from the respective examination board or the Association of Indian Universities (AIU). *For B.Des. candidates, portfolio submission is mandatory. Each candidate is solely responsible for ensuring that they meet the prescribed eligibility criteria for the Undergraduate Programme at WPU GŌA. WPU GŌA reserves the right to verify eligibility at any stage of the admission process and to reject or cancel the candidature or admission of any candidate found to be ineligible. In such cases, the admission shall be deemed null and void, irrespective of the stage of the admission process or any prior communication of selection or admission. If results for any of the above exams are awaited at the time of registration, candidates must upload the respective admit card or registration acknowledgment. entries.title: Promoting Wellness Across Borders : Yoga Session for University of Mondragon Delegation
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU hosts a yoga session for a delegation from the University of Mondragon, Spain, promoting wellness and cultural exchange.
entries.text: Yoga Session for Mondragon University Delegation : MIT-WPU News Promoting Wellness Across Borders : Yoga Session for University of Mondragon Delegation MIT-WPU hosts a yoga session for a delegation from the University of Mondragon, Spain, promoting wellness and cultural exchange. MIT-WPU / News / Promoting Wellness Across Borders : Yoga Session for University of Mondragon Delegation News Promoting Wellness Across Borders : Yoga Session for University of Mondragon Delegation MIT-WPU hosts a yoga session for a delegation from the University of Mondragon, Spain, promoting wellness and cultural exchange. Happenings Happenings News Promoting Wellness Across Borders : Yoga Session for University of Mondragon Delegation On 07 February 2025, MIT-World Peace University hosted a rejuvenating yoga session for 38 students and 5 faculty members from the University of Mondragon, Spain. The session took place on the ground floor of the Chanakya building, offering a serene and peaceful environment. The session was conducted by Prof. Akash Homkar, Assistant Professor, Department of Wellness and Yogic Science, with the able assistance of his MSc students. The participants, both students and faculty, thoroughly enjoyed the session, which ran from 8 AM to 9 AM, leaving everyone refreshed and energized. Such initiatives continue to reflect MIT-WPU’s commitment to fostering global harmony and well-being through the practice of yoga and other collaborative programs. Related public links Happenings

entries.title: Public Health as a Career: What Every Medical Graduate Should Think About
entries.excerpt: Is public health the right path after MBBS or BDS? Dr. Prajkta Bhide explores how MPH opens careers in WHO, NGOs, policy & community health. Read now.
entries.text: Public Health Career Guide for Medical Graduates Public Health as a Career: What Every Medical Graduate Should Think About Is public health the right path after MBBS or BDS? Dr. Prajkta Bhide explores how MPH opens careers in WHO, NGOs, policy & community health. Read now. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Public Health as a Career: What Every Medical Graduate Should Think About Faculty Articles Public Health as a Career: What Every Medical Graduate Should Think About Is public health the right path after MBBS or BDS? Dr. Prajkta Bhide explores how MPH opens careers in WHO, NGOs, policy & community health. Read now. 1,155 words 5 min read For many medical and dental graduates, the path after graduation appears to have only two clear directions: prepare for PG entrance exams or enter clinical practice. Both are valuable choices. But they are not the only ones. There is another path where a doctor can use medical knowledge, understand disease more deeply, and still serve thousands, sometimes lakhs, of people through better systems, policies, and prevention. That path is public health. A growing number of MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS, and allied health graduates are now considering public health as a serious career option. This shift is not accidental. It comes from a larger realisation that healthcare does not begin and end inside hospitals. Many of the conditions doctors treat every day are shaped much earlier by nutrition, sanitation, air quality, work conditions, public policy, health awareness, access to care, and the strength of local health systems. Clinical medicine is designed to respond to illness. A patient comes in with symptoms. The doctor examines, diagnoses, treats, and follows up. This work is essential and deeply meaningful. Public health asks a different question. Wh

entries.title: Public Health Leadership for a New Era
entries.excerpt: Public health is no longer a subject that sits quietly in policy papers, research journals, or government offices. It is now part of everyday life.
entries.text: Public Health Leadership for a New Era | Facutly Article Public Health Leadership for a New Era Public health is no longer a subject that sits quietly in policy papers, research journals, or government offices. It is now part of everyday life. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Public Health Leadership for a New Era Faculty Articles Public Health Leadership for a New Era Public health is no longer a subject that sits quietly in policy papers, research journals, or government offices. It is now part of everyday life. 1,273 words 6 min read Why the Master of Public Health Matters More Than Ever Public health is no longer a subject that sits quietly in policy papers, research journals, or government offices. It is now part of everyday life. The COVID-19 pandemic made this clear to the whole world. It showed how quickly a health crisis can affect jobs, families, schools, travel, businesses, and entire economies. It also showed us something deeper. A country's strength does not depend only on hospitals and doctors. It also depends on surveillance systems, prevention strategies, public communication, health data, community trust, and trained professionals who can think beyond individual treatment. This is where public health becomes essential. As a discipline, public health asks a very different question from clinical medicine. Clinical care often asks, How do we treat this patient? Public health asks, Why are so many people falling ill in the first place, and what can we do to prevent it? That difference matters. India, like many countries, is facing health challenges that cannot be solved only inside hospitals. entries.text: MIT-WPU MBA Students Attend INSEAD International Immersion Programme Ram Charan School of Leadership Sends First-Year MBAs to INSEAD for International Immersion Programme MBA students from MIT-WPU's Ram Charan School of Leadership gain global exposure through an international immersion programme at INSEAD, one of the world's leading business schools. MIT-WPU / Events / Ram Charan School of Leadership Sends First-Year MBAs to INSEAD for International Immersion Programme Events Ram Charan School of Leadership Sends First-Year MBAs to INSEAD for International Immersion Programme MBA students from MIT-WPU's Ram Charan School of Leadership gain global exposure through an international immersion programme at INSEAD, one of the world's leading business schools. Events Happenings Ram Charan School of Leadership Sends First-Year MBAs to INSEAD for International Immersion Programme Events First-year MBA students from Ram Charan School of Leadership recently participated in an exceptional international immersion programme at the prestigious INSEAD in Singapore, focusing on leadership and strategy. The Leadership and Strategy Programme ran intensively from April 30 to May 1, 2024. It provided our students valuable exposure to global business and management perspectives. Whirlpool-img Designed for high-impact learning, the programme was divided into four intensive sessions spread across the two days. This structure allowed for deep engagement with the material and immediate application of learning. The esteemed programme director at INSEAD, Amitava Chattopadhyay, led three of the sessions, sharing his expertise on strategic decision-making and leadership. Students actively participated in Amitava's sessions by engaging in case study discussions and breakout room activities. These ac

Happenings Happenings News The Ram Charan School of Leadership (RSOL) at MIT World Peace University, Pune, has taken a significant step towards enhancing global exposure for its students through a strategic international collaboration. In the presence of Hon’ble Vice Chancellor Dr. R. M. Chitnis, MIT World Peace University, Pune formalised a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Twente (Netherlands) and Recess College (UK). This collaboration introduces a 2-week Global Immersion Programme for RSOL MBA students, designed to provide a well-rounded international learning experience. Key features of the programme include : Ram Charan School of Leadership Signs Strategic MoU for Global Immersion Programme A customised Leadership Live Lab Simulation International academic and industry exposure A 3-credit certification integrated into the MBA curriculum Through this initiative, students will gain global perspectives, engage with real-world leadership challenges, and develop skills essential for today’s dynamic business environment. This partnership reflects RSOL

entries.title: Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU Signs MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI)
entries.excerpt: Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU signs an MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI), Germany, boosting global exposure for MBA students.
entries.text: MIT-WPU RSoL Signs MoU with Berlin's BSBI, Germany Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU Signs MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI) Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU signs an MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI), Germany, boosting global exposure for MBA students. MIT-WPU / News / Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU Signs MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI) News Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU Signs MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI) Ram Charan School of Leadership, MIT-WPU signs an MoU with Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI), Germany, boosting global exposure for MBA students. 330 words 2 min read Happenings Happenings News In a significant step towards enhancing global learning opportunities and international exposure for its students, the Ram Charan School of Leadership (RSoL), MIT World Peace University, Pune, formalised a strategic Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Berlin School of Business and Innovation (BSBI), Germany. The MoU was formalised in the presence of Dr. R. M. Chitnis, Hon'ble Vice Chancellor, MIT-WPU, Pune, marking the beginning of a collaborative partnership aimed at enriching the academic experience of MBA students through international engagement and cross-cultural learning opportunities. The collaboration reflects RSoL's continued commitment to integrating global perspectives into management education and preparing students to thrive in an increasingly interconnected business environment. Through this partnership, students will gain access to opportunities that foster international exposure, experiential learning, and industry-relevant insights, enabling them to develop a broader understanding of global business practice

entries.title: Research — Department of Biosciences and Technology
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Biosciences and Technology Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. 01 Cancer, cell & regenerative biology Cancer cell biology, genome stability, stem cells, tissue engineering, bone biology, and protein aggregation. 02 Genomics, bioinformatics & microbiomes Genomics, transcriptomics, metagenomics, microbial and human microbiomes, protein structure, and computational biology. 03 Microbiology, immunology & bioprocessing Applied and environmental microbiology, immune profiling, microbial metabolites, enzyme technology, and bioprocess development. 04 Plant molecular biology & sustainable biotechnology Plant development, defence and metabolism; plant–microbe interactions; genome editing; and crop improvement. 05 Biosensors, diagnostics & bioimaging Electrochemical and optical biosensors, pathogen and molecular diagnostics, bioimaging, and preclinical workflows.

entries.title: Research — Department of Chemistry
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Chemistry Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. 01 Nanomaterials, molecular materials & photonics Nanomaterial synthesis, plasmonics and nanophotonics, metal-organic frameworks, molecular nanomagnets, switches, and fluorescent sensors. 02 Organic synthesis, catalysis & green chemistry Synthetic organic chemistry, ionic liquids, organocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysis, asymmetric synthesis, biocatalysis, and green synthesis. 03 Polymers, coatings & sustainable materials Liquid-crystalline and conducting polymers, biopolymers, nanocomposites, protective coatings, packaging, microplastics, and water purification. 04 Inorganic & environmental chemistry Metal complexes, wastewater treatment, pollutant degradation, and materials chemistry for environmental applications.

entries.title: Research — Department of Environmental Studies
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Environmental Studies Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU. Research The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action.

entries.title: Research — Department of Mathematics and Statistics
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Mathematics and Statistics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets.

entries.title: Research — Department of Physics
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Physics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. 01 Quantum, optics & photonics Nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, nanophotonics, photonic systems, plasmonics, and spectroscopy. Photonics Research Group Quantum Science & Technology Group 02 Materials, devices & nanoscience Semiconductor and dielectric thin films, nanomaterials and nanocomposites, sensors, photodetectors, and functional devices. 03 Astrophysics & fundamental physics Cosmic magnetic fields, galactic dynamics, high-energy theory, gravitation, and the mathematical structure of fundamental interactions. 04 Applied & computational physics Soft matter, quantum chemistry and computational physics; radiation processing of polymers; liquid acoustics; dielectric spectroscopy; and corrosion science.

It is imperative to recognise that energy serves as the bedrock upon which a nation's progress and economic development are built. Explore groundbreaking AI research, innovations, and advancements shaping the future of technology. Research Institute of Artificial Intelligence (AI) The Institute of AI, established in 2021, pioneers a novel approach by deriving inspiration from nature to develop AI solutions across diverse disciplines. The researchers of the institute mathematically model the intelligence in nature and then code it in the computer environment converting it into Artificial Intelligence. The institute is committed to ensuring that its research translates into practical solutions that benefit society, aligning technological progress with ethical standards and contributing positively to global issues. Importantly, the faculty members and students from different disciplines are expected to be involved in the institute projects. The institute actively collaborates with universities abroad as well as industries. The institute actively and regularly organizes different workshops and conferences. The interested faculty members/students can join this research endeavor. anand-kulkarni To be a leading Artificial Intelligence (AI) research institute that empowers the next generation of AI and Machine Learning (ML) researchers and practitioners to drive innovation by solving socially important problems. Karad Sir, is set to receive the prestigious Arutchelvar Dr. N. Mahalingam Award. This esteemed recognition, bestowed by the Kumarguru Institutions in Coimbatore, is a testament to Dr. Karad's exemplary contributions to society. VD_KARAD-award-img The Arutchelvar Dr. N. Mahalingam Award, named after the visionary founder of the Shakthi Group, was established in 2014 to honour individuals who have made significant strides in advancing industrial, social, and cultural landscapes, mirroring Dr. Mahalingam's illustrious legacy. VD_KARAD-award-img It celebrates luminaries who have significantly contributed to the growth and betterment of our community, echoing Dr. Mahalingam's multifaceted legacy as an industrialist, parliamentarian and dedicated follower of Gandhian principles. entries.title: Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation
entries.excerpt: Discover the Revvity & MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence, a hub for bioimaging and biophotonics research. Learn about our industry-academia collaboration.
entries.text: MIT-WPU & Revvity: Centre for Bioimaging Innovation Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Discover the Revvity & MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence, a hub for bioimaging and biophotonics research. Learn about our industry-academia collaboration. MIT-WPU / Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Revvity Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Discover the Revvity & MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence, a hub for bioimaging and biophotonics research. Learn about our industry-academia collaboration. Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Revvity – the Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation at MIT-WPU is an outcome of an innovative blend between the industry and academia to facilitate research, development, and innovation in the field of bioimaging and bio-photonics. The CoE involves state-of-the-art facilities including equipment such as confocal microscopes, flow cytometers, live animal imaging, microfluidics, gene extraction and advanced imaging software. Researchers of different domains such as life sciences, chemistry, physics, clinicians, pathologists, AI, engineering, and computer science would collaborate on valuable projects dedicated to developing novel imaging techniques and technologies. The centre will also offer training programmes and workshops for students, as well as research professionals, to stay updated about the latest happenings in their respective fields. This COE along with the advanced labs developed will be pivotal in pre-clinical development for new drug discovery. The entire workflow from target discovery, lead development and preclinical development can be carried ou

entries.title: Rural Immersion Program – SY Mechanical Engineering
entries.excerpt: The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-WPU organized a three-day Rural Immersion Program for Second Year Mechanical Engineering students at Ravangaon village.
entries.text: Rural Immersion Program for Mechanical Engineering Students Rural Immersion Program – SY Mechanical Engineering The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-WPU organized a three-day Rural Immersion Program for Second Year Mechanical Engineering students at Ravangaon village. MIT-WPU / Events / Rural Immersion Program – SY Mechanical Engineering Events Rural Immersion Program – SY Mechanical Engineering The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-WPU organized a three-day Rural Immersion Program for Second Year Mechanical Engineering students at Ravangaon village. Events Rural Immersion Program – SY Mechanical Engineering Happenings rural-immersion-program-1 Events rural-immersion-program-2 The Department of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-WPU organized a three-day Rural Immersion Program for Second Year Mechanical Engineering students at Ravangaon village. The program aimed to provide experiential learning by immersing students in rural life and fostering awareness of sustainable practices, governance, and socio-economic challenges. rural-immersion-program-3 During their stay, students lived in village conditions and actively engaged with the local community. Key activities included visits to the Gram Panchayat, poultry farms, Zilla Parishad school, and village medical facilities. Students also participated in Shramdaan at the Shirsai Temple and conducted household surveys covering over 30 homes to understand rural living conditions. rural-immersion-program-4 The program offered insights into agriculture-based livelihoods, rural infrastructure, education systems, and healthcare facilities. Students observed strong community collaboration while identifying areas for improvement such as waste management, sanitation, and healthcare access. rural-immersion-program-5 T

entries.title: Rural Immersion Programme
entries.excerpt: Rural immersion programmes by MIT-WPU are a unique, one-of-a-kind learning activity meticulously designed to sensitize the students to the lifestyle of the rural community and train them to apply their knowledge, modern technology and management concepts…
entries.text: Rural Immersion Programme By MIT-WPU Rural Immersion Programme Rural immersion programmes by MIT-WPU are a unique, one-of-a-kind learning activity meticulously designed to sensitize the students to the lifestyle of the rural community and train them to apply their knowledge, modern technology and management concepts to find solutions to problems faced by the rural communities. MIT-WPU / Rural Immersion Programme Rural Immersion Rural Immersion Programme Rural immersion programmes by MIT-WPU are a unique, one-of-a-kind learning activity meticulously designed to sensitize the students to the lifestyle of the rural community and train them to apply their knowledge, modern technology and management concepts to find solutions to problems faced by the rural communities. Rural Immersion Programme rural-img Students of each programme visit a village and understand the rural landscape, the community, and their culture. rural-img They get to work on various projects like water storage and conservation, waste recycling, irrigation system optimization, water recycling, solar power utilization etc for improving the rural landscape. rural-img These programmes are the perfect blend of hands-on, real-life learning experiences which hone the skills like critical thinking, community awareness, problem solving, sensitivity etc. Students develop a broader perspective and a deeper understanding of rural society and understand how their knowledge can lead to innovative solutions. The programmes indeed connect India with Bharat. rural-img Events Rural Immersion Programme 2025 A visit to Ravangaon Village, Daund Rural Immersion Programme 2025 A visit to Naigaon Village, Satara Rural Immersion Programme 2025 A visit to Khadaki Village, Daund Rural Immersion Programme 2025 A visit to Ranwad Vill

We consider entrance test performance, academics, essays, personal interactions, and extracurricular or professional achievements. Assessment Framework The internal assessment process holistically evaluates the following components : Candidate’s Profile Past Academic Records Entrance Examination Written Essay Online Personal Interaction Extracurricular Activities and/ or Professional Achievements ABC Model of Selection WPU GŌA places great importance on three interconnected dimensions (ABC) Agility & Empathic Responsiveness, which is defined by the ability to adapt to change, show resilience, and demonstrate empathy Broad-Minded Learning Orientation & Self-Development, which is demonstrated by aspects such as curiosity-driven learning, independent thinking, and openness to growth. entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU hosted a Vision Meet on semiconductor and renewable energy technologies, encouraging dialogue on innovation and future industries.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Vision Meet on Semiconductor & Renewable Energy Semiconductor and Renewable Energy Vision Meet Fosters Dialogue on Future Technologies MIT-WPU hosted a Vision Meet on semiconductor and renewable energy technologies, encouraging dialogue on innovation and future industries. MIT-WPU / Events / Semiconductor and Renewable Energy Vision Meet Fosters Dialogue on Future Technologies Events Semiconductor and Renewable Energy Vision Meet Fosters Dialogue on Future Technologies MIT-WPU hosted a Vision Meet on semiconductor and renewable energy technologies, encouraging dialogue on innovation and future industries. Events Happenings Events The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, successfully organised the Semiconductor & Renewable Energy Vision Meet on 24 January 2026, bringing together eminent academicians, industry leaders, policymakers, and researchers to discuss emerging trends and opportunities in future technologies. Semiconductor and Renewable Energy Vision Meet Fosters Dialogue on Future Technologies The event served as a platform for meaningful deliberations on semiconductor technologies, renewable energy, smart infrastructure, and workforce development, highlighting their role in shaping a sustainable and technologically advanced future. The programme featured insightful keynote addresses by distinguished experts, high-impact panel discussions, and student poster presentations, fostering knowledge exchange and collaboration between academia, industry, and policymakers. Events Shaping the Future of Sports: MIT-WPU’s 2nd Advisory Board Meeting Happenings sports-1 Events sports-2 The 2nd Sports Advisory Board Meeting successfully commenced on 9th June 2025 at the Dronacharya Building, MIT-WPU, Pune. The event brought together eminent sports leaders, accomplished experts, and university officials dedicated to elevating the standard of sports on campus. sports-3 The session opened with a welcome address and insightful opening remarks from Dr. Rahul V. Karad, Executive President, MIT-WPU and Chairman, Sports Advisory Board. The board reviewed ongoing sports initiatives and discussed comprehensive strategies for athlete and coach development, including motivational talks by Olympians and Arjuna Awardees, and workshops on injury prevention, sports psychology, and nutrition. A key outcome was the introduction of a 5% Sports Quota for admissions, opening new opportunities for talented athletes at state, national, and international levels. The board also introduced supportive policies for student-athletes, such as enhanced attendance flexibility, financial incentives and sports kit. sports-4 Further, the board approved partnerships with both government and p

entries.text: Shri. Ram Mohan Naidu Kinjarapu : MIT-WPU Awardee Shri. Ram Mohan Naidu Kinjarapu Shri. Kinjarapu entered active politics in 2012 and rose to become one of the foremost leaders in the Srikakulam Parliamentary constituency. He became the constituency In-charge in 2013. He also fought the 2014 general elections and won the elections with a resounding victory of 127,576 votes. It made him the second youngest Member of Parliament in the 16th Lok Sabha. He introduced a private member’s bill on a railway zone with headquarters at Visakhapatnam in Andhra Pradesh. This reflected his concern for regional development. MIT-WPU / Awardee Profiles / Shri. Shekhar Sen is a visionary who has redefined Indian musical theatre throughout his career. Known for his monoact plays, he has effectively fused devotional music with theatrical storytelling to appeal to an audience from all walks of life. Shri. Sen has issued and performed in more than 200 bhajan albums and also delivered over 1200 concerts worldwide, establishing himself as a global icon throughout his illustrious life. Prominent performances include Kabeer at the Lok Sabha on 4 May 2005 and the iconic Vivekanand at the Rashtrapati Bhavan on 27 April 2013, which affirm his status as a cultural ambassador. Shri Sen has been recognised with numerous awards for his contribution. In 2015, he received the Padma Shri Award — one of the highest civilian awards conferred by the Government of India, which recognises his contributions in the field of arts. In 2001, the Sangeet Natak Academy, Uttar Pradesh, also recognised his contributions to theatre with the Safdar Hashmi Puraskaar. He also won th

Swanand Kirkire Renowned Indian Lyricist, Writer, Playback Singer and Actor Swanand Kirkire is an Indian lyricist, playback singer, writer, assistant director, actor and dialogue writer, both in television and Hindi films. Kirkire won the National Film Award for Best Lyrics twice: first in 2007 for the song "Bande Me Tha Dum...Vande Mataram" from the 2006 film, Lage Raho Munna Bhai, and then in 2009, for the song "Behti Hawaa Sa Tha Woh..." from the film 3 Idiots. He received Filmfare Award nomination for Best Lyrics for the song "Piyu Bole" in Parineeta (2005). Swanand Kirkire was born and brought up at Rambagh in Indore-based Marathi speaking family to Chintamani (father) and Neelambari (mother), both of whom are classical singers. However he had no formal training in singing. He moved to Delhi after doing his graduation in commerce. In 1996, Swanand graduated from National School of Drama and started doing theatre. Swanand's first brush with direction came when he wrote and directed a play on freedom fighter Bhagat Singh in New Delhi. During his theatre days, he learn

Recently, she was honored with the most prestigious and highest sports award by the State Government of Maharashtra, the Shiv Chhatrapati Award, for her exceptional achievements in the field of Roller Skating. Throughout her career, she has represented India in World and Asian Championships. She has consistently won medals at the national level for the past 15 years and has secured 7 consecutive national gold medals. In total, she has earned over 100 medals and trophies.

entries.title: SLDP 2025 : Where Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive
entries.excerpt: MIT-WPU hosted the Social Leadership Development Programme 2025 from August 6-8, bringing together social leaders, innovators, and students to focus on community-driven change.
entries.text: SLDP 2025 : Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive | Events MIT-WPU SLDP 2025 : Where Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive MIT-WPU hosted the Social Leadership Development Programme 2025 from August 6-8, bringing together social leaders, innovators, and students to focus on community-driven change. MIT-WPU / Events / SLDP 2025 : Where Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive Events SLDP 2025 : Where Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive MIT-WPU hosted the Social Leadership Development Programme 2025 from August 6-8, bringing together social leaders, innovators, and students to focus on community-driven change. Events SLDP 2025 : Where Leaders Rise and Communities Thrive Happenings sldp-1 Events MIT-WPU hosted the Social Leadership Development Programme (SLDP) 2025 from August 6-8, bringing together social leaders, innovators, and students to focus on community-driven change. The three-day programme featured experts from various fields sharing practical insights on leadership, environmental conservation, entrepreneurship, sustainable development and more. sldp-2 Over these three transformative days, distinguished changemakers, social innovators, and passionate youth came together under the powerful banner of #CommunitiesForChange. What emerged was more than knowledge sharing; it was the birth of a movement that bridges ancient wisdom with modern solutions. sldp-3 The programme began with inspiring words from Prof. Fernando Garibay, Founder and CEO of The Garibay Institute for Soft Power and Public Diplomacy, and Lt. Gen. Ajai Kumar Singh (Retd.), Member of the National Security Advisory Board. Their presence set the tone for what would become an unforgettable journey. Also, through powerful sessions on community empowerment and grassroots leadership, voices like Ms. Mukta Chaitanya

entries.title: Smashing Success: Girls Badminton Team Qualifies for Khelo India Games
entries.excerpt: Smashing success! The MIT-WPU Girls Badminton Team has qualified for the prestigious Khelo India Games. Read about their road to victory here.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Girls Badminton Team Qualifies for Khelo India Games Smashing Success: Girls Badminton Team Qualifies for Khelo India Games Smashing success! The MIT-WPU Girls Badminton Team has qualified for the prestigious Khelo India Games. Read about their road to victory here. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Smashing Success: Girls Badminton Team Qualifies for Khelo India Games Achievements Smashing Success: Girls Badminton Team Qualifies for Khelo India Games Smashing success! The MIT-WPU Girls Badminton Team has qualified for the prestigious Khelo India Games. Read about their road to victory here. Achievement Achievements manvi-singhai MIT World Peace University, Pune, proudly commends the Girls Badminton Team for their spirited performance at the All India Inter Zone Inter University Badminton (Women) Tournament 2026, hosted by PES University, Bengaluru. Facing intense competition, Shreeya Utpat, Kunjal Mandlik, Sanika Sutar, and Bhavna Agrawal demonstrated exceptional grit, discipline, and teamwork. Their commendable effort has earned them qualification for the prestigious Khelo India Games. The team’s achievement reflects MIT-WPU Pune’s strong sporting culture and commitment to holistic development. We congratulate the players on representing the university with distinction and wish them continued success at the Khelo India Games.

Started in 2002 as a Bundeli newspaper, Khabar Lahariya employs Dalit, Muslim, Adivasi, and Other Backward Class women as reporters and editors. Awardee Profiles About Us Social Initiatives BANA Awardee Profiles Smt. Kavita Bundelkhandi Smt. Kavita Bundelkhandi Editor-in-Chief & Co-Founder, Khabar Lahariya CEO, Chambal Media Smt. Kavita Devi is the editor in chief of Khabar Lahariya, a news website that focuses on rural news told from a feminist perspective. Started in 2002 as a Bundeli newspaper, Khabar Lahariya employs Dalit, Muslim, adivasi and Other Backward Class women as reporters and editors. At one point, the paper had eight editions in five languages, and was sold in eight districts of Uttar Pradesh and Bihar. For six months after finishing her education, she began working with Mahila Dakiya, a newsletter run by the same centre. This was her first stint as a reporter. Working with Mahila Dakiya helped her realise the importance of a local newspaper that covered and brought forth the stories of her community. With help from the Delhi-based NGO Nirantar, she began Khabar Laharya, a women-run newspaper, with the aim to cover local news that went largely unreported and neglected by mainstream med

Naina Lal Kidwai is the former President of the Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry. Currently, she is serving as the Chairman of Max Financial Services Ltd. Along with serving as the Chairman of HSBC Asset Management (India) Pvt Ltd and HSBC InvestDirect (India) Ltd. She is on the Governing Board of NCAER, Audit Advisory Board of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India, and on the National Executive Committee of CII and FICCI. She serves as a Group General Manager of HSBC Holdings Plc since October 2006. She also served as the Chief Executive Officer of HSBC India of HSBC Holdings Plc since 5 May 2006. For her praiseworthy work, Smt. Kidwai has also secured many accolades. Kidwai has secured the distinguished Padma Shri award for her contributions in the areas of trade and industry. Smt. Palki Sharma Journalist, Managing Editor, Network 18 Journalism is one of the most prominent pillars of Democracy. Some Journalists make it a point to keep that pillar firm, by their extraordinary contribution. Ms. Palki Sharma Upadhyay is one of those journalists. As a journalist as well as a news anchor, Ms. Palki Sharma Upadhyay started her career with Doordarshan, India’s largest broadcasting network, in 2002 as a news anchor and reporter. She then joined Hindustan Times in 2004 and moved to CNN-News 18 to become its anchor and Senior Editor. In 2019, she joined WION, a multinational English news channel based in New Delhi. After working for three years as the Managing Editor of the channel, she resigned in September 2022. Currently, she is the face of a new show launched on First Post. In 2007, she was awarded the Best News Reader Award. During the News Broadcasting Awards (ENBA), she was awarded the ‘Best International News Show’ in English for her program, “Gravitas.” Ms. Palki Sharma Upadhyay could be proudly called as one of the most popular youth icons in modern Journalism. She is also a keen commentator on social and cultural change in liberalising India. She has her own strategy consulting practice and works with an impressive list of Indian and global companies, helping them across sectors, and describes her mission as bringing market focus to their business strategy. Supriya Sule Hon’ble Member of Parliament, Lok Sabha Supriya Sule is an Indian politician from the Nationalist Congress Party and currently a Member of Parliament (MP) in the 16th Lok Sabha (India's house of elected representatives). She is the daughter of the President of the Nationalist Congress Party, Sharad Pawar. In 2009, she contested the elections for the Baramati Lok Sabha constituency and won. This constituency was previously represented by her father. She was a Member of Parliament, representing Maharashtra in the Rajya Sabha. In 2011, she launched a statewide campaign against female foeticide. Recently, she has been honored with Mumbai Women of the Decade Achievers Award by ALL Ladies League for her outstanding contribution to social service. Born to Sharad and Pratibha Sharadchandra Pawar on 30 June 1969 in Pune. She was educated at Jai Hind Coll

National Legislators' Conference (NLC) Bharat National Legislators’ Conference Bharat (NLC Bharat) is an avenue for Members of Legislative Assemblies (MLAs) and Members of Legislative Councils (MLCs) across the nation to gather at one place and exchange ideas in this historic conference. The first edition of NLC Bharat took place in Mumbai from 15th to 17th June 2023. MLAs and MLCs from across the political parties and from all the States and UTs were provided with a unique opportunity to interact with each other. The first edition of the conference was conducted with the objectives of cross-learning and enhancement of legislative effectiveness at its core. Student Clubs Society of Women Engineers The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a non-profit educational and service organisation that originated in the United States in 1950. SWE stands as a global advocate for women in engineering and technology, driving transformative change and fostering inclusivity within the field. With a robust membership of approximately 40,000 individuals worldwide, our organisation is organised into nearly 100 professional sections, 300 collegiate sections, and 60 global affiliate groups. At MIT-WPU, we are proud to be part of the SWE network, dedicated to empowering and advancing women in engineering and technology. We believe in creating an inclusive and supportive community where women can thrive, succeed, and make significant contributions to their respective fields.
