entries.title: SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit
entries.excerpt: Discover how SOET students at MIT-WPU enhanced their personal and professional growth through an immersive visit to the Life Transformation Centre (LTC), fostering holistic learning, leadership, and self-development.
entries.text: SOET Students Explore Holistic Learning Through LTC Visit at MIT-WPU SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Discover how SOET students at MIT-WPU enhanced their personal and professional growth through an immersive visit to the Life Transformation Centre (LTC), fostering holistic learning, leadership, and self-development. MIT-WPU / Events / SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Events SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Discover how SOET students at MIT-WPU enhanced their personal and professional growth through an immersive visit to the Life Transformation Centre (LTC), fostering holistic learning, leadership, and self-development. Events SoET Students Experience Holistic Learning Through Immersive LTC Visit Happenings ltc-1 Events Students from the School of Engineering and Technology (SoET), MIT-WPU, Pune recently participated in an immersive learning experience at the Life Transformation Centre (LTC), designed to foster personal growth, leadership, discipline, and community engagement. Students from the Departments of Mechanical Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering (EEE), and Civil Engineering actively participated in a variety of experiential activities, including agriculture, military training, sports, team-building exercises, and community living. The Life Realisation Program also encouraged students to take responsibility for daily activities, helping them develop self-reliance and a strong sense of discipline. ltc-2 Through hands-on learning and collaborative experiences, participants enhanced essential life skills such as teamwork, leadership, resilience, communication, and social responsibility. Events Happenings SPANDAN 2026: Celebrating Innovation, Culture, and Synergy at MIT-WPU Pune Events The flagship Annual Social Gathering of MIT World Peace University, Pune, recently concluded as a magnificent four-day odyssey of talent, tradition, and togetherness. Held from 16–18 and on 25 February 2026, the event brought together diverse disciplines under the ‘One University, One Family’ ethos, serving as a vibrant canvas where academic rigor met cultural brilliance. spandan1 The festivities commenced with an electrifying showcase where the spirit of innovation harmonised with high-energy performances. This momentum surged into the second day, marked by a seamless blend of professional synergy and creative flair across diverse disciplines. The celebration continued with vibrant presentations that highlighted students’ multifaceted brilliance in bridging the gap between scientific inquiry and community well-being. The grand finale brought the entire university community together in a spectacular celebration of talent, unity, and shared purpose. This ultimate expression of the SPANDAN spirit showcased a unique fusion of artistic expression and intellectual excellence,

FUELING AMBITION, INSPIRING TRIUMPH Archery Overview Athletics A strong sports infrastructure offers numerous benefits, from fostering community engagement and strengthening social connections to encouraging talent development and generating economic opportunities through sporting events. It also plays a vital role in enhancing overall quality of life. The Dronacharya Sports Complex exemplifies this vision by providing high-quality sporting facilities that inspire individuals to participate in physical activity, promote healthier lifestyles, support positive mental well-being, and contribute to reduced healthcare costs over time. entries.text: MIT-WPU Elite Sportsperson Scholarship Sports @ MIT-WPU If the student has secured a top 5 ranking position in a tournament (Badminton, Chess, Table Tennis, Shooting, Archery etc.) conducted by or affiliated with a recognized govt. sponsored/Govt. official sports organization or federation at National or International level, then he/she will be eligible for the participation scholarship. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Sports / Sports @ MIT-WPU Life Wpu Sports @ MIT-WPU If the student has secured a top 5 ranking position in a tournament (Badminton, Chess, Table Tennis, Shooting, Archery etc.) conducted by or affiliated with a recognized govt. sponsored/Govt. official sports organization or federation at National or International level, then he/she will be eligible for the participation scholarship. Any candidate who participates/wins a medal at west zone inter-university, all India inter- university, national or international level, while representing MIT-WPU or playing for any Govt. sponsored/ Govt. recognized official sports organization [IOA, BCCI, etc.] in any nationally/ internationally recognized sporting event; in individual or team sports; would be eligible for the following scholarship for the remaining period of the programme, only if he/she participates and wins a medal on the National / International Level. Sports Infrastructure A strong sports infrastructure has several advantages, including enhancing community involvement and social interaction, encouraging talent development, generating economic benefits through events, and improving quality of life in general. sports-complex High-quality sporting venues can motivate individuals to engage in physical activity, improving mental health outcomes and lowering healthcare expenditures. Professional players are drawn to well-equipped infrastructure, which also supports regional sports organizations and may even encourage young athletes to pursue sports as a vocation. It also acts as a location for occasions that draw guests and bring in money for the neighborhood economy. Overall, a strong sports infrastructure benefits social and individual well-being. entries.text: Strengthening India's Infrastructure Training Programme | MIT-WPU Events Strengthening India's Infrastructure: MIT-WPU's Training Programme for Border Roads Organisation Officers MIT‑WPU conducts a specialised training programme for Border Roads Organisation officers to strengthen national infrastructure. MIT-WPU / Events / Strengthening India's Infrastructure: MIT-WPU's Training Programme for Border Roads Organisation Officers Events Strengthening India's Infrastructure: MIT-WPU's Training Programme for Border Roads Organisation Officers MIT‑WPU conducts a specialised training programme for Border Roads Organisation officers to strengthen national infrastructure. Events Happenings Events Strengthening India's Infrastructure: MIT-WPU's Training Programme for Border Roads Organisation Officers MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU) recently conducted a two-week intensive training programme for young officers of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO). Led by Prof. Dr. Sandeep Potnis, the programme focused on "Planning, Design, Construction and Equipment Management for Tunnel Construction." This initiative facilitated a valuable exchange of knowledge, equipping BRO officers with the expertise needed to develop critical road infrastructure in border areas, supporting our Armed Forces. Through this collaboration, MIT-WPU reaffirms its commitment to national development by enhancing the capabilities of those who serve the nation. This programme highlights the university's role in shaping the future of India's infrastructure, fostering a stronger and more secure nation for generations to come. Related public links Happenings

Life@MIT-WPU Student Council EMPOWERING VOICES, SHAPING FUTURES Student Election Student elections at MIT-WPU are a crucial opportunity for our student body to exercise their democratic rights and actively shape the future of our university community. Through these elections, students have the chance to select their representatives who will advocate for their interests, voice their concerns, and work towards enhancing the overall student experience. The university encourages all students to participate in this democratic process, as it plays a vital role in ensuring that our university continues to evolve in a way that reflects the aspirations and needs of its diverse student population. Perks and Benefits Student council will have their special Uniform as council members. Pursuing the M.Tech. eMobility programme at MIT World Peace University has been one of the most rewarding academic and professional experiences of my life. The curriculum is thoughtfully designed with a strong industry focus and is regularly updated to reflect the latest advancements in the electric vehicle (EV) sector. Each module strengthens both conceptual understanding and practical skills, preparing students to contribute meaningfully to the evolving mobility ecosystem. The faculty have been instrumental throughout this journey, providing constant guidance, encouragement, and career support. MIT-WPU / Life Wpu / Students' Welfare Life Wpu Students' Welfare Discover the Student Affairs at MIT-WPU, committed to improve student life by means of extracurricular activities, support systems, and a vibrant campus community. Overview Tanvi Raut Welcome to the vibrant center of student life at MIT-WPU – the Student Affairs Department. As the guardians of all matters related to students, we are wholeheartedly committed to enhancing your campus experience. Whether it's supervising student council elections, nurturing a diverse range of student clubs, orchestrating exciting events, or providing essential support services, our purpose is to ensure that your time at MIT-WPU is both unforgettable and rewarding. Our mission revolves around empowering and involving students, fostering personal development, leadership skills, and a deep sense of belonging within our dynamic community. Join us in shaping your educational journey, creating lasting memories, and fully embracing the diverse spectrum of campus life. Anti-Ragging Committee at MIT-WPU MIT-WPU Anti-Ragging Committee is dedicated to preserving a safe and respectful environment on the campus, the Anti-Ragging Committee at MIT-WPU is committed to preventing any form of bullying, intimidation, coercion, or harassment. The committee enforces a zero-tolerance policy against these malpractices, and conducts various awareness programmes and seminars. The committee takes quick and strict actions towards the reported incidents, ensuring the well-being and safety of all the students. entries.excerpt: Discover how Syntropy 2026 at MIT-WPU brought together innovation, research, and engineering excellence through student-led projects, technology showcases, and collaborative learning.
entries.text: MIT-WPU Syntropy 2026 Showcases Student Innovation & Research SYNTROPY 2026 Showcased Innovation, Research, and Engineering Excellence Discover how Syntropy 2026 at MIT-WPU brought together innovation, research, and engineering excellence through student-led projects, technology showcases, and collaborative learning. MIT-WPU / Events / SYNTROPY 2026 Showcased Innovation, Research, and Engineering Excellence Events SYNTROPY 2026 Showcased Innovation, Research, and Engineering Excellence Discover how Syntropy 2026 at MIT-WPU brought together innovation, research, and engineering excellence through student-led projects, technology showcases, and collaborative learning. Events SYNTROPY 2026 Showcased Innovation, Research, and Engineering Excellence Happenings syntrophy12 Events The Department of Chemical Engineering and Materials Science & Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune, successfully organised SYNTROPY 2026 from 26th to 28th March 2026. Centred around the theme "Brewing Brilliance," the three-day event brought together students to showcase their technical expertise, creativity, and problem-solving abilities through a series of engaging competitions and activities. syntrophy2 The event featured paper and poster presentations, mock placement rounds, domain-specific workshops, and interactive challenges, including Mat Shark, Pick n Play, Risk Resolve, Scribble the System, Jeopardy, Escape the Lab, Treasure Hunt, and Fungus Warfare. Participants also took part in a cultural evening that celebrated talent beyond academics. syntrophy3 SYNTROPY 2026 provided students with a platform to strengthen their technical knowledge, enhance teamwork and communication skills, and apply classroom learning to real-world engineering challenges, reflecting MIT-WPU's commitment to experiential lear

entries.text: Taneesha Kotecha -The student Achiever At MIT-WPU Taneesha Kotecha MIT-WPU congratulates Ms. Taneesha Kotecha FY BBA (Business Analytics) student, on her outstanding performance at the WTT Youth Contender Vadodara, 2025. She secured a Silver medal in Under 19 Mixed Doubles, showcasing her dedication and competitive spirit. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Taneesha Kotecha Achievements Taneesha Kotecha MIT-WPU congratulates Ms. Taneesha Kotecha FY BBA (Business Analytics) student, on her outstanding performance at the WTT Youth Contender Vadodara, 2025. She secured a Silver medal in Under 19 Mixed Doubles, showcasing her dedication and competitive spirit. Achievement Achievements MIT-WPU proudly congratulates Ms. Taneesha Kotecha, FY BBA (Business Analytics) student, for her remarkable table tennis achievements: Silver Medal in U-19 Girls at WTT Youth Contender, Havirov, Czech Republic (March 2025). Taneesha Kotecha Silver Medal in U-19 Mixed Doubles at WTT Youth Contender, Vadodara, India. Historic Bronze Medal in Mixed Doubles at the 29th Asian Youth Table Tennis Championships, Tashkent, Uzbekistan. This isn’t just any medal—it is the first-ever Mixed Doubles medal from Maharashtra and only the second in Indian Table Tennis history at the Asian Junior level . Taneesha and Ankur Bhattacharjee made history by defeating Japan’s top pair, YOSHIYAMA Kazuki & MENDE Rin, in a spectacular semifinal win.

MIT‑WPU’s Team CloudWeave shines at SIH 2024 with innovative, impact‑driven tech solutions to real‑world problems. Events Happenings Events The Smart India Hackathon (SIH) is a countrywide initiative by the Ministry of Education, Government of India, designed to involve students in tackling actual problems by means of creative solutions. Initiated to promote a culture of innovation and solution-finding, SIH motivates participants to create practical applications that can profoundly influence society. This year, the hackathon has drawn thousands of teams, exemplifying India's dedication to fostering young talent. All About SIH 2024 Team Cloudweave from MIT-WPU Shines Bright at the Smart India Hackathon 2024! Smart India Hackathon 2024 was held on December 10, uniting more than 86,000 teams of students from all over the country. Hosted by prestigious institutions like IIT Roorkee and IIT Hyderabad, this year's event, held concurrently at 51 locations, showcasing over 250 problem statements provided by different ministries and industries. Participants showcase a diverse range of projects spanning fields such as agriculture, healthcare, and technology. The final event was scheduled on December 11 and 12, featuring teams showcasing their creative projects to a group of specialists, with the successful teams earning monetary rewards and acknowledgement f

entries.excerpt: We are dedicated towards designing and manufacturing an All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) for competing in BAJA SAE competitions worldwide.
entries.text: Team Piranha Racing’ official BAJA SAE team of MIT-WPU: MIT-WPU Achiever Team Piranha Racing’ is the official BAJA SAE team of MIT-WPU We are dedicated towards designing and manufacturing an All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) for competing in BAJA SAE competitions worldwide. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Team Piranha Racing’ is the official BAJA SAE team of MIT-WPU Achievements Team Piranha Racing’ is the official BAJA SAE team of MIT-WPU We are dedicated towards designing and manufacturing an All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) for competing in BAJA SAE competitions worldwide. Achievement Achievements st-achiv-img Team Piranha Racing’ is the official BAJA SAE team of MIT-WPU. We are dedicated towards designing and manufacturing an All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) for competing in BAJA SAE competitions worldwide. The team consists of about 30 aspiring engineers from the first year to the final year aiming to build the best BAJA SAE car in the world. Our ultimate goal is to be recognized as the best BAJA SAE team in the world. st-achiv-img In seeking to achieve this, we hope to promote a culture of applied learning, encourage students to pursue exciting careers in engineering and also imbibe in them a sense of teamwork and dedication. As a part of Piranha Racing, students get acquainted with the latest computer design and analysis softwares like SOLIDWORKS, Ansys, Hypermesh etc. Students also learn innovative manufacturing and fabrication processes along with various validation and testing methods. The team has participated in BAJA SAE Korea (2014), BAJA SAE Rochester, USA (2016) and BAJA SAE Maryland, USA (2018) as part of international events along with various national events like BAJA SAE India and Enduro Student India.

entries.text: Vegapod Hyperloop Team Felicitated by Nitin Gadkari : MIT-WPU News Team Vegapod Hyperloop Receives Recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari MIT‑WPU’s Vegapod Hyperloop team receives recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari for pioneering transport innovation. MIT-WPU / News / Team Vegapod Hyperloop Receives Recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari News Team Vegapod Hyperloop Receives Recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari MIT‑WPU’s Vegapod Hyperloop team receives recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari for pioneering transport innovation. Happenings Happenings News Team Vegapod Hyperloop Receives Recognition from Union Minister Nitin Gadkari Team Vegapod Hyperloop, a student-led research team from MIT World Peace University, was invited to meet with Union Minister of Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari. He praised their work on developing hyperloop technology and encouraged them to seek support from NITI Aayog for further development. Team Vegapod Hyperloop’s deputation to the Minister’s house included faculty members. Prof. Dr. Ganesh Kakandikar (Associate Dean, Faculty of Engineering and Technology), Prof. Dr. Shivprakash Barve (Programme Director, Department of Mechanical Engineering), and students- Arkoday Basak (Team Captain), Shubham Gandhi (Team Manager) and Shubham Apsangi (Team Vice-Captain). Team Vegapod Hyperloop is also building up its modest hyperloop prototype which will be demonstrated at ETH Zurich, Switzerland, where they will be representing India at the 2024 edition of the European Hyperloop Week. This endeavour not only showcases India's technological innovation but also underscores the transformative potential of hyperloop technology, offering myriad benefits such as reduced carbon footprint, enhanced travel efficie

entries.text: Team Vegapod Hyperloop at Europe Hyperloop Week 2025 | Achievers Team Vegapod Hyperloop Shines at European Hyperloop Week 2025 MIT World Peace University proudly celebrates Team Vegapod Hyperloop's extraordinary achievement at the European Hyperloop Week 2025, which took place in July. MIT-WPU / Achievements / Team Vegapod Hyperloop Shines at European Hyperloop Week 2025 Achievements Team Vegapod Hyperloop Shines at European Hyperloop Week 2025 MIT World Peace University proudly celebrates Team Vegapod Hyperloop's extraordinary achievement at the European Hyperloop Week 2025, which took place in July. 254 words 1 min read Achievement Vegapod Achievements Vegapod MIT World Peace University proudly celebrates Team Vegapod Hyperloop's extraordinary achievement at the European Hyperloop Week 2025, which took place in July in Groningen, Netherlands. Competing against 35 elite teams from the world's leading universities, our students secured 8th place globally and emerged as the only Asian team to successfully demonstrate their prototype. Vegapod Ms. Sophie Hermans, Deputy Prime Minister of the Netherlands, and Ms. Annelies Pleijte, Mayor of Veendam, personally visited the team and commended their exceptional performance. This marks our 4th consecutive participation, with the team achieving Top 2 placement in the Outreach and Showcasing categories while being one of only 2 Indian teams to qualify for finals. Vegapod The team developed an advanced prototype featuring dual single-sided linear induction motors, four EMS electromagnetic cores for levitation, and sustainable innovations including organic paraffin wax cooling, flax fibre aeroshell, and coconut fibre brake pads. Despite logistical challenges, they successfully assembled their complete track and pod system within 3 d

Tech Training Initiatives for Future Medical Professionals The first initiative, 'Enhancing 3D Competencies of Medical Scholars', offers free one-week training for medical students in various 3D technologies. This comprehensive course includes 3D printing, 3D scanning of bones and dental implants, and demonstrations of 3D experience software. It also features a seminar on the use of biomaterials. MIT-WPU / Events / Technophilia 2026 Events Technophilia 2026 Dive into the future at Technophilia 2026, MIT-WPU's premier tech fest. Explore cutting-edge innovations, tech workshops, and live demonstrations. Don’t miss out—register today! Events Happenings Events Technophilia 2026 was a two-day state-level event conducted Department of Polytechnic and Skill Development, on 2 nd April and 3 rd April 2026. The event featured a mix of technical and non-technical competitions designed to test and showcase the knowledge, creativity, problem-solving ability, and practical skills of diploma-level students from various institutions. The primary objective was to foster innovation, team spirit, and inter-collegiate collaboration through well-structured, theme-based events. This year, the event witnessed overwhelming participation from students across the state, with approximately 298 students actively competing in various challenges. The competitions were held on-campus, utilizing the institution's classrooms and laboratories Technophilia was featured with following technical events hosted by every program at Department of Polytechnic 2 nd April 2026 : Modelverse Prompt War Technophilia 2026 Codelock Solidsprint Sparktech Technophilia also featured Non-Technical Events on 3 rd April 2026 : Slow Cycling Tug Of War Octopus Race Related public links Happenings

As a TEDx University event, we follow the well-established TED talk conference format, offering diverse topics and content free from bias. Student Clubs TEDxMIT-WPU TEDx1 TEDxMIT-WPU is a local gathering where we curate and share live TED-like talks and performances with our community. As a TEDx University event, we follow the well-established TED talk conference format, offering diverse topics and content free from bias. The 'x' in TEDx signifies our independent status and as a university event, we are entirely student-driven, operating within the framework set by the TED Foundation. Team TEDxMIT-WPU comprises a group of inquisitive individuals from various disciplines across our campus, all eager to absorb knowledge and insights from our esteemed speakers. Committed to our promise of providing a "brain spa," we have hosted truly unique and inspiring speakers, including thought leaders such as Gaur Gopal Das (spiritual guidance), Ashish Chanchlani (youth entertainment), Mohammed Salim Khan (social media influencer), Vedant Lamba (Entrepreneurship and Sneaker Culture), Deepanshu Singh (Governance) and many more. entries.excerpt: The students of the Department of Polytechnic were proudly awarded 'The Best Startup Award' at the Pune Startup Expo 2025.
entries.text: The Best Startup Award | Achievement : Department of Polytechnic & Skill development The Best Startup Award The students of the Department of Polytechnic were proudly awarded 'The Best Startup Award' at the Pune Startup Expo 2025. MIT-WPU / Achievements / The Best Startup Award Achievements The Best Startup Award The students of the Department of Polytechnic were proudly awarded 'The Best Startup Award' at the Pune Startup Expo 2025. Achievement Achievements The-Best-Startup-Award The students of the Department of Polytechnic were proudly awarded 'The Best Startup Award' at the Pune Startup Expo 2025. The prestigious award was presented by Hon. Shri Ashish Shelar, Minister of Information & Technology, and Dr. Rahul V. Karad, further adding to the honor of the achievement. Team Leader Aditya Shevate, along with team members Aarushi Javale, Aditya Patil , Janavi Honrao ,Om Desai, under the mentorship of Prof. Prerna Patil represented at Pune Startup Expo 2025.

entries.title: The MIT World Peace University welcomes Prof. Solanki's Energy Swaraj Yatra, paving the way for a greener tomorrow.
entries.excerpt: In a praiseworthy endeavor to raise awareness about climate change and corrective actions, Prof. Solanki has been on the Energy Swaraj Yatra for more than 1000 days, starting in November 2020 and set to continue until December 2030
entries.text: Energy Swaraj Yatra Visits MIT‑WPU The MIT World Peace University welcomes Prof. Solanki's Energy Swaraj Yatra, paving the way for a greener tomorrow. In a praiseworthy endeavor to raise awareness about climate change and corrective actions, Prof. Solanki has been on the Energy Swaraj Yatra for more than 1000 days, starting in November 2020 and set to continue until December 2030 MIT-WPU / News / The MIT World Peace University welcomes Prof. Solanki's Energy Swaraj Yatra, paving the way for a greener tomorrow. News The MIT World Peace University welcomes Prof. Solanki's Energy Swaraj Yatra, paving the way for a greener tomorrow. In a praiseworthy endeavor to raise awareness about climate change and corrective actions, Prof. Solanki has been on the Energy Swaraj Yatra for more than 1000 days, starting in November 2020 and set to continue until December 2030 309 words 1 min read Happenings Happenings News In a praiseworthy endeavor to raise awareness about climate change and corrective actions, Prof. Solanki has been on the Energy Swaraj Yatra for more than 1000 days, starting in November 2020 and set to continue until December 2030. Prof. Solanki's unwavering commitment to the cause is not just a journey but a lifestyle. He has pledged to forsake the comforts of home, dedicating himself to the cause 365 days a year, living within a solar-powered bus equipped with facilities for work, sleep, and cooking. The MIT World Peace University welcomes Prof. Solanki's Energy Swaraj Yatra, paving the way for a greener tomorrow. Having covered over 45,000 kilometers during the Energy Swaraj Yatra, Prof. Solanki has engaged with communities nationwide, delivering over 800 lectures to approximately 2,40,000 individuals. entries.text: Triumph 62nd National Skating Championship 2024: MIT-WPU Achiever Triumph at the 62nd National Skating Championship 2024 We are thrilled to celebrate the outstanding achievements of our students at the 62nd National Championship 2024, held in Bangalore from December 5th to December 15th. Their dedication, perseverance, and exceptional skills have brought home well-deserved accolades! MIT-WPU / Achievements / Triumph at the 62nd National Skating Championship 2024 Achievements Triumph at the 62nd National Skating Championship 2024 We are thrilled to celebrate the outstanding achievements of our students at the 62nd National Championship 2024, held in Bangalore from December 5th to December 15th. Their dedication, perseverance, and exceptional skills have brought home well-deserved accolades! Achievement Achievements We are thrilled to celebrate the outstanding achievements of our students at the 62nd National Championship 2024, held in Bangalore from December 5th to December 15th. Their dedication, perseverance, and exceptional skills have brought home well-deserved accolades! NSC Achievements at a Glance : Jinesh Nanal (TY B.Tech CSE) : Two Gold Medals in Speed Slalom & Classic Slalom Shreyasi Joshi (SY B.Tech CSE) : Two Gold Medals in Speed Slalom & Classic Slalom NSC-1 Kuhoo Khandekar (FY M.Sc Integrated Biotechnology) : Bronze Medal in Speed Slalom A special recognition goes to Jinesh Nanal and Shreyasi Joshi, who have been crowned National Champions at the 62nd National Championships 2024. They are the only skaters across India to win two gold medals in both events respectively, marking an extraordinary achievement in the world of skating. entries.text: Udaan Sports Club At MIT-WPU Udaan At Udaan Sports Club, we believe in the holistic development of students, nurturing both their minds and bodies. Sports are not just physical activities; they shape personalities, enhance growth, and contribute to overall character development. We adhere to the philosophy that a sound mind resides in a sound body, as advocated by the Greek philosopher Thales. MIT-WPU / Udaan Udaan At Udaan Sports Club, we believe in the holistic development of students, nurturing both their minds and bodies. Sports are not just physical activities; they shape personalities, enhance growth, and contribute to overall character development. We adhere to the philosophy that a sound mind resides in a sound body, as advocated by the Greek philosopher Thales. Student Clubs Udaan At Udaan Sports Club, we believe in the holistic development of students, nurturing both their minds and bodies. Sports are not just physical activities; they shape personalities, enhance growth, and contribute to overall character development. We adhere to the philosophy that a sound mind resides in a sound body, as advocated by the Greek philosopher Thales. Our mission is to foster the growth and development of our students by promoting the significance of sports in their lives. We aim to provide a platform for students to engage in physical activities that boost their confidence, maintain good health, enhance mental alertness, and reduce stress. entries.title: Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration of National Startup Day
entries.excerpt: The National Startup Day event, “Udyamostav 2025”, hosted by Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), was a landmark celebration of innovation, entrepreneurship, and the transformative potential of startups.
entries.text: Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration | MIT-WPU Events Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration of National Startup Day The National Startup Day event, “Udyamostav 2025”, hosted by Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), was a landmark celebration of innovation, entrepreneurship, and the transformative potential of startups. MIT-WPU / Events / Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration of National Startup Day Events Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration of National Startup Day The National Startup Day event, “Udyamostav 2025”, hosted by Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), was a landmark celebration of innovation, entrepreneurship, and the transformative potential of startups. Events Happenings Udyamostav 2025: A Remarkable Celebration of National Startup Day Events The National Startup Day event, “Udyamostav 2025”, hosted by Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU), was a landmark celebration of innovation, entrepreneurship, and the transformative potential of startups. This prestigious event, inspired by the spirit of Shark Tank India, brought together aspiring entrepreneurs, established investors, academicians, and policymakers for an exhilarating day of idea sharing, collaboration, and inspiration. Event Highlights udyamatsov Pitching to Investors : Budding entrepreneurs had the opportunity to pitch their innovative business ideas to a panel of distinguished investors and mentors. This dynamic format, reminiscent of Shark Tank, offered real-time feedback and funding opportunities to promising ventures. Keynote Addresses : Esteemed leaders and visionaries from the startup ecosystem shared insights on the future of entrepreneurship in India, emphasizing innovation, resilience, and sustainable developmen

Our club is dedicated to raising awareness about the social issues that impact our city and society at large. Student Clubs UMED Social Club UMED Social Club, established in 2022, is a dynamic addition to the MIT-WPU School of Commerce. Our club is dedicated to raising awareness about the social issues that impact our city and society at large. We have already initiated impactful activities, such as creating a documentary on the NIRMALAY collection drive during Ganpati Puja and organising activities at SINHAGAD. Moreover, we have reached out to orphanages and spent quality time with special children, with many more such activities in the pipeline. Executive offices Stewardship of the university Founder President’s Office Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad Founder President Prof. Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad established MAEER in 1983 and founded MIT World Peace University. His institutional vision brings education, science, spirituality, and the pursuit of world peace into one public mission. Founder President’s profile → Executive President’s Office Dr. Rahul V. Karad Executive President Dr. Rahul V. Karad serves as Managing Trustee and Executive President of MAEER’s MIT Group of Institutions and Executive President of MIT-WPU. His work centres on value-based education, youth leadership, and public life. Executive President’s profile → Executive Director’s Office Dr. Aditi Karad Executive Director Dr. Aditi Karad guides the university’s academic and institutional direction, with an emphasis on outcome-driven curricula, experiential learning, leadership development, and social impact. entries.text: University Philosophy · WPU Goa University Philosophy Discover WPU Goa’s philosophy focused on transdisciplinary education, experiential learning, and holistic development for future-ready global leaders. WPU Goa / About / University Philosophy About WPU Goa University Philosophy Discover WPU Goa’s philosophy focused on transdisciplinary education, experiential learning, and holistic development for future-ready global leaders. Our Transdisciplinary Approach The greatest problems facing humanity, such as climate change, migration or health disparity, cannot be solved by specialists working in isolation. We need professionals who are trained in their own disciplinary field and are able to collaborate across differences. This requires a fundamentally different education and research. What Is Transdisciplinary Education? Transdisciplinarity is the guiding academic and research philosophy of WPU GŌA. It reshapes how knowledge is created, taught, and applied by integrating insights from multiple disciplines with the lived experience and expertise of practitioners, communities, industry, and policymakers. Transdisciplinary Education begins with real-world problems, not academic silos, and works to generate knowledge that is meaningful, actionable, and socially relevant. Traditional universities often isolate knowledge within disciplinary boundaries, making collaboration difficult. Today’s global and local challenges such as climate crises, public health inequities, urban crowding, and technological disruption are too complex to be understood or solved by any single field. Student Clubs Vegapod Hyperloop Team Vegapod Hyperloop is a dynamic, student-led initiative incubated at MIT-WPU, comprising of 40 talented students from various disciplines, dedicated towards advancing hyperloop technology. We craft sub-scale hyperloop prototypes, incorporating cutting-edge technologies such as levitation, vacuum systems, contactless propulsion, regenerative braking, power electronics, and cooling systems. Vegapod-05 We have proudly represented India and MIT-WPU on the global stage, showcasing our innovations at prestigious forums like the SpaceX Hyperloop Competition, European Hyperloop Week and the Canadian Hyperloop Conference. Our mission is to develop a sustainable mode of transportation for the near future. Our Achievements SpaceX 2019 : Ranked amongst top 3 teams in Asia and top 30 out of 1,600 teams worldwide. Vegapod-04 EHW 2022 (Delft, the Netherlands) : Represented India with a fully operational and cost-efficient pod. EHW 2023 (Scotland, the UK) : Ranked in the top 6 globally, the only Asian team to qualify for the final demonstration and successfully clearing the Testing and Safety Documentation (TSD) process. This award is a testament to their exceptional efforts in promoting the values and potential of hyperloop technology to a global audience. vegapod-hyperloop-flag Originating from our esteemed School of Mechanical Engineering, Team Vegapod Hyperloop has been showcasing their innovative work on the international stage since 2019. Their participation in significant events such as the SpaceX Hyperloop Pod Competition has placed them at the forefront of this revolutionary field. Their dedication to advancing hyperloop technology is not only a matter of technical prowess but also of inspiring others to see the transformative possibilities of this cutting-edge transportation system. entries.text: VISA Guidelines For Study In India VISA Guidelines Research the visa policies for overseas students intending to attend MIT World Peace University. Get basic knowledge about the application process, criteria, and pointers for obtaining a student visa to study in India. MIT-WPU / Study In India / VISA Guidelines Study In India VISA Guidelines Research the visa policies for overseas students intending to attend MIT World Peace University. Get basic knowledge about the application process, criteria, and pointers for obtaining a student visa to study in India. Study in India Once Admission Letter / Visa Letter has been issued student should immediately apply for Student Visa at Indian Embassy / High Commission. Make sure that your Student Visa is endorsed to MIT-WPU. Make sure that you have applied for Visa well in advance and time. It generally takes 3-8 weeks to get the Indian Visa thus it is advisable to apply for the visa accordingly and consult the Indian High Commission/Embassy, if required. Change of University or Institution cannot be considered. In this case you would have to go back to your home country and apply for a new visa. Make sure, if you have entered India on basis of Student Visa endorsed to MIT-WPU, then it is your responsibility to ensure that you directly join and report to the University. The Visa endorsed on the name of the University or obtained on the basis of university documents cannot be used for any other purpose like employment, admission to any other university/college/institute/academy etc. or for non-regular (distance/online) mode of education. International Day of Peace International Day of Peace: A Background On the proposal of the United Nations General Assembly in 1981, the International Day of Peace was first observed on 21st September 1982. This day marked a global observance dedicated to promoting peace throughout the world and encouraging people across the globe to build a culture of peace. Peace Walk@MIT-WPU Campus International Day of Peace With a theme of “Cultivating a Culture of Peace” as proposed by the United Nations for 2024, MIT-WPU organized a Peace Walk in its Kothrud Campus. entries.title: What is BBA in International Business, and How Is It Different from a Regular BBA?
entries.excerpt: What is a BBA in International Business, and how does it differ from a regular BBA? Explore the curriculum, career scope, and global advantages.
entries.text: BBA in International Business: How It Differs What is BBA in International Business, and How Is It Different from a Regular BBA? What is a BBA in International Business, and how does it differ from a regular BBA? Explore the curriculum, career scope, and global advantages. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / What is BBA in International Business, and How Is It Different from a Regular BBA? Faculty Articles What is BBA in International Business, and How Is It Different from a Regular BBA? What is a BBA in International Business, and how does it differ from a regular BBA? Explore the curriculum, career scope, and global advantages. 644 words 3 min read Business is no longer constrained by geography. A product developed in India might incorporate raw materials from Vietnam, technology from Germany, funding from Singapore and customers from the Middle East. This is why students today need more than a basic understanding of management. They need to understand how business operates across countries, cultures, currencies, regulations and markets. A BBA in International Business is designed for students who want to develop this global perspective early in their careers. Also called BBA IB, this programme focuses on business management in an international setting. While a regular BBA introduces students to the fundamentals of management, marketing, finance, HR, operations and entrepreneurship, a Bachelor of Business Administration in International Business takes a more in-depth look at cross-border trade, global strategy, international finance, global marketing and cross-cultural management. Rohit Kumar Sharma What makes BBA IB different from a regular BBA? The primary distinction is in the programme’s focus. A regular BBA prepares students for general business roles across industries.

entries.title: Why B.Com (Financial Analysis) Is a Strong Pathway to a Career in Finance
entries.excerpt: Discover why a B.Com in Financial Analysis is a strong pathway to a rewarding finance career. Explore skills, scope, and job opportunities ahead.
entries.text: B.Com International Accounting & Finance Career Benefits Why B.Com (Financial Analysis) Is a Strong Pathway to a Career in Finance Discover why a B.Com in Financial Analysis is a strong pathway to a rewarding finance career. Explore skills, scope, and job opportunities ahead. MIT-WPU / Faculty Articles / Why B.Com (Financial Analysis) Is a Strong Pathway to a Career in Finance Faculty Articles Why B.Com (Financial Analysis) Is a Strong Pathway to a Career in Finance Discover why a B.Com in Financial Analysis is a strong pathway to a rewarding finance career. Explore skills, scope, and job opportunities ahead. 970 words 4 min read These days, careers in finance require much more than just an interest in numbers. Theory is no longer the sole building block of a career in finance. These days, they want to employ students who have an understanding of markets, speak the language of financial statements, analyse risk, think ethically and apply their knowledge in a real business context. Not just this, more and more sectors like investment, advisory, research, portfolio management, corporate finance and wealth management are also expanding in this field. Simultaneously, a global finance ecosystem requires its professionals to be better prepared, more analytical and ready for the industry. This is why the right undergraduate pathway is so very consequential. The finance sector, for its part, continues to exist in an epic context of workforce shortfall, with the WHO suggesting a foreseen right now span of 11 million health workers by 2030 alone, thus underscoring how intensely professional skill pipelines matter between sectors. MIT-WPU / Events / World Tuberculosis Day Events World Tuberculosis Day Department of Public Health recently organizes two impactful events, showcasing their commitment to advancing healthcare and research. Events World Tuberculosis Day Happenings World-Tuberculosis-Day-img Events World-Tuberculosis-Day-img The Department of Public Health recently organizes two impactful events, showcasing their commitment to advancing healthcare and research. The World Tuberculosis Day event, held at MIT-World Peace University in Pune on March 24, 2023, epitomized a concerted effort in combating tuberculosis on a global scale. The event themed "Yes! We can end TB!" provided the participants with profound insights, cutting-edge research updates, and engaging discussions. Notably, the afternoon session infused fun-filled activities promoting TB awareness, fostering a sense of community involvement. This event underscored the collective determination to combat TB and marked a significant step towards a TB-free world. Related public links Happenings Department of Public Health

Everything you need to know B.Des. Communication Design It combines visual craft with strategic thinking, research, behavioural insight, technology, and systems thinking. Students complete two specialisations (one Within-Domain and one Cross-Domain), engage in intensive studios every semester, undertake extensive immersions, andwork on real-world communication projects, preparing them not just to create visuals, but to design meaningful, effective, and responsible communication systems. Yes. You choose one Within-Domain Specialisation and one Cross-Domain Specialisation. You can also design a custom Cross-Domain pathway under faculty guidance and select Directed Electives to further personalise your learning. • Visual Communication & Identity Systems Design • Animation and VFX Design • Interaction Design A four-week, credit-bearing, self-directed period after Semester 5 where students pursue an independent design research project, product development exploration, or applied design challenge. It builds design independence and often informs the final Capstone. Highly practical. The total programme fee is INR 9,00,000 per year. The programme fee for the inaugural batch is INR 7,50,000 per year only. Winter term/Summer term: If you are required to stay on campus during the winter or summer term, you must obtain special permission, and an additional fee will be applicable. Security Deposit of INR 50,000 is to be paid at the time of the Offer Acceptance Fee payment. The Security Deposit will be refunded at the time of graduation or withdrawal from the programme. The event served as a platform to introduce the vision, academic philosophy, and distinctive learning environment of WPU GŌA while addressing queries related to higher education and career opportunities. The sessions highlighted the university’s student-centric approach, immersive learning ecosystem, industry engagement, global exposure opportunities, and emphasis on holistic development. The university’s senior leadership interacted with attendees, providing insights into WPU GŌA’s academic framework and the wide range of opportunities available to students. The WPU GŌA Open House in Pune brought together aspiring students, parents, and academic leaders for an engaging exchange of ideas, aspirations, and possibilities. The event offered a firsthand look at WPU GŌA’s future-focused approach to education, where immersive learning, industry exposure, global perspectives, and holistic development come together to create meaningful learning experiences. From insightful interactions to exploring the opportunities that lie ahead, the Open House reflected WPU GŌA’s vision of shaping future-ready leaders equipped to thrive in a dynamic world. entries.excerpt: WPU GŌA has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Makers Asylum Foundation that opens new avenues of learning in the fields of innovation, entrepreneurship, design and maker-led education.

WPU Goa / News / WPU GŌA Signs MoU with Makers Asylum Foundation to Advance Innovation and Maker-Led Learning News and ideas WPU GŌA Signs MoU with Makers Asylum Foundation to Advance Innovation and Maker-Led Learning WPU GŌA has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Makers Asylum Foundation that opens new avenues of learning in the fields of innovation, entrepreneurship, design and maker-led education. WPU GŌA has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Makers Asylum Foundation that opens new avenues of learning in the fields of innovation, entrepreneurship, design and maker-led education. The collaboration brings together WPU GŌA’s academic ecosystem and Makers Asylum’s hands-on maker culture to help students move beyond classroom learning and engage with real-world problem-solving. This association will enable students and faculty to explore workshops, programmes, events, mentoring, guest sessions and knowledge-sharing initiatives that promote experimentation, creativity and practical application. The partnership will also enable academic exchange and unlock opportunities for collaborative programs, grants, fellowships, and institutional partnerships through government, philanthropies, corporate and other sources. The MoU seeks to build access to such opportunities to strengthen the link between ideas, skills, and meaningful impact. The collaboration gives stude

entries.title: Xchange Sphere 2026: Fostering Global Entrepreneurship
entries.excerpt: MIT World Peace University successfully hosted Xchange Sphere 2026 on 2 February 2026, a unique India-Spain entrepreneurship exchange that brought together students from MIT-WPU, University of Mondragon, and Travelling University, Spain.
entries.text: Xchange Sphere 2026: Global Startup Pitches at MIT-WPU Xchange Sphere 2026: Fostering Global Entrepreneurship MIT World Peace University successfully hosted Xchange Sphere 2026 on 2 February 2026, a unique India-Spain entrepreneurship exchange that brought together students from MIT-WPU, University of Mondragon, and Travelling University, Spain. MIT-WPU / Events / Xchange Sphere 2026: Fostering Global Entrepreneurship Events Xchange Sphere 2026: Fostering Global Entrepreneurship MIT World Peace University successfully hosted Xchange Sphere 2026 on 2 February 2026, a unique India-Spain entrepreneurship exchange that brought together students from MIT-WPU, University of Mondragon, and Travelling University, Spain. Events Happenings Events MIT World Peace University successfully hosted Xchange Sphere 2026 on 2 February 2026, a unique India–Spain entrepreneurship exchange that brought together students from MIT-WPU, University of Mondragon, and Travelling University, Spain. Xchange Sphere 2026: Fostering Global Entrepreneurship The event featured 20 startup teams—10 from MIT-WPU and 10 from Spain—who presented innovative business ideas through concise three-minute pitches, followed by interactive Q&A sessions. The platform encouraged participants to exchange ideas, receive constructive feedback, and refine their entrepreneurial thinking in a collaborative international environment. A key highlight of the event was the opportunity for students from both countries to engage in meaningful cross-cultural interactions, sharing diverse perspectives on innovation, market potential, business scalability, and entrepreneurship. These discussions not only broadened their global outlook but also encouraged collaborative problem-solving and knowledge exchange. The presence of the Spanis

Achievement Achievements yashodhan-patil MIT-WPU, Pune, proudly congratulates Yashodhan Patil, First Year BBA student, for his stellar performance at the 63rd National Roller Skating Championship 2025—securing Gold in Street and Bronze in Park (Skateboarding). This national-level achievement showcases his dedication, discipline, and ability to excel beyond academics, representing MIT-WPU, Pune with distinction. Yashodhan's success inspires peers and exemplifies the pursuit of excellence in sports and studies.

entries.text: Young Democrates Club At MIT-WPU Young Democrates The Young Democrats Club is an inspiring initiative led by students of BA (Hons) Political Science. It embodies the essence of democracy and serves as a platform for students to engage in a wide range of activities related to political awareness and leadership. MIT-WPU / Young Democrates Young Democrates The Young Democrats Club is an inspiring initiative led by students of BA (Hons) Political Science. It embodies the essence of democracy and serves as a platform for students to engage in a wide range of activities related to political awareness and leadership. Student Clubs Young Democrates The Young Democrats Club is an inspiring initiative led by students of BA (Hons) Political Science. It embodies the essence of democracy and serves as a platform for students to engage in a wide range of activities related to political awareness and leadership. From guest lectures by influential policymakers and political icons to stimulating debates, elocution and insightful polls, we aim to enhance students' understanding of the political landscape. This club nurtures the potential for future political leaders. The Young Democrats Club is committed to fostering an environment that encourages democratic participation and civic engagement. Our activities extend beyond the classroom, providing opportunities for students to interact with social workers and eminent personalities. We organise thought-provoking events and campaigns that delve deep into political scenarios and stimulate meaningful discussions among our members.
